title: Epic: Migrate OAuth scopes from transitional to granular (production only)
description: Currently OAUTH_SCOPE is a single constant 'atproto transition:generic' used in all environments. For production, we should use granular scopes that precisely describe what the app needs: repo access to hypercert collections + profiles, blob uploads, and RPC calls. For loopback (local dev), transition:generic must remain (ATProto requirement for loopback clients). The granular scope constants already exist in lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts (HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, RPC_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE) but are unused. However, HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE is incomplete ŌĆö it's missing collections the app writes to (app.certified.actor.profile, app.bsky.actor.profile, org.hypercerts.claim.attachment, org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails, org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation, org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag). Success: production deployments request only the granular permissions needed; loopback still uses transition:generic; all existing functionality works.

title: Update HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE to include all collections the app writes to
description:
## Files
- lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts (modify)

## What to do
The `HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE` constant (line 11-12) is missing several collections that the app and SDK internally write to. Update it to include ALL collections from `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` that the app needs write access to.

Current scope has these collections:
- org.hypercerts.claim.activity Ō£ō
- org.hypercerts.claim.contribution Ō£ō  
- org.hypercerts.claim.evaluation Ō£ō
- org.hypercerts.claim.evidence Ō£ō
- org.hypercerts.claim.measurement Ō£ō
- org.hypercerts.claim.collection Ō£ō
- org.hypercerts.claim.rights Ō£ō
- org.hypercerts.claim.funding Ō£ō
- app.certified.location Ō£ō

Add these missing collections:
- org.hypercerts.claim.attachment (used by addAttachment in app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts)
- org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails (SDK creates these internally during hypercert creation with contributions)
- org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation (SDK creates these internally during hypercert creation with contributions)
- org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag (SDK may create these for work scope references)
- app.certified.actor.profile (used by profile create/update in app/api/profile/update/route.ts)
- app.bsky.actor.profile (used by bsky profile create/update in app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts)

Use `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` to build the scope string programmatically instead of hardcoding collection names. Import it and construct the scope like:

```typescript
import { HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";

const REPO_COLLECTIONS = [
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CLAIM,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.RIGHTS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.LOCATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTOR_INFORMATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.MEASUREMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.EVALUATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.ATTACHMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.COLLECTION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.FUNDING_RECEIPT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.WORK_SCOPE_TAG,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.BSKY_PROFILE,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CERTIFIED_PROFILE,
];

const collectionParams = REPO_COLLECTIONS.map(c => `collection=${c}`).join("&");
export const HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE = `repo?${collectionParams}&action=create&action=update&action=delete`;
```

Also remove the comment "// Future granular scopes (not yet used)" on line 10 since they will now be used.

## Don't
- Change RPC_SCOPE or BLOB_SCOPE ŌĆö they are already correct
- Change how the SDK is initialized
- Change any exports or the default export
acceptance_criteria:
1. HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE includes all collections from HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS that the app writes to (at minimum: activity, rights, location, contributionDetails, contributorInformation, measurement, evaluation, attachment, collection, funding receipt, workScopeTag, bsky profile, certified profile).
2. Collection names are sourced from the HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS constant, not hardcoded strings.
3. The scope string retains action=create&action=update&action=delete.
4. The "Future granular scopes" comment is removed.
5. npm run build succeeds with no type errors.
notes: Merged into hypercerts-02l.2 ŌĆö scope definition and env-switching are one atomic change

title: Make OAUTH_SCOPE environment-aware: granular for production, transitional for loopback
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)
- lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts (modify)

## What to do
Currently `OAUTH_SCOPE` in `lib/config.ts` (line 19) is a single constant:
```typescript
export const OAUTH_SCOPE = [ATPROTO_SCOPE, TRANSITION_SCOPES.GENERIC].join(" ");
```

This is used everywhere regardless of environment. Change it so:
- **Loopback (local dev):** keeps `"atproto transition:generic"` (ATProto requirement)
- **Production:** uses the granular scopes: `ATPROTO_SCOPE + HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE + RPC_SCOPE + BLOB_SCOPE`

### In lib/config.ts:

1. Import `HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE`, `RPC_SCOPE`, `BLOB_SCOPE` from `./hypercerts-sdk` ŌĆö BUT this creates a circular dependency since `hypercerts-sdk.ts` imports from `config.ts`. Instead, move the scope constants into `config.ts` or create a new file. The cleanest approach: move the granular scope construction into `config.ts` itself.

   Import `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` (already importing from there) and build the scopes in `config.ts`:

```typescript
import { ATPROTO_SCOPE, TRANSITION_SCOPES, HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";

// Granular repo scope ŌĆö all collections the app needs write access to
const REPO_COLLECTIONS = [
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CLAIM,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.RIGHTS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.LOCATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTOR_INFORMATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.MEASUREMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.EVALUATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.ATTACHMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.COLLECTION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.FUNDING_RECEIPT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.WORK_SCOPE_TAG,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.BSKY_PROFILE,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CERTIFIED_PROFILE,
];

const HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE = `repo?${REPO_COLLECTIONS.map(c => "collection=" + c).join("&")}&action=create&action=update&action=delete`;
const RPC_SCOPE = "rpc:app.bsky.actor.getProfile?aud=did:web:api.bsky.app%23bsky_appview";
const BLOB_SCOPE = "blob?accept=video/*&accept=image/*";

const GRANULAR_SCOPE = [ATPROTO_SCOPE, HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, RPC_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE].join(" ");
const LOOPBACK_SCOPE = [ATPROTO_SCOPE, TRANSITION_SCOPES.GENERIC].join(" ");
```

2. Change `OAUTH_SCOPE` to be a function of the base URL (loopback detection already exists in this file):

```typescript
function getOAuthScope(baseUrl: string): string {
  if (isLoopback(baseUrl)) {
    return LOOPBACK_SCOPE;
  }
  return GRANULAR_SCOPE;
}
```

3. Replace the current `OAUTH_SCOPE` constant (line 19) with the function call. Since `baseUrl` is computed at module level (line 154), place the scope computation after it:

```typescript
export const OAUTH_SCOPE = getOAuthScope(baseUrl);
```

   Move this line to after `const baseUrl = getBaseUrl();` (around line 154), or keep the export at the top and make it lazy. The simplest: move the export to after line 154.

4. Update the JSDoc comment above OAUTH_SCOPE to explain the dual behavior.

### In lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts:

5. Remove the now-redundant scope constants (`HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE`, `RPC_SCOPE`, `BLOB_SCOPE`) since they are now defined in `config.ts`. Keep the `export { OAUTH_SCOPE }` re-export.

## Don't
- Change the loopback detection logic
- Change how `buildClientId` works (it already receives scope as a parameter)
- Change the SDK initialization (it already uses `config.scope`)
- Change `client-metadata.json/route.ts` ŌĆö it already imports OAUTH_SCOPE from config
- Break the circular dependency: config.ts must NOT import from hypercerts-sdk.ts
acceptance_criteria:
1. When running locally (loopback), OAUTH_SCOPE equals "atproto transition:generic".
2. When running in production (non-loopback), OAUTH_SCOPE contains granular scopes: atproto, repo?collection=...&action=..., rpc:..., blob?....
3. The scope constants (HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, RPC_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE) are removed from lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts.
4. No circular dependency between config.ts and hypercerts-sdk.ts.
5. All existing imports of OAUTH_SCOPE continue to work (from both lib/config and lib/hypercerts-sdk).
6. npm run build succeeds with no type errors.
close_reason: a3b2ede Make OAUTH_SCOPE environment-aware: granular for production, loopback for dev

title: Fix BLOB_SCOPE to allow all MIME types, not just image/* and video/*
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)

## What to do
Line 38 currently restricts blob uploads to only images and videos:
```typescript
const BLOB_SCOPE = "blob?accept=video/*&accept=image/*";
```

The app uploads attachments of arbitrary types (markdown, PDF, GeoJSON, etc.) via the add-attachment flow. This causes the error: `Failed to upload blob: Missing required scope "blob:text/markdown"`.

Change BLOB_SCOPE to use the wildcard MIME type `*/*` which matches any content type:
```typescript
const BLOB_SCOPE = "blob:*/*";
```

Note the syntax difference: `blob:*/*` (colon, single MIME pattern) vs `blob?accept=...` (query params, multiple patterns). The `blob:*/*` form is the correct ATProto scope syntax for a single wildcard MIME type. The SDK's `hasPermission` function confirms `blob:*/*` matches any specific MIME type like `blob:text/markdown`.

## Don't
- Change any other scope constants
- Add individual MIME types ŌĆö use the wildcard instead
acceptance_criteria:
1. BLOB_SCOPE on line 38 of lib/config.ts is changed to "blob:*/*".
2. npm run build succeeds with no type errors.
close_reason: b403f8b fix BLOB_SCOPE to use wildcard */* for all MIME types

title: Test: Remove RPC_SCOPE from granular OAuth scope
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)

## What to do
Remove `RPC_SCOPE` from the `GRANULAR_SCOPE` array. The app calls `app.bsky.actor.getProfile` via the public Bluesky API (`https://public.api.bsky.app`) in `lib/api/external/bluesky.ts`, not through the authenticated ATProto session, so this RPC scope permission is unnecessary.

1. Remove `RPC_SCOPE` from the `GRANULAR_SCOPE` array (line 40-45). Change from:
```typescript
const GRANULAR_SCOPE = [
  ATPROTO_SCOPE,
  HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE,
  RPC_SCOPE,
  BLOB_SCOPE,
].join(" ");
```
To:
```typescript
const GRANULAR_SCOPE = [
  ATPROTO_SCOPE,
  HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE,
  BLOB_SCOPE,
].join(" ");
```

2. Remove the `RPC_SCOPE` constant definition (lines 36-37):
```typescript
const RPC_SCOPE =
  "rpc:app.bsky.actor.getProfile?aud=did:web:api.bsky.app%23bsky_appview";
```

## Don't
- Change HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE, or LOOPBACK_SCOPE
- Change any other file
- Remove BSKY_PROFILE from REPO_COLLECTIONS (that is still needed for bsky profile editing)
acceptance_criteria:
1. RPC_SCOPE constant is removed from lib/config.ts.
2. GRANULAR_SCOPE no longer includes any rpc: scope.
3. LOOPBACK_SCOPE is unchanged.
4. npm run build succeeds with no type errors.
close_reason: 510d690 cancelled: reversing decision, RPC_SCOPE will be added back

title: Epic: Replace SDK read operations with native atproto repo calls
description:
## Why
Phase 2 of the SDK removal migration. Phase 1 (hypercerts-acu) replaced the auth layer. Now we replace all read operations that use `ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get/list`, `ctx.scopedRepo.records.get`, `repo.profile.getCertifiedProfile`, and `repo.profile.getBskyProfile` with direct `agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord` and `agent.com.atproto.repo.listRecords` calls.

## Goal
After this epic, every read operation uses native `@atproto/api` Agent methods. No `scopedRepo` read calls remain. All `@ts-expect-error` comments on read operations are removed.

## Reference pattern
`lib/queries.ts` already has working native atproto read helpers:
- `getRecord<T>(rkey, did, agent, collection)` ŌåÆ `agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({ repo: did, collection, rkey })`
- `getRecordWithURI<T>(uri, agent, fallbackCollection)` ŌåÆ parses AT-URI then calls getRecord

## Scope
17 read operations across these files:
- Profile reads: app/page.tsx, providers/SignedInProvider.tsx, app/profile/page.tsx, app/bsky-profile/page.tsx, lib/create-actions.ts (getActiveProfileInfo), app/api/profile/update/route.ts (2x), app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (2x)
- Hypercert list: app/hypercerts/page.tsx
- Hypercert get: app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx (2x), app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx
- Generic record gets: lib/create-actions.ts (getMeasurementRecord, getEvaluationRecord, getEvidenceRecord, getContributorInformationRecord)

## Constraints
- Do NOT touch write operations (create, update, delete) ŌĆö those are Phase 3-4
- Profile update routes have both reads and writes ŌĆö only migrate the reads, keep @ts-expect-error on writes
- The app must build (pnpm build) after all tasks complete
- Keep @hypercerts-org/sdk-core in package.json (still needed for type imports in other files)

## Success
- All read operations use agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord or listRecords
- No scopedRepo read calls remain
- pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: 52ffa54 All Phase 2 read operation tasks completed and verified

title: Migrate profile reads to native atproto getRecord ŌĆö page.tsx, SignedInProvider, profile/page, bsky-profile/page, getActiveProfileInfo
description:
## Files
- app/page.tsx (modify)
- providers/SignedInProvider.tsx (modify)
- app/profile/page.tsx (modify)
- app/bsky-profile/page.tsx (modify)
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify ŌĆö only getActiveProfileInfo function, lines 16-29)

## What to do
Replace all `repo.profile.getCertifiedProfile()` and `repo.profile.getBskyProfile()` calls with direct `agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord()` calls. These are all read-only profile fetches.

### Pattern for certified profile reads:
```ts
// Old:
const profile = await repo.profile.getCertifiedProfile();

// New:
const profileResult = await agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({
  repo: agent.assertDid,
  collection: 'app.certified.actor.profile',
  rkey: 'self',
}).catch(() => null);
const profile = profileResult?.data?.value as Record<string, unknown> | null ?? null;
```

### Pattern for bsky profile reads:
```ts
// Old:
const profile = await repo.profile.getBskyProfile();

// New:
const profileResult = await agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({
  repo: agent.assertDid,
  collection: 'app.bsky.actor.profile',
  rkey: 'self',
}).catch(() => null);
const profile = profileResult?.data?.value as Record<string, unknown> | null ?? null;
```

**IMPORTANT**: Profile records use rkey `'self'` ŌĆö this is the ATProto convention for singleton records (one per repo). Verify this by checking the SDK source or testing.

### File-by-file changes:

**app/page.tsx** (line 29-32):
- Currently: `await personalRepo.profile.getCertifiedProfile().catch(() => null)`
- Replace with getRecord call using `agent.assertDid` and collection `'app.certified.actor.profile'`
- The `personalRepo` variable is from `getAgent()` ŌĆö it's already an Agent
- Fields used: `profile?.displayName`, `profile?.handle`

**providers/SignedInProvider.tsx** (lines 18-28):
- Currently stubbed out with TODO comment (from Phase 1 hotfix)
- Re-implement: get agent, call getRecord for certified profile
- Fields used: `profile?.avatar` (passed to `convertBlobUrlToCdn`), `profile?.handle`
- Need to re-add `import { getAgent } from '@/lib/atproto-session'` and `import { convertBlobUrlToCdn } from '@/lib/utils'`

**app/profile/page.tsx** (line 22):
- Currently: `await repo.profile.getCertifiedProfile()`
- Replace with getRecord call
- Fields used: displayName, description, pronouns, website, avatar (via convertBlobUrlToCdn), banner (via convertBlobUrlToCdn)

**app/bsky-profile/page.tsx** (line 21):
- Currently: `await repo.profile.getBskyProfile()`
- Replace with getRecord call using collection `'app.bsky.actor.profile'`
- Fields used: displayName, description, avatar, banner

**lib/create-actions.ts** ŌĆö `getActiveProfileInfo` (lines 16-29):
- Currently: `ctx.scopedRepo.profile.getCertifiedProfile()`
- Replace with: `ctx.agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({ repo: ctx.targetDid, collection: 'app.certified.actor.profile', rkey: 'self' })`
- Note: uses `ctx.agent` and `ctx.targetDid` (from getRepoContext), NOT agent.assertDid
- Fields used: profile.displayName, profile.handle
- Remove the `@ts-expect-error` comment on this read

### Profile record rkey:
The rkey for profile records is `'self'`. This is standard ATProto convention. Verify by checking: `grep -r 'self' node_modules/@hypercerts-org/sdk-core/` or the lexicon definitions.

## Don't
- Don't touch write operations in profile update routes (those are Phase 3-4)
- Don't change any non-profile code
- Don't remove @ts-expect-error comments on write operations
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 5 files use agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord for profile reads
2. No repo.profile.getCertifiedProfile() or repo.profile.getBskyProfile() calls remain in these files
3. All @ts-expect-error comments on profile READ operations are removed
4. Profile data (displayName, handle, avatar, etc.) is correctly extracted from getRecord response
5. pnpm build passes
close_reason: 8897381 Migrate profile reads to native atproto getRecord

title: Migrate profile reads in API update routes ŌĆö profile/update and bsky/update

## What to do
Replace the profile read operations in these API routes with direct `agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord()` calls. These routes have BOTH reads and writes ŌĆö only migrate the reads in this task.

### app/api/profile/update/route.ts ŌĆö 2 reads to migrate:

title: Migrate hypercert get reads in detail and edit pages to native getRecord
description:
## Files
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx (modify)
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace `viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get(decodedUri)` with a direct `agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord()` call. There are 3 instances total (2 in detail page, 1 in edit page).

### How scopedRepo.hypercerts.get(uri) works:
It takes an AT-URI like `at://did:plc:xxx/org.hypercerts.claim.activity/rkey123`, parses it to extract the DID, collection, and rkey, then calls getRecord. We need to do the same parsing.

### Use the existing parseAtUri helper:
`lib/utils.ts` exports `parseAtUri(uri)` which returns `{ did, collection, rkey } | null`. Use this.

### Pattern:
```ts
import { parseAtUri } from '@/lib/utils';

// Old:
const cert = await viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get(decodedUri);

// New:
const parsed = parseAtUri(decodedUri);
const certResult = parsed ? await viewCtx.agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({
  repo: parsed.did,
  collection: parsed.collection || 'org.hypercerts.claim.activity',
  rkey: parsed.rkey,
}).catch(() => null) : null;
const cert = certResult ? { record: certResult.data.value } : null;
```

### app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx ŌĆö 2 reads:

**Read 1 (line 32)** ŌĆö in `generateMetadata`:
```ts
// @ts-expect-error
const cert = await viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get(decodedUri);
```
Replace with getRecord pattern. Fields used: `cert.record.title`, `cert.record.shortDescription`

**Read 2 (line 128)** ŌĆö in page body:
```ts
// @ts-expect-error
const cert = await viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get(decodedUri);
```
Replace with getRecord pattern. Fields used: `cert.record.image` (destructured), `cert.record` (rest spread to component)

### app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx ŌĆö 1 read:

**Read 1 (line 66)**:
```ts
// @ts-expect-error
const cert = await viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get(decodedUri);
```
Replace with getRecord pattern. Fields used: `cert.record.image` (destructured), `cert.record` (rest spread to form)

### Important:
- The `ownerDid` is already extracted from the URI earlier in each file via `extractDidFromAtUri(decodedUri)`
- The `viewCtx` is created with `getRepoContext({ targetDid: ownerDid })` ŌĆö so `viewCtx.agent` is authenticated as the current user but we read from `ownerDid`'s repo
- Use `parsed.did` (or `ownerDid`) as the `repo` parameter
- Remove all `@ts-expect-error` comments on these read lines
- Keep the `OrgHypercertsDefs` import (used for image type casting in the template)

## Don't
- Don't change the image blob URL logic
- Don't change the template/JSX rendering
- Don't remove OrgHypercertsDefs import
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 3 scopedRepo.hypercerts.get() calls replaced with agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord
2. AT-URI is parsed using parseAtUri to extract did, collection, rkey
3. @ts-expect-error comments removed from read lines
4. cert.record shape is preserved for downstream template usage
5. pnpm build passes
close_reason: 4d2f976 Migrate hypercert get reads to native getRecord

title: Verify build passes after all Phase 2 read migrations
description:
## Files
- Any file that fails pnpm build due to Phase 2 changes

## What to do
After tasks .1 through .5 are complete, run `pnpm build` and fix any remaining type errors caused by the read migrations.

### Expected issues:
1. **Type mismatches**: The native `getRecord` returns `{ data: { uri, cid, value: unknown } }` ŌĆö callers may need type assertions on `value` to match what they expect
2. **Missing imports**: If any file needs `parseAtUri` or other utils that weren't imported before
3. **Null handling**: Native getRecord throws on 404 instead of returning null ŌĆö callers that relied on null returns may need try/catch wrappers

### Process:
1. Run `pnpm build`
2. Fix each error:
   - Type errors on read results: add appropriate type assertions
   - Missing imports: add them
   - Null handling: wrap in try/catch where needed
3. Repeat until build succeeds

title: Fix: logout() server action in create-actions.ts does not clear user-did cookie

title: Fix: post-write getBlobURL call in certified profile update route uses fragile as-unknown-as cast on stored blob object (from hypercerts-fpyy.5)
description:
Review of hypercerts-fpyy.5 found a type-safety issue in app/api/profile/update/route.ts.

## Problem

After writing the certified profile record, the route reads it back with `getRecord` and then calls `getBlobURL` on the returned `avatar`/`banner` fields (lines 189ŌĆō204):

```ts
getBlobURL(
  updated?.avatar as unknown as Parameters<typeof getBlobURL>[0],
  repo.assertDid,
  pdsUrl,
)
```

`AppCertifiedActorProfile.Record.avatar` is typed as `$Typed<Uri> | $Typed<SmallImage> | { $type: string }`, but `getBlobURL` expects `BlobRef | string | undefined`. The double cast hides a genuine type mismatch.

At runtime this works incidentally because the stored blob object (`blob.original`) has a `ref` property that `getBlobURL` checks for ŌĆö but this is undocumented and fragile. If the certified profile lexicon is ever updated to store a URI string instead of a blob object, this will silently return `undefined` and the avatar URL will be empty.

## Fix

Either:
1. Add a helper that safely extracts a URL from the certified profile's avatar union type (checking for `$type: 'blob'` and extracting the CID, or `$type: 'org.hypercerts.defs#uri'` and returning the URI string directly), OR
2. Extend `getBlobURL` to accept the certified profile avatar union type explicitly.

This is lower priority than hypercerts-ei70 (the validation failure) but should be addressed to avoid future breakage.
close_reason: 8cb56d0 Fix: replace fragile as-unknown-as cast with type-safe getCertifiedProfileImageURL helper

title: Epic: Replace ePDS OAuth session cookie with Redis-backed state store
description: The ePDS OAuth callback frequently fails with 'No OAuth session cookie found' because the epds-oauth-session cookie (sameSite: strict) is not sent on cross-site redirects from the ePDS authorization server back to our callback. The fix is to store transient OAuth state (codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk) in Redis keyed by the state parameter ŌĆö exactly how the standard ATProto flow already works via RedisStateStore. This eliminates the cookie dependency entirely. Success: ePDS login flow works reliably without the session cookie. Key constraint: do not change the standard ATProto flow (Flow A) at all.

title: Add RedisEpdsStateStore class to redis-state-store.ts
description:
## Files
- lib/redis-state-store.ts (modify)

## What to do
Add a new exported interface and class to the existing file:

```typescript
export interface EpdsOAuthState {
  codeVerifier: string;
  dpopPrivateJwk: JsonWebKey;
}
```

Add a new class `RedisEpdsStateStore` with the same get/set/del pattern as the existing `RedisStateStore`, but:
- Prefix: `"epds-oauth-state:"` (stored as a const `EPDS_STATE_PREFIX`)
- Stored type: `EpdsOAuthState` instead of `NodeSavedState`
- TTL on set: 600 seconds (same as `STATE_EXPIRATION_SECONDS` ŌĆö reuse the existing constant)
- Methods: `set(state: string, data: EpdsOAuthState): Promise<void>`, `get(state: string): Promise<EpdsOAuthState | undefined>`, `del(state: string): Promise<void>`
- Uses the same `redisClient` import already in the file

Then export a singleton instance from `lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts`:
```typescript
import { RedisEpdsStateStore } from "./redis-state-store";
export const epdsStateStore = new RedisEpdsStateStore();
```

## Don't
- Modify the existing `RedisStateStore` or `RedisSessionStore` classes in any way
- Change any existing exports or their types
- Make the class generic ŌĆö keep it simple and concrete
acceptance_criteria:
1. `RedisEpdsStateStore` class exists in `lib/redis-state-store.ts` with `set`, `get`, `del` methods
2. Uses prefix `epds-oauth-state:` (distinct from `oauth-state:`)
3. `set` stores JSON with 600s TTL via `redisClient.set(key, JSON.stringify(data), { EX: 600 })`
4. `get` returns parsed `EpdsOAuthState` or `undefined`
5. `del` removes the key
6. `EpdsOAuthState` interface is exported with `codeVerifier: string` and `dpopPrivateJwk: JsonWebKey`
7. `epdsStateStore` singleton is exported from `lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts`
8. Existing `RedisStateStore` and `RedisSessionStore` classes are unchanged
9. `npm run build` passes (no type errors)
close_reason: 5b3c9a2 Add RedisEpdsStateStore class and epdsStateStore singleton

title: Update ePDS callback route to read state from Redis instead of cookie
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Replace all cookie-based session reading with Redis-backed state retrieval.

### Imports to add:
```typescript
import { epdsStateStore } from "@/lib/hypercerts-sdk";
```

### Imports to remove:
```typescript
import { decodeEpdsSessionCookie, EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME } from "@/lib/epds-session-cookie";
```

### Changes in the GET handler:

1. **Replace lines 31-63** (the entire cookie read + decode + state verify block) with:
```typescript
// 2. Retrieve OAuth state from Redis
const oauthState = await epdsStateStore.get(state);
if (!oauthState) {
  console.error("[oauth/callback] No OAuth state found in Redis for state:", state);
  return NextResponse.redirect(new URL("/?error=auth_failed", config.baseUrl));
}

const { codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk } = oauthState;
```
Note: state verification is no longer needed because the state IS the key ŌĆö if Redis returns data for that state, it matches by definition.

2. **Add cleanup after successful token exchange** (after line 103, after confirming `tokenResponse.ok`):
```typescript
// Clean up the transient OAuth state from Redis
await epdsStateStore.del(state);
```
Place this AFTER the token exchange succeeds (after the `!tokenResponse.ok` check), not before. If the exchange fails, the state should remain so the user can retry within the 600s TTL.

3. **Remove lines 174-181** (step 14 ŌĆö the block that deletes the `epds-oauth-session` cookie from the response). This is no longer needed since there is no cookie to delete.

4. Keep everything else unchanged ŌĆö DPoP key restoration, token exchange, NodeSavedSession construction, Redis session write, user-did cookie set.

## Don't
- Change the token exchange logic
- Change the NodeSavedSession construction or Redis session write
- Change the user-did cookie (that stays ŌĆö it is set on the direct response to the user, not a cross-site redirect)
- Delete the state before the token exchange succeeds
- Touch any other route files
acceptance_criteria:
1. `epdsStateStore.get(state)` is called to retrieve OAuth state (no cookie reading)
2. No reference to `decodeEpdsSessionCookie` or `EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME` anywhere in the file
3. No `req.cookies.get(...)` call for the session cookie
4. `epdsStateStore.del(state)` is called AFTER the token exchange succeeds (after `tokenResponse.ok` check)
5. No block that deletes the `epds-oauth-session` cookie from the response (old step 14 is gone)
6. The `user-did` cookie set block (old step 13) is unchanged
7. DPoP key restoration, token exchange, NodeSavedSession construction are all unchanged
8. `npm run build` passes
close_reason: 2039877 Update ePDS callback route to read state from Redis instead of cookie

title: Fix: epdsStateStore.del(state) called after token exchange succeeds but before sub validation ŌĆö leaked state on invalid sub (from hypercerts-0lg)
description: Review of hypercerts-0lg found: In app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, epdsStateStore.del(state) is called at line ~80 immediately after tokenResponse.ok is confirmed (step 9ŌåÆcleanup), but BEFORE the sub validation check at step 10a. If tokenData.sub is missing or not a DID, the route redirects to /?error=auth_failed ŌĆö but the Redis state has already been deleted. This is actually correct behaviour (state should be consumed once the code is exchanged), but the placement is misleading: the comment says 'Clean up the transient OAuth state' yet it sits between step 9 and step 10, not at the end of the happy path. More critically, if sessionStore.set() throws (Redis down after token exchange), the state is already deleted and the user gets a 500 with no session written ŌĆö they cannot retry without starting a new login. The del should be moved to after sessionStore.set() succeeds, or wrapped in a try/finally that still deletes on error. Evidence: callback route lines 80-83 vs lines 95-105 (sub validation) and lines 140-145 (sessionStore.set).
close_reason: Subsumed by 0lg.6 ŌĆö GETDEL eliminates the separate del() call entirely, making placement irrelevant

title: Fix: no atomic get-and-delete on Redis state ŌĆö double-callback race condition allows state reuse (from hypercerts-0lg)
description:
## Files
- lib/redis-state-store.ts (modify)
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. `lib/redis-state-store.ts` ŌĆö Make `get()` atomic via GETDEL

Change `RedisEpdsStateStore.get()` to use `redisClient.getDel(key)` instead of `redisClient.get(key)`. This atomically reads and deletes the key in a single Redis round-trip, eliminating the race window where two concurrent callbacks can both read the same state.

```typescript
async get(state: string): Promise<EpdsOAuthState | undefined> {
  const key = `${EPDS_STATE_PREFIX}${state}`;
  const data = await redisClient.getDel(key);
  return data ? (JSON.parse(data) as EpdsOAuthState) : undefined;
}
```

The `del()` method stays as-is (it is still useful for explicit cleanup in other scenarios), but it will no longer be called from the callback route.

### 2. `app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts` ŌĆö Remove the separate `del()` call

Remove the line:
```typescript
// Clean up the transient OAuth state from Redis
await epdsStateStore.del(state);
```

This line (currently around line 83-84) is no longer needed because `get()` now atomically deletes. Removing it also eliminates:
- The del-placement bug (0lg.5): no separate del to misplace
- The del-error-propagation bug (0lg.9): no separate del that can throw and redirect a successful auth to an error page

## Don't
- Change `RedisStateStore` or `RedisSessionStore` (only change `RedisEpdsStateStore`)
- Change the login route
- Add any new imports or exports
- Change the `set()` or `del()` method signatures
acceptance_criteria:
1. `RedisEpdsStateStore.get()` uses `redisClient.getDel(key)` instead of `redisClient.get(key)`
2. No `epdsStateStore.del(state)` call exists in `app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts`
3. The `del()` method still exists on `RedisEpdsStateStore` (unchanged)
4. `RedisStateStore` and `RedisSessionStore` are unchanged
5. `npm run build` passes
close_reason: b6e41a0 fix: atomic get-and-delete for ePDS OAuth state

title: Cleanup: oauthSessionSecret / OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is now dead config ŌĆö remove or document (from hypercerts-0lg)
description: Review of hypercerts-0lg found: lib/config.ts still exposes config.oauthSessionSecret (mapped from OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET env var) and the comment on that field still says 'Must be 32+ characters. Only needed if ePDS login is used.' Now that lib/epds-session-cookie.ts has been deleted and the Redis-backed store requires no secret, this env var and config field are dead. Operators who read the config will be confused about whether they still need to set OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET. Either remove the field entirely (and update any deployment docs/env templates) or update the comment to say it is no longer required. Evidence: lib/config.ts line ~220 oauthSessionSecret field; grep for OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET returns zero hits in application code after the deletion of epds-session-cookie.ts.

title: Fix: DPoP private key stored in Redis without TLS ŌĆö add TLS support to redis.ts (from hypercerts-0lg)
description:
Review of hypercerts-0lg found: The DPoP private JWK (including the private 'd' parameter) is stored as plaintext JSON in Redis via RedisEpdsStateStore. The redis.ts client has no TLS configuration ŌĆö the socket config only sets host/port/reconnect/timeout, with no 'tls: true' or certificate options. The .env.example documents cloud Redis with only REDIS_PASSWORD, no TLS flag. In production, if Redis is accessed over a network (cloud Redis, Railway, Upstash, etc.), the private key material transits and rests unencrypted.

Evidence: lib/redis.ts lines 4-15 ŌĆö no tls field in socket config. lib/redis-state-store.ts line 68 ŌĆö JSON.stringify(data) stores dpopPrivateJwk including 'd' parameter. .env.example lines 48-51 ŌĆö cloud Redis example has no TLS env var.

Fix required:
1. Add REDIS_TLS env var support to lib/redis.ts (set socket.tls = true when REDIS_TLS=true)
2. Document REDIS_TLS=true in .env.example for cloud Redis deployments
3. Consider whether AES-256-GCM encryption of the JWK value at rest (before storing in Redis) is warranted, similar to the approach used in hypercerts-6ge.7 for the cookie

Severity: high ŌĆö private key material exposed in transit/at rest without TLS on cloud deployments.

title: Fix: epdsStateStore.del() not wrapped in try/catch ŌĆö successful auth redirects to error page on Redis failure (from hypercerts-0lg)
close_reason: Subsumed by 0lg.6 ŌĆö GETDEL eliminates the separate del() call entirely, so there is no del() to wrap in try/catch

title: Fix: epds-oauth-session cookie deletion missing security flags (from hypercerts-dhz)
description:
## Review finding from hypercerts-dhz (ePDS OAuth integration)

**File:** app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, lines 148-151

**Problem:**
The `epds-oauth-session` cookie is deleted with only `maxAge: 0` and `path: '/'`:
```ts
response.cookies.set(EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, '', {
  maxAge: 0,
  path: '/',
});
```

The original cookie was set with `httpOnly: true`, `secure: config.isProduction`, `sameSite: 'lax'`. Some browsers (notably Safari) require the deletion Set-Cookie to match the original cookie's security attributes to successfully delete it. If the flags don't match, the browser may ignore the deletion, leaving the signed session cookie alive past the callback.

**Fix:**
```ts
response.cookies.set(EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, '', {
  maxAge: 0,
  path: '/',
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: config.isProduction,
  sameSite: 'lax',
});
```

**Secondary finding:** The `user-did` cookie (line 140-145) is missing `sameSite`. This is consistent with the existing `app/api/auth/callback/route.ts` flow (which also omits it), but both should have `sameSite: 'lax'` to prevent CSRF on the session cookie.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.12 (commit c323ec7). Cookie deletion now includes httpOnly, secure, sameSite: lax flags (callback/route.ts lines 147-153).

title: Fix: DPoP nonce retry only fires on HTTP 400 ŌĆö misses 401 use_dpop_nonce errors (from hypercerts-dhz)
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.11 (commit b4f7ed9). Line 161 now checks res.status === 400 || res.status === 401.

title: Fix: email input Enter key triggers handle login form submit (from hypercerts-dhz.8/dhz.10)
description: Review of hypercerts-dhz.8 and dhz.10 found: The email InputGroupInput (line 97-103 of components/login-dialog.tsx) is inside the outer <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}> that handles handle-based login. Pressing Enter in the email field triggers handleSubmit(), which calls loginMutation.mutate(handle) with whatever is in the handle field ŌĆö not the email login flow. This is a UX bug: the user types their email, presses Enter expecting to start the ePDS flow, but instead triggers the handle-based ATProto login (or a login with an empty handle). Fix: either (a) move the email section outside the <form> tag, or (b) add onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); /* trigger email login */ } }} to the email input. Evidence: login-dialog.tsx line 42 opens <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>, line 118 closes it ŌĆö the email InputGroup at lines 92-116 is inside this form.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.16 (commit dcac8b3). Handle and Email are now separate components (HandleForm and EmailForm) with separate forms. Email form uses window.location.href, not a form submit. No cross-form Enter key issue.

title: Switch JWK key stripping from denylist to allowlist
acceptance_criteria: The PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS denylist is replaced with a PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS allowlist containing only public JWK fields (kty, crv, x, y, n, e, kid, alg). The key_ops: ["verify"] is still added to each key. No RSA private fields (p, q, dp, dq, qi, oth, d) can appear in the response. npm run build succeeds.

title: Remove raw error leak in logout route
description:
## Files
- app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 21.

**Original comment:**
> Raw error message leaked to the client. `(e as Error).message` may expose internal details (e.g., Redis connection errors, session store internals) in the API response. This is also inconsistent with the callback route which deliberately uses a generic error code. Additionally, if `e` is not an `Error` instance, `.message` will be `undefined`.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Replace the error response to use a generic message instead of the raw error:
```diff
     return NextResponse.json(
-      { error: `Logout failed: ${(e as Error).message}` },
+      { error: "Logout failed" },
       { status: 500 },
     );
```
2. Keep the existing server-side logging of the full error (the console.error on the line above).

**Context:**
The logout route catch block on line 17 already logs the full error server-side with console.error. The client response should not include the raw error message. The auth callback route already uses a generic error pattern ŌĆö this should be consistent.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818882953

## Dont
- Dont change the console.error logging line
- Dont change the HTTP status code (keep 500)
- Dont change the try block logic
- Dont introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: The logout route catch block returns { error: "Logout failed" } (or similar generic message) without including (e as Error).message or any dynamic error content. The console.error still logs the full error. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: b2c46cb fix: remove raw error leak in logout route

title: Fix loading/error state race in evaluations section
description:
## Files - components/hypercert-evaluations-section.tsx (modify) ## What to do CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 87. **Original comment:** > Loading and error states can render simultaneously. If `isLoadingLinks` is false but `isLoadingDetails` is true while `isErrorLinks` is also true, both the skeleton and error panel will render at the same time. Consider using an if/else-if chain or guarding error rendering with `!isLoading`. **Suggested fix:** Replace the three separate conditional blocks (lines 75-93) with a single if/else-if chain: ```tsx {isLoading ? (
) : isError ? (

Failed to load evaluations.

) : ( <> {evaluations && evaluations.length > 0 ? ( // ... existing content rendering ) : ( // ... existing empty state )} )} ``` The key change: the three separate `{isLoading ? ... : null}`, `{isError ? ... : null}`, `{!isLoading && !isError ? ... : null}` blocks become a single ternary chain so only one state renders at a time. **Context:** The component has `isLoading` (combined from isLoadingLinks and isLoadingDetails) and `isError` (combined from isErrorLinks and isErrorDetails). Currently lines 75-93 render them as three independent blocks, allowing skeleton and error to show simultaneously. CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818882983 ## Dont - Dont change the useMemo hooks or data fetching logic - Dont change the EvaluationSkeleton or error panel styling - Dont change the content rendering (evaluations.map) - Dont introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: The three separate conditional render blocks ({isLoading ? ...}, {isError ? ...}, {!isLoading && !isError ? ...}) are replaced with a single if/else-if ternary chain so only one of loading, error, or content renders at a time. The skeleton never appears alongside the error panel. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: 9555b4f fix: replace parallel conditional blocks with if/else-if chain in evaluations section

The onClick, className, and children (Camera icon + Upload span) are preserved. No <label> element without htmlFor remains in the upload trigger area. npm run build succeeds.

title: Fix loose includes(https) URL check in measurement view
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 118.

**Original comment:**
> `includes("https")` is a loose URL protocol check. `uri?.includes("https")` would match any URI containing "https" anywhere in the string (e.g., a path segment). Use `uri?.startsWith("https")` for a more precise protocol check.

**Suggested fix:**
Replace on line 117:
```diff
-                    uri={uri?.includes("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
+                    uri={uri?.startsWith("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
```

**Context:**
The URILink component receives a uri prop. The check determines whether to use the uri directly (if it is an https URL) or to wrap it with getPDSlsURI(). Using includes would incorrectly match URIs like "at://did:plc:xxx/app.certified.https.something" that contain "https" in a path segment.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818882990

## Dont
- Dont change the URILink component itself
- Dont change getPDSlsURI function
- Dont change other files (note: hypercert-evaluation-view.tsx has similar patterns but is NOT in scope of this comment)
- Dont introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: Line 117 of hypercert-measurement-view.tsx uses uri?.startsWith("https") instead of uri?.includes("https"). No other lines in this file are changed. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: 87309cc Fix loose includes(https) URL check in measurement view

title: Fix: CI workflow does not trigger on loopback branch ŌĆö PRs from loopback won't be checked
description:
Review of .github/workflows/ci.yml found: the workflow only triggers on pushes/PRs to 'main' and 'develop' branches (lines 5-7). The active development branch is 'loopback', which is not in the trigger list.

Evidence:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml lines 4-7:
  on:
    push:
      branches: [main, develop]
    pull_request:
      branches: [main, develop]
- The current branch is 'loopback' (git branch shows * loopback)
- All 156 changed files are on the loopback branch

This means no CI runs on any push to loopback, and no CI runs on PRs targeting loopback. Developers get no automated feedback.

Suggested fix:
Add 'loopback' to the branch lists, or use a wildcard:
  on:
    push:
      branches: [main, develop, loopback]
    pull_request:
      branches: [main, develop, loopback]

Or if loopback is a long-lived integration branch, consider triggering on all branches:
  on:
    push:
    pull_request:

title: Fix: CI workflow missing type-check and build steps ŌĆö TypeScript errors won't be caught
description:
Review of the loopback branch CI/CD tooling found: .github/workflows/ci.yml (line 14) only runs 'pnpm lint' and 'pnpm format:check'. There is no 'pnpm build' step and no 'npx tsc --noEmit' step. This means TypeScript type errors and Next.js build failures will NOT be caught by CI ŌĆö they will only surface when a developer runs 'pnpm build' locally or when a deployment fails.

Evidence:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml lines 34-38: only Lint and Format check steps
- Commit e9efe7f intentionally removed the build step (message: 'remove build step from CI workflow (hypercerts-amn)') ŌĆö but no type-check step was added as a replacement
- DEVELOPMENT.md line 298 documents 'npx tsc --noEmit' as the way to check types, but this is not in CI
- 'pnpm build' currently passes (verified locally), so re-adding it is safe

Suggested fix:
Add two steps to .github/workflows/ci.yml after 'Install dependencies':
  - name: Type check
    run: npx tsc --noEmit
  - name: Build
    run: pnpm build

If build is too slow for CI, at minimum add the type-check step. Also add a 'type-check' script to package.json: 'tsc --noEmit' so it can be run as 'pnpm type-check' consistently.

title: Fix: proxy.ts is dead code ŌĆö localhost redirect never executes, README/docs claim is false
description:
Review of proxy.ts and the Next.js build output found that the localhostŌåÆ127.0.0.1 redirect is never executed.

Evidence:
- proxy.ts exists at project root with a 'proxy()' function and 'config' matcher
- Next.js middleware MUST be in a file named 'middleware.ts' (not proxy.ts) to be auto-loaded
- find command: no middleware.ts exists anywhere in the project
- grep: proxy.ts is not imported by any other file
- .next/server/middleware-manifest.json: { 'middleware': {}, 'sortedMiddleware': [], 'functions': {} } ŌĆö NO middleware is registered
- next.config.ts line 8: 'skipProxyUrlNormalize: true' ŌĆö this option is also unnecessary since no middleware runs

Impact (HIGH):
- README.md line 45: 'The app automatically redirects localhost to 127.0.0.1' ŌĆö this is FALSE
- .env.example line 17: 'MUST use 127.0.0.1 (not localhost) for loopback oauth to work' ŌĆö correct, but the 'automatic redirect' claim in README is misleading
- Users who access http://localhost:3000 will NOT be redirected; OAuth will fail silently

Suggested fix:
Rename proxy.ts to middleware.ts at the project root. Next.js will automatically pick it up. The 'config' export with matcher '/:path*' is already correct middleware config format. No other changes needed.

After renaming, verify:
1. .next/server/middleware-manifest.json shows the middleware registered
2. Accessing http://localhost:3000 redirects to http://127.0.0.1:3000

title: Epic: Add hypercert editing, deletion, and management capabilities
description:
## Why
Currently there is no way to edit a hypercert after creation. Users need to be able to:
- Edit a hypercert's core fields (title, description, dates, image)
- Edit/update existing measurements
- Add more measurements, evaluations, contributions, and evidence to existing hypercerts
- Delete measurements, contributions, evaluations, and evidence
- Delete entire hypercerts

## What success looks like
- From the hypercert detail page, the owner can click Edit to modify core fields
- From the detail page, the owner can add new measurements/evaluations/contributions/evidence
- From the detail page, the owner can delete individual measurements/evaluations/contributions/evidence
- From the detail page, the owner can delete the entire hypercert
- All operations use the SDK's high-level methods (scopedRepo.hypercerts.update, .delete, .updateMeasurement, .addContribution, records.delete)
- Only the hypercert owner sees edit/delete controls

## Key constraints
- The SDK already supports all CRUD operations (hypercerts.update, hypercerts.delete, hypercerts.updateMeasurement, hypercerts.addContribution, records.update, records.delete)
- OAuth scopes already include create+update+delete permissions
- The existing create forms (measurement-form, evaluation-form, evidence-form) should be reusable for 'add more' flows
- The HypercertsBaseForm already has updateActions and certInfo props designed for edit mode
- Ownership check: compare session DID with the DID extracted from the hypercert AT-URI

## Scope
API routes + server actions + React Query hooks + UI for all edit/delete operations on hypercerts and their related records (measurements, evaluations, contributions, evidence).

### Evidence section specifics:

### Evaluations section specifics:

Look at how each section currently renders its items and add the delete button in a consistent position (top-right of each card).

- Do NOT change the card layout significantly

title: Fix: measurements delete button uses shared isPending ŌĆö all cards show spinner simultaneously (from hypercerts-2a3.9)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.9 found: In components/hypercert-measurements-section.tsx, a single `useDeleteRecordMutation` instance is shared across all measurement cards. When any delete is in progress, `deleteMutation.isPending` is true for ALL cards, causing every card's DeleteConfirmDialog to show the spinner/disabled state simultaneously. The evidence and evaluations sections correctly scope isPending per-item by checking `deleteRecordMutation.variables?.recordUri === recordUri`, but measurements does not. Fix: add the same per-item isPending check: `isDeleting={deleteMutation.isPending && deleteMutation.variables?.recordUri === measurement._uri}`.
close_reason: 9b382ea fix: scope isPending to per-item in measurements delete

title: Fix: edit page uses encoded URI in back-link but detail page expects encoded URI ŌĆö verify round-trip (from hypercerts-2a3.14)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.14 found: In app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx line 69, the back-link uses `/hypercerts/${hypercertUri}` where `hypercertUri` is the raw (still URL-encoded) param from Next.js. This is correct for the back-link. However, HypercertsEditForm (line 83) receives `decodedUri` (the decoded AT-URI) and internally re-encodes it for the cancel link (line 30: `encodeURIComponent(hypercertUri)`). This double-encode path is fine, but the edit page's own back-link (line 69) uses the raw encoded param directly ŌĆö if the param was double-encoded by the browser this could break. Low risk but should be consistent: use `encodeURIComponent(decodedUri)` in the back-link for clarity and consistency with how the detail page and add page construct their hrefs.

title: Fix: edit form submits with no changes ŌĆö no guard or user feedback (from hypercerts-2a3.14)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.14 found: In components/hypercerts-edit-form.tsx, the handleSubmit function (line 67) builds an updates object with only changed fields. If the user clicks 'Save Changes' without modifying anything, the updates object will only contain `{ hypercertUri }` with no actual field changes. The mutation will still fire and make a PUT request to /api/certs with an empty update. This wastes a network round-trip and gives no feedback to the user that nothing changed. Fix: before calling `updateMutation.mutate(updates)`, check if any fields beyond `hypercertUri` are present. If not, either show a toast ('No changes to save') or disable the Save button when the form is pristine.
close_reason: e8e6589 Fix: guard edit form submit when no changes, disable Save button when pristine

title: Fix: add/[type] page is a client component ŌĆö no ownership check, any user can navigate directly (from hypercerts-2a3.12)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.12 found: app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/add/[type]/page.tsx is a client component ('use client' on line 1). It has no ownership check ŌĆö any authenticated user can navigate directly to /hypercerts/<someoneElsesUri>/add/measurement and submit the form. The edit page (hypercerts-2a3.14) correctly does a server-side ownership check and redirects non-owners. The add page should do the same. Since it's a client component, the ownership check would need to be done either: (a) convert to a server component wrapper that checks ownership and passes hypercertUri down, or (b) add a client-side check using the session DID vs the DID extracted from the URI, and redirect if not owner. The forms themselves may also lack server-side auth, but the page-level guard is the first line of defense.
close_reason: 6ef8ee3 fix: add server-side ownership check to add/[type] page, converting to server component wrapper that redirects non-owners

title: Fix: add/[type] page title uses font-outfit but h1 on edit page uses font-syne ŌĆö inconsistent heading font (from hypercerts-2a3.12)

In app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx line 77, the h1 uses `font-[family-name:var(--font-syne)]`. The existing design system uses font-syne for headings (h1, h2, h3) and font-outfit for body/labels. The add page's h1 should use font-syne to match the edit page and the rest of the app.
close_reason: 066714c fix: h1 on add/[type] page uses font-syne (via add-record-form.tsx refactor)

title: Add API route: POST /api/certs/delete ŌĆö delete a hypercert
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/delete/route.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a POST route handler that deletes an existing hypercert.

**Request**: JSON body with:
- `hypercertUri` (string, required) ŌĆö AT-URI of the hypercert to delete

**Implementation**:
1. Call `getRepoContext()` ŌĆö return 401 if null
2. Parse JSON body, extract `hypercertUri`
3. Call `ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.delete(hypercertUri)`
4. Return JSON `{ success: true }` on success
5. Return 400 if `hypercertUri` missing
6. Return 500 with error message on failure

**Note**: The SDK delete does NOT cascade to related records (measurements, evaluations, etc). That is acceptable for now ŌĆö orphaned records are harmless in ATProto.

Follow the same error handling pattern as `app/api/certs/create/route.ts`.

## Dont
- Do NOT cascade-delete related records (measurements, evaluations, evidence, contributions)
- Do NOT use FormData ŌĆö use JSON body since there are no files
acceptance_criteria:
1. File app/api/certs/delete/route.ts exists and exports POST handler
2. Sending JSON { hypercertUri } returns { success: true }
3. Missing hypercertUri returns 400
4. Unauthenticated request returns 401
5. TypeScript compiles with no errors
close_reason: 9d99f8d superseded: delete moved to server action, API route not needed

The button contains a Trash2 icon and the text 'Delete', so the text provides some context, but the icon-only state (if text is ever hidden on small screens) would be inaccessible. More importantly, the default trigger in DeleteConfirmDialog (line 42-50 of delete-confirm-dialog.tsx) does have an aria-label, but when a custom trigger is passed, the aria-label responsibility falls on the caller. The detail view's custom trigger should add `aria-label="Delete hypercert"` to match the default trigger's pattern.

title: Fix: delete button overlaps card content on small screens ŌĆö absolute positioning without padding guard (from hypercerts-2a3.9)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.9 and hypercerts-2a3.10 found: In measurements, evidence, and evaluations sections, the delete button is positioned `absolute top-3 right-3` inside a `relative` wrapper div. The underlying card components (HypercertMeasurementView, HypercertEvidenceView, HypercertEvaluationView) were not designed with this overlay in mind and have no right-side padding to prevent their content from being obscured by the 32px (h-8 w-8) delete button. On narrow viewports or when card content extends to the right edge, the delete button will overlap text or other interactive elements. Fix: add `pr-12` (or similar) to the card's top-right area when isOwner is true, or restructure the card header to include the delete button in the flex layout rather than using absolute positioning.

title: Fix: edit form date comparison uses toISOString() vs record string ŌĆö timezone mismatch may cause false dirty state (from hypercerts-2a3.14)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.14 found: In components/hypercerts-edit-form.tsx lines 78-81, the dirty check for dates compares `startDate.toISOString()` against `record.startDate`. The record's startDate is stored as whatever string the SDK returns (likely a date-only string like '2024-01-15' or a full ISO string). `new Date('2024-01-15').toISOString()` returns '2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z' which will never equal '2024-01-15', causing the date to always be included in the update payload even when unchanged. This means every save will include startDate/endDate even if the user didn't touch them. Fix: normalize both sides of the comparison to the same format (e.g., compare date-only strings, or parse the record date through `new Date()` and compare ISO strings).
close_reason: 97171bd fix: normalize date comparison to YYYY-MM-DD to prevent false dirty state

title: Fix: contributors section shows '0 contributors' count even when loading (from hypercerts-2a3.11)
description: Review of hypercerts-2a3.11 found: In components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 81-84, the contributor count subtitle is always rendered: `{displayContributors.length} contributor(s)`. If `contributors` prop is undefined (not yet loaded or genuinely empty), `parseContributors(undefined)` returns an empty array, so the count shows '0 contributors' even during the loading state. The measurements, evidence, and evaluations sections conditionally render their count only when items.length > 0. The contributors section should do the same ŌĆö only show the count when displayContributors.length > 0, and hide it when loading or empty.
close_reason: 9b382ea fix: hide contributor count when loading or empty

However, in components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx line 135, the 'Add Contributor' button links to `/add/contribution` and is shown to owners. This creates a broken UX: the owner clicks 'Add Contributor', navigates to the add page, and sees 'coming soon'. The button should either be hidden until the contribution form is implemented, or the page should show a more informative message explaining why it's not available yet (e.g., 'Contribution management is coming soon. Check back later.') with a back button. Currently the page title also says 'Add Contribution to Hypercert' which is misleading.

title: Fix: PUT /api/certs and server actions leak internal error messages in 500 responses (from hypercerts-2a3.1)
description:
## Review finding from hypercerts-2a3.1

**File:** app/api/certs/route.ts (lines 57-62, 117-122)

**Problem:**
Both POST and PUT handlers include the raw SDK/internal error message in the 500 JSON response body:
```ts
error: `Failed to create hypercert: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`
error: `Failed to update hypercert: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`
```
This can expose internal implementation details, SDK internals, or PDS error messages to the client. The error is already logged server-side via `console.error`.

**Fix:**
Return a generic message to the client and keep the detail server-side only:
```ts
// Log full error server-side (already done)
console.error('Error creating hypercert:', e);
// Return generic message to client
return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Failed to create hypercert' }, { status: 500 });
```

Note: The existing `apiClient` and `apiClientFormData` in client.ts will surface this message to the UI via `APIError`, so the generic message is what users will see in toast notifications.
close_reason: 2d06eea fix: return generic 500 messages in PUT/POST /api/certs

title: Add ownership check utility and pass isOwner to detail view
description:
## Files
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx (modify)

## What to do

### 1. In `app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx`:
Compute `isOwner` by comparing the logged-in user DID with the DID from the hypercert URI.

The session is already fetched on line 71: `const [viewCtx, session] = await Promise.all([...])`.
The owner DID is already extracted on line 67: `const ownerDid = extractDidFromAtUri(decodedUri)`.

Add:
```typescript
const isOwner = Boolean(session?.did && ownerDid && session.did === ownerDid);
```

Pass `isOwner` as a prop to `<HypercertDetailsView>`:
```tsx
<HypercertDetailsView
  hypercertUri={decodedUri}
  record={certWithoutImage}
  imageUri={imageUri}
  isOwner={isOwner}
/>
```

### 2. In `components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx`:
Add `isOwner: boolean` to the props interface. Accept it and pass it down to the child section components:
- `<HypercertMeasurementsSection hypercertUri={hypercertUri} isOwner={isOwner} />`
- `<HypercertEvidenceSection hypercertUri={hypercertUri} isOwner={isOwner} />`
- `<HypercertEvaluationsSection hypercertUri={hypercertUri} isOwner={isOwner} />`
- `<HypercertContributorsSection contributors={contributors} isOwner={isOwner} hypercertUri={hypercertUri} />`

The child components do NOT need to be modified in this task ŌĆö they will accept `isOwner` as an optional prop and ignore it until the UI tasks wire it up. TypeScript will not error because we are adding an extra prop that the components dont destructure (React ignores unknown props on custom components).

Actually, to be type-safe, add `isOwner?: boolean` to each section component props. In each of these files, find the props type/interface and add `isOwner?: boolean`:
- components/hypercert-measurements-section.tsx
- components/hypercert-evidence-section.tsx
- components/hypercert-evaluations-section.tsx
- components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx

Just add the prop to the type ŌĆö do NOT use it yet.

## Dont
- Do NOT add any edit/delete buttons yet
- Do NOT change the visual appearance of any component
- Do NOT modify the session check logic

4. All 4 section components accept isOwner?: boolean in their props

title: Add delete buttons to measurements section on detail view
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-measurements-section.tsx (modify)

## What to do
When `isOwner` is true, show a delete button next to each measurement card. When clicked, it should delete the measurement record.

### Changes:
1. Destructure `isOwner` from props (already added as optional prop in task 2a3.7)
2. Import `useDeleteRecordMutation` from `@/queries/hypercerts`
3. Import `DeleteConfirmDialog` from `@/components/delete-confirm-dialog`
4. Import `useQueryClient` from `@tanstack/react-query`
5. Inside the component, call `useDeleteRecordMutation` and `useQueryClient`
6. For each measurement card, if `isOwner` is true, render a `<DeleteConfirmDialog>` with:
   - `itemType="measurement"`
   - `itemName={measurement.metric}` (the metric name)
   - `isDeleting={deleteMutation.isPending}`
   - `onConfirm` calls `deleteMutation.mutate({ recordUri })` where `recordUri` is the AT-URI of the measurement

**Getting the measurement URI**: The measurement links from `useMeasurementLinksQuery` return `{ did, collection, rkey }` objects. The AT-URI is `at://${did}/${collection}/${rkey}`. You need to pass the measurement URI alongside the measurement data. Modify the `useMemo` block to also track the URI for each measurement.

Currently measurements are collected as:
```typescript
if (q.isSuccess && q.data) items.push(q.data.value as Measurement);
```

Change to track URI too:
```typescript
type MeasurementWithUri = Measurement & { _uri: string };
// ...
if (q.isSuccess && q.data) {
  items.push({ ...(q.data.value as Measurement), _uri: `at://${measurementLinks[idx].did}/${measurementLinks[idx].collection}/${measurementLinks[idx].rkey}` });
}
```

On successful delete, invalidate the measurement queries:
```typescript
onSuccess: () => {
  queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["hypercerts", "measurements"] });
}
```

### Visual placement:
Position the delete button in the top-right corner of each measurement card using absolute positioning or flex layout.

## Dont
- Do NOT add edit/update functionality for measurements in this task
- Do NOT change the measurement card layout significantly
- Do NOT show delete buttons when isOwner is false/undefined
acceptance_criteria:
1. Delete button (trash icon) appears on each measurement card when isOwner=true
2. Delete button is hidden when isOwner is false/undefined
3. Clicking delete shows confirmation dialog
4. Confirming delete calls deleteRecord with the measurement AT-URI
5. After successful delete, measurement queries are invalidated (list refreshes)
6. TypeScript compiles with no errors
close_reason: 1d01010 Add delete buttons to measurements section on detail view

title: Fix: addContribution missing ownership check before putRecord (from hypercerts-czy.2)
description: Review of hypercerts-czy.2 found: addContribution() in lib/create-actions.ts (lines 128-162) calls putRecord with repo: ctx.activeDid but never validates that hypercertParsed.did === ctx.activeDid. If a caller passes a hypercertUri belonging to a different DID, the ATProto server will reject the write with an opaque authorization error instead of a clear 403. The original SDK (ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.addContribution) handled this internally. Fix: add an explicit check before the putRecord call: if (hypercertParsed.did !== ctx.activeDid) throw new Error('Forbidden ŌĆö URI DID does not match active session DID.');

title: Fix: OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET read directly from process.env in epds-session-cookie.ts, bypassing config (from hypercerts-dhz.4)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dhz found: lib/epds-session-cookie.ts reads OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET directly from process.env (line 26) instead of using config.oauthSessionSecret from lib/config.ts. This is an inconsistency ŌĆö all other env vars are read through the centralized config object.

The practical risk: if the env var name is ever changed or aliased in config.ts, epds-session-cookie.ts will silently continue reading the old name and throw 'OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is not configured' even when the config object has the value.

Additionally, config.ts already stores oauthSessionSecret (line 200) but it is never used ŌĆö dead config field.

Fix: change getSessionSecret() in epds-session-cookie.ts to import and use config.oauthSessionSecret instead of process.env.OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET directly. This makes the secret consistent with how all other env vars are accessed.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.14 (commit 98de1d5). epds-session-cookie.ts line 27 now reads config.oauthSessionSecret instead of process.env directly.

title: Fix: useAddLocationMutation does not invalidate any query cache after success
Non-standard: application_type sent in PAR request body ŌĆö RFC 9126 does not define this parameter
description:
Review of hypercerts-i1m.1 (commit f880d7b) found that `application_type` is being sent as a parameter in the PAR (Pushed Authorization Request) body.

## Problem

`application_type` is defined in RFC 7591 (OAuth Dynamic Client Registration) as a **client metadata field**, not as an authorization request parameter. RFC 9126 (PAR) and RFC 6749 (OAuth 2.0 core) do not define `application_type` as a valid authorization request parameter.

Sending it in the PAR body is non-standard. The ePDS server may:
- Silently ignore it (most likely, harmless today)
- Reject the request with an `invalid_request` error if it validates parameters strictly

## Evidence

```typescript
// app/api/oauth/login/route.ts:34-43
const parBody = new URLSearchParams({
  client_id: clientId,
  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
  response_type: "code",
  scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
  application_type: config.isLoopback ? "native" : "web",  // ŌåÉ non-standard PAR param
  state,
  code_challenge: codeChallenge,
  code_challenge_method: "S256",
});
```

The correct place to communicate `application_type` is the client metadata document (`/client-metadata.json`), which `buildClientMetadata()` already does correctly (lines 278, 300 of `lib/config.ts`).

## Risk

Low risk if the ePDS server is lenient. High risk if the ePDS server validates PAR parameters against the OAuth spec strictly. Should be verified against the actual ePDS server behavior and either documented as intentional (with evidence the server accepts it) or removed from the PAR body.

title: Fix: DPoP proof missing exp claim ŌĆö unbounded replay window (from security review)
description:
## File
lib/epds-helpers.ts, lines 113-120 (createDpopProof function)

## Problem
The DPoP proof JWT generated by `createDpopProof()` includes `iat` (issued-at) but not `exp` (expiration):
```ts
const payload: Record<string, unknown> = {
  jti: crypto.randomUUID(),
  htm: opts.method,
  htu: opts.url,
  iat: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),
  // exp is MISSING
};
```

RFC 9449 (DPoP) section 4.2 states that DPoP proofs SHOULD include an `exp` claim. Without `exp`, a captured DPoP proof remains valid indefinitely from the server's perspective (subject only to the server's own nonce-based replay detection). If the ePDS server does not enforce strict nonce replay detection, a captured proof could be replayed.

The `jti` claim provides some replay protection if the server tracks used JTIs, but without `exp` the server must store JTIs forever to guarantee uniqueness.

## Fix
Add an `exp` claim with a short lifetime (e.g. 60 seconds):
```ts
const now = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);
const payload: Record<string, unknown> = {
  jti: crypto.randomUUID(),
  htm: opts.method,
  htu: opts.url,
  iat: now,
  exp: now + 60,  // 60-second window per RFC 9449 recommendation
};
```

title: Fix: nyk.13 refactor reverted all 4 desktop left-alignment changes from nyk.14
description:
Review of hypercerts-nyk.14 found: the nyk.13 refactor commit (ce6a9cf, merged after 9a98af1) rewrote lib/atproto-branding.ts and reverted all 4 alignment changes that nyk.14 made.

Current HEAD state of lib/atproto-branding.ts has:
1. Line 86 comment: 'Desktop: right aligned' (should say left)
2. Line 96: background-position: right center (should be left center)
3. Line 146: background-position: right center (should be left center)
4. Line 781: justify-items: end (should be start)
5. Line 786: text-align: right (should be left)

All 4 acceptance criteria of nyk.14 are now failing:
- AC1: horizontal logo above H1 is NOT left-aligned on desktop (right center)
- AC2: certified-signin.svg H1 image is NOT left-aligned on desktop (right center)
- AC3: desktop .grid.grow.content-center uses justify-items: end (not start)
- AC4: .md:text-right still forces text-align: right

Fix: re-apply the 4 changes from nyk.14 to the current HEAD of lib/atproto-branding.ts:
  - background-position: left center for logo (line ~96)
  - background-position: left center for H1 certified-signin.svg (line ~146)
  - justify-items: start in desktop media query (line ~781)
  - text-align: left for .md:text-right in desktop media query (line ~786)

title: Fix: logout endpoint uses GET method ŌĆö CSRF risk for state-changing operation (from security review)
description: ## File app/api/oauth/logout/route.ts, line 5 ## Problem The logout endpoint is implemented as a GET handler: ```ts export async function GET() { // ... calls session.signOut() and deletes user-did cookie } ``` GET requests are state-changing (they sign out the user and revoke the session). This violates the HTTP spec (GET must be safe/idempotent) and creates a CSRF risk: any page on any origin can trigger logout by embedding an `` or similar tag. Browsers send cookies with cross-origin GET requests (subject to SameSite policy). The `user-did` cookie uses `SameSite: Lax` (default) which protects against cross-site form POSTs but NOT against cross-site GET requests triggered by ``, `

title: Fix: .vscode/settings.json missing editor.formatOnSave and prettier integration ŌĆö DX gap for new contributors
description:
Review of .vscode/settings.json found it only contains a personal color theme and is missing project-standard editor settings that would help new contributors.

Evidence:
- .vscode/settings.json only contains: { 'workbench.colorTheme': 'Kimbie Dark' }
- The project has prettier configured (.prettierrc.json) and eslint (eslint.config.mjs)
- No editor.formatOnSave, no defaultFormatter, no eslint integration settings

A new developer cloning this repo will not get automatic format-on-save unless they configure it manually. This defeats the purpose of having prettier + husky.

Suggested additions to .vscode/settings.json:
{
  'editor.formatOnSave': true,
  'editor.defaultFormatter': 'esbenp.prettier-vscode',
  'editor.codeActionsOnSave': {
    'source.fixAll.eslint': 'explicit'
  },
  'typescript.tsdk': 'node_modules/typescript/lib',
  '[typescript]': { 'editor.defaultFormatter': 'esbenp.prettier-vscode' },
  '[typescriptreact]': { 'editor.defaultFormatter': 'esbenp.prettier-vscode' }
}

Also consider adding .vscode/extensions.json with recommended extensions:
- esbenp.prettier-vscode
- dbaeumer.vscode-eslint
- bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss

Note: This issue is related to hypercerts-kpe (personal color theme should be removed from this file).

title: Fix: resolveRecordBlobs in blob-utils.ts calls getRepoContext for every blob in a record ŌĆö N+1 session restores
description:
## Problem
`lib/blob-utils.ts` `resolveBlobToUrl` (lines 8-24) calls `getRepoContext({ targetDid: ownerDid })` for every blob it resolves. `resolveRecordBlobs` (lines 30-57) recursively processes all fields in parallel, meaning if a record has 5 blob fields, `getRepoContext` is called 5 times concurrently.

While `getRepoContext` is wrapped with React `cache()`, this deduplication only works within a single RSC render pass. In Server Actions (where these functions are called from `create-actions.ts`), the cache may or may not deduplicate correctly depending on the Next.js version and execution context.

More critically, `resolveBlobToUrl` also calls `getSession()` separately from `getRepoContext` (line 15), meaning each blob resolution does TWO cookie reads and potentially TWO Redis lookups for the session.

## Fix
Hoist the session and PDS URL resolution out of the per-blob loop. Resolve them once before calling `resolveRecordBlobs`, then pass the resolved values down:

```ts
export async function resolveRecordBlobs(
  value: unknown,
  ownerDid: string,
  pdsUrl?: string, // pre-resolved, optional
): Promise<unknown> {
  // ...
}
```

Or resolve session + pdsUrl once in the callers (`getMeasurementRecord`, `getEvaluationRecord`, etc.) and pass them through.

## Evidence
- `lib/blob-utils.ts` lines 15-23: `getSession()` + `getRepoContext()` called per blob
- `lib/create-actions.ts` lines 155-159, 175-179, 195-199, 215-218: callers that trigger this

title: Fix: raw OAuth error code from authorization server passed unsanitized to client (from security review)
description:
## File
app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, line 21

## Problem
The `error` parameter received from the OAuth authorization server is forwarded directly to the client without sanitization:
```ts
const error = searchParams.get('error');
// ...
redirectUrl.searchParams.set('auth_error', error);  // raw, unsanitized
```

While `error_description` is sanitized (truncated to 200 chars), the `error` code itself is not. RFC 6749 defines a fixed set of error codes (e.g. `access_denied`, `invalid_request`), but a malicious or misconfigured authorization server could return an arbitrary string in the `error` parameter.

This value is then read by AuthErrorToast.tsx (line 13) and displayed in a toast notification. If the error code contains HTML or script content, it could be rendered in the UI. Even without XSS (React escapes by default), an attacker-controlled error code could display misleading messages to users (e.g. `error=Your+account+has+been+suspended+contact+support@evil.com`).

## Fix
Allowlist the error code against known OAuth error codes before forwarding:
```ts
const KNOWN_OAUTH_ERRORS = new Set([
  'access_denied', 'invalid_request', 'unauthorized_client',
  'unsupported_response_type', 'invalid_scope', 'server_error',
  'temporarily_unavailable', 'interaction_required', 'login_required',
  'consent_required', 'request_not_supported',
]);
const safeError = KNOWN_OAUTH_ERRORS.has(error) ? error : 'authorization_failed';
redirectUrl.searchParams.set('auth_error', safeError);
```
