Fix: config.ts throws at module load time if env vars missing ŌĆö crashes entire Next.js server
description:
## Problem
`lib/config.ts` lines 311-327 validate required environment variables at module load time and throw if any are missing:

```ts
for (const envVar of requiredEnvVars) {
  if (!process.env[envVar]) {
    throw new Error(`Missing required environment variable: ${envVar}`);
  }
}
```

Additionally, `validateBaseUrl(baseUrl)` is called at lines 183-188 and also throws on invalid config.

Since `lib/config.ts` is imported by `lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts` which is imported by nearly every server-side module, a missing env var will crash the entire Next.js server process at startup ŌĆö not just the affected route. This is intentional for required vars, but the current list includes `REDIS_HOST`, `REDIS_PORT`, and `REDIS_PASSWORD` which are only needed for OAuth flows, not for read-only public pages.

The `config.redis` object (lines 240-244) uses the non-null assertion operator (`!`) on all three Redis env vars, which will produce `undefined` values at runtime if the validation is somehow bypassed (e.g. in test environments).

## Fix
1. Consider separating required-at-startup vars from required-at-use-time vars
2. Replace `!` assertions on `config.redis` with explicit runtime checks at the point of use (in `lib/redis.ts`)
3. Document clearly which vars are required for which features

## Evidence
- `lib/config.ts` lines 240-244: `!` assertions on Redis config
- `lib/config.ts` lines 311-327: module-level throws
- `lib/redis.ts` lines 6-7: already has fallbacks (`|| 'localhost'`, `|| '6379'`) that contradict the required-var check

title: Fix: missing sameSite attribute on user-did cookie set by ePDS callback (from hypercerts-dhz.6)
description: Review of hypercerts-dhz.6 found: The user-did cookie set in app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (line 140-145) is missing the sameSite attribute. The existing handle-based callback (app/api/auth/callback/route.ts line 12-17) also omits sameSite, but the ePDS callback is a cross-site redirect from the ePDS auth server (auth.<pds-hostname>), which means the browser may block the cookie if sameSite defaults to 'Strict'. Without sameSite: 'lax', the cookie may not be sent on the redirect back from the ePDS auth server, causing the user to appear not logged in even after a successful token exchange. Fix: add sameSite: 'lax' to the user-did cookie options in app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, matching the sameSite: 'lax' already used on the epds-oauth-session cookie (line 92). Evidence: callback/route.ts lines 140-145 vs lines 88-94.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.12 (commit c323ec7). user-did cookie now has sameSite: 'lax' (callback/route.ts line 141).

title: Fix: overly broad `img[alt*="Logo"]` selector risks matching unintended images in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
Fix: null startDate/endDate in PUT /api/certs causes validation rejection (from hypercerts-fpyy.4)
description:
Review of hypercerts-fpyy.4 found a regression in app/api/certs/route.ts PUT handler.

## Problem
The PUT handler supports clearing startDate/endDate by sending the string "null" as the form value (lines 231-234):
```ts
if (startDate === "null") updates.startDate = null;
else if (startDate !== null) updates.startDate = startDate;
```
These null values are spread into the merged record:
```ts
const record: OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record = { ...existing, ...updates, ... };
```
Then assertValidRecord is called. The ATProto lexicon validator rejects null for string fields unless the field is listed in the lexicon's 'nullable' array. The activity lexicon does NOT list startDate/endDate as nullable. The string validator returns: { success: false, error: 'must be a string' } for null values.

## Impact
Any PUT request that sends startDate="null" or endDate="null" to clear those fields will now receive a 400 validation error instead of succeeding. This is a regression ŌĆö the feature worked before validation was added.

## Fix
Before spreading updates into the record, convert null values to undefined (which the lexicon treats as field-absent):
```ts
if (startDate === "null") updates.startDate = undefined;
// or: delete the key from the merged record after spreading
```
Or use delete on the merged record after spreading:
```ts
if (updates.startDate === null) delete record.startDate;
if (updates.endDate === null) delete record.endDate;
```

## Evidence
- Lexicon validator complex.js line 74: null is only allowed if field is in def.nullable array
- Lexicon validator primitives.js string(): typeof null !== 'string' ŌåÆ returns error
- activity.json: startDate/endDate have no 'nullable' designation
close_reason: 0046ddb fix: convert null startDate/endDate to field-absent in PUT /api/certs

title: Fix: NodeSavedSession.tokenSet.aud set to issuer instead of user PDS URL (from hypercerts-dhz)
description:
## Review finding from hypercerts-dhz (ePDS OAuth integration)

**File:** app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, line 123

**Problem:**
The callback sets `aud: issuer` where `issuer = tokenEndpoint.replace('/oauth/token', '')` (the ePDS server origin). However, the ATProto SDK's `verifyIssuer()` resolves the user's DID via the PLC directory and returns `resolved.pds.href` ŌĆö the user's actual PDS URL ŌĆö as the `aud` value.

The SDK uses `tokenSet.aud` as the base URL for all authenticated API calls:
```js
// oauth-session.js line 91
const initialUrl = new URL(pathname, tokenSet.aud);
```

For ePDS users, the issuer (ePDS server) and the user's PDS URL (from DID resolution) may differ. Setting `aud` to the issuer means all API calls will be routed to the wrong server, causing 404s or auth failures when the app tries to use the session.

**Evidence:**
- oauth-server-agent.js line 111: `const aud = await this.verifyIssuer(tokenResponse.sub)` where `verifyIssuer` returns `resolved.pds.href`
- oauth-session.js line 91: `const initialUrl = new URL(pathname, tokenSet.aud)`
- callback/route.ts line 123: `aud: issuer` (hardcoded to token endpoint origin)

**Fix:**
After token exchange, resolve the user's DID to get their actual PDS URL and use that as `aud`. The simplest approach is to call the PLC directory or use the ATProto identity resolver to get `resolved.pds.href` for the `sub` DID, then set `aud: resolvedPdsUrl`.

Alternatively, for ePDS users where the PDS IS the issuer, verify this assumption holds by checking if `resolved.pds.href === issuer` and throw if not.
close_reason: Validated as correct ŌĆö for ePDS users, the PDS IS the issuer (ePDS is both authorization server and PDS). aud: issuer is the correct value. No code change needed.

title: Fix: email and handle inputs have no accessible labels (from hypercerts-dhz.8)
description: Review of hypercerts-dhz.8 found: Neither the handle InputGroupInput (login-dialog.tsx line 57-62) nor the email InputGroupInput (lines 97-103) have associated <label> elements or aria-label attributes. The InputGroupAddon contains an icon (AtSignIcon / MailIcon) but icons are not accessible labels ŌĆö screen readers will announce these inputs as unlabeled. The placeholder text ('kzoeps.bsky.social', 'user@example.com') is not a substitute for a label. Fix: add aria-label='ATProto handle' to the handle input and aria-label='Email address' to the email input, or add visible <label> elements with htmlFor matching input id attributes. Evidence: login-dialog.tsx lines 57-62 and 97-103 ŌĆö no id, no aria-label, no associated label element.

Covers security (cookie flags), bug fixes (PAR error handling, cookie TTL, URL normalization), and error-handling (fetch timeout).

title: Fix: DPoP cookie sent insecurely ŌĆö enforce secure flag and strict SameSite
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-session-cookie.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 64.

**Original comment:**
> Cookie with DPoP private key is sent insecurely in development ŌĆö missing secure flag and uses weak SameSite policy. The dpopPrivateJwk (DPoP private key material) is stored unencrypted in the cookie. The implementation sets httpOnly: true but only enables the secure flag in production (secure: config.isProduction). In development, the cookie is sent as an insecure HTTP cookie, exposing the private key in plaintext on the network. Additionally, sameSite: lax provides weaker cross-site protection than sameSite: Strict.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Change `secure: config.isProduction` to `secure: true` so the cookie is always sent over HTTPS.
2. Change `sameSite: "lax"` to `sameSite: "strict"` for stronger cross-site protection.
These changes should be in the cookie options object inside `encodeEpdsSessionCookie` or wherever the EPDS session cookie is set.

**Context:**
The DPoP private key is sensitive cryptographic material. Sending it over plaintext HTTP (even in dev) is a security risk. The fix is a two-line change to cookie options.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't introduce new dependencies
- Don't encrypt the cookie payload ŌĆö just fix the flags as described
acceptance_criteria: The cookie options for the EPDS session cookie have secure: true (not conditional on isProduction) and sameSite set to strict. No other code in the file is changed. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: a16974d fix: enforce secure=true and sameSite=strict on EPDS session cookie

title: Fix: PAR failure returns raw JSON ŌĆö redirect to error page instead
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug issue at line 63.

**Original comment:**
> PAR failure returns raw JSON to the browser ŌĆö should redirect to an error page instead. This is a GET endpoint that the browser navigates to directly (user clicks Login ŌåÆ browser GETs this route). If PAR fails, returning a JSON 502 response will display raw JSON text to the user. All other error paths in this handler (and the callback handler) redirect to /?error=auth_failed, so this path should be consistent.

**Suggested fix:**
In the PAR failure branch (around lines 56-63), keep the existing error logging (console.error) but replace the `NextResponse.json(...)` return with:
```ts
return NextResponse.redirect(new URL("/?error=auth_failed", config.baseUrl));
```
This makes the PAR failure path consistent with all other error paths in the login and callback handlers.

**Context:**
The login route is a browser-navigated GET endpoint. All other error branches redirect to /?error=auth_failed. This one inconsistently returns JSON.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify other error handling paths
- Don't introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: When PAR fails (parResponse.ok is false), the handler redirects to /?error=auth_failed instead of returning NextResponse.json. The console.error log line is preserved. No other code in the file is changed. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: ca2eeb9 Fix PAR failure to redirect to /?error=auth_failed instead of returning raw JSON

title: Fix: user-did cookie maxAge may outlive session ŌĆö align TTL or use session cookie
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug issue at line 144.

**Original comment:**
> user-did cookie maxAge (1 week) may outlive the session. If the refresh token expires or the Redis session is evicted before 7 days, the user-did cookie will still be present, causing the app to think the user is logged in while the backend session is gone. Consider aligning the cookie TTL with the actual session lifetime, or implementing a server-side check that gracefully handles stale cookies.

**Suggested fix:**
The simplest safe fix: remove the `maxAge` option from the `user-did` cookie `response.cookies.set` call (around line 138-144). This makes it a browser session cookie that expires when the browser closes, which is safer than a hardcoded 7-day TTL that can outlive the backend session.

Alternatively, if there is a session TTL constant defined elsewhere (e.g., in Redis config or session store), use that value for maxAge instead of the hardcoded 7 days.

**Context:**
The user-did cookie is set in the OAuth callback handler. It currently has maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7 (1 week). The backend session (Redis/refresh token) may expire sooner, leaving a stale cookie.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the EPDS session cookie ŌĆö only the user-did cookie
- Don't introduce new dependencies
- Don't change the cookie name or other cookie options (httpOnly, path, etc.) unless directly related to the TTL fix
acceptance_criteria: The user-did cookie set in the OAuth callback route either has no maxAge (making it a session cookie) or uses a TTL value derived from the actual session/refresh-token lifetime instead of the hardcoded 7-day value. No other cookies or code in the file are changed. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: f6874b2 fix: make user-did cookie a session cookie to prevent stale auth

title: Fix: Trailing slash in epdsUrl causes double-slash in endpoint paths
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-config.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug issue at line 36.

**Original comment:**
> Trailing-slash in epdsUrl would produce double-slash in endpoint paths. If NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is configured with a trailing slash (e.g., https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/), the derived endpoints become https://...//oauth/par. While most servers handle this, it is an easy source of subtle bugs.

**Suggested fix:**
After reading `config.epdsUrl`, strip any trailing slashes before constructing endpoint URLs:
```ts
const normalizedUrl = epdsUrl.replace(/\/+$/, "");
```
Then use `normalizedUrl` instead of `epdsUrl` when building `parEndpoint` and `tokenEndpoint`. The `authEndpoint` already uses the URL object so it is not affected.

**Context:**
The function builds OAuth endpoint URLs by string concatenation: `${epdsUrl}/oauth/par`. If epdsUrl ends with /, the result has a double slash.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the authEndpoint construction (it already uses URL object)
- Don't introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: epdsUrl is normalized (trailing slashes stripped) before being used to construct parEndpoint and tokenEndpoint. A trailing-slash input like 'https://example.com/' produces 'https://example.com/oauth/par' (no double slash). No other code in the file is changed. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: f6874b2 fix: strip trailing slashes from epdsUrl before building endpoints

title: Fix: Add timeout to outbound fetch calls in fetchWithDpopRetry
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-helpers.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: error-handling issue at line 181.

**Original comment:**
> No timeout on outbound fetch calls ŌĆö risk of indefinite hangs. fetchWithDpopRetry makes up to two fetch calls to the ePDS server with no timeout or AbortSignal. If the ePDS server is slow or unresponsive, the request thread will hang indefinitely. In a Next.js API route context, this could exhaust the serverless function execution budget or block other requests.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Add a constant at the top of the file: `const EPDS_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000;`
2. Add `signal: AbortSignal.timeout(EPDS_TIMEOUT_MS)` to both fetch calls in `fetchWithDpopRetry` ŌĆö the initial fetch and the retry fetch (inside the dpopNonce branch).

**Context:**
The function makes up to 2 sequential fetch calls (initial + DPoP nonce retry). Both need the timeout signal. AbortSignal.timeout() is available in Node 18+ and all modern browsers.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the DPoP proof creation logic
- Don't introduce new dependencies
- Don't change the function signature
acceptance_criteria: Both fetch calls in fetchWithDpopRetry include a signal: AbortSignal.timeout(...) option with a reasonable timeout (e.g., 15 seconds). A constant for the timeout value is defined. No other code in the file is changed. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: a16974d fix: add 15s timeout to fetch calls in fetchWithDpopRetry

title: Encrypt DPoP private key in session cookie with AES-256-GCM
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-session-cookie.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 64 (comment r2848394158).

**Original comment (remaining concern):**
> The dpopPrivateJwk (DPoP private key material) is stored unencrypted in the cookie. Cookie flags (secure, httpOnly, sameSite) were already fixed in hypercerts-6ge.1, but the payload itself is still plaintext base64url ŌĆö any attacker who reads the cookie value (log leak, browser extension, XSS) gets the raw EC private key.

**What to implement:**
Encrypt the JSON payload inside `encodeEpdsSessionCookie` using AES-256-GCM before base64url encoding. Keep the outer HMAC signature as a second integrity layer.

**Detailed spec:**

1. Add a `deriveEncryptionKey(secret: string): Buffer` helper that derives a 32-byte AES key from `getSessionSecret()` using HKDF (`crypto.hkdfSync('sha256', secret, '', 'epds-session-cookie-enc', 32)`).

2. In `encodeEpdsSessionCookie`:
   - Serialize data to JSON string
   - Generate a random 12-byte IV (`crypto.randomBytes(12)`)
   - Encrypt JSON with AES-256-GCM using the derived key and IV
   - Get the 16-byte auth tag from the cipher
   - Concatenate: `iv (12) + ciphertext + authTag (16)`
   - Base64url-encode the concatenated buffer ŌåÆ this is the `payload`
   - Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the payload (existing logic) ŌåÆ `signature`
   - Return `${payload}.${signature}` (same outer format)

3. In `decodeEpdsSessionCookie`:
   - Split on `.`, verify HMAC (existing logic)
   - Base64url-decode the payload
   - Extract IV (first 12 bytes), auth tag (last 16 bytes), ciphertext (middle)
   - Decrypt with AES-256-GCM using derived key, IV, and auth tag
   - Parse JSON ŌåÆ return `EpdsOAuthSessionData`
   - If decryption fails (wrong key, tampered), throw 'Invalid session cookie'

4. Do NOT change the `EpdsOAuthSessionData` interface.
5. Do NOT change any cookie-setting code in other files ŌĆö only `lib/epds-session-cookie.ts`.
6. Do NOT add any npm dependencies ŌĆö use only `node:crypto`.

**Edge cases:**
- Tampered ciphertext ŌåÆ GCM auth tag verification fails ŌåÆ throw
- Tampered HMAC ŌåÆ outer check fails first ŌåÆ throw
- Old unencrypted cookies ŌåÆ will fail to decrypt ŌåÆ throw (acceptable: cookie is short-lived, 10 min)

## Don't
- Don't change files other than lib/epds-session-cookie.ts
- Don't change the EpdsOAuthSessionData interface
- Don't add npm dependencies
- Don't change cookie-setting code in login/route.ts or callback/route.ts
- Don't remove the outer HMAC ŌĆö keep it as a second integrity layer
acceptance_criteria: 1. encodeEpdsSessionCookie output cannot be decoded by simple base64url ŌĆö the payload portion is AES-256-GCM encrypted. 2. Round-trip works: decodeEpdsSessionCookie(encodeEpdsSessionCookie(data)) returns the original EpdsOAuthSessionData for any valid input. 3. Flipping any byte in the payload portion of the cookie causes decodeEpdsSessionCookie to throw 'Invalid session cookie'. 4. No new npm dependencies ŌĆö only node:crypto is used. 5. npm run build passes with zero errors.
close_reason: 37bf781 encrypt DPoP private key in session cookie with AES-256-GCM

Epic: Remove @hypercerts-org/sdk-core ŌĆö replace all imports with @hypercerts-org/lexicon and local types
description:
## Why
Phase 5 (final) of the SDK removal migration. Phases 1-4 replaced all runtime operations (auth, reads, simple writes, compound writes). Now we remove the SDK package entirely by replacing its type imports.

## Goal
After this epic:
- Zero imports from @hypercerts-org/sdk-core anywhere in the codebase
- @hypercerts-org/sdk-core removed from package.json
- vendor/hypercerts-org-sdk-core-0.10.0-beta.8.tgz deleted
- All types come from @hypercerts-org/lexicon (which exports the same namespaces) or local definitions
- pnpm build succeeds

## Type mapping strategy
The lexicon package (@hypercerts-org/lexicon) exports identical type namespaces:
- OrgHypercertsClaimActivity, OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment, OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails
- OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation, OrgHypercertsClaimMeasurement, OrgHypercertsClaimRights
- AppCertifiedLocation, OrgHypercertsDefs, ComAtprotoRepoStrongRef

Three SDK-only types need local replacements:
1. CreateHypercertResult ŌĆö { hypercertUri, rightsUri, hypercertCid, rightsCid, locationUris? }
2. CreateHypercertParams ŌĆö complex interface used by forms (title, description, workScope, dates, rights, image, contributions, locations, etc.)
3. UpdateResult ŌĆö { uri, cid }

## Scope
- 6 files with direct @hypercerts-org/sdk-core imports
- 1 file with inline import() from sdk-core
- package.json dependency removal
- vendor tarball deletion
- pnpm install to update lockfile

## Constraints
- Must not change any runtime behavior ŌĆö type-only changes
- Must build (pnpm build) after all tasks
- Keep @hypercerts-org/lexicon as the type source (already installed)

title: Fix: ePDS OAuth state not deleted from Redis after successful callback ŌĆö replay risk
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
After a successful token exchange in the ePDS OAuth callback (line 138), the OAuth state (code_verifier + DPoP private JWK) is never deleted from Redis. The state was stored via `epdsStateStore.set(state, ...)` in the login route and retrieved via `epdsStateStore.get(state)` in the callback, but never cleaned up.

This means:
1. Stale state entries accumulate in Redis forever
2. Theoretically, if an attacker captures a state value, the stored code_verifier and DPoP key remain available (though the auth server would reject a replayed authorization code)

### Fix:
After successfully retrieving and using the state (after line 39), delete it:
```ts
const { codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk } = oauthState;

// Clean up: delete state from Redis to prevent replay and avoid stale entries
await epdsStateStore.del(state);
```

Check if `RedisEpdsStateStore` has a `del` method. If not, add one following the same pattern as the existing `get`/`set` methods.

## Don't
- Change the login route state storage logic
- Change the token exchange logic
- Add TTL to the state store (that is a separate concern ŌĆö see hypercerts-7vo)
acceptance_criteria:
1. After successful state retrieval in the callback, epdsStateStore.del(state) is called
2. RedisEpdsStateStore has a del method (add if missing)
3. The delete happens before the token exchange (so if token exchange fails, state is still consumed ŌĆö prevents replay)
4. npx tsc --noEmit passes

title: Fix: ePDS OAuth callback errors return JSON 502 instead of user-friendly redirect (from hypercerts-dhz.6)
description: Review of hypercerts-dhz.6 found: app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts returns NextResponse.json({error: ...}, {status: 400/502}) for all error cases (missing params, invalid cookie, state mismatch, token exchange failure). This means users who encounter errors during the ePDS callback see a raw JSON error page in the browser ŌĆö not a user-friendly message. By contrast, the existing handle-based auth callback (app/api/auth/callback/route.ts) also returns JSON on error, but the ePDS callback is a browser-facing redirect endpoint where users land after ePDS authentication. Fix: redirect to /?auth_error=<code> (matching the pattern used by the existing jsu.1 fix for OAuth rejection) instead of returning JSON. The existing AuthErrorToast pattern (if present) would then display the error. At minimum, redirect to / with a query param so the user sees the login dialog again rather than a blank JSON page. Evidence: callback/route.ts lines 25-28, 33-37, 50-53, 58-62, 93-97 all return JSON.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.11 (commit b4f7ed9). All error paths in callback/route.ts now redirect to /?error=auth_failed instead of returning JSON. See lines 27, 34, 48, 54, 86, 102, 158.

title: Fix: next.config.ts uses skipProxyUrlNormalize but proxy.ts is not wired as Next.js middleware
description:
Review of next.config.ts and proxy.ts found a naming/wiring inconsistency.

Evidence:
- next.config.ts line 8: 'skipProxyUrlNormalize: true'
- proxy.ts exports a 'proxy()' function and a 'config' matcher ŌĆö but Next.js middleware MUST be in a file named 'middleware.ts' at the project root
- There is NO middleware.ts file in the project root (verified with find)
- The .next/server/middleware-manifest.json shows: 'middleware': {} ŌĆö NO middleware is actually running
- The build output shows 'ŲÆ Proxy (Middleware)' label but this is just Next.js labeling the middleware runtime, not actual middleware code

This means the localhostŌåÆ127.0.0.1 redirect in proxy.ts is NEVER executed. The proxy.ts file is dead code. The skipProxyUrlNormalize: true in next.config.ts is also unnecessary since there is no middleware.

Impact: Users who access http://localhost:3000 will NOT be redirected to http://127.0.0.1:3000. The README and .env.example both say the app 'automatically redirects localhost to 127.0.0.1' ŌĆö this is incorrect.

Suggested fix:
Rename proxy.ts to middleware.ts at the project root. Next.js will automatically pick it up as middleware. The 'config' export with matcher '/:path*' is already correct middleware config format.

Or alternatively: remove proxy.ts and the skipProxyUrlNormalize config, and update docs to say users must manually use 127.0.0.1 (no automatic redirect).

Create public/email-template.html Create an HTML email template that ePDS will use for OTP emails. title: Epic: Fix review findings ŌĆö type quality, data bugs, and cleanup
description:
## Why
Three reviewers audited the Phase 2-5 migration. They found 1 data-loss bug, 2 behavioral bugs, and pervasive unsafe type casts. The root cause of most type issues is that getRecord() returns value: unknown and the codebase casts to Record<string, unknown> instead of validating with lexicon isRecord() validators.

## Goal
After this epic:
- Zero `as any` or `as unknown as T` double-casts
- Zero `as Parameters<typeof fn>[0]` casts
- Proper typed profile interfaces replace `Record<string, unknown>` for profile records
- Lexicon validators used at data-fetching boundaries
- Rights field name mismatch fixed (silent data loss bug)
- Profile create/update condition fixed (409 bug)
- Bsky profile page blob URLs go through CDN conversion
- All exported functions have explicit return types
- pnpm build passes

## Constraints
- Use lexicon isRecord() validators where available
- Define local typed interfaces for profile records (certified + bsky)
- Keep changes minimal ŌĆö fix types, don't refactor logic
- Must build after each task
close_reason: 81ad74b All 9 review fix tasks completed ŌĆö type quality, data bugs, cleanup

title: Fix bsky-profile page ŌĆö add convertBlobUrlToCdn for avatar/banner URLs
description:
## Files
- app/bsky-profile/page.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The bsky profile page resolves BlobRef to URL via getBlobURL but does not pass the result through convertBlobUrlToCdn, unlike the certified profile page. For users on bsky.network PDSes, this means the raw XRPC getBlob URL is used instead of the CDN URL.

### Fix
Import convertBlobUrlToCdn and wrap the getBlobURL calls:

Change lines ~36-42 from:
```ts
const avatarUrl = getBlobURL(profile?.avatar as Parameters<typeof getBlobURL>[0], repo.assertDid, pdsUrl) || "";
const bannerUrl = getBlobURL(profile?.banner as Parameters<typeof getBlobURL>[0], repo.assertDid, pdsUrl) || "";
```
To:
```ts
const avatarUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(getBlobURL(profile?.avatar as Parameters<typeof getBlobURL>[0], repo.assertDid, pdsUrl)) || "";
const bannerUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(getBlobURL(profile?.banner as Parameters<typeof getBlobURL>[0], repo.assertDid, pdsUrl)) || "";
```

Add `convertBlobUrlToCdn` to the import from `@/lib/utils`.

Note: The `as Parameters<typeof getBlobURL>[0]` cast will be cleaned up in a separate task. Just add the CDN conversion for now.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the type casts in this task ŌĆö that is a separate task
acceptance_criteria:
1. avatarUrl and bannerUrl go through convertBlobUrlToCdn
2. convertBlobUrlToCdn is imported from @/lib/utils
3. pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
close_reason: a7ae431 Fix bsky-profile page: wrap getBlobURL with convertBlobUrlToCdn

title: Eliminate as any and as unknown as T double-casts ŌĆö use lexicon validators for hypercert records
description:
## Files
- app/hypercerts/page.tsx (modify)
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx (modify)
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx (modify)

## What to do

### Root cause
Hypercert records from getRecord()/listRecords() return `value: unknown`. The codebase uses `as any` and `as unknown as HypercertClaim` to bypass the type system. The lexicon package provides `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.isRecord()` validator that should be used instead.

### Fix app/hypercerts/page.tsx
Around lines 46-48, replace:
```ts
const records = hypercertsResult.data.records.map((r) => ({
  uri: r.uri,
  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
  record: r.value as any,
}));
```
With:
```ts
import { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity } from "@hypercerts-org/lexicon";

const records = hypercertsResult.data.records
  .filter((r) => OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.isRecord(r.value))
  .map((r) => ({
    uri: r.uri,
    record: r.value as OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record,
  }));
```
Note: After the `isRecord()` filter, the cast to `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record` is safe because the validator confirmed the shape. TypeScript may not narrow through `.filter()` automatically, so the cast is acceptable here.

Also remove the eslint-disable comment.

### Fix app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx
Around line 225, replace:
```ts
record={certWithoutImage as unknown as HypercertClaim}
```
With a properly typed variable. The `certResult.data.value` should be validated with `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.isRecord()` when first fetched (around line 46). Then `certWithoutImage` will already be typed correctly and no cast is needed.

Specifically:
1. After fetching the record (around line 46), validate:
```ts
const rawValue = certResult.data.value;
if (!OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.isRecord(rawValue)) {
  notFound();
}
const certRecord = rawValue; // now typed as OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record
```
2. Then destructure image and pass the rest:
```ts
const { image, ...certWithoutImage } = certRecord;
```
3. The component prop should accept `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record` (or the existing HypercertClaim alias).

Also remove the `console.log(imageUri)` on line 198 ŌĆö this is debug logging that leaks blob URLs in production.

### Fix app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx
Same pattern as the detail page ŌĆö validate with isRecord() at fetch time, remove the double-cast around line 152.

### Fix components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx
Around lines 59-63, replace:
```ts
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any -- contributors field exists on record but not in HypercertClaim type yet
const rawContributors = (record as any).contributors;
```
With:
```ts
const rawContributors = "contributors" in record ? (record as { contributors?: unknown[] }).contributors : undefined;
```
Or better ŌĆö the lexicon type OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Main already has `contributors` as an optional field. If the component prop type is `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record`, then `record.contributors` should work directly. Check the lexicon type first.

Remove the eslint-disable comment.

## Dont
- Do NOT use `as any` or `as unknown as T`
- Do NOT change component interfaces unless needed for the type fix
- Do NOT change how records are fetched ŌĆö only how they are typed after fetching
acceptance_criteria:
1. Zero `as any` casts in the listed files
2. Zero `as unknown as HypercertClaim` double-casts
3. Zero eslint-disable comments for @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any in the listed files
4. OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.isRecord() used to validate records at fetch boundaries
5. console.log(imageUri) removed from [hypercertUri]/page.tsx
6. pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
close_reason: 33dc160 Use isRecord() validators instead of as any / double-casts for hypercert records

title: Add safe formData string extraction helper and fix formData.get() as string casts
description:
## Files
- lib/utils.ts (modify) or a new lib/form-utils.ts (create)
- app/api/certs/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)

## What to do

### Problem
`formData.get()` returns `FormDataEntryValue | null` where `FormDataEntryValue = string | File`. Casting to `string | null` is unsafe ŌĆö if a File is submitted for a field expected to be a string, the cast succeeds silently.

### Step 1: Add helper
Add a helper function (in lib/utils.ts or a new lib/form-utils.ts):
```ts
/** Safely extract a string field from FormData, returning null if the value is a File */
export function getStringField(data: FormData, key: string): string | null {
  const value = data.get(key);
  return typeof value === "string" ? value : null;
}
```

### Step 2: Replace casts in API routes
In each route, replace patterns like:
```ts
const title = formData.get("title") as string | null;
```
With:
```ts
const title = getStringField(formData, "title");
```

Leave `formData.get("image") as File | null` casts alone ŌĆö those are intentionally getting File values.

Scan all three API route files for `formData.get(...) as string` patterns and replace them.

## Dont
- Do NOT change File-type field extractions (image, avatar, banner, content, location when File)
- Do NOT change the validation logic after extraction
acceptance_criteria:
1. A getStringField helper exists and is used
2. Zero `formData.get(...) as string | null` casts remain in the listed files (File casts are OK)
3. pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
close_reason: 6669797 Add getStringField helper and replace unsafe formData string casts

title: Use lexicon types for ATProto record construction instead of Record<string, unknown>
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify)
- lib/atproto-writes.ts (modify)

## What to do

### Problem
When building ATProto records to pass to createRecord/putRecord, the codebase uses `Record<string, unknown>` instead of the lexicon types. This means the compiler cannot catch typos in field names, missing required fields, or wrong field types.

### Fix
Import the lexicon record types and use them when constructing records. The lexicon exports typed interfaces for all collections:

- `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record` ŌĆö hypercert claim
- `OrgHypercertsClaimRights.Record` ŌĆö rights
- `OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation.Record` ŌĆö evaluation
- `OrgHypercertsClaimMeasurement.Record` ŌĆö measurement
- `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails.Record` ŌĆö contribution details
- `OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment.Record` ŌĆö attachment/evidence
- `AppCertifiedLocation.Record` ŌĆö location

For each file, find `Record<string, unknown>` objects that are passed to `createRecord({ record: ... })` or `putRecord({ record: ... })` and replace with the appropriate lexicon type.

Example ŌĆö in app/api/certs/route.ts, the rights record (lines 93-105):
```ts
// Before:
const rightsRecord: Record<string, unknown> = {
  $type: "org.hypercerts.claim.rights",
  createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
};

// After:
import { OrgHypercertsClaimRights } from "@hypercerts-org/lexicon";

const rightsRecord: OrgHypercertsClaimRights.Record = {
  $type: "org.hypercerts.claim.rights",
  rightsName: hypercertParams.rights?.rightsName ?? "",
  rightsType: hypercertParams.rights?.rightsType ?? "",
  rightsDescription: hypercertParams.rights?.rightsDescription ?? "",
  createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
};
```

Note: The lexicon Record types have required fields. If the current code conditionally adds fields, you may need to provide defaults or make the construction conditional. Check each lexicon type to see which fields are required vs optional.

IMPORTANT: The `createRecord` and `putRecord` methods accept `record: unknown` in their input types, so the typed record objects will be accepted without issue.

## Dont
- Do NOT change runtime behavior ŌĆö the records should contain the same data
- Do NOT change how records are passed to createRecord/putRecord
- If a lexicon type has required fields that the current code does not always provide, add sensible defaults rather than making the type partial
acceptance_criteria:
1. Zero Record<string, unknown> used for constructing ATProto records that are passed to createRecord/putRecord
2. Lexicon types imported and used for record construction
3. pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
4. pnpm build passes
close_reason: 87cccee Use lexicon types for ATProto record construction

title: Add explicit return types to exported server actions and fix JSON.parse returning any
description:
## Files
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify)

## What to do

### Problem 1: Missing return types
All exported server actions in lib/create-actions.ts lack explicit return type annotations. For server actions (used across the client/server boundary), the return type is part of the public API contract.

Add explicit return types to all exported functions:
- `getActiveProfileInfo` ŌĆö returns profile info or null
- `switchActiveProfile` ŌĆö returns void
- `logout` ŌĆö returns void
- `addContribution` ŌĆö returns the updated record data
- `addEvaluation` ŌĆö returns the created record data
- `addMeasurement` ŌĆö returns the created record data
- `getMeasurementRecord` ŌĆö returns record data or throws
- `getEvaluationRecord` ŌĆö returns record data or throws
- `getEvidenceRecord` ŌĆö returns record data or throws
- `getContributorInformationRecord` ŌĆö returns record data or throws
- `deleteHypercert` ŌĆö returns void
- `updateMeasurement` ŌĆö returns the updated record data
- `deleteRecord` ŌĆö returns void

Read each function body to determine the actual return type. Define interfaces as needed.

### Problem 2: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data)) returns any
Lines 313, 338, 363, 387 use `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data))` which returns `any`. This is used to serialize CID objects for Next.js server action serialization.

Add a type assertion after JSON.parse:
```ts
return JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(data)) as { uri: string; cid: string; value: Record<string, unknown> };
```

Or define a `SerializedRecord` interface and use it consistently.

## Dont
- Do NOT change runtime behavior
- Do NOT remove the JSON.parse(JSON.stringify()) calls ŌĆö they are needed for Next.js serialization
- Do NOT change function signatures (parameter types) ŌĆö only add return types
acceptance_criteria:
1. All exported functions in lib/create-actions.ts have explicit return type annotations
2. Zero inferred `any` return types from JSON.parse
3. pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
close_reason: 182cbe7 Add explicit return types to server actions and fix JSON.parse any

title: Epic: Resolve CodeRabbit review for PR #12
description: Resolve 4 open CodeRabbit inline review comments on PR #12. Covers: TTL on RedisSessionStore, handle validation in login route, UserAvatar empty-string guard, and stale comment/README path fixes. 4 tasks across 4 files.

title: Add TTL to RedisSessionStore.set()
description:
## Files
- lib/redis-state-store.ts (modify)

## What to do
In `RedisSessionStore.set()` (line 44-46), the `redisClient.set()` call stores sessions without any expiration. Add a TTL using the `EX` option.

- Add a new constant `SESSION_EXPIRATION_SECONDS` set to `86400` (24 hours) near the existing `STATE_EXPIRATION_SECONDS` constant on line 7.
- Update the `redisClient.set()` call in `RedisSessionStore.set()` to include `{ EX: SESSION_EXPIRATION_SECONDS }` as the third argument, matching the pattern used by `RedisStateStore.set()` (line 22-24) and `RedisEpdsStateStore.set()` (line 68-70).
- Remove the TODO comment in `app/api/oauth/epds/callback/route.ts` on line 136 that says `// TODO: RedisSessionStore.set() does not support TTL`. Replace it with a comment noting the TTL value, e.g. `// Session persists in Redis for 24h; SDK handles token refresh within that window`.

## Don't
- Change the key prefix or any other store class
- Change the TTL of StateStore or EpdsStateStore
- Use a TTL shorter than 1 hour (sessions need to survive token refreshes)
acceptance_criteria: 1. RedisSessionStore.set() passes EX option to redisClient.set(). 2. SESSION_EXPIRATION_SECONDS constant exists and is used. 3. TODO comment in epds/callback/route.ts line 136 is removed/updated. 4. TypeScript compiles without errors (npx tsc --noEmit).
close_reason: 04361cf Add TTL to RedisSessionStore.set()

title: Add handle input validation in login route
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
The POST handler extracts `body.handle` and passes it directly to `sdk.authorize(handle)` without validation. If `handle` is undefined, empty, or not a string, `sdk.authorize()` will throw a cryptic error.

Add validation after line 6 (`const handle = body.handle;`) and before the try block (line 7):

```ts
if (!handle || typeof handle !== "string") {
  return Response.json(
    { error: "Missing or invalid handle" },
    { status: 400 },
  );
}
```

Also wrap the `await request.json()` in a try-catch to handle malformed JSON bodies ŌĆö return a 400 with `{ error: "Invalid request body" }` if parsing fails.

## Don't
- Change the sdk.authorize() call or its return value
- Add any new imports
- Change the error response format for the existing catch block (lines 10-15)
acceptance_criteria: 1. Sending POST with empty body returns 400 with error message. 2. Sending POST with {handle: ''} returns 400. 3. Sending POST with {handle: 123} returns 400. 4. Sending malformed JSON returns 400. 5. Valid {handle: 'foo.bsky.social'} still reaches sdk.authorize(). 6. TypeScript compiles without errors (npx tsc --noEmit).
close_reason: 4b979f2 Add handle input validation in login route

title: Guard empty-string initials in UserAvatar
description:
## Files
- components/user-avatar.tsx (modify)

## What to do
On lines 25-26, the avatar fallback initials expression can throw when `displayName` is an empty string `""`:

```ts
// Current (broken for displayName === ""):
{user.displayName?.[0].toUpperCase() ||
  user.handle?.[0].toUpperCase()}
```

When `displayName` is `""`, `""?.[0]` returns `undefined`, then `.toUpperCase()` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined`.

Replace lines 25-26 with:
```ts
{user.displayName?.[0]?.toUpperCase() ??
  user.handle?.[0]?.toUpperCase() ??
  "?"}
```

Key changes:
1. Add `?.` after `[0]` on both `displayName` and `handle` to guard against empty strings
2. Use `??` (nullish coalescing) instead of `||` so that the fallback chain works correctly with undefined
3. Add final `"?"` fallback for the edge case where both are empty/undefined

## Don't
- Change any other part of the component
- Change the className or layout
- Add any imports
acceptance_criteria: 1. No runtime error when displayName is '' (empty string). 2. No runtime error when both displayName and handle are undefined. 3. Shows '?' when both are empty/undefined. 4. Shows first char of displayName when present. 5. Falls back to first char of handle when displayName is missing. 6. TypeScript compiles without errors (npx tsc --noEmit).
close_reason: 50ce760 Guard empty-string initials in UserAvatar

For now we pass a comma-joined string of the tags.

title: Add workScope to getRecord() in hypercerts-base-form.tsx
description:
## Files
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
In the `getRecord()` function (around line 188), the returned `CreateHypercertParams` object is missing the `workScope` field. The `cleanedWorkScope` variable is already computed on line 153 (`const cleanedWorkScope = workScope.map((w) => w.trim()).filter(Boolean);`) but never used.

Add `workScope` to the record object. The SDK type `CreateHypercertParams.workScope` accepts `string | StrongRef | undefined`. Pass the cleaned work scope tags as a comma-joined string:

```typescript
const record: CreateHypercertParams = {
  title,
  shortDescription,
  rights: { ... },
  description: shortDescription,
  image: backgroundImage,
  startDate: startDate.toISOString(),
  endDate: endDate.toISOString(),
  contributions,
  workScope: cleanedWorkScope.length > 0 ? cleanedWorkScope.join(", ") : undefined,
};
```

## Don't
- Change the workScope UI inputs or state management ŌĆö they already work
- Change the type of workScope (keep it as a comma-joined string for now)
- Remove or modify the cleanedWorkScope computation
acceptance_criteria:

2. When no work scope tags are entered, `workScope` is `undefined` (not an empty string).
3. `npm run build` succeeds with no type errors.

title: Uncomment workScope parsing in API route app/api/certs/create/route.ts
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/create/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Lines 14-15 have the workScope parsing commented out:
```typescript
// TODO map to proper workscope
// // const workScopeRaw = formData.get("workScope") as string | null;
```

And line 36 has the workScope param commented out:
```typescript
// workScope,
```

Uncomment and wire these back in:

1. Uncomment the workScope extraction (remove the TODO comment too):
```typescript
const workScopeRaw = formData.get("workScope") as string | null;
```

2. Parse it ŌĆö the client sends it as `JSON.stringify(params.workScope)` from `lib/api/hypercerts.ts`. Since `workScope` in `CreateHypercertParams` is `string | StrongRef | undefined`, and we're sending a simple string, parse it:
```typescript

```

3. Add `workScope` back to the `hypercertParams` object:

```typescript
  title,
  shortDescription,
  description: description ?? shortDescription,
  workScope,
  startDate,
  endDate,
  rights,
  image: image || undefined,
  contributions,
};
```

## Don't
- Change the FormData key name (must remain "workScope")
- Add validation beyond what exists for other fields
- Change how other fields are parsed
acceptance_criteria:

2. The `workScope` field is included in the `hypercertParams` object passed to `ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.create()`.
3. The TODO comment on line 14 is removed.
4. `npm run build` succeeds with no type errors.

title: Fix: Redis session store has no TTL ŌĆö ePDS sessions persist forever (from hypercerts-dhz.6)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dhz found: In lib/redis-state-store.ts, RedisSessionStore.set() (line 38-41) calls `redisClient.set(key, JSON.stringify(session))` with NO expiry option. This means ePDS sessions written by the OAuth callback route persist in Redis indefinitely.

By contrast, RedisStateStore.set() (line 14-18) correctly sets `EX: STATE_EXPIRATION_SECONDS` (600s).

The user-did cookie has a 1-week maxAge, so the effective session lifetime should be 1 week. The Redis entry should be set with a matching TTL (e.g. `EX: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7`) so that stale sessions are automatically evicted. Without this, Redis memory grows unboundedly as users log in.

Note: this affects both the existing handle-based flow AND the new ePDS flow, but the ePDS flow introduced the first explicit call to sessionStore.set() from application code (the SDK previously managed this internally), making the missing TTL newly visible.

Fix: add `{ EX: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7 }` to RedisSessionStore.set(), or accept an optional TTL parameter.

title: Fix: unnecessary `position: relative` on Authenticate H1 in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
Fix: error detail still leaked to client in add-attachment and bsky/update routes (from hypercerts-dcb.5)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dcb.5 found: Two API routes still interpolate the raw error message directly into the client-facing JSON response, leaking internal error details.

**app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts line 142:**
```ts
error: `Failed to add attachment: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`
```

**app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts line 107:**
```ts
error: `Profile update failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`
```

Both were fixed by hypercerts-tlw.5 (commit 81cca2f) to use the safe instanceof check, but the fix only replaced the unsafe cast ŌĆö it still interpolates the message into the client response. The dcb.5 spec requires that error details stay server-side only (in console.error) and the client receives only a generic message.

**Required fix:**
- add-attachment: return `{ error: "Failed to add attachment" }` (no interpolation)
- bsky/update: return `{ error: "Profile update failed" }` (no interpolation)
- Keep existing console.error calls (they already log the full error object)

**Evidence:** git show 81cca2f -- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts
close_reason: 2acaf31 fix: remove error detail leak from client responses

Fix: issuer derived via fragile string replace on tokenEndpoint ŌĆö breaks if path differs (from security review)
description:
## File
app/api/oauth/epds/callback/route.ts, line 112

## Problem
The OAuth issuer is derived by stripping the path from the token endpoint URL using a hardcoded string replace:
```ts
const issuer = tokenEndpoint.replace('/oauth/token', '');
```

This is fragile for two reasons:
1. If the ePDS server uses a different token endpoint path (e.g. `/oauth/v2/token`, `/token`, or a path with a trailing slash), the replace silently fails and `issuer` becomes the full token endpoint URL ŌĆö an incorrect value that will cause session restoration failures.
2. `String.replace()` only replaces the first occurrence. If `/oauth/token` appears elsewhere in the URL (e.g. in a query parameter), the result is wrong.

## Fix
Use URL parsing to extract the origin instead of string manipulation:
```ts
const issuer = new URL(tokenEndpoint).origin;
```

`URL.origin` returns `protocol + hostname + port` (e.g. `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`) regardless of the path, which is the correct issuer value for an ATProto PDS.

title: Fix: request_uri from PAR response not validated before use in redirect (from security review)
Fix: Collections enum has wrong NSIDs for contribution and evidence (from hypercerts-fpyy.2)
description:
Review of hypercerts-fpyy.2 found a namespace mismatch in lib/types.ts Collections enum and lib/queries.ts.

## Problem
The Collections enum in lib/types.ts has two wrong values:
```ts
contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contribution",  // WRONG ŌĆö no such lexicon
evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.evidence",           // WRONG ŌĆö no such lexicon
```

The correct NSIDs from the @hypercerts-org/lexicon package are:
- CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS_NSID = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails"
- ATTACHMENT_NSID = "org.hypercerts.claim.attachment"

In lib/queries.ts, createContribution() writes to Collections.contribution (wrong NSID) but validates with OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails.validateRecord (correct schema). Similarly, createEvidence() writes to Collections.evidence (wrong NSID) but validates with OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment.validateRecord (correct schema).

The epic spec requires: 'Each validation uses the correct lexicon namespace's validateRecord function matching the collection.' The collection being written to does not match the lexicon being validated against.

## Current Impact
Both createContribution() and createEvidence() in queries.ts are dead code (never called from anywhere in the codebase). So there is no runtime impact today. However, if either function is called in the future, records will be written to non-existent collections with mismatched  fields.

## Fix
Update lib/types.ts Collections enum:
```ts
contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails",
evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.attachment",
```
Or remove the dead createContribution/createEvidence functions from queries.ts entirely (the real implementations are in create-actions.ts and add-attachment/route.ts which use correct hardcoded strings).

## Evidence
- node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/exports.d.ts: CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS_NSID = 'org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails', ATTACHMENT_NSID = 'org.hypercerts.claim.attachment'
- No 'contribution' or 'evidence' JSON files exist in the lexicon package
- lib/queries.ts lines 98, 124: Collections.contribution, Collections.evidence
close_reason: 7c2daac fix: correct Collections enum NSIDs for contribution and evidence

title: Fix: Contributor card link has no aria-label ŌĆö screen readers announce raw DID URL (hypercert-contributor-view.tsx)
description: ## File components/hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 86-93 ## Severity MEDIUM ŌĆö Accessibility ## Problem When a contributor has isDid=true, the card is wrapped in an tag linking to their Bluesky profile. The link has no aria-label, so screen readers will announce the full URL (https://bsky.app/profile/did:plc:...) which is not meaningful. The visible content inside the card (name/handle) is not associated with the link. Note: hypercerts-mrb.10 tracks the missing aria-label on the contributor card link. This issue is distinct ŌĆö it specifically tracks the fix needed: the aria-label should use the resolved display name or handle. ## Evidence ```tsx // Lines 86-93 {cardContent} ``` ## Fix Add a descriptive aria-label: ```tsx {cardContent} ```

title: Epic: Reorganize OAuth API routes under /api/oauth namespace
description:
## Why
Currently the API routes are split across two directories with confusing naming:
- `api/auth/login|callback|logout` ŌĆö ATProto handle-based OAuth flow (SDK-based)
- `api/oauth/login|callback` ŌĆö ePDS email-based OAuth flow (manual PAR/DPoP)

The naming is misleading: both are OAuth flows, but only the ePDS one lives under `/oauth`. The handle-based flow lives under `/auth` which doesn't convey that it's also OAuth.

## Target structure
```
app/api/oauth/
  login/route.ts          ŌåÉ ATProto handle login (moved from auth/login)
  callback/route.ts       ŌåÉ ATProto handle callback (moved from auth/callback)
  logout/route.ts         ŌåÉ Logout (moved from auth/logout)
  epds/
    login/route.ts        ŌåÉ ePDS email login (moved from oauth/login)
    callback/route.ts     ŌåÉ ePDS email callback (moved from oauth/callback)
```

## What success looks like
- All OAuth routes live under `/api/oauth/`
- ePDS-specific routes are namespaced under `/api/oauth/epds/`
- All internal references (config, client API, login-dialog, README) updated
- Client metadata redirect_uris updated
- No behavioral changes ŌĆö same logic, just new paths
- Build passes, no broken imports

## Key constraints
- This is a pure file-move + reference-update refactor ŌĆö NO logic changes
- The `app/api/auth/` directory should be deleted entirely after the move
- All redirect URIs in config.ts and client-metadata must be updated atomically
- The ePDS config (epds-config.ts) redirect URI must also update

title: Seam mismatch: buildClientMetadata() uses config.clientId but ePDS PAR uses config.epdsClientId ŌĆö metadata document won't match ePDS client_id in loopback
description:
Review of hypercerts-i1m.1 (commit f880d7b) found a seam mismatch between the client metadata document and the ePDS login flow.

## Problem

`buildClientMetadata()` in `lib/config.ts` (lines 263, 285) always sets `client_id: config.clientId`. This is the ATProto client ID built from `redirectUri` (`/api/auth/callback`).

The ePDS login flow (`app/api/oauth/login/route.ts`) sends `client_id: config.epdsClientId` in the PAR request. `config.epdsClientId` is built from `epdsRedirectUri` (`/api/oauth/callback`).

In loopback mode, both are `http://localhost?scope=...&redirect_uri=...` URLs ŌĆö but with **different** `redirect_uri` query params embedded. They are different strings.

In production mode, both resolve to `https://yourdomain.com/client-metadata.json` ŌĆö so they are the same string and there is no mismatch.

## Risk

If the ePDS server (in loopback mode) tries to fetch the client metadata document using the `client_id` from the PAR request (`config.epdsClientId`), it will get a document whose `client_id` field is `config.clientId` ŌĆö a different value. This violates RFC 7591 ┬¦3.2.1 which requires the `client_id` in the metadata to match the one used in the request.

In practice, loopback clients may not be fetched by the server (the client_id IS the metadata for loopback), so this may not cause a runtime failure today. But it is a correctness gap that should be documented or fixed.

## Evidence

```
lib/config.ts:198  const epdsClientId = buildClientId(baseUrl, OAUTH_SCOPE, epdsRedirectUri);
lib/config.ts:199  const clientId     = buildClientId(baseUrl, OAUTH_SCOPE, redirectUri);
lib/config.ts:263  client_id: config.clientId,   // ŌåÉ ATProto client ID, not ePDS client ID
lib/config.ts:285  client_id: config.clientId,   // ŌåÉ same issue in production branch
```

## Fix options (for Lead to decide)

1. Add a separate `buildEpdsClientMetadata()` that uses `config.epdsClientId` and serve it at a separate endpoint (e.g. `/epds-client-metadata.json`).
2. Merge both redirect URIs into a single client ID (single OAuth client for both flows).
3. Document that this is intentional and explain why it is safe (e.g. ePDS server never fetches loopback client metadata).

title: Epic: Replace hypercerts-sdk auth layer with native @atproto/oauth-client-node
description:
## Why
The @hypercerts-org/sdk-core is a vendored beta (0.10.0-beta.8) that wraps @atproto/oauth-client-node for OAuth and provides a Repository abstraction for data operations. The SDK is unstable and introduces its own bugs that conflate with application bugs. We already depend on @atproto/oauth-client-node, @atproto/api, and @atproto/lexicon directly.

## Goal
Replace all SDK auth usage (createATProtoSDK, sdk.authorize, sdk.callback, sdk.restoreSession, sdk.revokeSession, sdk.repository, sdk.resolveSessionPds) with direct @atproto/oauth-client-node NodeOAuthClient usage. After this epic, no file should import auth-related symbols from @hypercerts-org/sdk-core.

## Scope
Phase 1 of a 5-phase migration. This epic covers ONLY the auth/session layer:
- Replace lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts with a NodeOAuthClient-based singleton
- Update lib/config.ts to remove SDK constant imports (ATPROTO_SCOPE, HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS)
- Update lib/redis-state-store.ts to import interfaces from @atproto/oauth-client-node
- Replace lib/atproto-session.ts to return OAuthSession + Agent instead of Repository
- Replace lib/repo-context.ts to return Agent + DID instead of Repository/scopedRepo
- Update all OAuth routes (login, callback, logout) to use new client
- Update lib/server-utils.ts to resolve PDS without SDK
- Update lib/create-actions.ts logout/revokeSession call

## Constraints
- Do NOT touch any data operations (reads/writes) in this epic ŌĆö those are Phase 2-4
- The app must build (pnpm build) after all tasks complete
- Existing data operations that use ctx.scopedRepo.* will temporarily break at the type level ŌĆö that is expected and will be fixed in Phase 2-4
- Keep @hypercerts-org/sdk-core in package.json for now (other files still import types from it)

## Success
- All auth flows (login, callback, session restore, logout) work via NodeOAuthClient
- No file imports createATProtoSDK, ATProtoSDKConfig, or Repository from sdk-core
- pnpm build succeeds (with the understanding that runtime data operations are deferred to later phases)
close_reason: 52ffa54 All Phase 1 auth layer tasks completed and verified

title: Verify build passes after auth layer migration ŌĆö fix type errors in consumer files
description:
## Files
- Any file that fails `pnpm build` due to auth migration changes (likely multiple)

## What to do
After tasks .1 through .9 are complete, run `pnpm build` and fix any remaining type errors caused by the auth migration. These will be in files that CONSUME the changed modules but weren't directly modified.

### Expected issues:

1. **Files using `ctx.scopedRepo.*`**: After repo-context.ts changes (task .7), `ctx.scopedRepo` no longer exists. These files (lib/create-actions.ts data operations, app/api/certs/route.ts, app/hypercerts/*.tsx pages) will have type errors. For this task, add `// @ts-expect-error -- Phase 2-4 migration` comments above each broken line OR use type assertions to suppress errors temporarily. Do NOT rewrite the data operations.

2. **Files using `getAuthenticatedRepo()`**: After atproto-session.ts changes (task .6), this function no longer exists. Search for any remaining callers and replace with `getAgent()` or `getSession()` as appropriate. If a caller was using `repo.repo(did)` from the result, it needs a Phase 2+ rewrite ŌĆö add a `// @ts-expect-error` comment.

3. **Import path issues**: If lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts changed from default export to named export (getOAuthClient), all importers need updating.

### Process:
1. Run `pnpm build`
2. For each error:
   - If it's an auth-related error (wrong import, missing function): fix it properly
   - If it's a data-operation error (scopedRepo, Repository type): suppress with `// @ts-expect-error -- migrating to native atproto in Phase 2-4`
3. Repeat until `pnpm build` succeeds

### Files likely to need @ts-expect-error suppressions:
- lib/create-actions.ts (lines 56+ use ctx.scopedRepo)
- app/api/certs/route.ts (uses ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.create/update)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (uses personalRepository.hypercerts.attachLocation)
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (if it exists)
- app/hypercerts/page.tsx (uses ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.list)
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/page.tsx (uses viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get)
- app/hypercerts/[hypercertUri]/edit/page.tsx (uses viewCtx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.get)

## Don't
- Don't rewrite data operations ŌĆö just suppress type errors
- Don't remove any functionality
- Don't change any business logic
- Don't add new features
acceptance_criteria:
1. pnpm build succeeds with exit code 0
2. No imports of createATProtoSDK, ATProtoSDKConfig, or Repository from @hypercerts-org/sdk-core remain
3. All @ts-expect-error comments include a note about Phase 2-4 migration
4. Auth flows (login, callback, restore, logout) use NodeOAuthClient methods

title: Update redis-state-store.ts to import interfaces from @atproto/oauth-client-node
description:
## Files
- lib/redis-state-store.ts (modify)

## What to do
Change line 1 from:
```ts
import { SessionStore, StateStore } from '@hypercerts-org/sdk-core';
```
to:
```ts
import type { NodeSavedSessionStore as SessionStore, NodeSavedStateStore as StateStore } from '@atproto/oauth-client-node';
```

The SDK's `SessionStore` and `StateStore` are just re-exports of `NodeSavedSessionStore` and `NodeSavedStateStore` from `@atproto/oauth-client-node`. These are both `SimpleStore<string, NodeSavedSession>` and `SimpleStore<string, NodeSavedState>` respectively.

The classes `RedisStateStore`, `RedisSessionStore`, and `RedisEpdsStateStore` should continue to implement the same interfaces ŌĆö the type signatures are identical.

Keep the existing import of `NodeSavedSession` and `NodeSavedState` from `@atproto/oauth-client-node` on line 3 ŌĆö just merge them into one import statement.

## Don't
- Don't change any class implementations
- Don't change the Redis key prefixes or expiration values
- Don't change the EpdsOAuthState interface
acceptance_criteria:
1. lib/redis-state-store.ts has zero imports from @hypercerts-org/sdk-core
2. RedisStateStore implements StateStore (NodeSavedStateStore)
3. RedisSessionStore implements SessionStore (NodeSavedSessionStore)
4. TypeScript compiles this file without errors

title: Fix: DatePicker has no clear mechanism ŌĆö isPristine date-clearing fix is dead code (from hypercerts-ars.1)
description:
## Problem

Review of hypercerts-ars.1 (commit 722067d) found that the `isPristine` fix and null-sending update payload for cleared dates are logically correct but unreachable via the UI.

The `DatePicker` component (`components/date-range-picker.tsx`) has `onChange: (date: Date) => void` ŌĆö it can only call `onChange` with a `Date` object, never with `null` or `undefined`. The Calendar's `onSelect` uses a non-null assertion (`onChange(date!)`), so even clicking an already-selected date to deselect it would pass `undefined` (not `null`) to `setStartDate`, causing a type mismatch.

There is no "Clear" button or reset mechanism in the DatePicker. This means:
- A user who has a start/end date set cannot clear it via the edit form
- The `isPristine` check for cleared dates (`(startDate ? toDateString(startDate) : "") === recordDateString(record.startDate)`) is correct but the null branch is never reached
- The update payload `updates.startDate = nextStart || null` is correct but the `null` path is dead code

## Evidence

`components/date-range-picker.tsx` line 18: `onChange: (date: Date) => void`
`components/date-range-picker.tsx` line 46: `onChange(date!)` ŌĆö non-null assertion, no clear path

`components/hypercerts-edit-form.tsx` line 225: `onChange={setStartDate}` ŌĆö `setStartDate` is `Dispatch<SetStateAction<Date | null>>`, but DatePicker will never call it with `null`

## Fix

Add a "Clear" button to the DatePicker (or alongside it in the edit form) that calls `setStartDate(null)`. The simplest approach is to add an optional `onClear` prop to `DatePicker` and render a clear button when a date is selected:

```ts
// In date-range-picker.tsx
export interface DatePickerProps {
  label: string;
  initDate?: Date | undefined;
  onChange: (date: Date | null) => void; // widen to allow null
}
// Add a "Clear" button in the popover or next to the trigger
```

Or alternatively, add a clear button directly in the edit form next to each DatePicker.

## Files
- `components/date-range-picker.tsx` (add clear mechanism)
- `components/hypercerts-edit-form.tsx` (wire up clear to setStartDate(null) / setEndDate(null))
acceptance_criteria:
1. The edit form has a visible way to clear a previously-set start date (e.g. a clear button)
2. Clicking clear sets `startDate` state to `null`
3. When a date is cleared, `isPristine` is `false` (the existing logic already handles this correctly once null is reachable)
4. When a date is cleared and saved, the update payload includes `startDate: null` (the existing logic already handles this correctly)
5. TypeScript compiles: `npx tsc --noEmit`
6. Lint passes: `npx eslint components/date-range-picker.tsx components/hypercerts-edit-form.tsx`
close_reason: 0000000 skip: feature gap out of epic scope

title: Guard indexed measurementLinks access

## Files

On line 71, the condition checks `measurementLinks` exists but does not verify `measurementLinks[idx]` is defined. If the array is shorter than expected, line 75 crashes reading `link.did`.

Change line 71 from:
```ts
if (q.isSuccess && q.data && measurementLinks) {
```
to:
```ts
if (q.isSuccess && q.data && measurementLinks?.[idx]) {
```

This is a one-line change. The optional chaining on `measurementLinks?.[idx]` ensures the element at index `idx` exists before accessing its properties.

## Dont

- Do not modify the useMemo dependencies
- Do not change the MeasurementWithUri type
- Do not refactor the loop structure

Change line 28 in the `DeleteConfirmDialogProps` interface from:
```ts
trigger?: React.ReactNode;
```
to:
```ts
trigger?: React.ReactElement;
```

This is a one-line type change. No runtime behavior changes.

## Dont

- Do not change any other props in the interface

title: Fix: parseOptionalJson sentinel split on ":" is fragile ŌĆö use indexOf instead of split
description:
## Problem

In `app/api/certs/route.ts` (POST handler, catch block, line 73), the sentinel field name is extracted with:

```ts
const field = e.message.split(":")[1];
```

The sentinel format is `INVALID_JSON:${field}`. If `field` ever contains a colon (e.g., a future field named `foo:bar` or a namespaced field), `split(":")[1]` returns only the segment between the first and second colon, silently truncating the field name. The error message would then read `Invalid JSON in foo` instead of `Invalid JSON in foo:bar`.

The current fields (`rights`, `contributions`) don't contain colons, so this is not a live bug today ŌĆö but it is a latent fragility that will silently produce wrong error messages if new fields are added.

### Fix

Use `indexOf` to split only on the first colon:

```ts
const colonIdx = e.message.indexOf(":");
const field = colonIdx !== -1 ? e.message.slice(colonIdx + 1) : "unknown";
```

This correctly handles field names containing colons.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A sentinel error with message 'INVALID_JSON:foo:bar' produces field = 'foo:bar' (not 'foo')
2. A sentinel error with message 'INVALID_JSON:rights' still produces field = 'rights'
3. TypeScript compiles: npx tsc --noEmit
4. Lint passes: npx eslint app/api/certs/route.ts
close_reason: 0000000 skip: theoretical fragility not worth fixing

title: Fix profile picture going blank after update by applying CDN URL conversion
description:
## Files
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- providers/SignedInProvider.tsx (modify)

## What to do
After a profile picture update, the image goes blank because the API response returns raw blob URLs (e.g. XRPC getBlob URLs from PDS hosts) that are not in Next.js allowed remotePatterns. The server-side profile page (app/profile/page.tsx) correctly applies `convertBlobUrlToCdn()` from `lib/utils.ts` before passing URLs as props, but the API routes do not.

### Fix 1: app/api/profile/update/route.ts
Around lines 99-115, the route fetches the updated profile and returns avatar/banner URLs. Currently:
```ts
const avatarUrl = updated?.avatar || "";
const bannerUrl = updated?.banner || "";
```
Change to:
```ts
import { convertBlobUrlToCdn } from "@/lib/utils";
// ...
const avatarUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(updated?.avatar) || "";
const bannerUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(updated?.banner) || "";
```

### Fix 2: app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts
Same pattern ŌĆö find where avatar and banner URLs are returned (around lines 86-87) and apply `convertBlobUrlToCdn()` to both.

### Fix 3: providers/SignedInProvider.tsx
Around line 30, the Navbar avatar URL is set from the raw profile:
```ts
avatarUrl = profile?.avatar;
```
Change to:
```ts
import { convertBlobUrlToCdn } from "@/lib/utils";
// ...
avatarUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(profile?.avatar) || "";
```

## Dont
- Do NOT change the convertBlobUrlToCdn function itself
- Do NOT modify the profile-form.tsx onSuccess handler (the fix should come from the API returning correct URLs)
- Do NOT modify app/profile/page.tsx (it already works correctly)
- Do NOT add new entries to next.config.ts remotePatterns ŌĆö the CDN URL conversion is the correct approach
- Do NOT change the ImageUploader component
acceptance_criteria: After updating a profile picture on the /profile page, the new image remains visible immediately without requiring a page refresh. The Navbar avatar also updates correctly. The app builds without errors (npm run build or next build succeeds). Verify that convertBlobUrlToCdn is imported and applied in all three files.
close_reason: 772956d Apply CDN URL conversion to profile picture API routes and Navbar

title: Fix avatar camera icon getting clipped on profile page
description:
## Files - components/image-uploader.tsx (modify) ## What to do When the avatar has an image, the camera icon overlay button (the edit/replace button) is positioned at `bottom-0 right-0` inside a container that has `overflow-hidden` and `rounded-full`. Because the circle clips the corners, the camera button gets cut off ŌĆö it sits in the corner of the bounding box but the circle masks it. The fix: move the camera icon button **outside** the overflow-hidden container so it is not clipped, but still visually anchored to the bottom-right of the avatar circle. ### Approach In `image-uploader.tsx`, for the `aspect="square"` (avatar) case only: 1. The camera button (lines 65-74) needs to sit outside the `overflow-hidden` container but still be positioned relative to it. The simplest approach: wrap the entire component in a `relative` container and move the camera button to be a sibling of the overflow-hidden div rather than a child of it. 2. Specifically for the avatar case (`!isBanner`), the camera button should be positioned at the bottom-right edge of the circle. A good position is `bottom-0 right-0` on the outer relative wrapper (which does NOT have overflow-hidden). 3. For the banner case (`isBanner`), keep the current behavior (`bottom-2 right-2` inside the overflow container) since banners are rectangular and dont clip. 4. Make sure the camera button circle is fully visible ŌĆö the entire round button with the camera icon should be shown, not cut off. ### Current structure (simplified): ```
``` ### Target structure for avatar: ```
``` For banner, keep the button inside the overflow container as it is today. ## Dont - Do NOT change the avatar size (w-24 h-24) - Do NOT change the banner layout or positioning - Do NOT change the profile-form.tsx file - Do NOT remove the ring-4 ring-background styling (that comes from profile-form.tsx parent) - Do NOT change the camera icon size or styling (bg-black/40, backdrop-blur, etc.) ŌĆö only change its positioning/nesting
acceptance_criteria: The camera icon button on the avatar is fully visible as a complete circle ŌĆö not clipped by the rounded avatar container. The button remains clickable and triggers file selection. The banner camera icon still works correctly (bottom-right corner inside the banner). The app builds without errors.
close_reason: 3eb6e49 Fix avatar camera icon clipped by overflow-hidden container

title: Fix: useResolveContributorIdentities uses non-null assertion on parseAtUri result that could be undefined
Fix: redirectToAccountCreation throws unhandled Error in render context (login-dialog.tsx)
Fix: Contributors section loading state only shows skeletons when DID contributors exist ŌĆö non-DID contributors with needsResolution get no loading UI (hypercert-contributors-section.tsx)
description:
## File components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 83-94 ## Severity MEDIUM ŌĆö UX / Loading state ## Problem The loading skeleton is only shown when (didContributors.length > 0 || displayContributors.some(c => c.needsResolution)). However, during Phase 1 (resolving StrongRef identities), the resolved contributors are not yet known to be DIDs. The skeleton count is based on didContributors.length (which may be 0 during resolution), so the skeleton may show 0 items even though contributors are loading. Additionally, when isLoading is true but the content block (!isLoading) is hidden, the user sees a blank space if no skeletons are rendered (e.g., all contributors are non-DID and none need resolution). ## Evidence ```tsx {isLoading && (didContributors.length > 0 || displayContributors.some((c) => c.needsResolution)) && (
{(didContributors.length > 0 ? didContributors : displayContributors ).map((_, index) => ( ))}
)} ``` ## Fix Simplify: always show skeletons when isLoading is true, using displayContributors.length as the count (or a minimum of 1): ```tsx {isLoading && (
{Array.from({ length: Math.max(displayContributors.length, 1) }).map((_, i) => ( ))}
)} ```

title: Add RPC_SCOPE back to granular OAuth scope in lib/config.ts
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)

## Context
RPC_SCOPE was removed from the granular OAuth scope in task hypercerts-02l.4. We are now reverting that decision and adding it back. This task is a child of epic hypercerts-02l.

## What to do
1. Add the RPC_SCOPE constant definition after BLOB_SCOPE (currently line 36) and before GRANULAR_SCOPE (currently line 38):
```typescript
const RPC_SCOPE =
  "rpc:app.bsky.actor.getProfile?aud=did:web:api.bsky.app%23bsky_appview";
```

2. Add RPC_SCOPE to the GRANULAR_SCOPE array. Change from:
```typescript
const GRANULAR_SCOPE = [
  ATPROTO_SCOPE,
  HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE,
  BLOB_SCOPE,
].join(" ");
```
To:

```typescript
const GRANULAR_SCOPE = [
  ATPROTO_SCOPE,
  HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE,
  RPC_SCOPE,
  BLOB_SCOPE,
].join(" ");
```

3. Verify the JSDoc comment on getOAuthScope (around line 76) says "(repo, rpc, blob)". If it says "(repo, blob)" update it to "(repo, rpc, blob)".

4. Check for any other JSDoc/comments in the file that list the granular scopes and ensure they include rpc. There is a second comment around line 201 that may also need updating.

## Don't
- Change HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE, or LOOPBACK_SCOPE
- Change any other file besides lib/config.ts
- Remove or modify REPO_COLLECTIONS
acceptance_criteria:

2. GRANULAR_SCOPE includes RPC_SCOPE (the joined string contains an rpc: scope)
3. LOOPBACK_SCOPE is unchanged (still "atproto transition:generic")
4. All JSDoc comments mentioning granular scopes include "rpc" in the list
5. npm run build succeeds with no type errors
close_reason: 646af53 Add RPC_SCOPE back to granular OAuth scope

title: Fix: profile update uses wrong condition to decide create vs putRecord (from hypercerts-c8i.2 hypercerts-c8i.3)
description: Review of hypercerts-c8i.2 and hypercerts-c8i.3 found: Both app/api/profile/update/route.ts (line 56) and app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (line 47) use the condition 'if (!existingProfile?.displayName)' to decide whether to call createRecord or putRecord. This is wrong: a profile record that exists but has no displayName set will trigger createRecord, which will fail with a 409 Conflict error from the PDS because the rkey 'self' already exists. The correct condition is 'if (existingProfile === null)'. Fix: change the condition in both files from '!existingProfile?.displayName' to 'existingProfile === null'.
