title: Refactor: use app.bsky.actor.getProfile instead of raw getRecord for profile reads
description:
## Files
- app/bsky-profile/page.tsx (modify)
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- lib/types.ts (modify ŌĆö remove BskyActorProfile if no longer used)

## Context
Currently, both files fetch the Bluesky profile using the low-level \`com.atproto.repo.getRecord\` API and then cast the result with \`as BskyActorProfile\`. The \`@atproto/api\` Agent has a typed \`getProfile\` method (\`agent.getProfile({ actor: did })\`) that returns \`AppBskyActorDefs.ProfileViewDetailed\` ŌĆö a fully typed response where \`avatar\` and \`banner\` are already resolved to URL strings (not BlobRef objects).

## What to do

### 1. app/bsky-profile/page.tsx (lines 23-30)
Replace:
\`\`\`ts
const profileResult = await repo.com.atproto.repo
  .getRecord({ repo: repo.assertDid, collection: "app.bsky.actor.profile", rkey: "self" })
  .catch(() => null);
const profile = profileResult?.data?.value as BskyActorProfile | undefined;
\`\`\`
With:
\`\`\`ts
const profileResult = await repo.getProfile({ actor: repo.assertDid }).catch(() => null);
const profile = profileResult?.data;
\`\`\`

Since \`ProfileViewDetailed.avatar\` and \`ProfileViewDetailed.banner\` are already URL strings, you can also remove the \`getBlobURL\`/\`convertBlobUrlToCdn\` calls (lines 34-39) and the \`resolveSessionPds\` call (lines 32-33). Just use \`profile?.avatar ?? ""\` and \`profile?.banner ?? ""\` directly.

Remove the \`import type { BskyActorProfile }\` and the \`import { getBlobURL, convertBlobUrlToCdn }\` and \`import { resolveSessionPds }\` and \`import { getSession }\` (only \`getAgent\` is needed). Remove the \`import { getSession }\` only if it's not used elsewhere in the file ŌĆö currently it's only used for \`resolveSessionPds\` which is being removed.

### 2. app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts

**First getRecord (lines 38-46):** This one is used to check profile existence AND get the raw record for merging in the putRecord call. The raw record (with BlobRef objects) is needed for the merge. Keep this getRecord call BUT improve the typing. Instead of casting to \`BskyActorProfile\`, use the SDK's type:
\`\`\`ts
import type { AppBskyActorProfile } from "@atproto/api";
// ...
const existingProfile = existingResult?.data?.value as AppBskyActorProfile.Record | undefined ?? null;
\`\`\`
Check if \`AppBskyActorProfile\` is exported from \`@atproto/api\`. If not, check \`@atproto/api\`'s exports for the right namespace. The type lives at \`node_modules/@atproto/api/dist/client/types/app/bsky/actor/profile.d.ts\`. Look at the actual exports from \`@atproto/api\` index to find the correct import path. If no typed export exists for the profile record type, keep the existing \`BskyActorProfile\` type from \`lib/types.ts\` for this one usage.

**Second getRecord (lines 116-123):** This is only used to return the updated profile to the client. Replace with \`getProfile\`:
\`\`\`ts
const updatedResult = await repo.getProfile({ actor: repo.assertDid }).catch(() => null);
const updated = updatedResult?.data;
\`\`\`
Since \`ProfileViewDetailed.avatar\` and \`ProfileViewDetailed.banner\` are already URL strings, remove the \`getBlobURL\`/\`convertBlobUrlToCdn\`/\`resolveSessionPds\`/\`getSession\` calls (lines 126-135) and simplify the response:
\`\`\`ts
return NextResponse.json({
  ok: true,
  profile: {
    displayName: updated?.displayName || "",
    description: updated?.description || "",
    avatar: updated?.avatar || "",
    banner: updated?.banner || "",
  },
});
\`\`\`

Remove unused imports: \`getBlobURL\`, \`convertBlobUrlToCdn\`, \`getSession\`, \`resolveSessionPds\`. Keep \`revalidatePath\` (still used on line 114).

### 3. lib/types.ts
If \`BskyActorProfile\` is no longer imported anywhere after the above changes, remove the interface (lines 114-121). If the first getRecord in the update route still uses it, keep it.

## Don't
- Don't change the create/putRecord logic in the update route ŌĆö that still needs raw record manipulation
- Don't change how blob uploads work
- Don't change the profile form component or any client-side code
- Don't add new dependencies
- Don't change the API response shape (the profile object returned must still have displayName, description, avatar, banner as strings)
acceptance_criteria:
1. app/bsky-profile/page.tsx uses agent.getProfile() instead of com.atproto.repo.getRecord to fetch the profile
2. app/bsky-profile/page.tsx has no 'as BskyActorProfile' type cast
3. app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts post-update fetch (previously lines 116-123) uses getProfile() instead of getRecord
4. app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts post-update response no longer needs getBlobURL/convertBlobUrlToCdn since getProfile returns URL strings
5. No unused imports remain in either file
6. The API response shape is unchanged: { ok: true, profile: { displayName: string, description: string, avatar: string, banner: string } }
7. npm run build succeeds with no type errors
8. BskyActorProfile is removed from lib/types.ts if no longer referenced, or kept if still needed by the first getRecord in the update route
close_reason: 2af74cd refactor: use agent.getProfile() instead of getRecord for bsky profile reads

title: Fix: duplicate inputRef causes upload label broken in no-image state (from hypercerts-b08.3)
description: Review of hypercerts-b08.3 found: two <input ref={inputRef}> elements are rendered simultaneously when imageUrl is falsy (no-image state). Lines 89-95 (inside the <label>) and lines 100-106 (always-rendered hidden input) both use ref={inputRef}. React assigns the ref to the last-rendered element (lines 100-106), which is outside the <label>. The label wraps the first input but inputRef points to the second, so clicking the upload label in the no-image state may not trigger file selection correctly. Evidence: components/image-uploader.tsx lines 89-106 in commit 3eb6e49. Fix: remove the always-rendered hidden input (lines 100-106) since the label-wrapped input (lines 89-95) already handles the no-image case, and the avatar camera button (lines 110-118) calls inputRef.current?.click() directly which works fine without a second input.
acceptance_criteria: Only one <input ref={inputRef}> element exists in image-uploader.tsx at any time. File upload works in both states: (1) clicking the dashed upload area when no image is set triggers file selection, (2) clicking the camera icon button when an image exists triggers file selection. The app builds without errors.
close_reason: c54bd52 fix: remove duplicate inputRef ŌĆö single hidden input always rendered

title: Add lexicons/ to ESLint globalIgnores
description:
## Files
- eslint.config.mjs (modify)

## What to do

Add `"lexicons/**"` to the `globalIgnores` array in `eslint.config.mjs`. The lexicons/ directory contains auto-generated TypeScript types from ATProto lexicon definitions. These files have many unused imports by design (they export types that consumers may or may not use). They should not be linted.

The globalIgnores array currently has:
```js
globalIgnores([
  ".next/**",
  "out/**",
  "build/**",
  "next-env.d.ts",
])
```

Add `"lexicons/**"` to this array.

## Don't
- Do NOT modify any files in lexicons/
- Do NOT change any ESLint rules
- Do NOT touch any other config
acceptance_criteria:
1. eslint.config.mjs has "lexicons/**" in the globalIgnores array
2. npm run lint no longer reports any warnings from lexicons/ files
3. npm run lint still reports issues in non-lexicon files (i.e. we did not accidentally ignore too much)
4. npx tsc --noEmit passes

title: Refactor: consolidate 5 scattered `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
description:
Review of hypercerts-nyk.15 found that lib/atproto-branding.ts now has 5 separate `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks scattered across the file (lines 119-127, 171-175, 207-226, 264-268, 337-348). This is a maintainability concern ŌĆö a developer reading the file must scan all 437 lines to understand the full dark mode behavior.

Additionally, the `main.flex.flex-col.items-center > h1.text-primary` selector appears in two separate unconditional rule blocks (lines 232-234 and 258-261) that could be merged into one.

Evidence: lib/atproto-branding.ts ŌĆö count of dark media queries:
- Lines 119-127: logo invert
- Lines 171-175: H1 signin SVG swap
- Lines 207-226: left panel background + text colors
- Lines 264-268: Authenticate H1 color
- Lines 337-348: button visibility

Recommendation: Consolidate into 1-2 dark mode blocks (one for structural/layout rules, one for color overrides), or at minimum add a table-of-contents comment at the top listing all dark mode overrides. Also merge the split `main.flex.flex-col.items-center > h1.text-primary` rules at lines 232-234 and 258-261 into a single block.

This is a refactor/cleanup task, not a functional bug.

title: Fix: redundant `background-position: left center` in mobile logo override in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
Epic: Replace SDK simple write operations with native atproto calls
description:
## Why
Phase 3 of the SDK removal migration. Phases 1-2 replaced auth and reads. Now we replace the simple write operations: deletes and profile creates/updates.

## Goal
After this epic, deleteHypercert, deleteRecord, and all profile create/update operations use native atproto calls. No scopedRepo write calls remain for these operations.

## Scope
6 write operations:
- deleteHypercert (lib/create-actions.ts) ŌĆö scopedRepo.hypercerts.delete ŌåÆ agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord
- deleteRecord (lib/create-actions.ts) ŌĆö scopedRepo.records.delete ŌåÆ agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord
- createCertifiedProfile (app/api/profile/update/route.ts) ŌĆö uploadBlob for avatar/banner + createRecord
- updateCertifiedProfile (app/api/profile/update/route.ts) ŌĆö uploadBlob for avatar/banner + putRecord (merge with existing)
- createBskyProfile (app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts) ŌĆö same pattern
- updateBskyProfile (app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts) ŌĆö same pattern

## Constraints
- Do NOT touch complex compound writes (hypercert create/update, addContribution, addEvaluation, addMeasurement, addAttachment, attachLocation, updateMeasurement) ŌĆö those are Phase 4
- Profile updates must handle: File ŌåÆ uploadBlob ŌåÆ BlobRef, null ŌåÆ remove field, undefined ŌåÆ preserve existing
- The app must build (pnpm build) after all tasks complete

## Success
- All 6 simple write operations use native atproto calls
- No @ts-expect-error comments on these operations
- pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: 52ffa54 All Phase 3 simple write tasks completed and verified

title: Migrate deleteHypercert and deleteRecord to native atproto deleteRecord
description:
## Files
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify ŌĆö 2 functions only)

## What to do
Replace the 2 delete operations with direct `agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord()` calls.

### deleteHypercert (lines 255-274):
Current:
```ts
// @ts-expect-error
await ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.delete(params.hypercertUri);
```

The SDK's `hypercerts.delete(uri)` parses the AT-URI and calls deleteRecord. We already have the parsed URI from `parseAtUri` on line 262.

New:
```ts
await ctx.agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord({
  repo: ctx.activeDid,
  collection: parsed.collection || 'org.hypercerts.claim.activity',
  rkey: parsed.rkey,
});
```

Remove the `@ts-expect-error` comment.

### deleteRecord (lines 321-343):
Current:
```ts
// @ts-expect-error
await ctx.scopedRepo.records.delete({
  collection: parsed.collection,
  rkey: parsed.rkey,
});
```

New:
```ts
await ctx.agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord({
  repo: ctx.activeDid,
  collection: parsed.collection,
  rkey: parsed.rkey,
});
```

Remove the `@ts-expect-error` comment.

### Both functions already have:
- DID ownership validation (parsed.did !== ctx.activeDid check)
- AT-URI parsing via parseAtUri
- Error handling

## Don't
- Don't touch any other functions in create-actions.ts
- Don't change the ownership validation logic
- Don't change the return values ({ success: true })
acceptance_criteria:
1. deleteHypercert uses ctx.agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord
2. deleteRecord uses ctx.agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord
3. Both @ts-expect-error comments removed
4. Ownership validation preserved
5. pnpm build passes
close_reason: 66d397b Migrate deleteHypercert and deleteRecord to native atproto deleteRecord

title: Fix: getBlobURL in lib/utils.ts always returns JPEG format for CDN URLs regardless of actual MIME type
Fix: ePDS email login button has no loading/disabled state during redirect (from hypercerts-dhz.8/dhz.10)
description: Review of hypercerts-dhz.8 and dhz.10 found: The 'Sign in with email' button (login-dialog.tsx lines 105-116) has no loading state and no disabled state. When clicked, window.location.href is set and the browser navigates away ŌĆö but there is a brief moment before navigation where the user could click again. More importantly, the handle-based 'Sign In' button (line 71-78) shows a Spinner and is disabled while loginMutation.isPending, but the email button has no equivalent feedback. The spec (dhz.10 acceptance criteria) does not require a loading state, but the UX is inconsistent: one button shows loading, the other does not. Additionally, if the PAR request to ePDS takes time (it involves a network round-trip + DPoP nonce retry), the user sees no feedback at all ŌĆö the button just sits there. Fix: add a local isNavigating state that is set to true on click, show a Spinner and disable the button while isNavigating is true. Evidence: login-dialog.tsx line 105-116 vs lines 71-78.

title: Fix: Evaluation and Measurement data cast to local interfaces without validation (evaluations-section.tsx, measurements-section.tsx)
Fix: Trash icon buttons in evaluation-form.tsx and measurement-form.tsx missing aria-label
description:
## Files
- components/evaluation-form.tsx lines 278-286, 447-457, 513-526
- components/measurement-form.tsx lines 375-383, 630-639

## Severity
MEDIUM ŌĆö Accessibility

## Problem
Several icon-only Trash buttons in evaluation-form.tsx and measurement-form.tsx are missing aria-label. Screen readers will announce these as unlabeled buttons. The contributions-form.tsx has the same issue (line 230-238).

Note: Some buttons DO have aria-label (e.g., evaluation-form.tsx line 255 'Remove evaluator', measurement-form.tsx line 352 'Remove measurer') but the pattern is inconsistent ŌĆö the manual DID/URI remove buttons do not.

## Evidence
```tsx
// evaluation-form.tsx line 278 ŌĆö no aria-label
<Button
  variant="ghost"
  size="icon"
  onClick={() => removeManualDid(index)}
  disabled={manualDids.length === 1 || mutation.isPending}
  className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-destructive"
>
  <Trash className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

## Fix
Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons:
```tsx
<Button
  type="button"
  variant="ghost"
  size="icon"
  aria-label="Remove DID"
  onClick={() => removeManualDid(index)}
  ...
>
  <Trash className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

title: Epic: Replace SDK complex compound write operations with native atproto calls
description:
## Why
Phase 4 (final) of the SDK removal migration. Phases 1-3 replaced auth, reads, and simple writes. Now we replace the 8 remaining complex compound write operations.

## Goal
After this epic, zero @ts-expect-error comments remain. Zero imports from @hypercerts-org/sdk-core for runtime operations. The SDK can be removed in Phase 5 (cleanup).

## Scope ŌĆö 8 operations:
1. addEvaluation ŌĆö single createRecord (simplest)
2. addMeasurement ŌĆö createRecord + optional location creation
3. updateMeasurement ŌĆö getRecord + merge + putRecord + optional location creation
4. addContribution ŌĆö creates contributorInformation + contributionDetails records, then updates hypercert
5. hypercerts.create ŌĆö compound: upload image, create rights, process contributors, create claim (with content-hash rkey), create attachments
6. hypercerts.update ŌĆö getRecord + merge + optional image upload + putRecord
7. addAttachment ŌĆö resolve subjects to StrongRefs, upload content blobs, optional location, createRecord
8. attachLocation ŌĆö create location record, then update hypercert's locations array

## Key patterns needed:
- StrongRef resolution: given an AT-URI, fetch the record to get its CID, return { uri, cid }
- Location creation: create app.certified.location record, handle string/File/URL content
- Blob upload: agent.com.atproto.repo.uploadBlob(blob) ŌåÆ BlobRef
- Content-hash rkey: hypercert claim uses rkey format 'hc2:{sha256}' based on record content

## Constraints
- Must build (pnpm build) after all tasks
- Keep @hypercerts-org/sdk-core in package.json for now (type imports remain ŌĆö Phase 5)

## Success
- Zero @ts-expect-error comments on scopedRepo calls
- All 8 operations use native atproto calls
- pnpm build succeeds

title: Fix: Duplicate LocationEntry interface declaration in measurement-form.tsx
Epic: Resolve CodeRabbit review for PR #7
description: Resolve all open CodeRabbit inline review comments on PR #7 (quick-fixes branch). 10 comments across 10 files, grouped into 8 tasks covering security (JWK allowlist), bugs (undefined profile vars, simultaneous loading/error states, loose URL check), error handling (3 API routes), and style/nit fixes.

title: Fix: JWK private key stripping ŌĆö switch denylist to allowlist
description:
## Files
- app/jwks.json/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 68.

**Original comment:**
> Private key stripping may be incomplete for RSA JWKs. The filter only removes d, use, and key_ops. For EC keys this is sufficient. However, if RSA keys are ever used, the fields p, q, dp, dq, qi, and oth are also private and would be leaked through this endpoint. Consider using an allowlist of public fields instead of a denylist.

**Suggested fix:**
Replace the PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS denylist with a PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS allowlist. Define:
```ts
const PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS = ["kty", "crv", "x", "y", "n", "e", "kid", "alg"] as const;
```
Then filter entries to keep only keys in PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS, and append `key_ops: ["verify"]` to each result.

**Context:**
This is the JWKS endpoint that exposes public keys for OAuth signature verification. Leaking private key material would be a critical security issue. The current denylist approach is fragile ŌĆö an allowlist is safer.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the key generation or storage logic
- Don't change the route handler signature or response structure
acceptance_criteria: The keys mapping in app/jwks.json/route.ts uses an allowlist (PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS or equivalent) containing only public JWK fields (kty, crv, x, y, n, e, kid, alg) instead of a denylist. The old PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS array is removed. Each key in the response still includes key_ops: ["verify"]. No RSA private fields (d, p, q, dp, dq, qi, oth) can appear in the JSON response regardless of key type. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: 9555b4f fix: replace PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS denylist with PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS allowlist in jwks.json route ŌĆö already implemented as part of hypercerts-1hr.3

title: Fix: activeProfileName/Handle always undefined in SignedInProvider
description:
## Files
- providers/SignedInProvider.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 20.

**Original comment:**
> activeProfileName and activeProfileHandle are declared as const and never assigned, so they are always undefined. Unlike avatarUrl and handle (which are let and populated from the profile), these remain unused yet are passed to Navbar.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Change `const activeProfileName: string | undefined = undefined;` to `let activeProfileName: string | undefined = undefined;`
2. Change `const activeProfileHandle: string | undefined = undefined;` to `let activeProfileHandle: string | undefined = undefined;`
3. Inside the `if (repo)` block, after the profile is fetched, add:
   ```ts
   activeProfileName = profile?.displayName;
   activeProfileHandle = profile?.handle;
   ```

**Context:**
These variables are passed as props to the Navbar component. Without this fix, the Navbar never receives the user's display name or handle, even when the profile is successfully loaded.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the Navbar component itself
- Don't change how avatarUrl or handle are assigned
acceptance_criteria: In providers/SignedInProvider.tsx, activeProfileName and activeProfileHandle are declared with let (not const). Both are assigned from the profile data inside the if (repo) block: activeProfileName = profile?.displayName and activeProfileHandle = profile?.handle. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: 092e10e fix: populate activeProfileName and activeProfileHandle in SignedInProvider - already implemented, TypeScript compiles cleanly

title: Fix: loading and error states render simultaneously in evaluations section
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-evaluations-section.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 87.

**Original comment:**
> If isLoadingLinks is false but isLoadingDetails is true while isErrorLinks is also true, both the skeleton and error panel will render at the same time. Consider using an if/else-if chain or guarding error rendering with !isLoading.

**Suggested fix:**
Replace the three separate conditional renders with a single if/else-if chain:
```tsx
{isLoading ? (
  ...skeleton...
) : isError ? (
  ...error panel...
) : (
  ...content...
)}
```
This ensures only one of skeleton, error, or content renders at any time. Use a combined loading flag if needed: `const isLoading = isLoadingLinks || isLoadingDetails;`

**Context:**
The component currently has three independent conditional blocks that can overlap. The skeleton shows during loading, the error panel shows on error, and content shows when neither ŌĆö but the guards are not mutually exclusive.

## Don't
- Don't change the skeleton component itself
- Don't change the error panel content or styling
- Don't change the data fetching hooks
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
acceptance_criteria: In components/hypercert-evaluations-section.tsx, the loading skeleton, error panel, and content are rendered in a mutually exclusive if/else-if chain (or equivalent ternary). It is impossible for both the skeleton and error panel to be visible at the same time. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: 9487246 Fix already implemented: loading/error/content rendered in mutually exclusive if/else-if chain

title: Fix: loose includes("https") URL check in measurement view
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 118.

**Original comment:**
> uri?.includes("https") is a loose URL protocol check. It would match any URI containing "https" anywhere in the string (e.g., a path segment). Use uri?.startsWith("https") for a more precise protocol check.

**Suggested fix:**
Change:
```tsx
uri={uri?.includes("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
```
To:
```tsx
uri={uri?.startsWith("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
```

**Context:**
The URILink component receives a uri prop. If the URI is already an https URL, it's used directly; otherwise it's converted via getPDSlsURI(). The current includes() check could incorrectly match URIs like `at://user.example/https-path`.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the URILink or getPDSlsURI implementations
- Don't change other URI checks elsewhere in the file
acceptance_criteria: In components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx, the URI check uses uri?.startsWith("https") instead of uri?.includes("https"). The includes("https") pattern does not appear anywhere in this file. TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: 87309cc Fix loose includes(https) URL check ŌĆö already applied

title: Fix: safe error handling across 3 API routes (add-location, profile/update, auth/logout)
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged 3 related error-handling issues:

1. [error-handling] Line 102 in add-location/route.ts: `(e as Error).message` leaks internal error details to the client and is undefined if e is not an Error instance. Replace with safe extraction: `const detail = e instanceof Error ? e.message : "Unknown error";` and use a generic message in the response (do NOT include the raw error message in the client response ŌĆö keep it server-side only via console.error which is already present).

2. [error-handling] Line 128 in profile/update/route.ts: Same `(error as Error).message` unsafe cast pattern. Replace with `error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)` for the server-side log, and return only a generic error message to the client.

3. [error-handling] Line 21 in auth/logout/route.ts: Raw error message leaked via template literal `Logout failed: ${(e as Error).message}`. Change to return only `{ error: "Logout failed" }` without the raw message. Keep the existing server-side logging.

**Suggested fixes:**
For all 3 routes:
- Use `e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)` for server-side logging
- Return only generic error messages to the client (no `.message` interpolation)
- Keep existing console.error/logging calls intact

**Context:**
These are API routes that catch errors and return JSON responses. Leaking internal error messages (Redis connection errors, SDK internals, file paths) to API consumers is a security concern. The server-side logging already captures the full error.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in these files
- Don't remove the existing console.error/logging calls
- Don't change the HTTP status codes
- Don't introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: In all 3 files: (1) The pattern (e as Error).message or (error as Error).message does not appear in any client-facing NextResponse.json() call. (2) Each catch block uses instanceof Error check for safe error extraction in server-side logging. (3) Client responses contain only generic error messages without interpolated error details. (4) Existing console.error calls are preserved. (5) TypeScript compiles without errors.
close_reason: f5fbb5d fix: safe error handling in add-location and profile/update routes

title: Epic: Add ePDS email/OTP login (scrappy e2e)
description:
Add ePDS email/OTP login as a second login option alongside the existing handle-based SDK login. The goal is to validate the full flow end-to-end as fast as possible ŌĆö especially that manually constructing a NodeSavedSession and writing it to Redis works with sdk.restoreSession(). Polish comes later.

Success looks like: a user can click 'Sign in with email', enter their email, get redirected to ePDS, enter the OTP, get redirected back, and the app recognizes them as logged in (getSession/getAuthenticatedRepo work).

Key constraints:
- ePDS uses a separate PDS URL (NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL env var)
- sdk.authorize() cannot be used because it requires a handle, not an email
- We must do PAR + PKCE + DPoP manually for the login handler
- sdk.callback() cannot be used because it expects state in Redis (written by sdk.authorize)
- After manual token exchange, we construct a NodeSavedSession and write it to RedisSessionStore so sdk.restoreSession() works transparently
- The user-did cookie is set identically to the existing flow
- UX is scrappy ŌĆö just needs to work, not look good
close_reason: All 12 children closed. All P1 discovered bugs closed. Most P2 discovered bugs closed (already fixed in dhz.11-16). E2E flow validated working with both Flow 1 (emailŌåÆdirect OTP) and Flow 2 (no emailŌåÆePDS collects). Remaining P2/P3 discovered issues (lv8, 7vo, 6a6, cev, x2b) are polish items for a follow-up epic.

title: Add NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL and OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET to config
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)
- .env.example (modify)

## What to do
Add two new config values:

1. `epdsUrl` ŌĆö read from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL`. This is the ePDS PDS base URL (e.g. `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`). Add it to the `config` object. Do NOT add it to the required env vars validation ŌĆö it should be optional (only needed if ePDS login is used).

2. `oauthSessionSecret` ŌĆö read from `process.env.OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET`. This is a server-only secret (32+ chars) used to HMAC-sign the transient OAuth session cookie. Add it to the `config` object. Do NOT add to required env vars ŌĆö it should be optional.

3. Add `epdsRedirectUri` ŌĆö `${redirectBaseUrl}/api/oauth/callback` to the config object.

4. In `.env.example`, add a new section documenting both vars with comments explaining their purpose.

## Don't
- Add these to the required env vars array (they're optional)
- Change any existing config values
- Add any NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix to OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET (it's server-only)
acceptance_criteria:
1. config.epdsUrl returns the value of NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL when set
2. config.oauthSessionSecret returns the value of OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET when set  
3. config.epdsRedirectUri returns ${redirectBaseUrl}/api/oauth/callback
4. .env.example documents both new vars with examples
5. App still starts without these vars set (no validation errors)
6. pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: 9f485b0 Add epdsUrl, oauthSessionSecret, epdsRedirectUri to config

title: Support Flow 1 ŌĆö pass email as login_hint on auth redirect URL only (not PAR body)
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (modify)
- components/login-dialog.tsx (modify)

## Context
We discovered from the ePDS demo source (packages/demo/src/app/api/oauth/login/route.ts) that:
- The PAR body must NEVER include login_hint (PDS core validates it as ATProto identity, rejects emails)
- The login_hint with an email goes ONLY on the auth redirect URL query param
- The auth service (login-page.ts) reads login_hint from the redirect URL, checks for '@', and if present skips the email form and goes straight to OTP entry

This means one route can handle both Flow 1 and Flow 2 ŌĆö the only difference is whether login_hint is appended to the auth redirect URL.

## What to do

### app/api/oauth/login/route.ts
1. Read optional `email` from query params: `const email = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get('email')`
2. PAR body stays exactly as-is ŌĆö NO login_hint (this is already correct after dhz.9)
3. When building the auth redirect URL, conditionally append login_hint:
```typescript
const authUrl = new URL(authEndpoint)
authUrl.searchParams.set('client_id', clientId)
authUrl.searchParams.set('request_uri', request_uri)
if (email) {
  authUrl.searchParams.set('login_hint', email)
}
const response = NextResponse.redirect(authUrl.toString())
```
4. Keep all other logic identical (DPoP, PKCE, PAR, cookie, etc.)

### components/login-dialog.tsx
The login dialog should have 3 login methods:
1. **Handle login** (existing, unchanged) ŌĆö handle input + 'Sign In' button
2. **Email login** (Flow 1) ŌĆö email input + 'Sign in with email' button
3. **ePDS login** (Flow 2) ŌĆö 'Sign in with email' button works even when email is empty

Implementation:
1. Add back the email state: `const [email, setEmail] = useState('')`
2. Add back the MailIcon import from lucide-react
3. After the existing handle login section, add an 'or' divider
4. Add an email input field (type='email', placeholder='user@example.com') using the same InputGroup pattern
5. Add a 'Sign in with email' button that:
   - If email is non-empty: navigates to `/api/oauth/login?email=${encodeURIComponent(email)}` (Flow 1)
   - If email is empty: navigates to `/api/oauth/login` (Flow 2)
   - The button is ALWAYS enabled (works for both flows)
   - Uses `window.location.href` for navigation (GET redirect, not fetch)

## Don't
- Add login_hint to the PAR body ŌĆö this is what caused the original 400 error
- Remove or change the existing handle-based login
- Change the callback route
- Add any new dependencies or mutation hooks
acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/oauth/login?email=test@example.com redirects to auth endpoint with login_hint=test@example.com in the URL
2. GET /api/oauth/login (no email) redirects to auth endpoint WITHOUT login_hint in the URL
3. Neither request includes login_hint in the PAR body
4. LoginDialog shows: handle input + Sign In, then 'or' divider, then email input + 'Sign in with email' button
5. 'Sign in with email' button works with or without email entered
6. Existing handle-based login still works
7. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
close_reason: 82d32d0 feat: support Flow 1 email login_hint on auth redirect URL

title: Fix: add try/catch to both OAuth routes + DPoP nonce retry on 401
acceptance_criteria:
1. Both routes have top-level try/catch that logs errors and redirects to /?error=auth_failed
2. Callback route returns redirects (not JSON) for all error cases
3. fetchWithDpopRetry retries on both 400 and 401 when dpop-nonce header is present
4. All error cases log to console.error with descriptive messages
5. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
6. Happy path is unchanged
close_reason: b4f7ed9 fix: add try/catch to OAuth routes + DPoP nonce retry on 401

title: Fix: cookie security flags ŌĆö sameSite on user-did, proper deletion of epds-oauth-session
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Add sameSite: 'lax' to user-did cookie (fixes hypercerts-5jd / hypercerts-6r0)

The user-did cookie is set without sameSite. Since the callback is a cross-site redirect from `auth.<pds-hostname>` back to our app, some browsers (especially Safari) may block the cookie without an explicit `sameSite: 'lax'`.

Change the user-did cookie setting from:
```typescript
response.cookies.set('user-did', tokenData.sub, {
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: config.isProduction,
  maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7,
  path: '/',
})
```

To:
```typescript
response.cookies.set('user-did', tokenData.sub, {
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: config.isProduction,
  sameSite: 'lax',
  maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7,
  path: '/',
})
```

### 2. Fix epds-oauth-session cookie deletion (fixes hypercerts-0p8)

The cookie deletion currently only sets maxAge=0 and path, but Safari requires matching httpOnly/secure/sameSite flags to properly delete a cookie.

Change from:
```typescript
response.cookies.set(EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, '', {
  maxAge: 0,
  path: '/',
})
```

To:
```typescript
response.cookies.set(EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, '', {
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: config.isProduction,
  sameSite: 'lax',
  maxAge: 0,
  path: '/',
})
```

## Don't
- Change any other cookie settings
- Change the session construction logic
- Change the login route
acceptance_criteria:
1. user-did cookie includes sameSite: 'lax'
2. epds-oauth-session deletion includes httpOnly, secure, sameSite flags matching the original set
3. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
close_reason: c323ec7 fix: cookie security flags ŌĆö sameSite lax on user-did, proper deletion of epds-oauth-session

title: Fix: validate tokenData.sub as DID + add TTL to Redis session
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Validate tokenData.sub as a DID before using it (fixes hypercerts-xp2)

After parsing the token response, validate that `sub` looks like a DID before writing it to Redis and setting it as a cookie. An empty or malformed sub would poison the session store.

Add after line ~100 (after parsing tokenData):
```typescript
// Validate sub is a DID
if (!tokenData.sub || !(tokenData.sub.startsWith('did:plc:') || tokenData.sub.startsWith('did:web:'))) {
  console.error('[oauth/callback] Invalid sub in token response:', tokenData.sub)
  return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/?error=auth_failed', config.baseUrl))
}
```

### 2. Add TTL to Redis session (fixes hypercerts-7vo)

Currently `sessionStore.set(did, session)` writes with no expiry. ePDS sessions would persist in Redis forever.

Check if `sessionStore.set` accepts a TTL parameter. If it does, pass a reasonable TTL (e.g. 7 days = 604800 seconds, matching the user-did cookie maxAge).

If `sessionStore.set` does NOT accept a TTL (check the RedisSessionStore implementation in lib/redis-session-store.ts), then add a comment noting this limitation:
```typescript
// TODO: RedisSessionStore.set() does not support TTL ŌĆö sessions persist until manually deleted
// The SDK handles token refresh internally, so stale sessions get refreshed or error out
```

## Don't
- Change the NodeSavedSession shape
- Change the DPoP or PKCE logic
- Add new dependencies
acceptance_criteria:
1. Token response sub is validated as did:plc: or did:web: before use
2. Invalid sub results in redirect to /?error=auth_failed with console.error log
3. Redis TTL is set if sessionStore supports it, or a TODO comment is added
4. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
close_reason: 6da38d4 fix: validate tokenData.sub as DID + add TTL TODO to Redis session

title: Fix: use config.oauthSessionSecret instead of direct process.env + fix auth endpoint port handling
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-session-cookie.ts (modify)
- lib/epds-config.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Use config.oauthSessionSecret instead of process.env (fixes hypercerts-2wn)

In `lib/epds-session-cookie.ts`, the HMAC secret is read directly from `process.env.OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET` instead of using the centralized `config.oauthSessionSecret` that was added in dhz.1.

Change the import and secret reading:
```typescript
import { config } from '@/lib/config'

// In encodeEpdsSessionCookie:
const secret = config.oauthSessionSecret
if (!secret) {
  throw new Error('OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is not configured')
}

// In decodeEpdsSessionCookie:
const secret = config.oauthSessionSecret
if (!secret) {
  throw new Error('OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is not configured')
}
```

### 2. Fix auth endpoint to preserve port from NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL (fixes hypercerts-vmn)

In `lib/epds-config.ts`, the auth endpoint is constructed using only `hostname`:
```typescript
authEndpoint: `https://auth.${new URL(epdsUrl).hostname}/oauth/authorize`
```

This drops the port if `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` includes one (e.g. `https://localhost:3001`). Use `host` instead of `hostname` to preserve the port:
```typescript
const url = new URL(epdsUrl)
const authHost = url.port ? `auth.${url.hostname}:${url.port}` : `auth.${url.hostname}`
// authEndpoint: `${url.protocol}//${authHost}/oauth/authorize`
```

Also use `url.protocol` instead of hardcoding `https://` ŌĆö this allows local development with http.

## Don't
- Change the HMAC algorithm or cookie format
- Change the endpoint paths (/oauth/par, /oauth/token, /oauth/authorize)
- Add new dependencies
acceptance_criteria:
1. epds-session-cookie.ts reads secret from config.oauthSessionSecret, not process.env
2. Auth endpoint preserves port when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL includes one
3. Auth endpoint uses the protocol from the URL (not hardcoded https)
4. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
close_reason: 98de1d5 fix: use config.oauthSessionSecret + preserve port in auth endpoint

title: Redesign login UX: two-door dialog with distinct handle vs email paths
description:
## Files - components/login-dialog.tsx (rewrite) - components/navbar.tsx (modify) - app/globals.css (modify ŌĆö add new animations/styles) ## Design Concept: Two Doors The login dialog presents two distinct visual "doors" ŌĆö side-by-side panels that represent two different identity paradigms: **Left door: Handle** ŌĆö technical, precise, monospace-flavored. For users who already have an ATProto identity. **Right door: Email** ŌĆö warm, approachable, familiar. For users who just want to sign in with their email. Each door is a clickable card. Clicking one expands it into the active form while the other recedes. This is progressive disclosure ŌĆö you choose your path first, then the relevant form appears. ### Visual Design **Initial state (two doors side by side):** - Dialog opens showing two equal-width cards/panels side by side - Left card: dark/charcoal background (`create-accent`), white text. Icon: `AtSignIcon`. Title: "Handle". Subtitle: "Sign in with your ATProto identity" - Right card: warm/light background (subtle warm tint or glass-panel). Icon: `MailIcon`. Title: "Email". Subtitle: "Sign in with a one-time code" - Both cards have a subtle hover effect (slight scale, border glow) - Cards should feel like actual doors/portals ŌĆö generous padding, centered content, maybe a subtle gradient or texture difference between them **Selected state (one door expanded):** - Clicking a door transitions to showing only that door's form - The selected door expands to fill the dialog width - The other door shrinks to a small pill/chip at the top (so you can switch back) - Transition should be smooth ŌĆö use CSS transitions on width/opacity/transform - The form content fades in with staggered animation (use existing `stagger-children` class) **Handle form (when handle door is selected):** - Same as existing: handle InputGroup with AtSignIcon, Sign In button, Create account link - Uses `useLoginMutation` for the submit - Shows Spinner when pending **Email form (when email door is selected):** - Email InputGroup with MailIcon (optional ŌĆö user can leave empty for Flow 2) - "Continue" button ŌĆö navigates to `/api/oauth/login?email=...` or `/api/oauth/login` - Small helper text: "Enter your email to skip straight to the code, or leave blank" - Uses `window.location.href` for navigation **Only show email door when `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` is set.** If not set, show only the handle form (no doors, just the form directly). ### Navbar Changes Replace the Popover login in navbar.tsx with a Dialog: 1. Remove the inline form state (`handle`, `handleSubmit`) from navbar 2. Import `Dialog`, `DialogContent`, `DialogTrigger` from `@/components/ui/dialog` 3. Import `LoginDialog` from `@/components/login-dialog` 4. The "Sign In" button becomes a `DialogTrigger` 5. `DialogContent` wraps `` with `sm:max-w-xl` or `sm:max-w-2xl` (wider than default to fit two doors) 6. Add `DialogTitle` with sr-only text for accessibility 7. Keep all signed-in state (dropdown, links, avatar) completely unchanged ### Component Structure ```tsx // login-dialog.tsx export default function LoginDialog() { const [selectedDoor, setSelectedDoor] = useState<'handle' | 'email' | null>(null) const hasEpds = !!process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL // If no ePDS, skip doors and show handle form directly if (!hasEpds) { return } return (
{selectedDoor === null ? ( // Two doors side by side ) : ( // Selected door expanded with form setSelectedDoor(null)} /> )}
) } ``` ### Aesthetic Details - Use Syne for door titles, Outfit for subtitles and form labels - The handle door should feel "technical" ŌĆö darker, maybe a subtle monospace hint in the subtitle - The email door should feel "friendly" ŌĆö lighter, warmer - Transition between doors and expanded state: 300ms ease-out, opacity + transform - The "back" action (clicking the inactive pill) should reverse the animation - Use the existing `glass-panel` class for the email door background - Use `create-accent` bg for the handle door - Add a subtle noise texture (`noise-bg`) to the handle door for depth - Hover states: slight translateY(-2px) + shadow increase ### Animations to add to globals.css ```css @keyframes expandDoor { from { opacity: 0.8; transform: scale(0.95); } to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } } .animate-expand-door { animation: expandDoor 0.3s ease-out both; } ``` ## Don't - Add framer-motion or any new npm dependencies ŌĆö CSS animations only - Change any backend routes or auth logic - Change the signed-in navbar state (dropdown, avatar, links) - Break the existing handle login flow (useLoginMutation must still work) - Break the ePDS email flow (window.location.href to /api/oauth/login must still work) - Use generic tab components ŌĆö this should feel custom and intentional - Make the doors look like boring cards ŌĆö they should have distinct visual character
acceptance_criteria:
1. Navbar 'Sign In' button opens a Dialog (not a Popover) containing LoginDialog
2. LoginDialog shows two distinct door panels when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set
3. Clicking a door expands it to show the relevant form, other door becomes a switchable pill
4. Handle door form: handle input + Sign In + Create account ŌĆö uses useLoginMutation
5. Email door form: optional email input + Continue ŌĆö navigates to /api/oauth/login
6. When NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is NOT set, LoginDialog shows handle form directly (no doors)
7. Transitions between states are smooth (CSS, 300ms)
8. Handle door has dark/charcoal aesthetic, email door has light/warm aesthetic
9. Navbar no longer has inline login form state ŌĆö all login logic is in LoginDialog
10. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
11. Existing handle login still works end-to-end
12. Existing email login still works end-to-end
close_reason: 7ad9152 feat: two-door login dialog with handle/email paths

title: Redesign login dialog: pill toggle tabs, same width as original popover
description:
## Files - components/login-dialog.tsx (rewrite) - components/navbar.tsx (modify) ## Context The two-door design (dhz.15) was too wide and extended outside the navbar. We need a compact pill toggle design that fits within the same width as the original popover (w-80 / sm:max-w-sm). ## What to do ### components/login-dialog.tsx ŌĆö Full rewrite Replace the two-door design with a pill toggle (segmented control) at the top that switches between Handle and Email forms. **Layout:** 1. A heading: 'Sign In' (Syne, bold) 2. A pill toggle with two segments: 'Handle' | 'Email' 3. Below the toggle, the active form (handle or email) with a smooth crossfade **Pill toggle design:** - A rounded-full container with a muted background (`bg-muted`) - Two buttons inside, each taking 50% width - The active button has `bg-background` (white in light mode) with a subtle shadow ŌĆö looks like it's raised/selected - Inactive button is transparent with muted text - Transition the active indicator smoothly (use CSS transition on the buttons themselves ŌĆö no sliding indicator needed, just swap bg/text colors with transition-all duration-200) - Use Outfit font, text-sm, font-medium **Handle form (when Handle pill is active):** - Handle InputGroup with AtSignIcon (same as existing) - Sign In button (uses `useLoginMutation`) - Create an account ghost button - Shows Spinner when pending **Email form (when Email pill is active):** - Email InputGroup with MailIcon (optional input) - Continue button ŌĆö navigates to `/api/oauth/login?email=...` or `/api/oauth/login` - Small helper text: 'Enter your email for a direct code, or continue without' - Uses `window.location.href` **Conditional rendering:** - Only show the pill toggle when `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` is set - If not set, show only the handle form directly (no toggle) **Component structure:** ```tsx export default function LoginDialog() { const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'handle' | 'email'>('handle') const hasEpds = !!process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL return (

Sign In

Choose how to continue

{hasEpds && ( )} {activeTab === 'handle' || !hasEpds ? ( ) : ( )}
) } ``` ### components/navbar.tsx ŌĆö Keep Dialog, fix width Keep the Dialog approach from dhz.15 (not Popover), but change the DialogContent width: - Change `sm:max-w-xl` to `sm:max-w-sm` (same width as the original popover was w-80) - Keep DialogTitle sr-only for accessibility - Keep all signed-in state unchanged ### Styling details - Pill toggle: `rounded-full bg-muted p-1` container, buttons inside with `rounded-full px-4 py-1.5` - Active button: `bg-background text-foreground shadow-sm` - Inactive button: `text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground` - Form transition: use `animate-fade-in` on the form content when switching tabs - Keep existing font conventions: Syne for headings, Outfit for body/labels - Keep existing color conventions: `create-accent` for primary actions ## Don't - Use the two-door design ŌĆö it's too wide - Add framer-motion or any new dependencies - Change any backend routes - Change the signed-in navbar state - Make the dialog wider than sm:max-w-sm - Remove the DoorSelection, ExpandedDoor, etc. components from dhz.15 ŌĆö just replace them entirely with the new pill toggle approach
acceptance_criteria:
1. LoginDialog shows a pill toggle with Handle | Email segments when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set
2. Clicking a pill segment switches the form below it
3. Active pill has white/raised background, inactive is transparent
4. Dialog width is sm:max-w-sm (same as original popover width)
5. Handle form: input + Sign In + Create account ŌĆö uses useLoginMutation
6. Email form: optional input + Continue ŌĆö navigates to /api/oauth/login
7. When NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is NOT set, shows handle form only (no toggle)
8. Dialog is centered and doesn't extend beyond the navbar
9. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
10. Both login flows still work end-to-end
close_reason: dcac8b3 redesign login dialog: pill toggle tabs, sm:max-w-sm width

title: Create lib/epds-helpers.ts ŌĆö PKCE + DPoP utilities
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-helpers.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a new file with the following exports, using only `node:crypto` (no new dependencies):

1. `generateCodeVerifier()` ŌĆö returns `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('base64url')`
2. `generateCodeChallenge(verifier: string)` ŌĆö returns SHA-256 hash of verifier as base64url
3. `generateState()` ŌĆö returns `crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('base64url')`
4. `generateDpopKeyPair()` ŌĆö generates EC P-256 key pair, returns `{ privateKey: KeyObject, publicJwk: JsonWebKey, privateJwk: JsonWebKey }`
5. `restoreDpopKeyPair(privateJwk: JsonWebKey)` ŌĆö recreates KeyObject from serialized JWK, returns `{ privateKey: KeyObject, publicJwk: JsonWebKey }`
6. `createDpopProof(opts: { privateKey: KeyObject, jwk: object, method: string, url: string, nonce?: string, accessToken?: string })` ŌĆö creates a DPoP proof JWT (ES256, typ: dpop+jwt). If nonce provided, include in payload. If accessToken provided, include `ath` (SHA-256 hash of token, base64url).
7. Internal `derToRaw(der: Buffer)` ŌĆö converts DER-encoded ECDSA signature to raw r||s format (64 bytes). Not exported.

The exact implementation is in the ePDS tutorial: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/ePDS/blob/main/docs/tutorial.md ŌĆö copy it verbatim with proper TypeScript types.

Also export a convenience function:
8. `fetchWithDpopRetry(url: string, body: URLSearchParams, privateKey: KeyObject, publicJwk: object): Promise<Response>` ŌĆö wraps the nonce retry pattern (first request without nonce, if 400 + dpop-nonce header, retry with nonce).

## Don't
- Add any npm dependencies
- Use jose or any other JWT library ŌĆö use raw node:crypto only
- Export derToRaw
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at lib/epds-helpers.ts
2. All 8 functions listed above are exported (except derToRaw which is internal)
3. No imports other than node:crypto
4. pnpm build succeeds
5. TypeScript compiles without errors
close_reason: 3ce9e52 Create lib/epds-helpers.ts ŌĆö PKCE + DPoP utilities

title: Create lib/epds-config.ts ŌĆö ePDS endpoint derivation
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-config.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a module that derives ePDS OAuth endpoints from the config.

Export a function `getEpdsEndpoints()` that reads `config.epdsUrl` and returns:
```typescript
{
  parEndpoint: string    // ${epdsUrl}/oauth/par
  authEndpoint: string   // https://auth.${hostname}/oauth/authorize
  tokenEndpoint: string  // ${epdsUrl}/oauth/token
}
```

Where `hostname` is extracted from `epdsUrl` using `new URL(epdsUrl).hostname`.

Example: if `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL=https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`:
- PAR: `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/oauth/par`
- Auth: `https://auth.pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/oauth/authorize`
- Token: `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/oauth/token`

Also export `getEpdsClientId()` which returns `config.clientId` (same client ID as the existing flow ŌĆö the app is the same OAuth client regardless of login method).

And `getEpdsRedirectUri()` which returns `config.epdsRedirectUri`.

Throw a clear error if `config.epdsUrl` is not set when these functions are called.

## Don't
- Import anything other than `@/lib/config`
- Hardcode any URLs
- Make this a class ŌĆö plain exported functions are fine
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at lib/epds-config.ts
2. getEpdsEndpoints() returns correct PAR, Auth, Token URLs derived from NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL
3. Auth endpoint uses 'auth.' subdomain prefix
4. Throws descriptive error if NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is not set
5. pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: cc7df81 Create lib/epds-config.ts ŌĆö ePDS endpoint derivation

title: Create lib/epds-session-cookie.ts ŌĆö signed transient OAuth cookie
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-session-cookie.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a module for encoding/decoding a signed HttpOnly cookie that stores transient OAuth state during the ePDS login flow. This cookie lives for 10 minutes (matching PAR request_uri lifetime) and is deleted after the callback.

### Types
```typescript
interface EpdsOAuthSessionData {
  state: string
  codeVerifier: string
  dpopPrivateJwk: JsonWebKey  // from node:crypto
}
```

### Functions

1. `encodeEpdsSessionCookie(data: EpdsOAuthSessionData): string`
   - JSON.stringify the data
   - Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the JSON using `config.oauthSessionSecret`
   - Return `${base64url(json)}.${base64url(hmac)}`
   - Throw if `config.oauthSessionSecret` is not set

2. `decodeEpdsSessionCookie(cookie: string): EpdsOAuthSessionData`
   - Split on '.'
   - Verify HMAC matches
   - Parse and return the data
   - Throw 'Invalid session cookie' if HMAC doesn't match or parsing fails

### Cookie name
Export `EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME = 'epds-oauth-session'`

## Don't
- Add any npm dependencies (use node:crypto only)
- Store the cookie yourself ŌĆö just encode/decode the value. The route handlers will set/delete the cookie.
- Use JWT format ŌĆö the simple payload.signature format is sufficient
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at lib/epds-session-cookie.ts
2. encodeEpdsSessionCookie produces a string in format 'base64url.base64url'
3. decodeEpdsSessionCookie correctly round-trips data encoded by encodeEpdsSessionCookie
4. decodeEpdsSessionCookie throws on tampered data (modified payload or signature)
5. Throws if OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is not configured
6. pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: f089177 Add lib/epds-session-cookie.ts ŌĆö signed transient OAuth cookie

title: Create app/api/oauth/login/route.ts ŌĆö ePDS PAR + redirect
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a GET route handler: `GET /api/oauth/login?email=user@example.com`

### Flow
1. Read `email` from `req.nextUrl.searchParams.get('email')`. If missing, return 400 JSON error.
2. Call `generateDpopKeyPair()`, `generateCodeVerifier()`, `generateCodeChallenge(verifier)`, `generateState()` from `@/lib/epds-helpers`.
3. Get endpoints from `getEpdsEndpoints()`, client ID from `getEpdsClientId()`, redirect URI from `getEpdsRedirectUri()` ŌĆö all from `@/lib/epds-config`.
4. Build PAR body as URLSearchParams:
   - client_id, redirect_uri, response_type: 'code', scope: 'atproto transition:generic', state, code_challenge, code_challenge_method: 'S256', login_hint: email
5. Call `fetchWithDpopRetry(parEndpoint, parBody, privateKey, publicJwk)` from `@/lib/epds-helpers`.
6. If PAR response is not ok, return 502 JSON error with status text.
7. Parse `{ request_uri }` from PAR response JSON.
8. Encode session cookie: `encodeEpdsSessionCookie({ state, codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk: privateJwk })`
9. Build auth URL: `${authEndpoint}?client_id=${clientId}&request_uri=${request_uri}&login_hint=${email}` (all URI-encoded)
10. Create `NextResponse.redirect(authUrl)`
11. Set cookie on the response: name=EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, value=encoded cookie, httpOnly=true, secure=config.isProduction, sameSite='lax', maxAge=600, path='/'
12. Return the redirect response.

### Imports
- `NextRequest`, `NextResponse` from 'next/server'
- helpers from `@/lib/epds-helpers`
- config functions from `@/lib/epds-config`
- cookie functions from `@/lib/epds-session-cookie`
- `config` from `@/lib/config` (for isProduction)

## Don't
- Accept POST ŌĆö this is GET only (browser redirect)
- Use sdk.authorize() ŌĆö that's the whole point
- Forget login_hint in BOTH the PAR body AND the auth redirect URL (prevents flash of email form)
acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/oauth/login?email=test@example.com returns a 307 redirect
2. Redirect URL points to auth.{epds-hostname}/oauth/authorize with client_id, request_uri, and login_hint params
3. Response sets epds-oauth-session HttpOnly cookie with maxAge=600
4. GET /api/oauth/login without email param returns 400 JSON error
5. pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: 306dbc3 Add ePDS PAR + redirect login route

title: Create app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts ŌĆö token exchange + Redis session + user-did cookie
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a GET route handler: `GET /api/oauth/callback?code=...&state=...`

This is the critical task ŌĆö it does the manual token exchange and constructs a NodeSavedSession that the SDK can restore.

### Flow
1. Read `code` and `state` from query params. If either missing, return 400.
2. Read the `epds-oauth-session` cookie. If missing, return 400 ('No OAuth session found').
3. Call `decodeEpdsSessionCookie(cookieValue)` to get `{ state: savedState, codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk }`.
4. Verify `state === savedState`. If mismatch, return 400 ('State mismatch').
5. Call `restoreDpopKeyPair(dpopPrivateJwk)` to get `{ privateKey, publicJwk }`.
6. Build token body as URLSearchParams: grant_type='authorization_code', code, redirect_uri=getEpdsRedirectUri(), client_id=getEpdsClientId(), code_verifier=codeVerifier.
7. Call `fetchWithDpopRetry(tokenEndpoint, tokenBody, privateKey, publicJwk)`.
8. If token response is not ok, return 502 with error details.
9. Parse token response JSON. Expected shape: `{ access_token, token_type, scope, expires_in, sub, refresh_token? }`.
10. **Construct NodeSavedSession** and write to Redis:
```typescript
import { sessionStore } from '@/lib/hypercerts-sdk'

const nodeSavedSession = {
  dpopJwk: dpopPrivateJwk,  // the original private JWK from the cookie
  tokenSet: {
    iss: getEpdsEndpoints().tokenEndpoint.replace('/oauth/token', ''),  // issuer is the PDS origin
    sub: tokenResponse.sub,  // DID e.g. 'did:plc:abc123'
    aud: getEpdsEndpoints().tokenEndpoint.replace('/oauth/token', ''),  // audience = PDS
    scope: tokenResponse.scope || 'atproto transition:generic',
    access_token: tokenResponse.access_token,
    token_type: 'DPoP' as const,
    refresh_token: tokenResponse.refresh_token,
    expires_at: tokenResponse.expires_in
      ? new Date(Date.now() + tokenResponse.expires_in * 1000).toISOString()
      : undefined,
  },
}
await sessionStore.set(tokenResponse.sub, nodeSavedSession)
```
11. Create redirect response to `/`.
12. Set `user-did` cookie on the response (same as existing auth callback): httpOnly=true, secure=config.isProduction, maxAge=60*60*24*7, path='/'.
13. Delete the `epds-oauth-session` cookie (set maxAge=0).
14. Return the redirect.

### Critical notes on NodeSavedSession shape
- `dpopJwk` must be the **private** JWK (includes 'd' parameter) ŌĆö this is what the SDK uses to create DPoP proofs for subsequent API calls
- `iss` (issuer) should be the PDS origin URL (e.g. 'https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app') ŌĆö the SDK uses this to find the server metadata for token refresh
- `aud` should match the PDS URL that the DID resolves to ŌĆö for ePDS users this is the same as iss
- `token_type` must be literally 'DPoP' (capital D, capital P)
- The `authMethod` field can be omitted (it's optional, defaults to legacy handling)

### Imports
- NextRequest, NextResponse from 'next/server'
- restoreDpopKeyPair, fetchWithDpopRetry from '@/lib/epds-helpers'
- getEpdsEndpoints, getEpdsClientId, getEpdsRedirectUri from '@/lib/epds-config'
- decodeEpdsSessionCookie, EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME from '@/lib/epds-session-cookie'
- sessionStore from '@/lib/hypercerts-sdk'
- config from '@/lib/config'

## Don't
- Use sdk.callback() ŌĆö it won't work (state not in Redis state store)
- Generate a new DPoP key pair ŌĆö MUST restore from the cookie (same key used in PAR)
- Forget to delete the epds-oauth-session cookie after use
- Forget the 'DPoP' token_type in the session (SDK will reject it otherwise)
acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/oauth/callback?code=X&state=Y with valid epds-oauth-session cookie performs token exchange
2. On success, writes a NodeSavedSession to Redis via sessionStore.set(did, session)
3. Sets user-did cookie with the DID from token response
4. Deletes epds-oauth-session cookie
5. Redirects to /
6. Returns 400 if state param doesn't match cookie state
7. Returns 400 if epds-oauth-session cookie is missing
8. pnpm build succeeds
9. CRITICAL: after this callback, sdk.restoreSession(did) must succeed ŌĆö this is the whole point of the scrappy e2e
close_reason: 5ced804 add ePDS OAuth callback route ŌĆö token exchange + Redis session + user-did cookie

title: Add /api/oauth/callback to redirect_uris in client-metadata.json
description:
## Files
- app/client-metadata.json/route.ts (modify)
- lib/config.ts (modify ŌĆö buildClientMetadata function)

## What to do
The ePDS callback goes to `/api/oauth/callback` which is different from the existing `/api/auth/callback`. The client metadata's `redirect_uris` array must include both.

1. In `lib/config.ts`, update `buildClientMetadata()` to include both redirect URIs in the `redirect_uris` array:
   - `config.redirectUri` (existing: `${redirectBaseUrl}/api/auth/callback`)
   - `config.epdsRedirectUri` (new: `${redirectBaseUrl}/api/oauth/callback`) ŌĆö but ONLY if `config.epdsUrl` is set

2. In the loopback mode block AND the production mode block, change:
   ```typescript
   // Before
   redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
   // After
   redirect_uris: config.epdsUrl 
     ? [config.redirectUri, config.epdsRedirectUri]
     : [config.redirectUri],
   ```

## Don't
- Change the client_id
- Change any other metadata fields
- Always include the ePDS redirect URI even when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is not set
acceptance_criteria:
1. When NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set, GET /client-metadata.json returns redirect_uris with both /api/auth/callback and /api/oauth/callback
2. When NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is NOT set, redirect_uris only contains /api/auth/callback (no change from current behavior)
3. pnpm build succeeds
close_reason: 7cc6302 Add /api/oauth/callback to redirect_uris when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set

or

title: Switch ePDS login to Flow 2 ŌĆö remove login_hint from PAR body
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (modify)
- components/login-dialog.tsx (modify)

## What to do

### Problem
The ePDS PDS core validates `login_hint` as an ATProto identity (handle/DID), not an email address. Passing `login_hint=user@example.com` in the PAR body causes a 400 error: `Invalid login_hint`. We need to switch from Flow 1 (app collects email) to Flow 2 (ePDS collects email).

### Changes to app/api/oauth/login/route.ts
1. Change the route from requiring an `email` query param to not requiring it. The route should work as `GET /api/oauth/login` with no params.
2. Remove `login_hint: email` from the `parBody` URLSearchParams (line 50).
3. Remove `&login_hint=${encodeURIComponent(email)}` from the auth URL construction (line 82).
4. Remove the email validation check at the top (lines 22-28 that return 400 if email is missing).
5. Keep everything else identical ŌĆö DPoP, PKCE, PAR request, cookie, redirect all stay the same.

### Changes to components/login-dialog.tsx
1. Remove the email input field and its state variable.
2. Change the 'Sign in with email' button to just navigate to `/api/oauth/login` (no query params).
3. The button label can be 'Sign in with ePDS' or 'Sign in with email' ŌĆö doesn't matter for the scrappy version.
4. The button should always be enabled (no disabled state since there's no email to validate).
5. Keep the existing handle-based login completely unchanged.

## Don't
- Remove the DPoP nonce retry logic
- Change the callback route
- Change any other files
- Add login_hint anywhere ŌĆö that's what's broken
acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/oauth/login (no query params) returns a 307 redirect to the ePDS auth page
2. The PAR body does NOT contain login_hint
3. The auth redirect URL does NOT contain login_hint
4. The LoginDialog has a button that navigates to /api/oauth/login without any query params
5. Existing handle-based login still works
6. pnpm tsc --noEmit succeeds
close_reason: 395fe55 Switch ePDS login to Flow 2 ŌĆö remove login_hint

title: Epic: Apply Vercel React best practices to changed files on quick-fixes-test branch
description:
## Why
Audit of all files changed on quick-fixes-test vs main revealed 25+ violations of Vercel React Best Practices across 5 categories. These impact page load time, bundle size, and rendering correctness.

## What success looks like
All identified violations are fixed. No new violations introduced. Build and lint pass. The codebase follows Vercel's recommended patterns for async operations, bundle optimization, server-side performance, and conditional rendering.

## Key constraints
- Only modify files already changed on quick-fixes-test branch (no scope creep)
- Do NOT change behavior ŌĆö only performance/correctness patterns
- Build and lint must pass after each task
- Preserve existing Prettier formatting

## Scope
- 6 async waterfall fixes in API routes (CRITICAL)
- 1 async parallel fix in server page (CRITICAL)  
- 2 bundle size fixes (HIGH/MEDIUM)
- 2 server parallel fetching fixes (HIGH)
- 15 conditional rendering fixes across components (MEDIUM)
- 5 combined iteration fixes (LOW)

title: Fix async waterfalls in API routes: start auth promises early, await after validation
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/create/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Apply the `async-api-routes` pattern: start expensive auth promises early, await them after synchronous validation.

### add-attachment/route.ts
Current: `getRepoContext()` is called on ~line 63 AFTER all form parsing and validation (lines 6-61).
Fix: Start `const ctxPromise = getRepoContext();` immediately after `const data = await req.formData();` (no await). Then `const ctx = await ctxPromise;` on the line where `getRepoContext()` is currently called. This overlaps auth network latency with CPU-bound parsing.

### add-location/route.ts
Current: `getAuthenticatedRepo()` is called on ~line 79 AFTER all form parsing and validation (lines 7-77).
Fix: Start `const repoPromise = getAuthenticatedRepo();` immediately after `const data = await req.formData();`. Then `const personalRepository = await repoPromise;` where the current await is.

### create/route.ts
Current: `getRepoContext()` is called on ~line 44 AFTER field parsing and validation (lines 8-42).
Fix: Start `const ctxPromise = getRepoContext();` immediately after `const formData = await req.formData();`. Then `const ctx = await ctxPromise;` where the current await is.

## Don't
- Change any error handling behavior
- Change any validation logic or order
- Move the `req.formData()` call ŌĆö it must remain first since everything depends on it
- Add any new imports
acceptance_criteria:
1. In each of the 3 files, the auth function (getRepoContext or getAuthenticatedRepo) is started as a promise (no await) immediately after req.formData()
2. The promise is awaited later, after all synchronous validation
3. Build passes: `pnpm build` exits 0
4. Lint passes: `pnpm lint` exits 0
5. No behavioral changes ŌĆö same error responses, same status codes
close_reason: e036e44 fix: start auth promises early to overlap with form validation

title: Fix js-combine-iterations: combine map+filter into single loops in form components
description:
## Files
- components/contributions-form.tsx (modify)
- components/evaluation-form.tsx (modify)
- components/measurement-form.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace chained `.map()` + `.filter()` patterns with single-pass loops to avoid iterating arrays twice.

### contributions-form.tsx (lines ~66-69):
Current:
```ts
const mappedContributors = [
  ...contributors.map(({ did }) => did),
  ...manualContributors.filter((uri) => uri.trim() !== ""),
];
```
Fix:
```ts
const mappedContributors: string[] = [];
for (const c of contributors) mappedContributors.push(c.did);
for (const uri of manualContributors) {
  if (uri.trim() !== "") mappedContributors.push(uri);
}
```

### evaluation-form.tsx (lines ~173-176):
Current:
```ts
const allEvaluatorDids = [
  ...evaluators.map((e) => e.did),
  ...manualDids.filter((did) => did.trim() !== ""),
];
```
Fix: Same single-loop pattern.

### measurement-form.tsx (lines ~273-276):
Current:
```ts
const allMeasurerDids = [
  ...measurers.map((m) => m.did),
  ...manualDids.filter((did) => did.trim() !== ""),
];
```
Fix: Same single-loop pattern.

### measurement-form.tsx `buildLocationParams` (lines ~216-250):
Current:
```ts
return locationEntries
  .map((entry) => { ... })
  .filter((loc): loc is MeasurementLocationParam => { ... });
```
Fix: Use a single `for...of` loop that transforms and filters in one pass, pushing valid results into a result array.

### hypercerts-base-form.tsx (lines ~169-172):
Current:
```ts
const mappedContributors = [
  ...contributors.map(({ did }) => did),
  ...manualContributors.filter((uri) => uri.trim() !== ""),
];
```
Fix: Same single-loop pattern.

## Don't
- Change the resulting array contents or order
- Change any variable names used downstream
- Change any types
- Introduce any new dependencies
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 5 chained map+filter patterns are replaced with single-pass for...of loops
2. The resulting arrays contain the same elements in the same order
3. Build passes: `pnpm build` exits 0

title: Fix async waterfalls in profile update API routes: parallelize auth and formData
description:
## Files
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Apply the `async-parallel` pattern: `getAuthenticatedRepo()` and `req.formData()` are independent async operations currently awaited sequentially.

### bsky/update/route.ts
Current (lines 8, 13):
```ts
const repo = await getAuthenticatedRepo();  // line 8
if (!repo) { return 401; }
const formData = await req.formData();      // line 13
```
Fix: Use start-early-await-late pattern to avoid parsing body for unauthenticated requests:
```ts
const repoPromise = getAuthenticatedRepo();
const formData = await req.formData();
const repo = await repoPromise;
if (!repo) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: "Not authenticated" }, { status: 401 });
}
```

### update/route.ts
Current (lines 8, 13): Same sequential pattern.
Fix: Same start-early-await-late pattern as above.

## Don't
- Use Promise.all (we want to keep the early-exit guard for unauthenticated requests ŌĆö avoid parsing body unnecessarily if auth fails fast)
- Change any error messages or status codes
- Change any validation logic after formData parsing
- Modify the rest of the function after the auth check
acceptance_criteria:
1. In both files, getAuthenticatedRepo() is started as a promise (no await) BEFORE req.formData()
2. req.formData() is awaited first, then the repo promise is awaited
3. The !repo guard still returns 401 with the same error message
4. Build passes: `pnpm build` exits 0
5. Lint passes: `pnpm lint` exits 0
close_reason: d26d1ef parallelize auth and formData in profile update routes

title: Fix bundle-barrel-imports: use import type for lexicon namespace import
description:
## Files
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Fix the `bundle-barrel-imports` violation. The file uses `import * as Hypercert` which defeats tree-shaking and pulls runtime validators + multiformats/cid into the client bundle, even though only the TypeScript type `Hypercert.Record` is used.

### Current (line 11):
```ts
import * as Hypercert from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity";
```

### Fix:
```ts
import type { Record as HypercertRecord } from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity";
```

Then find all usages of `Hypercert.Record` in the file and replace with `HypercertRecord`. There should be approximately 1 usage around line 39 in the component props/type definition.

## Don't
- Change any runtime behavior
- Add any new runtime imports
- Modify any other imports in the file
- Change the component logic
acceptance_criteria:
1. The `import * as Hypercert` is replaced with `import type { Record as HypercertRecord }`
2. All usages of `Hypercert.Record` are replaced with `HypercertRecord`
3. No runtime import from the lexicon module exists (only `import type`)
4. Build passes: `pnpm build` exits 0
5. Lint passes: `pnpm lint` exits 0
close_reason: 6bb1a72 fix: use import type for lexicon namespace import (already applied as part of hypercerts-dqf.10)

title: Fix rendering-conditional-render: replace && with ternary in view components
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-evaluation-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-evidence-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-completion-step.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace `condition && (<JSX>)` patterns with `condition ? (<JSX>) : null` in all conditional renders where the condition could be a string or number (not already a boolean).

The `&&` operator can accidentally render `0` or `""` to the DOM when the left operand is falsy but not `false`/`null`/`undefined`.

### Rules:
1. If the condition is ALREADY a boolean expression (e.g., `isLoading && ...`, `step === 2 && ...`, `arr.length > 0 && ...`), convert to ternary for consistency but these are lower priority.
2. If the condition is a STRING variable (e.g., `description && ...`, `location && ...`, `imageUri && ...`), these MUST be converted because `""` would render to DOM.
3. If the condition is an OBJECT/ARRAY check (e.g., `evaluation.score != null && ...`), convert for consistency.

### Specific locations:

**hypercert-detail-view.tsx**: lines ~29, ~47, ~139
- `{imageUri && (` ŌåÆ `{imageUri ? (` ... `) : null}`
- `{record.shortDescription && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{record.description && (` ŌåÆ ternary

**hypercert-evaluation-view.tsx**: lines ~57, ~101, ~126, ~151
- `{evaluation.score != null && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{evaluation.content && evaluation.content.length > 0 && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{evaluation.measurements && evaluation.measurements.length > 0 && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{evaluation.location && (` ŌåÆ ternary

**hypercert-evidence-view.tsx**: lines ~76, ~83
- `{evidence.description && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{evidence.content && evidence.content.length > 0 && (` ŌåÆ ternary

**hypercert-measurement-view.tsx**: lines ~59, ~104
- `{measurement.measurementMethodURI && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{measurement.evidenceURI && measurement.evidenceURI.length > 0 && (` ŌåÆ ternary

**hypercert-completion-step.tsx**: lines ~62, ~76, ~91, ~103, ~112
- `{hypercertInfo?.hypercertCid && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{!hypercertInfo?.hypercertCid && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{onBack && (` ŌåÆ ternary
- `{onCreateAnother && (` ŌåÆ ternary

## Don't
- Change any component logic or behavior
- Change any CSS classes or styling
- Add or remove any JSX elements
- Change any prop types or interfaces
acceptance_criteria:
1. All `condition && (<JSX>)` patterns in the 5 listed files are replaced with `condition ? (<JSX>) : null`
2. No `&&` used for conditional rendering remains in these files (search for pattern: JSX element preceded by &&)
3. Build passes: `pnpm build` exits 0
4. Lint passes: `pnpm lint` exits 0
5. No visual or behavioral changes
close_reason: 720a96a fix: replace && with ternary in view components
