title: Fix: return_to open redirect bypass ŌĆö //evil.com passes startsWith('/') check (from security review)
Fix: .env.example missing OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET documentation ŌĆö required for ePDS flow
description:
Review of .env.example found that OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is documented as commented-out (line 72) with no indication of when it is required vs optional.

Evidence:
- .env.example line 72: '# OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET=your-32-plus-char-secret-here' (commented out)
- lib/config.ts line 234: 'oauthSessionSecret: process.env.OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET' ŌĆö it is read but the value is only used when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set
- The README optional variables table (line 77-82) does NOT list OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET at all ŌĆö it is completely absent from the README env var documentation
- The .env.example comment says 'Server-only secret used to HMAC-sign the transient OAuth session cookie' but the ePDS flow now uses Redis (not cookies) for state storage

Issues:
1. OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is missing from README.md environment variables table entirely
2. The .env.example comment says it signs a 'session cookie' but the ePDS state is now stored in Redis (hypercerts-0lg epic moved it to Redis) ŌĆö the comment is stale
3. It is unclear whether this variable is still used at all after the Redis migration

Suggested fix:
1. Audit whether OAUTH_SESSION_SECRET is still used after the ePDS cookieŌåÆRedis migration (hypercerts-0lg)
2. If still used: add it to README.md optional variables table with accurate description
3. If no longer used: remove it from .env.example and lib/config.ts
4. Update the .env.example comment to accurately describe current usage

title: Fix: unnecessary 'as any' cast on contributors in hypercert-detail-view.tsx ŌĆö field exists on lexicon type (from hypercerts-73o)
description: Review of hypercerts-73o found: components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx line 60 casts record as any to access .contributors with a comment saying the field is not in HypercertClaim type yet. But OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Main (the lexicon type, activity.d.ts line 32) DOES have contributors?: Contributor[]. The record prop is typed as HypercertClaim.Record which aliases to Main (activity.d.ts line 42). The as any cast and the eslint-disable comment are both unnecessary and suppress type checking on a now-properly-typed field. Fix: remove the cast and access record.contributors directly. Evidence: components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx:59-63 vs node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity.d.ts:32,42.

title: Epic: Improve DX lint and formatting toolchain
description:
## Why
The scaffold has a solid ESLint + Prettier baseline (eslint-config-next bundles react-hooks + jsx-a11y), but is missing lint coverage for TanStack Query patterns (16 files use it), React Compiler compatibility (React 19 + Next 16), and Tailwind class sorting. Adding these catches real bugs earlier and reduces review friction.

## What success looks like
- `@tanstack/eslint-plugin-query` catches bad query key patterns and missing options across 16+ query files
- `eslint-plugin-react-compiler` warns on patterns that break auto-memoization (e.g. the profileMap issue in hypercerts-mrb.15)
- `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` auto-sorts Tailwind classes on format
- Existing 3 lint warnings are fixed and `no-unused-vars` is promoted to error so warnings don't accumulate
- `pnpm lint` and `pnpm build` pass cleanly after all changes

## Key constraints
- Do NOT add eslint-plugin-import (TypeScript covers this already)
- Do NOT add eslint-plugin-tailwindcss (v4 compat uncertain; use Prettier plugin instead)
- React compiler plugin runs in WARN mode only (not error) ŌĆö it's forward-looking, not blocking
- All changes must be compatible with ESLint 9 flat config format
- Preserve existing eslint-config-prettier as the last config (must stay last to disable conflicting rules)
close_reason: All 4 tasks completed: TanStack Query ESLint plugin, React Compiler ESLint plugin (warn), Prettier Tailwind class sorting, lint warnings fixed + no-unused-vars promoted to error

title: Install and configure @tanstack/eslint-plugin-query
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- eslint.config.mjs (modify)

## What to do
1. Run `pnpm add -D @tanstack/eslint-plugin-query`
2. In `eslint.config.mjs`, import the plugin's flat config and add it to the config array:
   ```js
   import pluginQuery from "@tanstack/eslint-plugin-query";
   // ...
   const eslintConfig = defineConfig([
     ...nextVitals,
     ...nextTs,
     ...pluginQuery.configs["flat/recommended"],
     prettierConfig, // MUST stay last
     globalIgnores([...]),
   ]);
   ```
3. The plugin's `flat/recommended` config enables these rules at warn/error:
   - `@tanstack/query/exhaustive-deps` (error)
   - `@tanstack/query/no-rest-destructuring` (warn)
   - `@tanstack/query/stable-query-client` (error)
4. Run `pnpm lint` ŌĆö if any new errors appear in the 16 query files, fix them in this same task (they are likely missing queryKey deps or unstable query client patterns)

## Don't
- Add any other ESLint plugins in this task
- Change the order of prettierConfig (must remain last before globalIgnores)
- Suppress errors with eslint-disable comments ŌĆö fix the underlying issue
acceptance_criteria:
1. `pnpm ls @tanstack/eslint-plugin-query` shows the package installed as a devDependency
2. `eslint.config.mjs` includes the plugin's flat/recommended config
3. prettierConfig is still the last config entry before globalIgnores
4. `pnpm lint` passes with zero errors (warnings from other plugins are acceptable)
5. `pnpm build` passes
close_reason: 97eb29b install and configure @tanstack/eslint-plugin-query

title: Install and configure eslint-plugin-react-compiler in warn mode
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- eslint.config.mjs (modify)

## What to do
1. Run `pnpm add -D eslint-plugin-react-compiler`
2. In `eslint.config.mjs`, import and add the plugin. The react-compiler plugin uses ESLint 9 flat config format:
   ```js
   import reactCompiler from "eslint-plugin-react-compiler";
   // ...
   const eslintConfig = defineConfig([
     ...nextVitals,
     ...nextTs,
     ...pluginQuery.configs["flat/recommended"],  // from ecf.1
     reactCompiler.configs.recommended,
     prettierConfig, // MUST stay last
     globalIgnores([...]),
   ]);
   ```
3. The recommended config sets `react-compiler/react-compiler` to `"error"`. Override it to `"warn"` so it's informational, not blocking:
   ```js
   {
     rules: {
       "react-compiler/react-compiler": "warn",
     },
   },
   ```
   Place this override object AFTER the recommended config but BEFORE prettierConfig.
4. Run `pnpm lint` ŌĆö note any new warnings (expected, not errors). Do NOT fix them in this task ŌĆö they are informational.

## Don't
- Set the rule to "error" ŌĆö it must be "warn" only
- Fix react-compiler warnings in this task (they are informational for now)
- Change the order of prettierConfig (must remain last before globalIgnores)
- Install babel-plugin-react-compiler (that's the build-time compiler, not the linter)
acceptance_criteria:
1. `pnpm ls eslint-plugin-react-compiler` shows the package installed as a devDependency
2. `eslint.config.mjs` includes the react-compiler recommended config
3. The `react-compiler/react-compiler` rule is overridden to "warn" (not "error")
4. prettierConfig is still the last config entry before globalIgnores
5. `pnpm lint` passes with zero errors (new warnings from react-compiler are acceptable)
6. `pnpm build` passes
close_reason: 0e3ab19 Add eslint-plugin-react-compiler in warn mode

title: Install and configure prettier-plugin-tailwindcss for class sorting
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- .prettierrc.json (modify)

## What to do
1. Run `pnpm add -D prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`
2. In `.prettierrc.json`, add the plugin to the existing empty plugins array:
   ```json
   {
     "semi": true,
     "singleQuote": false,
     "tabWidth": 2,
     "trailingComma": "all",
     "printWidth": 80,
     "plugins": ["prettier-plugin-tailwindcss"],
     "endOfLine": "lf"
   }
   ```
3. The plugin auto-detects Tailwind v4 config (CSS-based). No `tailwindConfig` option needed.
4. Run `pnpm format:check` to see how many files would change. Then run `pnpm format` to apply class sorting.
5. Stage and commit the formatting changes separately from the config change so the diff is reviewable:
   - First commit: package.json + .prettierrc.json (config change)
   - Second commit: all reformatted files (class sorting applied)

## Don't
- Add any other Prettier plugins
- Change any Prettier options other than the plugins array
- Manually reorder Tailwind classes ŌĆö let the plugin do it
- Skip running `pnpm format` ŌĆö the pre-commit hook will catch unsorted classes otherwise, creating noisy diffs in unrelated PRs
acceptance_criteria:
1. `pnpm ls prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` shows the package installed as a devDependency
2. `.prettierrc.json` has `"prettier-plugin-tailwindcss"` in the plugins array
3. `pnpm format:check` exits 0 (all files formatted, including Tailwind class sorting)
4. `pnpm lint` still passes
5. `pnpm build` still passes
close_reason: 97eb29b apply prettier-plugin-tailwindcss class sorting

Current `pnpm lint` output shows:

1. **app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts:140:11** ŌĆö `'message' is assigned a value but never used`
   - Remove the unused `message` variable or use it (check context ŌĆö it's likely a destructured error field that should be logged or returned)

2. **lib/contributor-utils.ts:59:10** ŌĆö `'extractDidFromAtUri' is defined but never used`
   - Remove the unused function, OR if it's exported and used elsewhere, check imports. If it's truly dead code, delete it.

3. **providers/SignedInProvider.tsx:5:10** ŌĆö `'cookies' is defined but never used`
   - Remove the unused `cookies` import

### Part 2: Promote no-unused-vars to error
In `eslint.config.mjs`, add a rule override AFTER the nextTs config but BEFORE prettierConfig:
```js
{
  rules: {
    "@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars": "error",
  },
},
```

This ensures future unused vars are caught as errors (blocking) rather than warnings (ignored).

## Don't
- Add eslint-disable comments to suppress the warnings
- Change the logic/behavior of any function ŌĆö only remove truly unused code
- Remove `extractDidFromAtUri` if it's exported and imported elsewhere (check with grep first)
acceptance_criteria:
1. `pnpm lint` exits with 0 errors AND 0 warnings (completely clean)
2. The `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` rule is set to "error" in eslint.config.mjs
3. No eslint-disable comments were added
4. `pnpm build` passes
5. No behavioral changes to any function
close_reason: ea5783c fix: remove 3 lint warnings and promote no-unused-vars to error

title: Fix: BlobRef instance fails AppCertifiedActorProfile.validateRecord ŌĆö avatar/banner upload always returns 400 (from hypercerts-fpyy.5)
description:
Review of hypercerts-fpyy.5 found a critical runtime bug in app/api/profile/update/route.ts.

## Problem

The code uploads a blob and assigns the raw `BlobRef` instance to `record.avatar` / `record.banner` (lines 82ŌĆō86 and 147ŌĆō151), then immediately calls `assertValidRecord('certifiedProfile', record, AppCertifiedActorProfile.validateRecord)`.

`AppCertifiedActorProfile.Record.avatar` is typed as `$Typed<Uri> | $Typed<SmallImage> | { $type: string }` ŌĆö **not** `BlobRef`. The lexicon validator rejects a `BlobRef` instance (it only accepts plain objects). Confirmed with:

```
AppCertifiedActorProfile.validateRecord({ $type: 'app.certified.actor.profile', avatar: blobRef })
// ŌåÆ { success: false, error: {} }
```

This means **any request that uploads an avatar or banner to the certified profile endpoint returns HTTP 400** ŌĆö the feature is completely broken.

## Evidence

- `app/api/profile/update/route.ts` lines 81ŌĆō86 (create path):
  ```ts
  if (avatarBlob)
    record.avatar = avatarBlob as unknown as AppCertifiedActorProfile.Record["avatar"];
  ```
- `app/api/profile/update/route.ts` lines 146ŌĆō151 (update path): same pattern.
- The `as unknown as` cast silences TypeScript but the validator still rejects the value at runtime.

## Fix

Use `avatarBlob.original` (the plain serialisable object) instead of the `BlobRef` instance:

```ts
if (avatarBlob) record.avatar = avatarBlob.original as AppCertifiedActorProfile.Record["avatar"];
if (bannerBlob) record.banner = bannerBlob.original as AppCertifiedActorProfile.Record["banner"];
```

`BlobRef.original` is `{ $type: 'blob', ref: {...}, mimeType: string, size: number }` which satisfies the `{ $type: string }` branch of the union and passes validation.
close_reason: 8cb56d0 fix: use BlobRef.original for avatar/banner ŌĆö already implemented as part of hypercerts-0j8f

title: Fix: Internal error messages exposed in URL via auth_error_description (from hypercerts-jsu.1)
description: Review of hypercerts-jsu.1 found: When sdk.callback() throws, the raw error message is placed in the auth_error_description URL query param (app/api/auth/callback/route.ts line 40: e instanceof Error ? e.message : 'An unexpected error occurred'). This can expose internal error details (OAuth state, internal URLs, library internals) in the browser address bar and server logs. Evidence: git show 1f18629 -- app/api/auth/callback/route.ts shows the catch block sets auth_error_description to e.message directly. Fix: use a generic user-facing message for callback_failed errors (e.g. 'Authentication failed. Please try again.') and log the full error server-side only. The error_description from the PDS (access_denied path) is fine to pass through as it is a standard OAuth param.
acceptance_criteria:
1. In the catch block of app/api/auth/callback/route.ts, auth_error_description is set to a generic string like "Authentication failed. Please try again." ŌĆö NOT e.message
2. The full error is still logged server-side via console.error (already exists, just verify it stays)
3. The access_denied early-return path (lines 9-17) is UNCHANGED ŌĆö PDS-provided error_description is fine to pass through
4. TypeScript compiles: npx tsc --noEmit passes
5. No other files modified
close_reason: 7026ba9 fix: use generic message for callback_failed auth_error_description

title: Epic: Update README to document ePDS email login flow and fill documentation gaps
description:
## Why
The README is significantly outdated after the ePDS email login flow was implemented. The codebase now has two distinct auth flows (standard ATProto handle-based and ePDS email-based with DPoP/PKCE/OTP), but the README only documents the standard flow. Multiple sections are incomplete or missing.

## What success looks like
- README accurately documents both authentication flows (standard ATProto + ePDS email)
- All environment variables are documented (including optional ones like NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL)
- Architecture diagram reflects the dual auth pathways and Redis stores
- Project structure matches the actual codebase
- A developer can clone the repo, read the README, and set up either auth flow without asking questions

## Key constraints
- Do NOT change any application code ŌĆö this is docs-only
- Keep the existing README structure where possible, extend rather than rewrite
- Keep it concise ŌĆö link to DEVELOPMENT.md for deep dives
- The ePDS flow is optional (only enabled when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set)

title: Update Environment Configuration section with all env vars
description:
## Files
- README.md (modify)

## What to do
Replace the "Environment Configuration > Required Variables" table (lines 64-73) with a complete table that includes ALL environment variables used by the codebase. Split into "Required" and "Optional" subsections.

### Required Variables (keep existing 6, update descriptions):
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL` | App base URL. Use `http://127.0.0.1:3000` for local dev. Falls back to `VERCEL_URL` on Vercel. |
| `ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE` | Private JWK (JWKS format) for OAuth client assertion. Generate with `pnpm run generate-jwk`. |
| `REDIS_HOST` | Redis server hostname (e.g., `localhost` for Docker, or cloud Redis host) |
| `REDIS_PORT` | Redis server port (default: `6379`) |
| `REDIS_PASSWORD` | Redis password (empty string for local Docker) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL` | Personal Data Server URL (e.g., `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`) |

### Optional Variables (NEW ŌĆö add after Required):
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` | ePDS URL for email-based login. When set, enables the Email login tab in the UI. Example: `https://epds1.test.certified.app` |
| `REDIS_USERNAME` | Redis username. Defaults to `default` when `REDIS_PASSWORD` is set. |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_HANDLE_RESOLVER` | Handle resolver URL. Defaults to `https://bsky.social`. |

### Test Server URLs subsection (update existing, lines 86-91):
Add the ePDS test URL after the PDS URL:
```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL=https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app
# Optional: Enable email login via ePDS
NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL=https://epds1.test.certified.app
```

## Dont

- Do NOT add NEXT_PUBLIC_VERCEL_TARGET_ENV ŌĆö internal Vercel detail
- Do NOT change any code files

title: Add ePDS Email Authentication section to README
description:
## Files
- README.md (modify)

## What to do
The current "Authentication > How It Works" section (lines 196-209) only documents the standard ATProto handle-based flow. Restructure this section to document BOTH flows.

### Changes to make:

1. **Rename the existing "How It Works" subsection** to "### Flow 1: Handle Login (Standard ATProto)"
   - Keep the existing 6-step flow description (lines 200-209) as-is

2. **Add a new subsection after it**: "### Flow 2: Email Login (ePDS)"
   - Add a note: "> Requires `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` to be set. When configured, an Email tab appears in the login dialog."
   - Document the flow step-by-step:
     1. User enters their email address (or leaves blank for the ePDS to collect it)
     2. App sends a Pushed Authorization Request (PAR) to the ePDS with PKCE challenge and DPoP proof
     3. App stores OAuth state (code verifier + DPoP private key) in Redis
     4. User is redirected to the ePDS authorization page
     5. ePDS sends a one-time password (OTP) to the users email
     6. User enters the OTP code on the ePDS page
     7. ePDS redirects back to `/api/oauth/callback` with an authorization code
     8. App exchanges the code for tokens using DPoP, creates a session in Redis
     9. User is authenticated ŌĆö same `user-did` cookie as the standard flow

3. **Add a brief "### How the Login UI Works" subsection** after both flows:
   - When only `NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL` is set: login dialog shows only the Handle tab
   - When `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` is also set: login dialog shows a pill toggle with Handle and Email tabs
   - Both flows result in the same session format ŌĆö downstream code is agnostic to the login method

### Key technical details to mention:
- The ePDS flow uses DPoP (Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession) with EC P-256 keys
- PKCE with S256 challenge method prevents authorization code interception
- OAuth state is stored in Redis (not cookies) to avoid cross-site redirect issues
- The ePDS flow supports custom branding (logo, colors) and a custom email template for OTP codes

## Dont
- Do NOT include code examples in this section (the existing server-side auth examples below already cover usage)
- Do NOT document internal implementation details of lib/epds-helpers.ts
- Do NOT mention the old cookie-based approach (it was replaced by Redis)
- Keep each flow description to ~10 lines max ŌĆö this is a README, not a spec
acceptance_criteria:
1. README has three subsections under Authentication: "Flow 1: Handle Login", "Flow 2: Email Login (ePDS)", and "How the Login UI Works"
2. The ePDS flow is documented as a numbered list with 8-10 steps
3. The note about NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL requirement is present
4. DPoP, PKCE, and OTP are mentioned
5. It is clear that both flows produce the same session format
6. No code examples in this section (those are in the next section)
7. The old cookie-based approach is NOT mentioned
close_reason: d1396f6 docs: add ePDS email auth section to README

title: Update Architecture diagram to include ePDS flow and Redis stores
description:
## Files
- README.md (modify)

## What to do
Replace the ASCII architecture diagram (lines 126-149) with an updated version that reflects the dual auth pathways and the three Redis stores.

### New diagram should show:
1. **Client (Browser)** ŌĆö React Query, Login Dialog (Handle tab + Email tab)
2. **Next.js API Routes** ŌĆö split into three columns:
   - `/api/auth/*` (standard ATProto OAuth ŌĆö SDK-managed)
   - `/api/oauth/*` (ePDS email OAuth ŌĆö hand-rolled DPoP/PKCE)
   - `/api/certs/*` + `/api/profile/*` (business logic)
3. **Hypercerts SDK** ŌĆö used by standard flow and business logic
4. **Redis** ŌĆö three stores:
   - `oauth-state:*` (standard ATProto state, 10min TTL)
   - `epds-oauth-state:*` (ePDS state: code verifier + DPoP key, 10min TTL)
   - `session:*` (persisted sessions for both flows, no TTL)
5. **PDS** ŌĆö standard ATProto PDS
6. **ePDS** ŌĆö email-based PDS (Certified Auth)
7. **OAuth metadata endpoints** ŌĆö `/client-metadata.json`, `/jwks.json`

### Also update the "Personal Data Server (PDS)" paragraph (lines 151-153):
Add a brief explanation of ePDS:
- **PDS** stores user data (profiles, hypercerts). Standard ATProto login authenticates against the users PDS.
- **ePDS (Email PDS)** is a Certified Auth PDS that supports email-based login with OTP verification. It serves the same role as a standard PDS but uses email instead of a handle for authentication.

| `lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts` | SDK initialization with OAuth config, Redis storage, handle resolver |
| `lib/redis-state-store.ts` | Three Redis-backed stores: sessions, standard OAuth state, ePDS OAuth state |
| `lib/repo-context.ts` | Helper to get authenticated repository context in server components |
| `lib/epds-config.ts` | Derives ePDS OAuth endpoints (PAR, auth, token) from `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL` |
| `lib/epds-helpers.ts` | PKCE code verifier/challenge, DPoP key generation and proof creation |
| `components/login-dialog.tsx` | Dual-mode login UI with Handle/Email pill toggle |
| `app/client-metadata.json/route.ts` | OAuth client metadata (RFC 7591) ŌĆö serves client_id, redirect_uris, branding |

## Dont
- Do NOT list every single file ŌĆö keep it to the most important ones
- Do NOT include .beads/ directory in the tree
- Do NOT include node_modules, .next, or other build artifacts
- Keep the tree to ~25 lines max

1. Project structure tree includes app/api/oauth/ directory with comment about ePDS
2. Project structure includes lib/epds-config.ts and lib/epds-helpers.ts
3. Project structure includes lib/redis.ts and lib/redis-state-store.ts
4. Project structure includes app/client-metadata.json/ and app/jwks.json/
5. Key Files table has 8 entries (up from 3)
6. Key Files table includes epds-config.ts, epds-helpers.ts, redis-state-store.ts, and login-dialog.tsx

8. No .beads/ directory in the tree

title: Add Redis Data Model section to README
description:
## Files
- README.md (modify)

## What to do
Add a new section "### Redis Data Model" inside the existing "Architecture Overview" section (after the PDS explanation, around line 153). This explains what Redis stores and why.

### Content to add:

```markdown
### Redis Data Model

Redis stores three types of data, managed by dedicated store classes in `lib/redis-state-store.ts`:

| Key Pattern | Store Class | TTL | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `oauth-state:{state}` | `RedisStateStore` | 10 min | Standard ATProto OAuth state (managed by SDK) |
| `epds-oauth-state:{state}` | `RedisEpdsStateStore` | 10 min | ePDS OAuth state (PKCE code verifier + DPoP private key) |
| `session:{did}` | `RedisSessionStore` | None | Persisted OAuth sessions for both flows (tokens, DPoP JWK) |

- OAuth state entries auto-expire after 10 minutes (login must complete within this window)
- ePDS state uses atomic get-and-delete to prevent replay attacks
- Sessions persist until the user logs out or the session is revoked
```

## Dont
- Do NOT add this as a top-level section ŌĆö nest it under Architecture Overview
- Do NOT document Redis connection configuration here (thats in Environment Configuration)
- Do NOT mention implementation details like getDel() ŌĆö keep it user-facing
- Keep it to the table + 3 bullet points max
acceptance_criteria:
1. A "Redis Data Model" subsection exists under Architecture Overview
2. Table has 3 rows: oauth-state, epds-oauth-state, session
3. TTLs are documented (10 min for state, None for sessions)
4. The three bullet points about expiry, replay prevention, and persistence are present
5. Section is concise (table + 3 bullets, no more)

title: Epic: Add lexicon validation before all AT Protocol record writes
description:
## Why
Currently 22 createRecord/putRecord calls across 6 files write AT Protocol records with zero lexicon validation. The @hypercerts-org/lexicon package provides validateRecord functions for every collection type, and lib/utils.ts has 3 dead validation wrappers that are never called. Invalid records can be written to repos, causing downstream issues.

## What success looks like
Every createRecord and putRecord call validates the record against its lexicon schema before writing. Invalid records return a 400 error (in API routes) or throw a descriptive error (in server actions/lib functions). The dead validation wrappers in lib/utils.ts are either used or removed.

## Key constraints
- Use validateRecord from @hypercerts-org/lexicon (already a dependency)
- For app.bsky.actor.profile, use AppBskyActorProfile.validateRecord from @atproto/api
- For app.certified.actor.profile, use AppCertifiedActorProfile.validateRecord from @hypercerts-org/lexicon
- Don't change record construction logic ŌĆö only add validation gates
- Validation errors should be descriptive (include which fields failed)
- npm run build must pass
close_reason: d61eed3 All 6 tasks + 4 review fixes complete ŌĆö lexicon validation on all 22 write sites

title: Create assertValidRecord helper and clean up dead validation wrappers in lib/utils.ts
description:
## Files
- lib/record-validation.ts (create)
- lib/utils.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Create lib/record-validation.ts
Create a generic validation helper that can be used across all write sites. The function should:

\`\`\`ts
import type { ValidationResult } from "@atproto/lexicon";

/**
 * Validates a record against its lexicon schema and throws if invalid.
 * @param label - Human-readable label for error messages (e.g. "activity", "contribution")
 * @param record - The record to validate
 * @param validateFn - The validateRecord function from the lexicon namespace
 * @throws Error with descriptive message if validation fails
 */
export function assertValidRecord<T>(
  label: string,
  record: unknown,
  validateFn: (v: unknown) => ValidationResult<T>,
): asserts record is T {
  const result = validateFn(record);
  if (!result.success) {
    throw new Error(\`Invalid \${label} record: \${result.error.message}\`);
  }
}
\`\`\`

Check the actual shape of \`ValidationResult\` from \`@atproto/lexicon\` to make sure the error access pattern is correct. Look at \`node_modules/@atproto/lexicon/dist/types.d.ts\` or similar. The validation result is a discriminated union: \`{ success: true; value: T } | { success: false; error: ValidationError }\`. \`ValidationError\` extends \`Error\` so \`.message\` is available.

### 2. Clean up lib/utils.ts
Remove the 3 dead validation wrappers (lines 90-121): \`validateHypercert\`, \`validateContribution\`, \`validateEvaluation\`. Also remove their now-unused imports:
- \`OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as Hypercert\` (line 4) ŌĆö check if used elsewhere in the file first
- \`OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as Contribution\` (line 5)
- \`OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation as Evaluation\` (line 6)

Only remove imports that become unused after removing the dead functions.

## Don't
- Don't add validation calls to any write sites yet (that's separate tasks)
- Don't change any other functions in lib/utils.ts
- Don't modify the parseAtUri or other utility functions
acceptance_criteria:
1. lib/record-validation.ts exists with an assertValidRecord function that takes (label, record, validateFn) and throws on invalid records
2. The function signature uses asserts return type for TypeScript narrowing
3. The 3 dead validation wrappers (validateHypercert, validateContribution, validateEvaluation) are removed from lib/utils.ts
4. Any imports that became unused after removing the dead wrappers are also removed
5. npm run build succeeds with no type errors
close_reason: eb2d634 add assertValidRecord helper and remove dead validation wrappers

title: Add lexicon validation to lib/queries.ts write functions
description:

## What to do
Add assertValidRecord calls before every createRecord/putRecord in lib/queries.ts. This file has 5 write functions that accept pre-built typed records. Even though they're typed, the records could have been constructed incorrectly upstream.

### Functions to add validation to:

1. **createHypercert** (line 58-68) ŌĆö collection: org.hypercerts.claim.activity
   \`\`\`ts
   import { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity } from "@hypercerts-org/lexicon";
   // Before createRecord:
   assertValidRecord("activity", record, OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`

2. **updateHypercert** (line 70-82) ŌĆö collection: org.hypercerts.claim.activity
   Same validation as createHypercert, before putRecord.

3. **createContribution** (line 84-94) ŌĆö collection: org.hypercerts.claim.contribution
   \`\`\`ts
   // Note: the type is OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails (already imported as HypercertContribution)
   assertValidRecord("contributionDetails", record, HypercertContribution.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`
   Wait ŌĆö check the actual collection name. The function uses \`Collections.contribution\`. Look at the Collections object (around line 10-20) to see what collection string it maps to. The validateRecord function must match the collection's lexicon.

4. **createEvaluation** (line 96-106) ŌĆö collection: org.hypercerts.claim.evaluation
   \`\`\`ts
   assertValidRecord("evaluation", record, HypercertEvaluation.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`

5. **createEvidence** (line 108-118) ŌĆö collection: org.hypercerts.claim.evidence
   The type is \`HypercertEvidence.Record\` which is aliased from \`OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment\`. Check if \`OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment\` has a \`validateRecord\`. If so:
   \`\`\`ts
   assertValidRecord("evidence", record, HypercertEvidence.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`

6. **createLocation** (line 120-130) ŌĆö collection: app.certified.location
   \`\`\`ts
   assertValidRecord("location", record, HypercertLocation.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`

### Imports to add:
\`\`\`ts
import { assertValidRecord } from "@/lib/record-validation";
\`\`\`
The lexicon namespace imports are already present (HypercertClaim, HypercertContribution, etc.) ŌĆö just use their .validateRecord.

For HypercertClaim (OrgHypercertsClaimActivity), it's currently imported as a type-only import. You'll need to change it to a value import since validateRecord is a runtime function. Check the current import style and adjust.

## Don't
- Don't change function signatures or return types
- Don't change how records are constructed (they come from callers)
- Don't add try/catch ŌĆö let the error propagate to the caller
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 6 write functions (createHypercert, updateHypercert, createContribution, createEvaluation, createEvidence, createLocation) call assertValidRecord before their createRecord/putRecord call
2. Each validation uses the correct lexicon namespace's validateRecord function matching the collection
3. assertValidRecord is imported from @/lib/record-validation
4. Lexicon imports are value imports (not type-only) since validateRecord is used at runtime
5. npm run build succeeds with no type errors

title: Add lexicon validation to app/api/certs/route.ts write operations
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Add assertValidRecord calls before every createRecord/putRecord in the certs API route. This file has both POST (create) and PUT (update) handlers.

### POST handler writes:

1. **Line ~109** ŌĆö createRecord for \`org.hypercerts.claim.rights\`
   Record typed as \`OrgHypercertsClaimRights.Record\`. Validate before write:
   \`\`\`ts
   assertValidRecord("rights", rightsRecord, OrgHypercertsClaimRights.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`
   OrgHypercertsClaimRights is already imported. Check if it's a type-only import and change to value import if needed.

2. **Line ~133** ŌĆö createRecord for \`org.hypercerts.claim.activity\`
   Record typed as \`OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record\`. Validate before write:
   \`\`\`ts
   assertValidRecord("activity", certRecord, OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`
   OrgHypercertsClaimActivity is already imported. Check if it's a type-only import.

### PUT handler writes:

3. **Line ~251** ŌĆö putRecord for \`org.hypercerts.claim.activity\`
   Fetches existing record, merges with form data updates, writes back. Validate the merged record before putRecord:
   \`\`\`ts
   assertValidRecord("activity", updatedRecord, OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.validateRecord);
   \`\`\`

### Error handling
This is an API route. Wrap the assertValidRecord call in the existing try/catch, or add a specific catch that returns a 400 response with the validation error message. The file already has try/catch blocks ŌĆö the thrown error from assertValidRecord will be caught and returned as a 500. Ideally, catch validation errors specifically and return 400:

\`\`\`ts
// Option: check before write and return 400
try {
  assertValidRecord("activity", record, OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.validateRecord);
} catch (e) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : "Validation failed" }, { status: 400 });
}
\`\`\`

Or simply let the existing try/catch handle it ŌĆö the error message will still be descriptive. Either approach is acceptable, but returning 400 for validation errors is preferred over 500.

### Imports to add:
\`\`\`ts
import { assertValidRecord } from "@/lib/record-validation";
\`\`\`
Ensure lexicon imports are value imports (not type-only).

## Don't
- Don't change record construction logic
- Don't change form data parsing
- Don't change the response shape
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 3 write operations in app/api/certs/route.ts have assertValidRecord called before their createRecord/putRecord

3. Each validation uses the correct lexicon namespace's validateRecord function

title: Fix: Navbar nav links have no aria-current='page' for active state (navbar.tsx)
description:
## File
components/navbar.tsx lines 72-93

## Severity
MEDIUM ŌĆö Accessibility

## Problem
The navbar uses conditional CSS classes to visually indicate the active route, but does not set aria-current='page' on the active link. Screen readers cannot identify which navigation link corresponds to the current page.

## Evidence
```tsx
<Link
  href="/hypercerts"
  className={pathname === '/hypercerts' ? 'bg-create-accent/10 text-create-accent' : 'text-muted-foreground'}
  // Missing: aria-current
>
  Hypercerts
</Link>
```

## Fix
```tsx
<Link
  href="/hypercerts"
  aria-current={pathname === '/hypercerts' || (pathname?.startsWith('/hypercerts') && pathname !== '/hypercerts/create') ? 'page' : undefined}
  className={...}
>
  Hypercerts
</Link>
```

Apply the same pattern to the 'Create' link.

title: Epic: Clean up loopback OAuth fix ŌĆö remove debug code, conditional application_type, dead imports
description:
## Context
Three changes were made to fix ePDS OAuth for loopback mode:
1. Added application_type: native to PAR request body
2. Created separate epdsClientId using the ePDS redirect URI
3. Fixed getEpdsClientId() to return config.epdsClientId

The fix works but leaves loose ends: a debug console.log, application_type hardcoded to native (breaks production), dead imports/comments from deleted loopback scope code, and a stale fallback scope in the callback route.

## Scope
- Remove debug log
- Make application_type conditional on loopback vs production
- Clean up dead TRANSITION_SCOPES import and stale doc comment
- Fix callback fallback scope to use OAUTH_SCOPE instead of hardcoded transition:generic
- All changes scoped to 3 files: app/api/oauth/login/route.ts, lib/config.ts, app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts

## Success Criteria
- No debug console.log in login route
- PAR request sends application_type: native for loopback, web for production
- No unused imports in config.ts
- Doc comments accurate (no references to deleted loopback scope)

title: Clean up loopback OAuth: remove debug log, conditional application_type, dead imports, fix fallback scope
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (modify)
- lib/config.ts (modify)
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Remove debug console.log (app/api/oauth/login/route.ts)
Delete line 33: `console.log(redirectUri)` ŌĆö this is a leftover debug log.

### 2. Make application_type conditional on loopback (app/api/oauth/login/route.ts)
Line 41 currently hardcodes `application_type: "native"` in the PAR body. This must be conditional:
- Import `config` (already imported on line 15)
- Change the PAR body construction to use `config.isLoopback`:

```ts
const parBody = new URLSearchParams({
  client_id: clientId,
  redirect_uri: redirectUri,
  response_type: "code",
  scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
  application_type: config.isLoopback ? "native" : "web",
  state,
  code_challenge: codeChallenge,
  code_challenge_method: "S256",
});
```

### 3. Remove unused TRANSITION_SCOPES import (lib/config.ts)
Line 14 imports `TRANSITION_SCOPES` from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core`. This is no longer used anywhere (LOOPBACK_SCOPE was deleted). Remove it from the import statement. The import should become:
```ts
import {
  ATPROTO_SCOPE,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS,
} from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";
```

### 4. Update stale doc comment (lib/config.ts)
Lines 193-197 (the OAUTH_SCOPE export comment) still reference loopback scope behavior that no longer exists:
```ts
/**
 * OAuth scope configuration ŌĆö environment-aware:
 * - Loopback (local dev): "atproto transition:generic" (ATProto requirement for loopback clients)
 * - Production: granular scopes (repo, rpc, blob) for precise permission requests
 */
export const OAUTH_SCOPE = GRANULAR_SCOPE;
```
Replace with:
```ts
/**
 * OAuth scope ŌĆö granular scopes (repo, rpc, blob) for all environments.
 */
export const OAUTH_SCOPE = GRANULAR_SCOPE;
```

### 5. Fix callback fallback scope (app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts)
Line 126 has a hardcoded fallback:
```ts
scope: (tokenData.scope ?? "atproto transition:generic") as NodeSavedSession["tokenSet"]["scope"],
```
Change to use the OAUTH_SCOPE constant:
```ts
scope: (tokenData.scope ?? OAUTH_SCOPE) as NodeSavedSession["tokenSet"]["scope"],
```
Add `OAUTH_SCOPE` to the import from `@/lib/config` on line 9:
```ts
import { config, OAUTH_SCOPE } from "@/lib/config";
```

## Don't
- Change the epdsClientId or clientId logic (that is correct)
- Change the buildClientMetadata() function
- Change any other OAuth flow logic
- Add new dependencies or files
acceptance_criteria:
1. No console.log(redirectUri) in app/api/oauth/login/route.ts
2. PAR body sends application_type: "native" when config.isLoopback is true, "web" when false
3. TRANSITION_SCOPES is not imported in lib/config.ts
4. The OAUTH_SCOPE doc comment does not reference loopback or transition:generic
5. Callback route uses OAUTH_SCOPE constant as fallback scope, not hardcoded "atproto transition:generic"
6. OAUTH_SCOPE is imported in app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts
7. npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
8. The existing unstaged changes in lib/config.ts and lib/epds-config.ts (epdsClientId addition, getEpdsClientId fix) are preserved ŌĆö do NOT revert those
close_reason: f880d7b clean up loopback OAuth: remove debug log, conditional application_type, dead imports, fix fallback scope

title: Fix: getBlobURL has dead-code condition blobRef.$type === 'string' (from hypercerts-73o.3)
description: Review of hypercerts-73o.3 found: lib/utils.ts getBlobURL() (line 25) has the condition 'blobRef.$type === "string"' which checks if the $type field of the object equals the literal string 'string'. This is almost certainly dead code ŌĆö no ATProto record type uses $type: 'string'. If it was intended to handle a string-typed blobRef, the first guard 'typeof blobRef === "string"' already covers that. This condition will never be true and returns blobRef.$type (the string 'string') as a URL, which would be a broken URL. Fix: remove this dead-code branch entirely.

title: Fix: loopback client metadata sets application_type='web' but comment says 'native'
description:
## Problem
In `lib/config.ts` line 263, the comment says:
```
// Loopback mode: no branding, application_type is "native"
```
But line 281 actually sets:
```ts
application_type: "web",
```

RFC 8252 (OAuth for Native Apps) specifies that loopback redirect clients should use `application_type: "native"`. The ATProto spec for loopback clients also expects `native`. Setting `web` for a loopback client may cause PDS servers to reject the OAuth flow or apply incorrect security policies.

## Fix
Change line 281 to:
```ts
application_type: "native",
```
And verify the PDS accepts this value. The comment is correct ŌĆö the code is wrong.

## Evidence
`lib/config.ts` line 263 (comment) vs line 281 (actual value).
notes: NOTE from doc review 2026-02-27: The comment was already fixed in commit 0369b4b (2026-02-26) ŌĆö current lib/config.ts line 263 reads '// Loopback mode: minimal metadata, application_type is "web"' which matches the code. However, the application_type: 'web' value itself may still be wrong per RFC 8252 (loopback clients should use 'native'). The code issue (wrong value) may still be valid even though the comment-code mismatch is resolved. See hypercerts-p2a for the remaining comment accuracy issue.

title: Fix: add-location route missing ownership check before putRecord (from hypercerts-c8i.1)
description: Review of hypercerts-c8i.1 found: app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (lines 100-119) fetches the hypercert from hypercertParsed.did but then calls putRecord with repo: personalRepository.assertDid without first checking that hypercertParsed.did === personalRepository.assertDid. If a user submits a hypercertUri belonging to another user, the ATProto server will reject the write with an opaque error. The original SDK (personalRepository.hypercerts.attachLocation) handled this internally. Fix: add an explicit check after parsing the URI: if (hypercertParsed.did !== personalRepository.assertDid) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 });

title: Fix open redirect via //evil.com bypass in OAuth callback
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Line 45 has an open redirect vulnerability:
```ts
const redirectPath = returnTo.startsWith("/") ? returnTo : "/";
```

The problem: `//evil.com` starts with `/` so it passes the check. When used in `new URL(redirectPath, config.baseUrl)` on line 47, the browser interprets `//evil.com` as a protocol-relative URL and redirects to `https://evil.com`.

Replace the `startsWith("/")` check with proper origin validation:
```ts
const resolved = new URL(returnTo, config.baseUrl);
const base = new URL(config.baseUrl);
const redirectPath = resolved.origin === base.origin
  ? resolved.pathname + resolved.search
  : "/";
```

This ensures:
- Normal relative paths like `/hypercerts/create` still work
- Paths with query strings like `/profile?tab=settings` still work
- `//evil.com` gets rejected because `resolved.origin` would be `https://evil.com` which !== base origin
- `https://evil.com` gets rejected for the same reason
- Empty/null returnTo falls back to `/` (the `|| "/"` on line 42 already handles this)

## Don't
- Change any other logic in the callback route
- Modify the error handling paths (lines 9-27)
- Touch the cookie setting logic (lines 34-39)
- Add any new dependencies
acceptance_criteria:
1. returnTo='/' redirects to base origin + '/'
2. returnTo='/hypercerts/create' redirects to base origin + '/hypercerts/create'
3. returnTo='/profile?tab=settings' redirects to base origin + '/profile?tab=settings'
4. returnTo='//evil.com' redirects to base origin + '/' (blocked)
5. returnTo='//evil.com/path' redirects to base origin + '/' (blocked)
6. returnTo='https://evil.com' redirects to base origin + '/' (blocked)
7. returnTo='' redirects to base origin + '/' (fallback from line 42)
8. returnTo=null (missing param) redirects to base origin + '/' (fallback from line 42)
9. No other lines in the file are changed beyond lines 44-45
10. File passes eslint and prettier
close_reason: d8e5f9f fix: prevent open redirect via protocol-relative URL in OAuth callback

title: Fix: dead `color: #ffffff` rule on ::after pseudo-element in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
description:
Review of hypercerts-nyk.15 found a dead CSS rule at line 167 of lib/atproto-branding.ts.

The rule:
```css
.grid h1.text-primary::after {
  ...
  color: #ffffff !important;  /* line 167 ŌĆö DEAD RULE */
}
```

The `::after` pseudo-element has `content: ""` (empty string) and renders only a background-image (the certified-signin SVG). The `color` property has no effect on a pseudo-element with no text content. This is a leftover artifact from a previous iteration where the pseudo-element may have had text content.

Evidence: lib/atproto-branding.ts lines 156-168. The pseudo-element uses background-image, not text, so color is irrelevant.

Fix: Remove the `color: #ffffff !important` line from `.grid h1.text-primary::after`.

title: Fix: atproto-branding.ts generateBrandingCss uses fragile CSS class selectors that will break if PDS updates its markup
description:
## File
lib/atproto-branding.ts lines 109, 143, 167, 199

## Severity
MEDIUM ŌĆö Fragility / maintainability

## Problem
The branding CSS targets PDS OAuth page elements using highly specific structural selectors:
- .grid h1.text-primary (line 109)
- main.flex.flex-col.items-center:not(:has([role='alert'])) > h1.text-primary::before (line 143)
- button[type='submit'].bg-primary (line 167)
- main.flex.flex-col.items-center > button:nth-of-type(2) (line 167)
- .grid.grow.content-center (line 199)

These selectors depend on the PDS's internal Tailwind class names and DOM structure. Any PDS update that changes class names, layout, or element order will silently break the branding without any error. The :nth-of-type(2) selector is especially fragile.

## Fix
This is a known limitation of CSS injection into third-party pages. Document the fragility explicitly with a comment block at the top of the function:
```ts
/**
 * WARNING: These selectors target internal PDS OAuth page markup.
 * They will break if the PDS updates its Tailwind classes or DOM structure.
 * When branding breaks, check the PDS OAuth page source and update selectors.
 * Last verified against: [PDS version/date]
 */
```

Also consider adding a CHANGELOG entry or test that verifies the selectors against a known PDS HTML snapshot.

title: Fix: react/cache used in API Route handlers ŌĆö wrong caching primitive
description:
## Problem
`getRepoContext` (lib/repo-context.ts line 41) and `getAuthenticatedRepo` / `getSession` (lib/atproto-session.ts lines 7, 27) are wrapped with React's `cache()` function. React `cache()` is designed for React Server Components and deduplicates calls within a single render pass. It is NOT designed for Next.js API Route handlers (which are plain Node.js request handlers, not RSC renders).

In API Routes, `cache()` provides no deduplication guarantee and may silently return stale data across requests in some Next.js versions. The correct primitive for per-request deduplication in API Routes is either:
- No caching (just call the function directly ŌĆö each request is isolated)
- `unstable_cache` from `next/cache` (for cross-request caching with explicit keys)

The current code works in Server Actions (which do run in an RSC-like context) but is semantically wrong in the API Route handlers that call `getAuthenticatedRepo()` directly.

## Affected files
- `lib/repo-context.ts` line 41: `export const getRepoContext = cache(...)`
- `lib/atproto-session.ts` lines 7, 27: `getAuthenticatedRepo` and `getSession` wrapped with `cache()`
- `app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts`, `app/api/certs/create/route.ts` call `getRepoContext()`
- `app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts`, `app/api/profile/update/route.ts`, `app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts` call `getAuthenticatedRepo()`

## Fix
Remove `cache()` wrapping from `getRepoContext`, `getAuthenticatedRepo`, and `getSession`. Each API Route request is already isolated ŌĆö no deduplication is needed. If deduplication within a single Server Action invocation is desired, keep `cache()` only on the Server Action path and export a separate uncached version for API Routes.

title: Epic: Handle OAuth rejection gracefully
description: When a user rejects the ATProto OAuth authorization, the callback URL includes error=access_denied. Currently the callback route lets sdk.callback() throw and returns raw JSON ({error, details}) to the browser ŌĆö terrible UX. Goal: detect the error param early in the callback route, redirect the user back to / with a query param like ?auth_error=access_denied, and show a toast on the client side explaining what happened. The user should land back on the login screen seamlessly. Success: rejecting auth on the PDS shows a toast and the login dialog, not raw JSON. Scope: callback route early-return + client-side toast. Out of scope: new error pages, middleware, changes to the login flow itself.

title: Fix: Collections enum in lib/types.ts uses stale collection names not matching SDK
description:
## Problem
`lib/types.ts` defines a `Collections` enum (lines 47-54) with hardcoded collection name strings that are out of sync with the SDK's `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` constants:

| Enum value | Enum string | SDK constant |
|---|---|---|
| `contribution` | `"org.hypercerts.claim.contribution"` | `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails"` |
| `evidence` | `"org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"` | No matching SDK constant (renamed to ATTACHMENT) |

The `Collections` enum is used in `lib/queries.ts` (lines 90, 114) for `createContribution` and `createEvidence` calls. These functions will write records to the wrong collection names, causing them to be invisible to any consumer that uses the correct SDK collection names.

## Fix
Replace the `Collections` enum with references to `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core`, or update the hardcoded strings to match the SDK:
- `contribution` ŌåÆ `"org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails"`
- `evidence` ŌåÆ `"org.hypercerts.claim.attachment"`

Note: `lib/queries.ts` appears to be legacy code (the new SDK-based approach uses `ctx.scopedRepo.hypercerts.*`). Verify whether these functions are still called anywhere before fixing, and consider removing them if they are dead code.

## Evidence
- `lib/types.ts` lines 47-54: stale enum values
- `node_modules/@hypercerts-org/sdk-core/dist/lexicons.d.ts` lines 322, 339: correct SDK values
- `lib/queries.ts` lines 90, 114: callers using the stale enum

But `{{logo_uri}}` resolves to `/certified.png` which is 530├Ś100px ŌĆö a wide horizontal logo with a ~5.3:1 aspect ratio. This causes the logo to be squished/distorted. ### Fix the img tag dimensions: Change the logo `` on lines 44-50 from: ```html ``` To: ```html ``` ### Why these values: - `max-width: 180px` keeps the logo reasonably sized in the 460px-wide email card (about 40% of card width) ŌĆö not too big, not too small - `height: auto` preserves the natural aspect ratio so the logo is never squished - Removed the fixed `width` and `height` HTML attributes because they force a specific aspect ratio - For email client compatibility, inline styles are used (email clients strip `

title: Fix: revert login UI from Dialog back to Popover (pre-doors layout)
description:
## Files
- components/navbar.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The login UI was changed from a Popover (anchored to the Sign In button, w-80, right-aligned) to a Dialog (centered modal, full-width on mobile). The user wants the original Popover layout back.

Revert navbar.tsx to use Popover instead of Dialog for the sign-in flow:

1. Replace Dialog/DialogContent/DialogTrigger/DialogTitle imports with Popover/PopoverContent/PopoverTrigger
2. Add back the \`open\`/\`setOpen\` state that the Popover needs
3. Use \`<PopoverContent align=\"end\" className=\"w-80 glass-panel border-border/60 p-4\">\` ŌĆö this gives the compact, right-aligned dropdown
4. Keep \`<LoginDialog />\` inside the PopoverContent ŌĆö the pill toggle component works fine in a Popover
5. Remove the sr-only DialogTitle (not needed for Popover)

Reference: the pre-doors navbar at commit 85505ad used Popover with w-80 and align=\"end\". The current LoginDialog component (pill toggle with HandleForm/EmailForm) should be kept as-is ŌĆö only the container changes from Dialog to Popover.

## Don't
- Don't modify login-dialog.tsx ŌĆö only navbar.tsx changes
- Don't change any other part of the navbar (logo, nav links, user dropdown)
- Don't add any new dependencies
- Don't change the LoginDialog component's internal layout
acceptance_criteria:
1. Sign In button opens a Popover anchored to the button (right-aligned), NOT a centered Dialog modal
2. Popover is approximately w-80 (320px) wide, not full-screen
3. Pill toggle (Handle | Email) renders correctly inside the Popover
4. Handle login flow works (enter handle, click Sign In)
5. Email login flow works (switch to Email tab, enter email, click Continue)
6. Popover closes when clicking outside
7. No Dialog/DialogContent/DialogTrigger/DialogTitle imports remain in navbar.tsx
8. No TypeScript errors: npx tsc --noEmit passes
close_reason: Wrong diagnosis ŌĆö the issue is in LoginDialog's root div missing max-w-sm, not the navbar Dialog vs Popover.

title: Fix: LoginDialog stretches full-width ŌĆö restore max-w-sm constraint
description:
## Files
- components/login-dialog.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The root div of LoginDialog (line 158) has className=\"w-full space-y-5\" but is missing max-w-sm. This causes it to stretch full-width when rendered directly in the page by SignedInProvider (providers/SignedInProvider.tsx line 48-50), which wraps it in a flex-grow centered container.

Before the pill-toggle rewrite (commit 85505ad), the root element was:
  <form className=\"grid w-full max-w-sm gap-6 py-10\">

The current root element is:
  <div className=\"w-full space-y-5\">

Fix: change line 158 from:
  <div className=\"w-full space-y-5\">
to:
  <div className=\"w-full max-w-sm space-y-5\">

This constrains the LoginDialog to 384px max width, matching the pre-rewrite layout. It still works inside the navbar Dialog (which is sm:max-w-sm anyway) because max-w-sm just becomes redundant there.

## Don't
- Don't change anything else in login-dialog.tsx
- Don't modify navbar.tsx or SignedInProvider.tsx
acceptance_criteria:
1. On the main page (not signed in), LoginDialog renders at max 384px wide (max-w-sm), centered ŌĆö NOT full viewport width
2. In the navbar Dialog, LoginDialog still renders correctly (max-w-sm is redundant but harmless)
3. Pill toggle and both forms (Handle/Email) still work
4. No TypeScript errors
close_reason: Added max-w-sm to LoginDialog root div

title: Fix: eslint-config-next version (16.0.1) does not match next version (16.0.7) in package.json
description:
Review of package.json found a minor version mismatch between next and eslint-config-next.

Evidence:
- package.json line 48: 'next': '16.0.7'
- package.json line 66: 'eslint-config-next': '16.0.1'

The eslint-config-next package should match the next version to ensure lint rules are consistent with the Next.js version in use. A 6-patch-version gap (16.0.1 vs 16.0.7) is unlikely to cause issues but is a maintenance smell ŌĆö if Next.js added new lint rules in 16.0.2-16.0.7, they won't be enforced.

Suggested fix:
Update eslint-config-next to match next:
  'eslint-config-next': '16.0.7'

Or use a caret range that tracks next:
  'eslint-config-next': '^16.0.7'

title: Fix: .vscode/settings.json commits personal color theme preference ŌĆö should be gitignored
description:
Review of .vscode/settings.json found it only contains a personal color theme preference:
  { "workbench.colorTheme": "Kimbie Dark" }

This is a personal developer preference, not a project-wide setting. Committing it forces all contributors to use 'Kimbie Dark' theme (or see a diff every time they change their theme).

Evidence:
- .vscode/settings.json line 2: 'workbench.colorTheme': 'Kimbie Dark'
- This is the only setting in the file ŌĆö no formatter, no editor config, no extension recommendations

Suggested fix:
Option A (preferred): Remove workbench.colorTheme from .vscode/settings.json entirely. Add useful project settings instead:
  {
    "editor.formatOnSave": true,
    "editor.defaultFormatter": "esbenp.prettier-vscode",
    "editor.codeActionsOnSave": { "source.fixAll.eslint": "explicit" },
    "typescript.tsdk": "node_modules/typescript/lib"
  }

Option B: Add .vscode/settings.json to .gitignore and use .vscode/settings.json.example for project settings.

The current state provides no DX value and actively harms contributors who use a different theme.

title: Change logout endpoint from GET to POST
acceptance_criteria:
1. GET /api/oauth/logout returns 405 Method Not Allowed (Next.js default for missing GET handler)
2. POST /api/oauth/logout returns 200 with { message: "Signed out successfully" } when authenticated
3. POST /api/oauth/logout returns 200 with { message: "Signed out successfully" } when not authenticated (session is null, no-op)
4. lib/api/auth.ts logout() uses method: "POST"
5. Only 2 lines changed total (one per file)
6. Files pass eslint and prettier

title: Fix: Contributors label in contributions-form.tsx has no htmlFor ŌĆö Label not associated with any input
description:
## File
components/contributions-form.tsx line 184

## Severity
MEDIUM ŌĆö Accessibility

## Problem
The Contributors Label element has no htmlFor attribute, so it is not programmatically associated with any input. Screen readers cannot identify which control this label describes. The same issue exists for the Evaluators label in evaluation-form.tsx (line 231) and the Measurers label in measurement-form.tsx (line 325).

## Evidence
```tsx
// contributions-form.tsx line 184
<Label className="font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)] text-sm font-medium">
  Contributors *
</Label>

// evaluation-form.tsx line 231
<Label className="text-muted-foreground ...">
  Evaluators *
</Label>

// measurement-form.tsx line 325
<Label className="text-muted-foreground ...">
  Measurers
</Label>
```

## Fix
Since these labels describe a Tabs component (not a single input), use aria-labelledby on the TabsList or wrap in a fieldset with legend:
```tsx
<fieldset>
  <legend className="font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)] text-sm font-medium">
    Contributors *
  </legend>
  <Tabs defaultValue="search" className="w-full">
    ...
  </Tabs>
</fieldset>
```

title: Fix: AuthErrorToast missing Suspense boundary (from hypercerts-jsu.1)
description: Review of hypercerts-jsu.1 found: AuthErrorToast uses useSearchParams() without a wrapping <Suspense> boundary. Next.js App Router requires Suspense around any component using useSearchParams() to avoid opting the entire page out of static rendering. See: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-search-params#static-rendering. Evidence: providers/AuthErrorToast.tsx uses useSearchParams() at line 10; providers/SignedInProvider.tsx renders <AuthErrorToast /> at line 37 with no Suspense wrapper. Fix: wrap <AuthErrorToast /> in <Suspense fallback={null}> in SignedInProvider.tsx. All routes are currently dynamic so this does not cause a runtime failure today, but it is a correctness violation that will break if any route becomes static.
acceptance_criteria:
1. AuthErrorToast is wrapped in <Suspense fallback={null}> in SignedInProvider.tsx
2. No other changes to AuthErrorToast.tsx or the callback route
3. TypeScript compiles: npx tsc --noEmit passes
4. The toast still works correctly (auth_error in URL ŌåÆ toast shown ŌåÆ URL cleaned)
close_reason: cdb3096 fix: wrap AuthErrorToast in Suspense boundary

title: Fix: getContentTypeColor is a dead function ŌĆö always returns same value (hypercert-evidence-view.tsx)
description:
## File
components/hypercert-evidence-view.tsx lines 27-30

## Severity
LOW ŌĆö Code quality / dead code

## Problem
getContentTypeColor() is defined as a function that always returns the same hardcoded string regardless of contentType. The comment says 'All content types use the same color' but the function signature implies it was intended to be content-type-aware. This is dead code that adds noise.

## Evidence
```tsx
const getContentTypeColor = () => {
  // All content types use the same color
  return 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800 dark:bg-blue-900 dark:text-blue-100';
};
```

## Fix
Either:
1. Remove the function and inline the class string directly on the Badge:
```tsx
<Badge className="bg-blue-100 text-blue-800 dark:bg-blue-900 dark:text-blue-100 shrink-0 font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)]">
```
2. Or implement actual per-type colors if that was the intent.

title: Fix: email login_hint parameter not validated ŌĆö arbitrary value forwarded to ePDS auth server (from security review)
Fix: secure cookie flag uses NODE_ENV, not HTTPS detection ŌĆö breaks Vercel preview (from hypercerts-dhz)
description:
## Review finding from hypercerts-dhz (ePDS OAuth integration)

**Files:**
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts, line 90: `secure: config.isProduction`
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, lines 142, (also app/api/auth/callback/route.ts line 14)
- lib/config.ts, line 172: `const isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'`

**Problem:**
`config.isProduction` is `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'`. On Vercel preview deployments, `NODE_ENV` is typically `'production'` so this usually works. However, the check is semantically wrong ŌĆö the `Secure` cookie flag should be set based on whether the connection is HTTPS, not on `NODE_ENV`.

More concretely: if someone runs the app with `NODE_ENV=development` on an HTTPS server (e.g., a staging environment with a custom domain), the `Secure` flag will be absent, making the cookies transmittable over HTTP. Conversely, the loopback check in `config.ts` already handles the HTTP/HTTPS distinction correctly for the base URL ŌĆö the cookie flag should use the same logic.

**Fix:**
Use `!config.isLoopback` instead of `config.isProduction` for the `secure` flag:
```ts
secure: !config.isLoopback,
```

This correctly sets `Secure` on any non-loopback deployment (which must be HTTPS per the existing `validateBaseUrl` check) and omits it on loopback (which uses HTTP).

This affects all three cookie-setting locations:
1. `app/api/oauth/login/route.ts` ŌĆö epds-oauth-session cookie
2. `app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts` ŌĆö user-did cookie
3. `app/api/auth/callback/route.ts` ŌĆö user-did cookie (existing flow, same issue)

title: Fix: Manual contributor/DID inputs in forms have no accessible label (contributions-form.tsx, evaluation-form.tsx, measurement-form.tsx)
description:
## Files - components/contributions-form.tsx lines 218-229 - components/evaluation-form.tsx lines 268-287 - components/measurement-form.tsx lines 365-384 ## Severity MEDIUM ŌĆö Accessibility ## Problem The dynamic list of manual contributor/DID input fields have no accessible label. Each Input is rendered inside a div with no associated label element and no aria-label. Screen readers cannot identify what these inputs are for. ## Evidence ```tsx // contributions-form.tsx lines 218-229 {manualContributors.map((uri, index) => (
updateManualContributor(index, e.target.value)} // No aria-label, no associated label element />
))} ``` ## Fix Add aria-label to each dynamic input: ```tsx updateManualContributor(index, e.target.value)} /> ``` Apply the same pattern to evaluation-form.tsx (DID inputs) and measurement-form.tsx (DID inputs and evidence URI inputs).

title: Epic: Wire up contributor addition to activity claims
description:
Contributors are not being added to activity claims due to three compounding issues:

1. **PRIMARY BUG**: In `app/api/certs/route.ts` POST handler (line 69-73), contributions data IS sent from the client form but the server has a TODO comment and silently discards it. The parsed result is never assigned or used.

2. **Disabled creation wizard step**: In `app/hypercerts/create/page.tsx`, the `HypercertContributionForm` component is commented out with `{/* TODO commented out for now while SDK addContribution stabilized */}`.

3. **No detail-view path**: In `components/add-record-form.tsx`, the contribution case shows 'Contribution form coming soon' placeholder instead of the actual form.

**What success looks like:**
- Contributors added during hypercert creation are persisted to the activity claim record
- Contributors can be added to existing hypercerts from the detail view
- The creation wizard includes a Contributors step
- The display logic (already working) shows the contributors correctly

**Key constraints:**
- The `addContribution` server action in `lib/create-actions.ts` already works correctly ŌĆö reuse it
- The `contributions-form.tsx` component already exists and works ŌĆö reuse it
- The display components (`hypercert-contributors-section.tsx`, `hypercert-contributor-view.tsx`) already work
- Must validate records with `assertValidRecord()` before every write
- Must follow existing ATProto patterns (StrongRef for cross-record references)

title: Process contributions in POST /api/certs after hypercert creation
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
In the POST handler, after the claim record is successfully created (after line 180 where `claimResult` is returned), process the contributions data that is currently being silently discarded.

1. Parse the `contributionsRaw` FormData field into a typed array. The client sends it as `JSON.stringify(params.contributions)` where each contribution has shape: `{ contributors: string[], role: string, contributionDescription?: string, startDate?: string, endDate?: string }`.

2. If contributions are present and non-empty, call `addContribution()` from `lib/create-actions.ts` for each contribution entry, passing:
   - `hypercertUri`: the `claimResult.data.uri` just created
   - `contributors`: the array of DID strings from the contribution
   - `contributionDetails`: `{ role, contributionDescription, startDate, endDate }`

3. **IMPORTANT**: `addContribution` is a `"use server"` action. You CANNOT call it directly from an API route. Instead, extract the core logic into a shared helper function. Create a new exported function `processContributions(ctx, hypercertUri, contributions)` in `lib/create-actions.ts` that contains the logic from `addContribution` lines 108-218 but takes a `RepoContext` instead of calling `getRepoContext()` internally. Then call this from both the API route and the existing `addContribution` action.

   Alternatively, since the API route already has `ctx` (the repo context), you can inline the contribution processing logic directly in the route, following the same pattern as `addContribution`: create `contributorInformation` records, create `contribution` record, build contributor entries with StrongRefs, then update the claim via `putRecord`.

4. Contribution processing should be best-effort ŌĆö if it fails, still return the created hypercert (log the error). The hypercert creation itself should not fail because of a contribution error.

5. Remove the TODO comment on line 69.

## Dont
- Do not change the FormData parsing or client-side code
- Do not change the claim record creation logic (lines 140-180)
- Do not make contribution processing block the response ŌĆö if contributions fail, still return the hypercert data
- Do not remove the `parseOptionalJson` validation call (it catches malformed JSON early)
acceptance_criteria:
1. When creating a hypercert with contributions via POST /api/certs, the contributions are persisted as child records (contributorInformation + contribution) and the activity claim record is updated with a contributors array
2. When creating a hypercert WITHOUT contributions, behavior is unchanged
3. If contribution processing fails, the hypercert is still returned successfully (no regression)
4. The TODO comment on line 69 is removed
5. pnpm run build passes

title: Wire up contribution form in the detail-view add-record page
description:
## Files
- components/add-record-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace the "Contribution form coming soon" placeholder with the actual `HypercertContributionForm` component.

1. Add a dynamic import for `HypercertContributionForm` from `@/components/contributions-form`, following the same pattern as the other form imports (lines 16-27). Use a skeleton loader ŌĆö either create a `ContributionFormSkeleton` in `form-skeletons.tsx` or reuse `MeasurementFormSkeleton` as a reasonable approximation.

2. In the `renderForm()` switch statement, replace the `case "contribution"` block (lines 90-95) with:
   ```tsx
   case "contribution":
     return (
       <ContributionsForm
         hypercertInfo={hypercertInfo}
         onNext={goBack}
         onBack={goBack}
       />
     );
   ```

3. The `ContributionsForm` component expects `BaseHypercertFormProps & { onBack?: () => void; onNext?: () => void }`. The `hypercertInfo` is already constructed on line 54-57 as a `CreateHypercertResult` which satisfies `BaseHypercertFormProps`. The `onNext` and `onBack` callbacks should both call `goBack` (navigate back to the hypercert detail page), matching the pattern used by measurement/evaluation/evidence forms.

- Do not modify the ContributionsForm component itself
- Do not change the routing or URL structure
- Do not remove the comment on line 28 about ContributionsForm being disabled (update it to say it is now enabled)

title: Add 'Add Contributor' button to the hypercert detail view contributors section
description:

   - Only renders when `isOwner` is `true`
