title: Fix: add-attachment route starts getRepoContext() before auth check ŌĆö wasted work on unauthenticated requests
description:
## Problem
In `app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts` line 7, `getRepoContext()` is called immediately (before any validation):

```ts
const data = await req.formData();
const ctxPromise = getRepoContext();  // ŌåÉ starts here
// ... 60+ lines of validation ...
const ctx = await ctxPromise;         // ŌåÉ awaited here
```

This is the same pattern used in `app/api/certs/create/route.ts` (line 8) and `app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts` (line 8). The intent is to parallelize auth with form parsing, which is a valid optimization.

However, `getRepoContext()` reads cookies and restores a Redis session. If the request fails validation (e.g. missing `hypercertUri`, invalid `evidenceMode`), the session restore work is wasted. More importantly, if the session restore fails with an unhandled error, it could mask the real validation error.

The pattern is inconsistent: `app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts` uses the same optimization (line 8: `const repoPromise = getAuthenticatedRepo()`) but `app/api/profile/update/route.ts` also uses it (line 8).

This is LOW severity but worth noting for consistency and to ensure the Promise rejection from `getRepoContext()` is always handled (it is ŌĆö the outer try/catch covers it).

## Recommendation
Document the intentional parallelization pattern with a comment, or move auth to before validation if the overhead is acceptable. The current approach is safe but could confuse future maintainers.

## Evidence
- `app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts` lines 7, 64
- `app/api/certs/create/route.ts` lines 8, 45
- `app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts` lines 8, 10

title: Epic: Fix loopback OAuth ŌĆö scope, client ID, and PAR request corrections
description:
## Context
The ePDS OAuth login flow is broken in loopback (local dev) mode. Three changes were made to fix it:
1. Added `application_type: native` to the PAR request body
2. Created a separate `epdsClientId` using the ePDS redirect URI
3. Fixed `getEpdsClientId()` to return `config.epdsClientId` instead of `config.clientId`

## Problem
These changes partially fix the issue but leave several bugs and loose ends:
- `OAUTH_SCOPE` is hardcoded to `GRANULAR_SCOPE` ŌĆö the `getOAuthScope()` function that returns `LOOPBACK_SCOPE` for loopback is dead code. ATProto spec requires loopback clients to use `atproto transition:generic`, not granular scopes.
- `application_type: native` is hardcoded in the PAR body unconditionally ŌĆö should only be sent for loopback, not production.
- A debug `console.log(redirectUri)` was left in the login route.
- The ePDS state in Redis is never cleaned up after a successful callback (replay risk).
- `buildClientMetadata()` still uses `config.clientId` (regular auth) not `config.epdsClientId` ŌĆö loopback metadata has wrong redirect_uri in client_id.
- The loopback client_id embeds granular scopes instead of loopback scopes because `OAUTH_SCOPE` is always granular.

## Success Criteria
- Loopback mode uses `LOOPBACK_SCOPE` everywhere: client ID, PAR request, client metadata
- Production mode uses `GRANULAR_SCOPE` everywhere
- `application_type` is `native` for loopback, `web` for production in PAR requests
- No debug logs left in code
- ePDS state cleaned up after successful callback
- All changes are scoped to loopback behavior only ŌĆö production flow unchanged
notes: Superseded ŌĆö re-scoped after clarification that granular scopes work for loopback

title: Epic: Display contributors on hypercert detail page
description:
## Why
The hypercert detail page currently shows measurements, evidence, and evaluations but completely omits contributor information. The upstream hypercerts-lexicon (https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-lexicon) has evolved the activity schema: contributors are now inline on the activity record as an array of contributor objects (each with contributorIdentity, contributionWeight, contributionDetails). The local scaffold's generated lexicon types still use the old schema (contributions as StrongRef[] to separate records). We need to:

1. Update the local lexicon types to match the upstream activity.json schema
2. Build the full contributor display pipeline on the detail page
3. Support both inline contributor identities (DID strings) and StrongRef-based contributorInformation records
4. Resolve contributor DIDs to profile info (display name, avatar) via Bluesky public API

## What success looks like
- The hypercert detail page shows a 'Contributors' section between the metadata grid and the description
- Each contributor displays: identity (resolved to profile name/avatar when possible), role, and weight
- Contributors with DID identities link to their Bluesky profile
- The section gracefully handles: no contributors, contributors with only DID strings, contributors with StrongRef to contributorInformation records
- Loading and error states match the existing section patterns (measurements, evidence, evaluations)

## Key constraints
- Follow the exact same architectural patterns as measurements/evidence/evaluations sections
- Use the upstream lexicon schema from https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-lexicon/blob/develop/lexicons/org/hypercerts/claim/activity.json as the source of truth
- Contributors are inline on the activity record (NOT fetched via Constellation backlinks like measurements/evidence)
- Must handle both contributorIdentity string format and StrongRef format
- Build and lint must pass after all tasks

title: Update activity lexicon types to match upstream contributor schema
acceptance_criteria:
1. The file compiles without TypeScript errors
2. `Contributor`, `ContributorIdentity`, `ContributorRole`, `WorkScopeString` interfaces are exported
3. `Main` interface has both `contributions?` (old) and `contributors?` (new) fields
4. `Main` interface has `startDate?` and `endDate?` fields alongside existing `workTimeFrameFrom`/`workTimeFrameTo`
5. Type guard functions `isContributor`, `isContributorIdentity`, `isContributorRole` are exported
6. `npm run build` passes (or `npx tsc --noEmit`)
notes: Cancelled: lexicon types come from @hypercerts-org/lexicon package, not local files. Replaced by mrb.7 (upgrade package).

title: Fix: contributor card link missing aria-label for accessibility (from hypercerts-mrb.4)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.4 found: In hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 86-93, the anchor tag wrapping the card for DID contributors has no aria-label. Screen readers will announce the full concatenated card text as the link label (name + handle + role + weight), which is verbose and confusing. The link should have an aria-label like 'View {name or identity} on Bluesky' to give screen reader users a concise, meaningful label. Additionally, the hover state only changes border color (transition-colors on the Card) but there is no visible focus ring for keyboard navigation ŌĆö the card needs a focus-visible outline. Evidence: components/hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 84-97.

title: Fix: long displayName overflows contributor card on mobile (from hypercerts-mrb.4)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.4 found: In hypercert-contributor-view.tsx line 41, the name

element has no overflow/truncation classes. When profile.displayName is present (the non-truncated branch, lines 42-44), a long display name (e.g. 80+ characters) will overflow the card and break the flex layout, especially on mobile where the card is narrow. The spec (mrb.4 description) says to use truncate class with max-w-[200px] for the identity fallback, but the displayName branch has no such constraint. Fix: add truncate and a max-width to the outer

or the displayName span. Evidence: components/hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 41-49.

title: Fix: contributor count subtitle shows '0 contributors' in empty state (from hypercerts-mrb.5)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.5 found: In hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 45-48, the count subtitle is always rendered, even when displayContributors.length === 0. This means the section header reads 'Contributors / 0 contributors' before showing the empty state panel below. The measurements section (hypercert-measurements-section.tsx lines 71-75) and evidence section (hypercert-evidence-section.tsx lines 66-70) both conditionally render the count subtitle only when length > 0. Fix: wrap the count

in a conditional: {displayContributors.length > 0 && (...)}. Evidence: components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 45-48 vs hypercert-measurements-section.tsx lines 71-75.

title: Fix: contributor list uses array index as React key (from hypercerts-mrb.5)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.5 found: In hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 56 and 66, both the skeleton list and the contributor view list use key={index}. Using array index as key causes React reconciliation issues if the list is ever filtered, sorted, or reordered ŌĆö components will not remount correctly and may show stale data. The contributor identity string (contributor.identity) is a more stable and semantically meaningful key. Fix: change key={index} to key={contributor.identity} on line 66 (and key={} is acceptable for skeletons since they are transient). Evidence: components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 55-57 and 66-72.

title: Fix: unused Award import in hypercert-detail-view.tsx (from hypercerts-mrb.6)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.6 found: In hypercert-detail-view.tsx line 12, the Award icon is imported from lucide-react but never used anywhere in the file. The mrb.6 spec explicitly says 'Do not remove the unused Award import' but this will cause a lint warning (no-unused-vars / @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars) and may fail the build if the project has strict lint rules. The import should either be removed (if truly unused) or used. Evidence: components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx line 12 ŌĆö Award is imported but does not appear in the JSX.

title: Fix: profileMap rebuilt on every render in useContributorProfilesQuery (from hypercerts-mrb.3)
Fix: type guards in contributor-utils.ts are ambiguous ŌĆö ContributorIdentity and StrongRef can both match (from hypercerts-mrb.2)
Fix: Collections enum uses wrong NSID for 'contribution' and 'evidence' (from hypercerts-mrb.7)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.7 found: `lib/types.ts` lines 48-53 defines the `Collections` enum. Two entries are mismatched against the installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` package:

1. `contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contribution"` ŌĆö The package has BOTH `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution` (old schema: `contributors: string[]`, `description`) AND `org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails` (new schema: `role`, `contributionDescription`, `startDate`, `endDate`). `lib/queries.ts` line 4 imports `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as HypercertContribution` and uses it for `createContribution` (line 84-94), but `Collections.contribution` still points to the OLD collection NSID `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution`. Records created via `createContribution` will be written to the wrong collection.

2. `evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"` ŌĆö The package's `OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment` (imported as `HypercertEvidence` in `lib/queries.ts` line 5) has `$type: 'org.hypercerts.claim.attachment'`, but `Collections.evidence` is `"org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"`. Records created via `createEvidence` (line 108-118) will be written to `org.hypercerts.claim.evidence` but the type system expects `org.hypercerts.claim.attachment`.

Evidence:
- `lib/types.ts` line 49: `contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contribution"`
- `lib/types.ts` line 50: `evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"`
- `lib/queries.ts` line 4: `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as HypercertContribution`
- `lib/queries.ts` line 5: `OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment as HypercertEvidence`
- Package NSID constants: `CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS_NSID = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails"`, `ATTACHMENT_NSID = "org.hypercerts.claim.attachment"`

Fix: Update `Collections` enum:
```ts
contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails",
evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.attachment",
```
Verify all callers of `Collections.contribution` and `Collections.evidence` still work after the rename. This is a runtime data-corruption bug ŌĆö records are being written to the wrong AT Protocol collection.

title: Fix: blob-display.tsx $type check uses wrong value for Uri type (from hypercerts-mrb.7)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.7 found: `components/blob-display.tsx` line 19 checks:
```ts
if (type === "app.certified.defs#uri") {
```

But the installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` package defines `OrgHypercertsDefs.Uri` with:
```ts
$type?: 'org.hypercerts.defs#uri';
```
(see `node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/types/org/hypercerts/defs.d.ts` line 7)

The check uses `app.certified.defs#uri` but the actual $type value in records is `org.hypercerts.defs#uri`. This means the URI branch will never match, and all URI-type content will fall through to the final `return "ŌĆö"`, silently hiding evidence/attachment links.

Similarly, the blob type check on line 24:
```ts
if (["smallBlob", "largeBlob", "blob"].includes(type || "")) {
```
This checks for bare names like `"smallBlob"` but the actual $type values are `"org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob"` and `"org.hypercerts.defs#largeBlob"`. This branch also never matches.

Evidence:
- `components/blob-display.tsx` lines 19, 24
- `node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/types/org/hypercerts/defs.d.ts` lines 7, 15, 23

Fix: Update the type checks to use the correct fully-qualified $type strings:
```ts
if (type === "org.hypercerts.defs#uri") { ... }
if (["org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob", "org.hypercerts.defs#largeBlob"].includes(type || "")) { ... }
```

title: Fix: query key namespace collision ŌĆö contributorProfile shares 'hypercerts' root with record-scoped keys (from hypercerts-mrb.3)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.3 found: `lib/api/query-keys.ts` line 38-39 adds:
```ts
contributorProfile: (did: string) =>
  [...queryKeys.hypercerts.all, "contributor-profile", did] as const,
```

This produces keys like `["hypercerts", "contributor-profile", "did:plc:abc"]`. However, there is already a separate `external.bluesky.profile` key (line 46-48) for the same Bluesky profile data:
```ts
profile: (actor: string) =>
  [...queryKeys.external.bluesky.all, "profile", actor] as const,
```

Two different query keys now cache the same underlying data (Bluesky profile for a DID). If any other part of the app uses `queryKeys.external.bluesky.profile(did)` to fetch or invalidate a profile, the contributor profile cache will not be invalidated, and vice versa. This is a cache coherence issue.

Additionally, the `contributorProfile` key is semantically an external Bluesky API call, not a hypercert record operation. Placing it under `queryKeys.hypercerts` is a namespace violation that will confuse future developers.

Fix: Either (a) reuse `queryKeys.external.bluesky.profile(did)` in `useContributorProfilesQuery` instead of introducing a new key, or (b) move `contributorProfile` under `queryKeys.external.bluesky` and document the deduplication intent.

title: Add contributor profile resolution utility
acceptance_criteria:
1. File `lib/contributor-utils.ts` exists and exports `DisplayContributor`, `parseContributor`, `parseContributors`
2. `parseContributors(undefined)` returns `[]`
3. `parseContributors([])` returns `[]`
4. `parseContributor({ contributorIdentity: { identity: "did:plc:abc" } })` returns `{ identity: "did:plc:abc", isDid: true, role: undefined, weight: undefined }`
5. `parseContributor({ contributorIdentity: { identity: "Alice" }, contributionDetails: { role: "Developer" }, contributionWeight: "50" })` returns `{ identity: "Alice", isDid: false, role: "Developer", weight: "50" }`
6. `parseContributor({ contributorIdentity: { uri: "at://did:plc:xyz/org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation/abc", cid: "bafy..." } })` returns `{ identity: "did:plc:xyz", isDid: true, identityRef: { uri: ..., cid: ... } }`
7. TypeScript compiles without errors

title: Fix: getProfile() throws on non-200 but retry:false leaves error state permanent (from hypercerts-mrb.3)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.3 found: `queries/hypercerts/use-contributor-profiles-query.ts` line 24 sets `retry: false`. The `getProfile` function in `lib/api/external/bluesky.ts` line 13 calls `externalApiClient` which throws an `APIError` on any non-2xx response (see `lib/api/client.ts` lines 132-136). With `retry: false`, a transient network error or a 429 rate-limit from the Bluesky public API will permanently mark the query as `isError` for the lifetime of the component. The contributor profile will never be retried even after the rate limit window expires.

The spec comment says 'Failed lookups are silently ignored' ŌĆö this is partially correct (the Map simply won't contain the DID), but the query stays in error state and will not re-attempt on the next mount.

Evidence:
- `use-contributor-profiles-query.ts` line 24: `retry: false`
- `lib/api/client.ts` lines 132-136: throws `APIError` on non-ok response
- No `throwOnError: false` or error boundary present

Fix: Consider `retry: 1` with exponential backoff for transient errors, or at minimum add `staleTime: 0` so a remount will re-attempt. Alternatively, wrap `queryFn` to catch and return `null` instead of throwing, making the error truly silent and allowing future retries.

title: Create HypercertContributorView component
description: ## Files - components/hypercert-contributor-view.tsx (create) ## What to do Create a single-contributor card component that displays one contributor's information. This follows the same pattern as `components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx`. ### Component signature: ```typescript "use client"; import type { DisplayContributor } from "@/lib/contributor-utils"; import type { BlueskyProfile } from "@/lib/api/types"; interface HypercertContributorViewProps { contributor: DisplayContributor; profile?: BlueskyProfile; // resolved Bluesky profile, if available } export default function HypercertContributorView({ contributor, profile }: HypercertContributorViewProps) { ... } ``` ### Layout: Use a Card (from `components/ui/card`) with `glass-panel rounded-xl border border-border/50` classes (matching measurement-view pattern). **Card content (single row layout):** 1. **Avatar area** (left): - If `profile?.avatar` exists: render a 40x40 rounded-full `` with the avatar URL - Else: render a 40x40 rounded-full div with `bg-create-accent/10` containing a `User` icon from lucide-react (size-5, text-create-accent) 2. **Info area** (center, flex-1): - **Name line**: - If `profile?.displayName` ŌåÆ show displayName in `text-sm font-[family-name:var(--font-syne)] font-semibold` - Else ŌåÆ show `contributor.identity` truncated (use `truncate` class, max-w-[200px]) - **Handle/DID line** (below name, smaller): - If `profile?.handle` ŌåÆ show `@{handle}` in `text-xs text-muted-foreground font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)]` - Else if `contributor.isDid` ŌåÆ show truncated DID in same style - **Role badge** (below handle, optional): - If `contributor.role` ŌåÆ show in a small pill: `inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)] bg-create-accent/10 text-create-accent border border-create-accent/20` 3. **Weight area** (right, optional): - If `contributor.weight` ŌåÆ show weight value in `text-lg font-[family-name:var(--font-syne)] font-bold text-create-accent` with a small "weight" label below in `text-xs text-muted-foreground` 4. **Link behavior**: - If `contributor.isDid` ŌåÆ wrap the entire card content in an anchor tag linking to `https://bsky.app/profile/${contributor.identity}` with `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` - Add hover state: `hover:border-create-accent/40 transition-colors` ### Icons to import: - `User` from lucide-react ## Don't - Fetch any data (all data comes via props) - Use Next.js Image component for avatars (use plain `` since avatars are external Bluesky CDN URLs) - Add any loading/error states (parent handles those) - Import from `@/queries/` (this is a pure presentational component)
acceptance_criteria:

10. TypeScript compiles without errors

title: Create HypercertContributorsSection component
description:
## Files - components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx (create) ## What to do Create the contributors section component. Contributors come from the activity record itself (passed as props), NOT from Constellation backlinks. **Import types from the package** (NOT from local `@/lexicons/`): ```typescript import type { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"; type Contributor = OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Contributor; ``` ### Component signature: ```typescript "use client"; interface HypercertContributorsSectionProps { contributors?: Contributor[]; } export default function HypercertContributorsSection({ contributors }: HypercertContributorsSectionProps) { ... } ``` ### Implementation: 1. Parse contributors using `parseContributors(contributors)` from `@/lib/contributor-utils` 2. ); ``` ## Don't - Import from local `@/lexicons/types/...` - Fetch data from Constellation backlinks - Create any server actions - Modify any existing component files

title: Upgrade @hypercerts-org/lexicon to beta.15 and migrate local lexicon imports
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- lib/utils.ts (modify)
- lib/queries.ts (modify)
- lib/types.ts (modify)
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify)
- components/blob-display.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The local `lexicons/` directory contains generated types from an outdated version of `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` (0.10.0-beta.3). The installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` package needs upgrading to `0.10.0-beta.15` which has the `contributors` field on the activity record. After upgrading, migrate all imports from local `@/lexicons/types/...` to the package.

### Step 1: Upgrade the package
```bash
npm install @hypercerts-org/lexicon@0.10.0-beta.15
```

### Step 2: Migrate imports in 5 files

The SDK re-exports all lexicon types. Use `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` or `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` for imports. The SDK already re-exports everything via:
```typescript
export { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity, OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment, OrgHypercertsClaimCollection, OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails, OrgHypercertsClaimContributorInformation, OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation, OrgHypercertsClaimMeasurement, OrgHypercertsClaimRights, OrgHypercertsDefs, AppCertifiedLocation, ComAtprotoRepoStrongRef } from "@hypercerts-org/lexicon";
```

**Migration mapping:**
| Old import | New import |
|---|---|
| `import * as Hypercert from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as Hypercert } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertClaim from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as HypercertClaim } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as Contribution from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/contribution"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as Contribution } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertContribution from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/contribution"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as HypercertContribution } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as Evaluation from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/evaluation"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation as Evaluation } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertEvaluation from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/evaluation"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation as HypercertEvaluation } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertEvidence from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/evidence"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment as HypercertEvidence } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertRights from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/rights"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimRights as HypercertRights } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertLocation from "@/lexicons/types/app/certified/location"` | `import { AppCertifiedLocation as HypercertLocation } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import { SmallBlob, Uri } from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/defs"` | `import { OrgHypercertsDefs } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` then use `OrgHypercertsDefs.SmallBlob` and `OrgHypercertsDefs.Uri` |

**IMPORTANT**: The old local `contribution.ts` had a different schema (single `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution` record with `hypercert`, `role`, `contributors[]`, `description`). The new package has `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails` (with `role`, `contributionDescription`, `startDate`, `endDate`). Check that the usages in `lib/types.ts` and `lib/queries.ts` still compile ŌĆö the `HypercertContributionData` type alias may need its `value` type updated.

### Step 3: Fix any type mismatches
After migration, run `npx tsc --noEmit` and fix any type errors caused by schema differences between the old local types and the new package types. Common issues:
- `workScope` changed from `string` to a union type
- `workTimeFrameFrom`/`workTimeFrameTo` renamed to `startDate`/`endDate`
- `contributions` (old StrongRef[]) vs `contributors` (new inline Contributor[])
- `location` (singular) vs `locations` (plural array)
- `evidence` field removed from activity (evidence is now via attachments)

For each breaking change, update the consuming code to handle both old and new field names where possible, or update to the new names if the old ones are unused.

### Step 4: Verify
```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run build
```

## Don't
- Delete the local `lexicons/` directory yet (other tasks may still reference it during transition; a separate cleanup task can handle removal)
- Upgrade `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` (only upgrade the lexicon package)
- Change any component rendering logic (only imports and type references)
- Break existing functionality ŌĆö if a type mismatch is too complex, add a `// TODO: update when SDK catches up` comment and use type assertion
acceptance_criteria:
1. `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` is at version `0.10.0-beta.15` in package.json
2. All 5 files (`lib/utils.ts`, `lib/queries.ts`, `lib/types.ts`, `components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx`, `components/blob-display.tsx`) import from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` or `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` instead of `@/lexicons/types/...`
3. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes (or only has pre-existing errors unrelated to this change)
4. `npm run build` passes
5. The `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Contributor` type is available via the upgraded package
6. No runtime behavior changes ŌĆö this is a pure import migration
close_reason: 217f5a5 upgrade lexicon to beta.15 and migrate local imports

title: Fix: non-DID contributors hidden during DID profile loading (from hypercerts-mrb.5)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.5 found: In hypercert-contributors-section.tsx line 62, the entire content block is gated on `!isLoading`. When a list has both DID and non-DID contributors, the non-DID contributors (which need no async resolution) are hidden until all DID profile queries settle. This causes an unnecessary blank period for mixed lists. Fix: render non-DID contributors immediately; only show skeletons in place of DID contributors that are still loading. Evidence: lines 53-83 of components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx.

title: Fix: missing error state in HypercertContributorsSection (from hypercerts-mrb.5)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.5 found: hypercert-contributors-section.tsx has no error handling UI. The measurements section (hypercert-measurements-section.tsx lines 87-93) and evidence section (hypercert-evidence-section.tsx lines 82-88) both render a red error panel when queries fail. The contributors section silently swallows profile-resolution failures (by design in the hook), but if parseContributors throws or the component itself errors, there is no fallback. At minimum, add a consistent error state matching the other sections for parity. Evidence: compare hypercert-contributors-section.tsx (no error branch) vs hypercert-measurements-section.tsx lines 87-93.

title: Fix: Authenticate page H1 text invisible (white on light background) from hypercerts-nyk.10
description: Review of hypercerts-nyk.10 found: The commit changed 'main.flex.flex-col.items-center > h1.text-primary' color to #ffffff (white). This selector targets the Authenticate page, which has a light background (#fafafa). The H1 'Authenticate' text is now white on a light background ŌĆö invisible to users. The spec intended to make text white only for the dark left panel on the sign-in page, but the selector used targets a different page entirely. Fix: either (a) remove the color override for this selector (the scoped .md:\\:bg-slate-100 .text-primary rule at line ~300 already handles the sign-in left panel), or (b) add a dark background to the Authenticate page main element. Evidence: git show 7669337 -- lib/atproto-branding.ts shows the change at line ~209-210. The Authenticate page background is #fafafa (line ~274, 282, 289 of atproto-branding.ts). File to fix: lib/atproto-branding.ts in branch karma/hyper-83-use-the-certified-branding-in-the-scaffold-app.

title: Fix: Stale JSDoc comments say 'rpc' scope is included but it isn't (from hypercerts-02l)
description:
Review of the OAuth scope migration (quick-fixes-test branch, epic hypercerts-02l) found stale JSDoc comments in lib/config.ts. Two comments still mention 'rpc' as part of the granular scope, but RPC_SCOPE was correctly removed from GRANULAR_SCOPE (per task hypercerts-02l.4). The comments are now misleading and will confuse future maintainers.

Evidence:
- Line 76: '* - Production: uses granular scopes (repo, rpc, blob) for precise permission requests'
- Line 201: '* - Production: granular scopes (repo, rpc, blob) for precise permission requests'

Actual GRANULAR_SCOPE (lines 38-42) only contains: ATPROTO_SCOPE, HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE ŌĆö no rpc: scope.

Fix: Update both comments to remove 'rpc' from the list:
  '* - Production: uses granular scopes (repo, blob) for precise permission requests'
close_reason: 510d690 cancelled: RPC_SCOPE being added back, JSDoc mentioning rpc is now correct

title: Fix: Contributors section count shows displayContributors.length before resolution ŌĆö may show wrong count during Phase 1 (hypercert-contributors-section.tsx)
description:
## File components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 76-78 ## Severity LOW ŌĆö UX ## Problem The contributor count in the section header always shows displayContributors.length (the raw parsed count before StrongRef resolution). This is correct for the final count, but during Phase 1 resolution, some contributors may have needsResolution=true and their identity is a placeholder AT URI, not a real DID. The count is accurate but the label may be confusing if some contributors fail to resolve (they would still be counted but shown with a placeholder identity). More importantly, the count uses displayContributors.length (pre-resolution) while the rendered list uses resolvedContributors (post-resolution). If resolution fails for some contributors, the count and list length will match, but the displayed identities may be AT URIs instead of DIDs. ## Evidence ```tsx

{displayContributors.length}{' '} {displayContributors.length === 1 ? 'contributor' : 'contributors'}

``` ## Fix Use resolvedContributors.length for the count (post-resolution), or add a loading indicator next to the count during resolution: ```tsx

{isResolvingIdentities ? '...' : resolvedContributors.length}{' '} {resolvedContributors.length === 1 ? 'contributor' : 'contributors'}

```

title: Epic: Add ATProto OAuth CSS branding to scaffold
description: Port the custom OAuth branding implementation from maearth-app to hypercerts-scaffold. The scaffold uses the same PDS (certified.app) but currently serves bare client metadata with no branding. Goal: when users authenticate via the scaffold in production mode, the PDS OAuth pages show custom branding (logo, colors, 'Sign in with Certified' imagery) instead of default Hypercerts styling. The PDS itself stays neutral ŌĆö each app injects its own branding via CSS in client metadata. Loopback mode cannot support branding (ATProto spec limitation) so branding only applies in production. Scope: create branding CSS module, update metadata endpoint, add placeholder assets. Out of scope: changing token_endpoint_auth_method, modifying the PDS itself.

title: Fix dark-on-dark text contrast in PDS sign-in dark panel
description:
## Files
- lib/atproto-branding.ts (modify)

## Context
The PDS sign-in page left panel background was changed to dark navy (`#0F2544`) in task .10. There is a small subtitle/label text right underneath the H1 heading (e.g. 'Enter your password' or 'Enter your identifier'). This text uses the class `text-slate-600` which the branding CSS globally overrides to `#4a4b53` (dark gray) at line ~327-334. On the dark navy panel, this dark text is invisible.

The form fields, inputs, and buttons in the right panel are fine ŌĆö this is ONLY about the subtitle text in the dark left panel.

## What to do
Add a scoped CSS override for the subtitle text inside the dark left panel container. The left panel container is `.md\\:bg-slate-100` on desktop.

Add this rule right after the existing scoped text override block (after line ~303):

```css
/* Subtitle text in dark left panel (e.g. 'Enter your password') */
.md\\:bg-slate-100 .text-slate-600,
.md\\:bg-slate-100 .text-slate-700,
.md\\:bg-slate-100 .text-slate-400,
.md\\:bg-slate-100 p {
  color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7) !important;
}
```

Use `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7)` (70% white) so it reads as a muted subtitle, not as prominent as the H1 heading which is full `#ffffff`.

Also add the mobile equivalent inside the existing `@media (max-width: 767px)` block, scoped to `.grid.grow.content-center`:

```css
.grid.grow.content-center .text-slate-600,
.grid.grow.content-center .text-slate-700,
.grid.grow.content-center .text-slate-400,
.grid.grow.content-center p {
  color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7) !important;
}
```

## Don't
- Change any global text color rules (lines ~319-334 must stay as-is)
- Change the right panel (form area) styling
- Change button colors or form input colors
- Modify the dark panel background color (`#0F2544`)
- Add overrides for form elements, inputs, or anything in the right panel
- Touch any files other than lib/atproto-branding.ts
acceptance_criteria:
1. The subtitle text (e.g. 'Enter your password') below the H1 in the dark left panel is legible ŌĆö light colored (rgba white ~70%)
2. The H1 heading itself remains full white (#ffffff)

4. Global text color rules at lines ~319-334 are NOT modified
5. Mobile layout subtitle text in the dark heading area is also light

title: Retheme PDS branding CSS to flow with PDS design system instead of fighting it
description:
## Files
- lib/atproto-branding.ts (modify)

## Context
The current branding CSS forces `color-scheme: light only` and overrides nearly every color class with hardcoded values and `!important`. This fights the PDS's own design system and causes issues like dark-on-dark text when we set the left panel to a dark color.

The PDS layout uses hardcoded Tailwind `slate-*` classes (NOT the `--color-contrast-*` scale) for the sign-in page. The left panel is `md:bg-slate-100` (`#f1f5f9`), the right panel inherits `bg-white`. The `--branding-color-primary` variable controls `text-primary` (H1 title), `bg-primary` (buttons), and the contrast scale hue for nav/footer/cards.

## What to do
Refactor `generateBrandingCss()` to work WITH the PDS instead of against it. The goal: set the right CSS variables so the PDS derives a navy-blue-tinted theme naturally, then only add targeted overrides for things the PDS can't do on its own.

### 1. Set branding CSS variables to navy theme
Replace the current `:root` block (lines ~68-83) with:
```css
:root {
  --branding-color-primary: 15 37 68;
  --branding-color-primary-contrast: 255 255 255;
  --branding-color-primary-hue: 216;
  --branding-color-error: 229 72 77;
  --branding-color-error-contrast: 255 255 255;
  --branding-color-error-hue: 358;
  --branding-color-warning: 245 159 0;
  --branding-color-warning-contrast: 43 45 49;
  --branding-color-warning-hue: 38;
  --branding-color-success: 43 138 62;
  --branding-color-success-contrast: 255 255 255;
  --branding-color-success-hue: 134;
}
```
This makes:
- `text-primary` / `bg-primary` = dark navy (`#0F2544`)
- `text-primary-contrast` = white
- The contrast scale gets a subtle navy-blue tint (hue 216)
- Buttons become navy instead of charcoal

### 2. Remove `color-scheme: light only !important`
Delete the `color-scheme` forcing. Let the PDS respect the user's system preference or its own defaults.

### 3. Left panel dark override (KEEP)
Keep the `md:bg-slate-100` override to `#0F2544` ŌĆö this is necessary because the PDS hardcodes `slate-100` for the left panel.

### 4. Left panel text overrides (KEEP)
Keep the scoped white text rules for `.md\\:bg-slate-100` ŌĆö these are necessary for the dark panel.

### 5. REMOVE most global color overrides
Delete the following sections that fight the PDS's native styling:
- `/* FORCE LIGHT COLOR SCHEME */` ŌĆö remove `color-scheme` forcing
- `/* MAIN BACKGROUNDS */` ŌĆö remove body, `.bg-white`, `.dark .bg-white` overrides. Let the PDS handle these.
- `/* TEXT COLORS - LIGHT THEME */` ŌĆö remove `.text-slate-900`, `.text-slate-600`, etc. overrides. The PDS's own Tailwind slate colors are fine.
- `/* ACCENT/PRIMARY COLORS */` ŌĆö remove `.text-primary`, `.bg-primary`, `.border-primary` overrides. These now come from the CSS variables.
- `/* FORM INPUTS */` ŌĆö remove input/textarea/select overrides. Let PDS style them.
- `/* USERNAME PREVIEW & PASSWORD STRENGTH */` ŌĆö remove `.bg-gray-200`, `.bg-gray-300`, text-gray-* overrides.
- `/* ACCOUNT SELECTION & FORM ITEMS */` ŌĆö remove `.bg-gray-100` overrides.
- `/* CHECKBOXES */` ŌĆö remove. PDS handles these via `accent-color` from primary.
- `/* INFO/WARNING/ERROR BOXES */` ŌĆö remove `.bg-contrast-*` overrides. The contrast scale now has the right hue.
- `/* BUTTONS */` ŌĆö remove the generic button color overrides (`.bg-primary`, hover states). Keep ONLY the button text/icon replacement rules (the `::before` and `::after` for 'Sign in with Certified').
- `/* LINKS */` ŌĆö remove. Let PDS handle link colors.
- `/* FORM TEXT ELEMENTS */` ŌĆö remove.
- `/* DROPDOWN/SELECT */` ŌĆö remove.
- `/* ERROR PAGE */` ŌĆö remove `.text-4xl`, `.text-5xl` overrides.
- `/* Input container */` overrides ŌĆö remove all the `.rounded-lg` input container styling.
- `/* Text that should be dark on light backgrounds */` ŌĆö remove the `.text-white` override (line ~444) which forces `.text-white` to dark. This was needed when forcing light mode but is harmful now.

### 6. KEEP these sections
- Logo replacement (`img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"]`) ŌĆö PDS can't do this natively
- H1 text replacement with `certified-signin.svg` ŌĆö PDS can't do this
- Button text/icon replacement (`::before`/`::after`) ŌĆö PDS can't do this
- Left panel dark background + scoped light text
- Mobile dark heading area
- Authenticate page logo and button replacements
- Error page logo suppression
- Desktop/mobile alignment rules

### 7. Secondary button overrides for the submit button
The submit button `button[type="submit"]` styling can be simplified. Since `bg-primary` now resolves to navy via the CSS variable, the button will naturally be navy. Keep only the text/icon replacement (`font-size: 0`, `::before`, `::after`).

### 8. Right panel lighter shade
The right panel (`bg-white`) will stay white by default. If you want it to be a lighter shade of navy instead of pure white, add:
```css
.bg-white,
.dark .bg-white {
  background-color: hsl(216 20% 97%) !important;
}
```
This gives a very subtle navy-tinted off-white that flows with the left panel. But this is optional ŌĆö pure white also works.

## Don't
- Remove the logo/image replacement CSS
- Remove the button text replacement CSS
- Remove the left panel dark background override
- Change the SVG asset URLs
- Touch any files other than lib/atproto-branding.ts
acceptance_criteria:
1. The `:root` block sets `--branding-color-primary: 15 37 68` and `--branding-color-primary-hue: 216`
2. No `color-scheme: light only` forcing remains
3. The left panel is still dark navy (`#0F2544`) with white text
4. The right panel uses the PDS's native light styling (no hardcoded overrides)
5. Buttons are navy (derived from primary variable) with the C logo + 'Sign in with Certified' text
6. The H1 still shows `certified-signin.svg` image
7. The horizontal logo still shows above the H1 with `filter: invert(1)`
8. Form inputs, text, links on the right panel look correct (PDS native styling)
9. The generated CSS is significantly shorter than before (most global overrides removed)
10. TypeScript compiles without errors (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
close_reason: ce6a9cf refactor: retheme PDS branding CSS to flow with PDS design system

title: Clean up PDS branding CSS: fix broad selector, remove dead code, consolidate dark mode blocks
description:
## Files
- lib/atproto-branding.ts (modify)

## What to do
Address 6 CSS quality issues found during review. Bundle them into a single cleanup pass.

### 1. Fix overly broad `img[alt*="Logo"]` selector (P3 ŌĆö hypercerts-5jk)
The substring match `alt*="Logo"` could accidentally affect any image with 'Logo' in its alt text on PDS pages. Change to exact match only:
```css
/* BEFORE */
img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"],
img[alt*="Logo"] {

/* AFTER */
img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"] {
```
Remove the `img[alt*="Logo"]` selector everywhere it appears (base rule, mobile rule, and the dark mode `filter: invert` rule). The exact `img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"]` match is sufficient.

### 2. Remove dead `color: #ffffff` on `::after` (P3 ŌĆö hypercerts-jgh)
The `.grid h1.text-primary::after` has `color: #ffffff !important` but `content: ""` ŌĆö no text to color. Remove the dead property:
```css
.grid h1.text-primary::after {
  content: "" !important;
  /* ... */
  /* color: #ffffff !important;  <-- DELETE */
}
```

### 3. Consolidate dark mode blocks (P4 ŌĆö hypercerts-c3t)
There are 5 scattered `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks throughout the file. Merge them into a single block at the end of the CSS (before the closing backtick). This makes dark mode behavior easy to find and maintain.

The consolidated block should contain ALL dark-mode-only rules:
- Left panel background override (`#0F2544`)
- Left panel white text overrides
- Left panel subtitle text overrides
- Logo `filter: invert(1)`
- Authenticate page logo `filter: invert(1)`
- Authenticate page H1 `color: #ffffff`
- H1 `certified-signin.svg` (dark variant) override
- Button bright blue override (`#3B82F6`)
- Mobile dark heading (combined with `max-width: 767px`)

### 4. Remove unnecessary `position: relative` (P4 ŌĆö hypercerts-84k)
The `main.flex.flex-col.items-center > h1.text-primary { position: relative !important; }` rule is unnecessary ŌĆö the `::before` pseudo-element is block-flow, not absolutely positioned. Remove it.

### 5. Remove redundant `width: 32px` override (P4 ŌĆö hypercerts-ua9)
The `div.w-8` container is already 32px in Tailwind. The override `width: 32px !important` is a no-op. Also remove the dead `object-position: left center` on `img[alt="Ma Earth"]` (32x32 square, object-position has no effect with `object-fit: contain`).

### 6. Remove redundant mobile `background-position` (P4 ŌĆö hypercerts-c7v)
The mobile override repeats `background-position: left center` which is already set in the base rule. Remove the redundant line from the mobile media query.

## Don't
- Change any functional behavior (colors, sizes, images, selectors that work correctly)
- Change the `:root` CSS variables
- Change SVG asset URLs
- Reorder rules in a way that changes specificity/cascade behavior
- Touch any files other than lib/atproto-branding.ts
acceptance_criteria:
1. No `img[alt*="Logo"]` substring selector remains ŌĆö only exact `img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"]`
2. No dead `color` property on empty `content: ""` pseudo-elements
3. At most 2 `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks in the entire CSS (one for general dark mode, one combined with max-width for mobile)
4. No `position: relative` on the Authenticate page H1
5. No redundant `width: 32px` on `div.w-8` container
6. No redundant `background-position` in mobile override
7. All existing visual behavior is preserved (light mode and dark mode look identical before and after)
8. TypeScript compiles without errors (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
close_reason: 0aeb7a0 Clean up PDS branding CSS: fix broad selector, remove dead code, consolidate dark mode blocks

title: Remove ngrok workaround from DEVELOPMENT.md and clarify certified PDS + trusted client requirement for branding
description:
## Files
- DEVELOPMENT.md (modify)

## What to do
Rewrite the 'Testing OAuth Branding Locally' section (lines 341-399) and remove the 'ngrok Issues' troubleshooting section (lines 412-419).

### 1. Remove the ngrok workaround entirely
Delete the entire 'The ngrok workaround:' subsection (lines 351-385). This includes:
- The ngrok install instructions
- The 6-step ngrok tunnel setup
- The paragraph about verifying metadata via ngrok URL
- The 'Important:' paragraph about PDS not rendering branding for untrusted ngrok URLs
- The 'Note:' about CORS/allowedDevOrigins for ngrok

### 2. Remove the 'ngrok Issues' troubleshooting section
Delete lines 412-419 (the entire 'ngrok Issues' subsection under 'Common Issues & Troubleshooting'). Since we're removing the ngrok workaround, this troubleshooting section is no longer relevant.

### 3. Rewrite the section header and intro
Replace the current 'Testing OAuth Branding Locally' header and intro with a section titled 'OAuth Branding' that clearly states:

**OAuth branding (custom logo, colors, CSS on the PDS sign-in pages) only works when BOTH conditions are met:**

1. **The PDS is a certified PDS** ŌĆö branding is a feature of certified PDS instances (e.g. `pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`). Standard/uncertified PDS instances do not support custom OAuth branding.
2. **Your app's client URL has been added to the PDS's trusted OAuth clients list** ŌĆö the PDS only applies branding CSS for client URLs registered in its `PDS_OAUTH_TRUSTED_CLIENTS` environment variable. Even if your app serves correct branding metadata at `/client-metadata.json`, the PDS will ignore it unless your URL is explicitly trusted.

### 4. Keep the loopback limitation explanation
Keep the existing paragraph about ATProto loopback mode limitation (line 347-349) ŌĆö it's still correct. Reword slightly to fit the new section structure:

'Additionally, branding cannot work in local development (loopback mode). When using a loopback `client_id` (localhost/127.0.0.1), the PDS auto-generates minimal client metadata and ignores custom branding fields. This is part of the ATProto specification.'

### 5. Keep 'Getting your domain trusted by the PDS' subsection
Keep lines 387-395 as-is. This is still the correct process.

### 6. Simplify the Vercel deployments note
Keep the Vercel note (lines 397-399) but simplify it:

'**Vercel deployments:** Your Vercel production URL must also be added to the PDS trusted clients list for branding to appear. The app serves branding metadata automatically ŌĆö the only requirement is that the URL is trusted by the PDS.'

### Final structure should be:
```
### OAuth Branding

<two conditions paragraph>

<loopback limitation paragraph>

#### Getting your domain trusted by the PDS
<existing content>

**Vercel deployments:**
<simplified note>
```

## Don't
- Modify any code files
- Remove the 'Getting your domain trusted by the PDS' subsection
- Change the loopback limitation explanation (just reword to fit)
- Add any new ngrok instructions or references
acceptance_criteria:
1. The ngrok workaround section (install, tunnel setup, 6 steps) is completely removed from DEVELOPMENT.md
2. The 'ngrok Issues' troubleshooting section is completely removed
3. The section clearly states branding requires BOTH: (a) a certified PDS and (b) the app URL being in the trusted OAuth clients list
4. The ATProto loopback limitation is still explained
5. The 'Getting your domain trusted by the PDS' subsection is preserved
6. The Vercel note is simplified but present
7. No references to ngrok remain anywhere in the file
8. No code files are modified
9. The markdown renders correctly (no broken formatting)
close_reason: 9836495 docs: remove ngrok workaround, clarify certified PDS + trusted client requirement

title: Fix: Remove Ma Earth CSS selectors from atproto-branding.ts (copy-paste artifact)
description:
Review of the scaffold codebase found Ma Earth references in lib/atproto-branding.ts ŌĆö a copy-paste artifact from the maearth-app project.

## Findings

All 4 references are in `lib/atproto-branding.ts`:

- **Line 112** (comment): `/* The small logo on the Authorize page has alt="Ma Earth" and is in a constrained container */`
- **Line 113** (functional CSS selector): `img[alt="Ma Earth"] {`
- **Line 121** (functional CSS selector): `.flex.items-center.justify-start.gap-2 > div.w-8:has(img[alt="Ma Earth"]),`
- **Line 122** (functional CSS selector): `div.w-8:has(img[alt="Ma Earth"]) {`

Lines 113, 121, and 122 are **functional code** ŌĆö CSS selectors that target an image with `alt="Ma Earth"`. In this scaffold, the authorize-page logo will have a different alt text, so these selectors are dead code that will never match. The comment on line 112 also incorrectly references Ma Earth.

## Fix

Replace the `alt="Ma Earth"` selectors with a generic selector that matches any small logo image on the Authorize page, or use the actual alt text that the scaffold's PDS OAuth page renders (e.g., `img[alt*="Logo"]` or `img[alt="Hypercerts"]`). Update the comment accordingly.

The block spans lines 111ŌĆō125 of `lib/atproto-branding.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `rg -i 'ma.?earth|maearth|ma_earth' lib/atproto-branding.ts` returns no matches
2. The CSS block that previously targeted `img[alt="Ma Earth"]` is replaced with a generic or scaffold-appropriate selector
3. The comment on line 112 no longer mentions Ma Earth
4. The file still compiles without TypeScript errors (`pnpm tsc --noEmit`)
5. The branding CSS still covers the Authorize page small logo use-case (selector targets something meaningful)
close_reason: 66e4025 Fix already applied in nyk.17: removed Ma Earth CSS selectors, replaced with img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"] selector; all acceptance criteria pass

title: Add buildClientMetadata function to lib/config.ts
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)

## What to do
Add a `buildClientMetadata()` function to `lib/config.ts` that returns the full OAuth client metadata object. This function replaces the inline object currently in `app/client-metadata.json/route.ts`.

**Why:** The metadata endpoint currently builds its response inline. We need a reusable function that conditionally includes branding CSS in production mode.

**Function signature:** `export function buildClientMetadata(): Record<string, unknown>`

**Behavior:**
1. If `config.isLoopback` is true (local dev), return metadata WITHOUT branding:
   ```
   {
     client_id: config.clientId,
     client_name: "Hypercerts Scaffold",
     client_uri: config.baseUrl,
     redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
     scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
     logo_uri: `${config.baseUrl}/favicon.ico`,
     grant_types: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
     response_types: ["code"],
     token_endpoint_auth_method: "none",
     application_type: "native",
     dpop_bound_access_tokens: true,
   }
   ```

2. If NOT loopback (production/preview), return metadata WITH branding:
   ```
   {
     client_id: config.clientId,
     client_name: "Hypercerts Scaffold",
     client_uri: config.baseUrl,
     redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
     scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
     logo_uri: `${config.baseUrl}/favicon.ico`,
     grant_types: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
     response_types: ["code"],
     token_endpoint_auth_method: "none",
     application_type: "web",
     dpop_bound_access_tokens: true,
     branding: {
       css: generateBrandingCss(config.baseUrl),
     },
   }
   ```

**Important:** Keep `token_endpoint_auth_method: "none"` in BOTH modes. Upgrading to `private_key_jwt` is a separate concern and out of scope for this task.

**Import:** Add `import { generateBrandingCss } from "./atproto-branding";` at the top of the file.

**Placement:** Add the function after the `config` export (around line 204), before the env var validation block.

## Dont
- Do not change token_endpoint_auth_method (keep "none" in both modes)
- Do not modify the config object itself
- Do not modify the metadata route file (separate task)
- Do not change any existing functions (isLoopback, getBaseUrl, etc.)
- Do not remove any existing exports
acceptance_criteria:
1. buildClientMetadata is exported from lib/config.ts
2. Function returns object with all required OAuth metadata fields
3. In loopback mode: no branding field, application_type is "native"
4. In non-loopback mode: branding.css field is present with CSS string from generateBrandingCss, application_type is "web"
5. token_endpoint_auth_method is "none" in both modes
6. Import of generateBrandingCss is added
7. Existing config export and all other functions are unchanged
8. TypeScript compiles without errors
close_reason: 8fda308 Add buildClientMetadata function with conditional branding

title: Update client-metadata.json route to use buildClientMetadata
description:
## Files
- app/client-metadata.json/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Replace the inline metadata object in the route handler with a call to `buildClientMetadata()`.

**Current code (replace entirely):**
```typescript
import { config, OAUTH_SCOPE } from "@/lib/config";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export async function GET() {
  return NextResponse.json({
    client_id: config.clientId,
    client_name: "Hypercert Scaffold",
    client_uri: config.baseUrl,
    redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
    scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
    logo_uri:`${config.baseUrl}/favicon.ico`,
    grant_types: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
    response_types: ["code"],
    token_endpoint_auth_method: "none",
    application_type: config.isLoopback ? "native" : "web",
    dpop_bound_access_tokens: true,
  });
}
```

**New code:**
```typescript
import { buildClientMetadata } from "@/lib/config";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

/**
 * OAuth Client Metadata Endpoint
 *
 * Returns OAuth client metadata according to RFC 7591.
 * In production mode, includes custom branding CSS for PDS OAuth pages.
 * In loopback mode, returns minimal metadata (PDS ignores it anyway).
 *
 * @see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591
 */
export async function GET() {
  return NextResponse.json(buildClientMetadata());
}
```

## Dont
- Do not add any logic to this file ŌĆö all logic lives in buildClientMetadata
- Do not import config or OAUTH_SCOPE directly (buildClientMetadata handles it)
- Do not add error handling (the metadata is static/deterministic)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Route file imports buildClientMetadata from @/lib/config
2. Route file does NOT import config or OAUTH_SCOPE directly
3. GET handler returns NextResponse.json(buildClientMetadata())
4. No inline metadata object remains
5. JSDoc comment explains production vs loopback behavior
6. TypeScript compiles without errors
7. Visiting /client-metadata.json returns valid JSON with all required OAuth fields
close_reason: 56cd67e refactor: update client-metadata.json route to use buildClientMetadata

title: Document ngrok setup for local branding testing
description:
## Files
- DEVELOPMENT.md (modify)

## What to do
Add a section to DEVELOPMENT.md documenting how to test OAuth branding locally using ngrok.

**Context:** ATProto OAuth loopback mode (localhost) cannot support branding CSS ŌĆö the PDS auto-generates minimal metadata and ignores custom fields. To test branding locally, developers need a public HTTPS URL via a tunnel service.

**Section to add (under a new heading "Testing OAuth Branding Locally"):**

1. Explain WHY branding does not appear in local dev (ATProto spec limitation ŌĆö loopback client_id only supports scope and redirect_uri params, PDS auto-generates metadata)
2. Explain the ngrok workaround:
   - Install ngrok: `npm install -g ngrok` or download from ngrok.com
   - Start tunnel: `ngrok http 3000`
   - Copy the HTTPS URL (e.g. `https://abc123.ngrok-free.app`)
   - Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://abc123.ngrok-free.app` in `.env.local`
   - Restart dev server
   - The app now enters production mode and serves full metadata with branding at `/client-metadata.json`
3. Note that `next.config.ts` may need `allowedDevOrigins` configured for ngrok domains (reference existing task hypercerts-scaffold-r25 if it exists)
4. Note that branding is automatically active on Vercel preview/production deployments ŌĆö no ngrok needed there

**Placement:** Add after the existing OAuth/authentication section in DEVELOPMENT.md. If no such section exists, add near the end before any appendix/reference sections.

## Dont
- Do not modify any code files
- Do not add ngrok as a dependency
- Do not add NGROK_DOMAIN to .env.example (optional, not required)
acceptance_criteria:
1. DEVELOPMENT.md contains a new section about testing OAuth branding locally
2. Section explains the ATProto loopback limitation (why branding does not appear locally)
3. Section includes step-by-step ngrok instructions
4. Section mentions Vercel preview deployments work automatically
5. No code files are modified
close_reason: 76835d7 Document ngrok setup for OAuth branding testing

title: Update DEVELOPMENT.md to clarify PDS trusted client requirement for branding
description:
## Files
- DEVELOPMENT.md (modify)

## What to do
Update the "Testing OAuth Branding Locally" section (around line 341) to clarify that branding CSS is only applied by the PDS for trusted client URLs.

**Key corrections:**

1. **Add a note at the top of the section** explaining that the PDS (`pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`) only applies branding CSS for client URLs registered in its `PDS_OAUTH_TRUSTED_CLIENTS` environment variable. Random ngrok URLs or unregistered Vercel preview URLs will NOT show branding even though the metadata is served correctly.

2. **Update the ngrok instructions** to clarify that:
   - Ngrok is still useful for testing that the metadata endpoint serves branding CSS correctly (you can verify by visiting `/client-metadata.json` and checking the `branding` field)
   - However, the PDS will NOT render the branding on its OAuth pages unless the ngrok URL is added to `PDS_OAUTH_TRUSTED_CLIENTS`

3. **Add a subsection "Getting your domain trusted by the PDS"** explaining:
   - To get a domain added to the PDS trusted clients list, contact @aspiers on GitHub
   - Provide the full client_id URL (e.g. `https://your-app.vercel.app/client-metadata.json`)
   - Production/stable URLs are preferred over ephemeral ngrok URLs

4. **Update the Vercel section** to note that Vercel preview/production URLs also need to be in the trusted list for branding to appear on the PDS OAuth pages.

5. **Keep the existing content about the ATProto loopback limitation** ŌĆö that is still correct and separate from the trusted client issue.

## Dont
- Do not modify any code files
- Do not remove the existing loopback limitation explanation (it is still correct)
- Do not change the ngrok setup steps themselves (they are still valid for metadata testing)
- Do not add any PDS configuration details beyond what is described above

The SVG is at `/certified-logo.svg` (39x43px, blue checkmark + white partial circle on transparent background). Since the scaffold UI has a light background, the white paths in the SVG won't be visible. The blue checkmark (#60A1E2) will be visible.

title: Fix: spurious color: #ffffff added to .grid h1.text-primary::after (from hypercerts-nyk.8)
description: Review of hypercerts-nyk.8 found: commit 52ce32a added 'color: #ffffff !important;' to the .grid h1.text-primary::after pseudo-element. This property is meaningless since content is empty string (the pseudo-element only shows a background-image). It was not in the spec and violates the 'Don't: Change any color values' constraint. Evidence: git show 52ce32a -- lib/atproto-branding.ts shows the ::after block has 'color: #ffffff !important;' added. Low severity since it has no visual effect, but it is dead code that should be removed.

title: Nit: buildClientMetadata loopback comment misleadingly highlights application_type as the key difference
description:
Review of lib/config.ts found a misleading inline comment in buildClientMetadata.

Evidence:
- lib/config.ts line 263: '// Loopback mode: minimal metadata, application_type is "web"'
- lib/config.ts line 281: 'application_type: "web"' (loopback block)
- lib/config.ts line 303: 'application_type: "web"' (production block)

The comment implies application_type is the distinguishing characteristic of loopback mode, but application_type: 'web' is IDENTICAL in both the loopback and production blocks. The actual difference is that the production block includes 'branding: { css: generateBrandingCss(...) }' while the loopback block does not.

The JSDoc above the function correctly says 'In loopback mode, returns minimal metadata without branding' ŌĆö but the inline comment contradicts this by pointing to application_type instead.

Fix: Change line 263 to:
// Loopback mode: same metadata as production but without branding.css (PDS ignores branding for loopback clients)

title: Fix: DEVELOPMENT.md Key Files table references non-existent providers/OAuthProvider.tsx
description:
Review of DEVELOPMENT.md found a stale file reference in the Key Files table.

Evidence:

title: Epic: Migrate codebase to @hypercerts-org/lexicon v0.10.0 stable
description:
## Why
The project upgraded from @hypercerts-org/lexicon 0.10.0-beta.14 to 0.10.0 stable. This introduced several breaking changes:

1. **Renamed type namespaces**: OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment ŌåÆ OrgHypercertsContextAttachment, OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation ŌåÆ OrgHypercertsContextEvaluation, OrgHypercertsClaimMeasurement ŌåÆ OrgHypercertsContextMeasurement, OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails ŌåÆ OrgHypercertsClaimContribution
2. **Renamed collection NSIDs**: org.hypercerts.claim.attachment ŌåÆ org.hypercerts.context.attachment, org.hypercerts.claim.evaluation ŌåÆ org.hypercerts.context.evaluation, org.hypercerts.claim.measurement ŌåÆ org.hypercerts.context.measurement, org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails ŌåÆ org.hypercerts.claim.contribution
3. **New required fields**: AppCertifiedActorProfile.Record now requires createdAt
4. **Field type changes**: description field on activity records changed from string to PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main
5. **Record type changes**: putRecord/createRecord now require typed record objects instead of unknown

## Success Criteria
- npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero lexicon-related errors (ignore .next/ cache errors)
- All collection NSID strings match v0.10.0 schema
- All type imports resolve correctly
- Runtime validation (validateRecord, isRecord) works with new namespaces

## Scope
22 files across lib/, components/, app/api/, and app/hypercerts/ directories. ~30 TypeScript errors to fix.

## Key Constraints
- Do NOT change any business logic or behavior
- Do NOT change API route signatures or response shapes
- Preserve all existing runtime validation patterns
- Keep backward-compatible AT-URI parsing (existing records in repos still use old collection names)

title: Delete or replace stale local lexicons/ directory with v0.10.0 canonical types
description:
## Files
- lexicons/lexicons.ts (delete or replace)
- lexicons/types/ (delete entire directory)
- Any files importing from local lexicons/ instead of @hypercerts-org/lexicon

## What to do
The project has a local `lexicons/` directory with hand-written/generated lexicon schemas and TypeScript types that are **completely out of sync** with the installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` v0.10.0 package.

### Key divergences in local lexicons/:
1. `lexicons/lexicons.ts` defines `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity` with:
   - `workScope` as required string (v0.10.0: optional WorkScopeString|CEL union)
   - `workTimeFrameFrom`/`workTimeFrameTo` fields (v0.10.0: `startDate`/`endDate`)
   - Missing `contributors`, `locations`, `rights` fields
2. `lexicons/lexicons.ts` defines `OrgHypercertsClaimContribution` with:
   - `hypercert` (StrongRef), `contributors` (string[]), `description`, `workTimeframeFrom/To` fields
   - v0.10.0 has: `role`, `contributionDescription`, `startDate`, `endDate`
3. `lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity.ts` defines `workScope: string` (required)

### What to do:
1. Search for any imports from the local `lexicons/` directory (e.g., `from "@/lexicons/..."` or `from "../../lexicons/..."`)
2. If any files import from local lexicons, migrate them to import from `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` instead
3. Delete the entire `lexicons/` directory ŌĆö all types should come from the npm package
4. If the local lexicons are used for anything else (e.g., PDS lexicon registration), document that as a follow-up issue

### Investigation needed:
Run `grep -r "from.*lexicons/" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" lib/ app/ components/ providers/` to find any imports from the local directory.

## Dont
- Do NOT delete if the local lexicons are actively imported by source files ŌĆö migrate imports first
- Do NOT delete if the local lexicons serve a purpose beyond type definitions (e.g., PDS schema registration)
- If unsure, just add a TODO comment at the top of lexicons/lexicons.ts noting it is stale
acceptance_criteria: No source files in lib/, app/, components/, or providers/ import from the local lexicons/ directory. If lexicons/ is deleted, the project still compiles. If lexicons/ is kept, it has a clear TODO comment noting it is stale.

title: Add missing $type on contributor union sub-objects in addContribution
description:
## Files
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify) ŌĆö lines 193-196

## What to do
In v0.10.0, union fields on `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Contributor` require `$type` to be present on every value. The ATProto lexicon validator rejects objects without `$type` in union positions with: "must be an object which includes the \"\$type\" property".

### Current code (lines 193-196):
```ts
const newContributors = contributorRefs.map((ref) => ({
  contributorIdentity: ref,          // ref = { uri: string, cid: string } ŌĆö MISSING $type
  contributionDetails: detailsRef,   // detailsRef = { uri: string, cid: string } ŌĆö MISSING $type
}));
```

### Fix:
```ts
const newContributors = contributorRefs.map((ref) => ({
  contributorIdentity: { $type: "com.atproto.repo.strongRef" as const, ...ref },
  contributionDetails: { $type: "com.atproto.repo.strongRef" as const, ...detailsRef },
}));
```

### Why this matters:
The `Contributor` type in v0.10.0 defines these fields as unions:
```ts
contributorIdentity: $Typed<ContributorIdentity> | $Typed<ComAtprotoRepoStrongRef.Main> | { $type: string };
contributionDetails?: $Typed<ContributorRole> | $Typed<ComAtprotoRepoStrongRef.Main> | { $type: string };
```

Every branch requires `$type`. Without it, `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.validateRecord()` at line 202 will throw at runtime.

### Also check:
Are there any OTHER places in the codebase where contributor objects are constructed without `$type`? Search for `contributorIdentity` and `contributionDetails` assignments.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the contributor parsing/reading code in lib/contributor-utils.ts (reading is fine ŌĆö stored records already have $type)
- Do NOT change the Contributor type definition
- Do NOT change how refs are resolved (resolveStrongRef already returns {uri, cid})
acceptance_criteria: After this fix plus tasks .1 and .2, calling addContribution should pass OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.validateRecord without throwing. Verify by checking that the contributor objects include $type: "com.atproto.repo.strongRef".
close_reason: b311cb6 Add $type on contributor union sub-objects in addContribution

title: Fix description field type: string ŌåÆ PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument on activity records
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/route.ts (modify) ŌĆö line ~137 (POST handler) AND line ~241 (PUT handler)
- lib/api/types.ts (modify) ŌĆö CreateHypercertParams type if description is typed as string
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify) ŌĆö where description field is constructed
- components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx (modify) ŌĆö line ~229 where description is rendered (TS error: Main not assignable to ReactNode)
- components/hypercert-evidence-view.tsx (modify) ŌĆö line ~84 where evidence.description is rendered (will show [object Object] after task .1 fixes imports)

## What to do
In v0.10.0, the `description` field changed from `string` to `PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main` on BOTH:
- `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record.description`
- `OrgHypercertsContextAttachment.Main.description` (evidence/attachment records)

### Step 1: Understand the LinearDocument structure
Import `PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument` from `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` (it re-exports from @atcute/leaflet). The structure is:
```ts
interface Main {
  blocks: { block: Block; $type?: string }[];
  $type?: "pub.leaflet.pages.linearDocument";
}
```
Where Block can be various block types (paragraph, blockquote, etc.). For simple text, wrap in a paragraph block.

### Step 2: Create a shared helper for LinearDocument <-> string conversion
Create a utility (e.g., in lib/utils.ts or lib/linear-document.ts):
```ts
import type { PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument } from "@hypercerts-org/lexicon";

export function stringToLinearDocument(text: string): PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main {
  return {
    blocks: [{ block: { plaintext: text, $type: "pub.leaflet.blocks.paragraph" } }],
    $type: "pub.leaflet.pages.linearDocument",
  };
}

export function linearDocumentToString(doc: PubLeafletPagesLinearDocument.Main | undefined): string {
  if (!doc?.blocks) return "";
  return doc.blocks.map(b => (b.block as any)?.plaintext ?? "").filter(Boolean).join("\n");
}
```
Check the actual @atcute/leaflet block types to get the exact structure right.

### Step 3: Fix record construction in app/api/certs/route.ts POST handler
The line `description: hypercertParams.description` currently passes a string. Convert it:
```ts
description: hypercertParams.description ? stringToLinearDocument(hypercertParams.description) : undefined
```

### Step 4: Fix record construction in app/api/certs/route.ts PUT handler (~line 241)
The PUT handler spreads the existing record and merges updates. The `description` field from form data is a string, but the existing record has a LinearDocument. Ensure the update also converts:
```ts
...(updates.description ? { description: stringToLinearDocument(updates.description) } : {}),
```

### Step 5: Fix rendering in components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx
The description field is no longer a string, so rendering it directly as ReactNode fails. Use the helper:
```ts
const descriptionText = linearDocumentToString(record.description);
```
Then render `descriptionText` instead of `record.description`.

### Step 6: Fix rendering in components/hypercert-evidence-view.tsx
Same issue ŌĆö `evidence.description` is now a LinearDocument. After task .1 fixes the import, this will render [object Object]. Use the same helper:
```ts
const descriptionText = linearDocumentToString(evidence.description);
```

### Step 7: Fix the form construction
In hypercerts-base-form.tsx, if description is being set as a string for the API params, ensure the API types accept string (and the route converts it), OR convert at the form level.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the shortDescription field ŌĆö it remains a string
- Do NOT change the API request/response shape if possible ŌĆö convert at the server route level
- Do NOT remove description support ŌĆö just adapt the type
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -c "description\|linearDocument\|LinearDocument\|not assignable to type.*ReactNode\|not assignable to type.*blocks" should return 0. The description field compiles correctly in both record construction and rendering contexts.
close_reason: 8a07a8a Fix description field type: string ŌåÆ LinearDocument on activity/attachment records

## What to do

In the UPDATE branch, ensure `createdAt` is preserved from the existing record (it likely already is via spread `...existingProfile`). Verify this.

title: Fix putRecord/createRecord record parameter type errors (unknown ŌåÆ typed record)
description:
## Files
- lib/queries.ts (modify) ŌĆö lines 97, 110, 123
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify) ŌĆö lines 163, 280, 363, 567
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify) ŌĆö line 200

## What to do
In v0.10.0, the `record` parameter in `com.atproto.repo.createRecord` and `com.atproto.repo.putRecord` now expects `{ [x: string]: unknown }` instead of `unknown`. Several places pass `unknown` typed values.

The TypeScript errors are all variants of:
```
error TS2322: Type "unknown" is not assignable to type "{ [x: string]: unknown; }"
```

### Root cause
These errors occur where records are passed through generic functions or where `assertValidRecord` narrows the type but the variable was originally typed as `unknown`.

### Fix approach
For each error site, add an explicit type assertion `as Record<string, unknown>` or better, type the variable correctly from the start:

1. **lib/queries.ts lines 97, 110, 123**: The `createEvaluation`, `createEvidence`, `createLocation` functions pass `record` to `createRecord`. Ensure the `record` parameter is typed as the specific `.Record` type (which extends `{ [x: string]: unknown }`).

2. **lib/create-actions.ts lines 163, 280, 363, 567**: These are in `addContribution`, `addEvaluation`, `addMeasurement`, `updateMeasurement`. The record objects should already be typed correctly after construction. Check if the issue is that `assertValidRecord` narrows to `T` but `T` doesnt satisfy `{ [x: string]: unknown }`. If so, add `as Record<string, unknown>` at the createRecord/putRecord call site, OR adjust the assertValidRecord return type.

3. **app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts line 200**: Same pattern ŌĆö the record is typed but the createRecord call doesnt accept it.

### Preferred fix
The cleanest fix is to update `assertValidRecord` in `lib/record-validation.ts` to narrow to `T & Record<string, unknown>` instead of just `T`. This would fix all call sites at once:
```ts
export function assertValidRecord<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
  label: string,
  record: unknown,
  validateFn: (v: unknown) => ValidationResult<T>,
): asserts record is T {
```

If that causes cascading type issues, fall back to adding `as Record<string, unknown>` at each createRecord/putRecord call site.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the actual record data being passed
- Do NOT remove validation calls
- Do NOT change the createRecord/putRecord API signatures
- Prefer fixing the root cause (assertValidRecord signature) over per-site casts

title: Fix hypercert-evidence-view.tsx implicit any parameters and content rendering

Two TypeScript errors in this file:

```
error TS7006: Parameter "contentItem" implicitly has an "any" type.
error TS7006: Parameter "index" implicitly has an "any" type.

The `content` field on `OrgHypercertsContextAttachment.Main` (formerly OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment.Main) is now typed as `($Typed<Uri> | $Typed<SmallBlob>)[] | undefined`. The map callback needs explicit types.

evidence.content?.map((contentItem: $Typed<OrgHypercertsDefs.Uri> | $Typed<OrgHypercertsDefs.SmallBlob>, index: number) => {
```

Or use the simpler approach ŌĆö since the array type is known, just annotate the parameters:
```ts
evidence.content?.map((contentItem, index: number) => {
```

title: Update measurement record: subject ŌåÆ subjects (singular to array)
description:
## Files
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify) ŌĆö lines 336-338 (addMeasurement), lines 530-540 (updateMeasurement)
- components/measurement-form.tsx (modify) ŌĆö if it references subject field

## What to do
In v0.10.0, `OrgHypercertsContextMeasurement.Record` changed `subject` (singular StrongRef) to `subjects` (optional array of StrongRefs).

### In lib/create-actions.ts addMeasurement (~line 336):
Current code:
```ts
const record: OrgHypercertsClaimMeasurement.Record = {
  $type: "org.hypercerts.claim.measurement",
  subject,  // singular StrongRef
  ...
};
```

Change to:
```ts
const record: OrgHypercertsContextMeasurement.Record = {
  $type: "org.hypercerts.context.measurement",
  subjects: [subject],  // wrap in array
  ...
};
```

### In lib/create-actions.ts updateMeasurement (~line 530-540):
Check if `subject` is read from existing record and update accordingly. The existing record may have `subject` (old) or `subjects` (new). Handle both:
```ts
// Preserve existing subjects, or migrate from old subject field
const existingSubjects = existing.subjects ?? (existing.subject ? [existing.subject] : undefined);
```

### In components/measurement-form.tsx:
Check if the form references `subject` field. The example AT-URI placeholder on line 154 uses `org.hypercerts.claim.evidence` which is also outdated ŌĆö but thats just placeholder text, low priority.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the addMeasurement function signature (params.subject stays singular ŌĆö the wrapping happens internally)
- Do NOT break backward compatibility with existing measurement records that have singular subject
- Do NOT change the measurement form UI
