title: Update DEVELOPMENT.md ŌĆö remove SDK vendor workflow, update architecture references
description:
## Files
- DEVELOPMENT.md (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Replace the "CRITICAL: Unreleased SDK Version" section (lines 1-35)
The entire top section about the unreleased SDK is obsolete. Replace with:

```markdown
# Development Guide

## Architecture Overview

This scaffold uses **native ATProto** for all record operations:

- **`@atproto/api`** ŌĆö `Agent` class for XRPC calls (`getRecord`, `createRecord`, `putRecord`, `deleteRecord`, `uploadBlob`)
- **`@atproto/oauth-client-node`** ŌĆö `NodeOAuthClient` for OAuth session management
- **`@hypercerts-org/lexicon`** ŌĆö TypeScript types and `validateRecord` functions for all Hypercerts collections

There is no SDK wrapper layer. The vendor directory contains a packed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` tarball (type definitions and validators only).
```

### 2. Remove "What is a Packed Package?" section (lines 38-54)
Delete entirely. The concept of a packed SDK is no longer relevant. If you want, keep a brief note about the lexicon package:

```markdown
## Vendor Package

The `vendor/` directory contains a packed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` tarball. This provides TypeScript types and `validateRecord` functions for Hypercerts collections. It is installed as a local dependency in `package.json`.
```

### 3. Remove "Updating the Vendor SDK Package" section (lines 58-204)
Delete the entire section (steps 1-9, troubleshooting). Replace with a shorter section about updating the lexicon package:

```markdown
## Updating the Lexicon Package

The lexicon package provides TypeScript types and record validators. To update:

1. Clone/pull the [Hypercerts SDK repository](https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-sdk)
2. Build: `pnpm install && pnpm build`
3. Pack: `cd packages/lexicon && pnpm pack`
4. Copy the `.tgz` to `vendor/`
5. Update `package.json` to point to the new tarball
6. Run `pnpm install`
7. Check for type errors: `npx tsc --noEmit`

If types have changed, update code in `lib/types.ts`, `lib/create-actions.ts`, and `lib/queries.ts` to match.
```

### 7. Update "Contributing" section (lines 400-477)

- In "Test thoroughly" list (lines 436-439): keep the same test items, they are still valid

### 8. Update "Common Issues" section

- Keep all other troubleshooting sections (Redis, OAuth, ngrok) ŌĆö they are still accurate

## Dont

- Do not remove the Prerequisites section ŌĆö it is still accurate
- Do not remove the Contributing section ŌĆö just update SDK references
- Do not remove troubleshooting sections for Redis, OAuth, ngrok ŌĆö still valid

title: Fix: bsky-profile page missing convertBlobUrlToCdn for avatar/banner URLs (from hypercerts-02w.1)
description: Review of hypercerts-02w.1 found: app/bsky-profile/page.tsx (lines 34-45) calls getBlobURL() for avatar and banner but does NOT call convertBlobUrlToCdn() on the result, unlike app/profile/page.tsx which does. For users on bsky.network PDSes, the raw XRPC getBlob URL will be returned instead of the CDN URL, causing images to load from the PDS directly rather than the CDN. The original SDK returned pre-resolved string URLs so this inconsistency was not visible before. Fix: wrap both getBlobURL() calls with convertBlobUrlToCdn() as done in app/profile/page.tsx.

title: Fix: REPO_COLLECTIONS missing badge collections ŌĆö add comment documenting intentional exclusion (from hypercerts-02l)
description:
Review of the OAuth scope migration (quick-fixes-test branch, epic hypercerts-02l) found that REPO_COLLECTIONS in lib/config.ts includes 13 of the 16 HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS from the SDK. Three badge collections are silently absent with no documentation:

Missing:
- BADGE_AWARD (app.certified.badge.award)
- BADGE_DEFINITION (app.certified.badge.definition)
- BADGE_RESPONSE (app.certified.badge.response)

The app does not currently write to badge collections (confirmed by reviewing all API routes and create-actions.ts), so this is not a runtime bug today. However:
1. There is no comment explaining WHY these are excluded.
2. If badge-writing is added to the app in the future, the OAuth scope will silently be too narrow, causing runtime authorization errors.

Fix: Add a comment above REPO_COLLECTIONS documenting the intentional exclusion:


If badge support is planned in the near term, consider adding the badge collections proactively instead.

title: Fix: useEvaluationLinksQuery / useEvidenceLinksQuery / useMeasurementLinksQuery missing enabled guard
description:
## Problem
Three query hooks fire immediately with whatever `hypercertUri` they receive, including empty strings:

- `queries/hypercerts/use-evaluations-query.ts` line 9: `useEvaluationLinksQuery(hypercertUri)`
- `queries/hypercerts/use-evidence-query.ts` line 9: `useEvidenceLinksQuery(hypercertUri)`
- `queries/hypercerts/use-measurements-query.ts` line 9: `useMeasurementLinksQuery(hypercertUri)`

None of these hooks have an `enabled: !!hypercertUri` guard. If a parent component renders before the URI is available (e.g. during loading), these hooks will fire a request to the Constellation API with an empty or undefined subject, wasting network requests and potentially causing API errors.

Compare with `queries/external/use-user-search-query.ts` which correctly uses `enabled: enabled && !!query.trim()`.

## Fix
Add an `enabled` guard to each hook:
```ts
export function useEvaluationLinksQuery(hypercertUri: string) {
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: queryKeys.hypercerts.evaluations(hypercertUri),
    queryFn: () => getEvaluationLinks(hypercertUri),
    enabled: !!hypercertUri,
  });
}
```
Apply the same fix to `useEvidenceLinksQuery` and `useMeasurementLinksQuery`.

## Evidence
- `queries/hypercerts/use-evaluations-query.ts` lines 8-13
- `queries/hypercerts/use-evidence-query.ts` lines 8-13
- `queries/hypercerts/use-measurements-query.ts` lines 8-13

title: Add ownership check before contribution child record writes in create-actions.ts
description:
## Files
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify)

## Context
CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/14#discussion_r2882190517

## What to do
In the \`addContribution\` function (~line 108-203), the code creates \`contributionDetails\` and \`contributorInformation\` records BEFORE verifying that the caller owns the target hypercert. If the hypercert belongs to another user, those child records are created but the final putRecord fails, leaving dangling records.

Move the ownership validation to BEFORE any child record writes:

1. Parse the hypercertUri and validate it immediately after getting repo context
2. Check \`hypercertParsed.did !== ctx.activeDid\` ŌĆö if mismatch, throw an error like \`"addContribution failed: cannot modify another user's hypercert"\`
3. Fetch the existing hypercert record (getRecord) before creating child records
4. Only THEN proceed to create contributionDetails and contributorInformation records
5. The existing \`resolveStrongRef\` call can be removed since we're already fetching the record for ownership validation

Also: when using \`hypercertParsed.collection\`, don't fall back to empty string ŌĆö use the validated collection value directly (it was already checked to be non-empty).

## Don't
- Don't change the record construction logic for contributionDetails or contributorInformation
- Don't change the contributor array merging logic
- Don't remove the assertValidRecord calls
acceptance_criteria:
1. Ownership check (hypercertParsed.did === ctx.activeDid) happens before any createRecord calls
2. If DID mismatch, function throws a descriptive error without creating any child records
3. The existing hypercert record is fetched before child record creation
4. No fallback to empty string for collection ŌĆö uses validated value
5. npm run build succeeds
close_reason: 0dc7535 fix: move ownership check before child record writes in addContribution

title: Compensate orphaned artifacts on attachment creation failure
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)

## Context
CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/14#discussion_r2882190471

## What to do
In the POST handler, blob uploads and optional location creation happen (~lines 143-173) before the final attachment record validation/creation (~lines 188-205). If the final step fails, uploaded blobs and created location records are left orphaned.

Blobs cannot be deleted via the AT Protocol API (no deleteBlob endpoint), so focus on compensating the location record. Track whether a new location record was created (vs resolving an existing one):

1. Add a \`createdLocationRef\` variable that's only set when a NEW location is created (not when resolving an existing URI)
2. Wrap the validation + createRecord in a try/catch
3. On failure, if \`createdLocationRef\` exists, delete it (best-effort):
\`\`\`ts
if (createdLocationRef) {
  const parsed = parseAtUri(createdLocationRef.uri);
  if (parsed) {
    await ctx.agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord({
      repo: ctx.activeDid,
      collection: parsed.collection || "app.certified.location",
      rkey: parsed.rkey,
    }).catch(() => undefined);
  }
}
\`\`\`
4. Return the appropriate error (400 for validation, re-throw for others)

Import \`parseAtUri\` from \`@/lib/utils\` if not already imported.

## Don't
- Don't try to delete uploaded blobs (no API for that)
- Don't change the blob upload or location creation logic
- Don't change the response shape for successful creation
acceptance_criteria:
1. A createdLocationRef variable tracks newly created (not resolved) location records
2. If attachment validation or createRecord fails, the created location record is deleted (best-effort)
3. Validation errors return 400, other errors re-throw
4. npm run build succeeds
close_reason: 8fc9e5c Compensate orphaned location records on attachment creation failure

title: Fix: addContribution makes redundant resolveStrongRef call whose result is discarded (from hypercerts-czy.2)
description: Review of hypercerts-czy.2 found: addContribution() in lib/create-actions.ts (lines 130-135) calls resolveStrongRef() to validate the hypercertUri, but the returned StrongRef value is discarded. Immediately after, getRecord() is called again on the same URI (lines 137-144), making two sequential network round-trips to the PDS for the same record. Fix: either (a) use the result of resolveStrongRef() to get the CID and skip the second getRecord, or (b) remove the resolveStrongRef() call and just use getRecord() directly (adding a null check on the result).

title: Epic: Add signup mode to login dialog instead of redirecting to PDS
description:
## Why
Currently when users click 'Create an account' in the login dialog, they get redirected away to the PDS URL (handle tab) or to /api/oauth/login without email (email tab). This is confusing because users leave the app and don't understand they're creating an account.

## What success looks like
- The login dialog supports a 'signup' mode that shows the same form fields but with signup-oriented wording
- 'Create an account' buttons switch the dialog to signup mode instead of redirecting
- In signup mode: heading says 'Create Account' instead of 'Sign In', subheading says 'Get started with your account', submit buttons say 'Create Account' / 'Sign Up', and there's a link back to sign-in mode ('Already have an account? Sign in')
- The underlying authentication logic remains IDENTICAL ŌĆö same API calls, same OAuth flows, same email/handle submission
- Both the navbar dialog and the full-page LoginDialog (in SignedInProvider) support the mode
- The home page 'Sign In to Continue' / 'Sign In to Get Started' placeholder buttons should also be able to trigger signup mode

## Key constraints
- NO changes to API routes or authentication logic ŌĆö this is purely a UI/UX change
- The LoginDialog component must accept an optional 'mode' prop (or use internal state) to toggle between sign-in and sign-up wording
- The PillToggle (Handle/Email tabs) should still work in both modes
- All existing sign-in functionality must continue to work exactly as before
close_reason: f7b4606 All 3 children completed ŌĆö signup mode for login dialog

title: Refactor LoginDialog to support sign-in vs sign-up mode via internal state
description:
## Files
- components/login-dialog.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Add a `mode` state to `LoginDialog` that toggles between `"signin"` and `"signup"`. The mode controls ALL text/wording in the dialog and also controls which tabs are visible.

### State changes:
1. Add `type AuthMode = "signin" | "signup"` type alias near the existing `Tab` type (line 16)
2. Add `const [mode, setMode] = useState<AuthMode>("signin")` to `LoginDialog` (alongside existing `activeTab` state)
3. Pass `mode` and `setMode` as props to `HandleForm` and `EmailForm`

### Key behavior: Hide Handle tab in signup mode
- When `mode === "signup"`, do NOT render the `PillToggle` at all, and ALWAYS render `EmailForm` (never `HandleForm`)
- When `mode === "signin"`, render the `PillToggle` (if `hasEpds`) and show `HandleForm` or `EmailForm` based on `activeTab` ŌĆö exactly as today
- When switching from signup back to signin, restore the `activeTab` state (it should persist across mode switches)

### Wording changes based on mode:

**LoginDialog heading:**
- signin: "Sign In"
- signup: "Create Account"

**LoginDialog subheading:**
- signin: "Choose how to continue"
- signup: "Get started with your account"

**HandleForm (only shown in signin mode):**
- Submit button: "Sign In" (unchanged)
- Bottom link: "Create an account" button that calls `setMode("signup")` instead of `redirectToAccountCreation()`
- Remove the `redirectToAccountCreation` function entirely

**EmailForm changes based on mode:**
- Submit button: signin ŌåÆ "Continue", signup ŌåÆ "Create Account"
- Helper text: signin ŌåÆ "Enter your email for a direct code, or continue without", signup ŌåÆ "Enter your email to create your account"
- Bottom link:
  - signin: "Don't have an account? Create one" ŌåÆ calls `setMode("signup")`
  - signup: "Already have an account? Sign in" ŌåÆ calls `setMode("signin")`
- In BOTH modes, the submit logic is identical: navigate to `/api/oauth/login?email=...` (or without email if empty). The ePDS handles both login and signup.

### Logic changes:
- **HandleForm submit:** calls `loginMutation.mutate(handle)` ŌĆö UNCHANGED (only shown in signin mode anyway)
- **EmailForm submit (both modes):** calls `handleContinue()` which navigates to `/api/oauth/login?email=...` ŌĆö UNCHANGED
- Remove the `redirectToAccountCreation` function from HandleForm
- Remove the `onClick` handler that does `window.location.href = "/api/oauth/login"` from EmailForm bottom button (replace with `setMode("signup")` in signin, `setMode("signin")` in signup)

### Component signatures:
```tsx
type AuthMode = "signin" | "signup";

function HandleForm({ mode, setMode }: { mode: AuthMode; setMode: (m: AuthMode) => void }) { ... }
function EmailForm({ mode, setMode }: { mode: AuthMode; setMode: (m: AuthMode) => void }) { ... }
```

### Rendering logic in LoginDialog:
```tsx
{/* Only show PillToggle in signin mode when ePDS is available */}
{mode === "signin" && hasEpds && <PillToggle active={activeTab} onChange={setActiveTab} />}

{/* In signup mode, always show EmailForm. In signin mode, show based on activeTab */}
{mode === "signup" ? (
  <EmailForm mode={mode} setMode={setMode} />
) : activeTab === "handle" || !hasEpds ? (
  <HandleForm mode={mode} setMode={setMode} />
) : (
  <EmailForm mode={mode} setMode={setMode} />
)}
```

## Don't
- Change any API routes or backend logic
- Change the PillToggle component itself (it stays the same, just conditionally rendered)
- Change any CSS classes or styling (only text content and conditional rendering changes)
- Remove the existing `useLoginMutation` or `handleContinue` logic
- Add any new dependencies or imports (except the AuthMode type)
- Show HandleForm in signup mode
- Change the component's default export signature ŌĆö LoginDialog should still be a no-props default export for now
acceptance_criteria:
1. LoginDialog renders with "Sign In" heading, "Choose how to continue" subheading, and PillToggle visible by default (signin mode)
2. Clicking "Create an account" in HandleForm switches to signup mode
3. In signup mode: heading is "Create Account", subheading is "Get started with your account"
4. In signup mode: PillToggle is NOT rendered (hidden)
5. In signup mode: only EmailForm is shown (HandleForm is never rendered)
6. In signup mode: EmailForm shows "Create Account" submit button and "Enter your email to create your account" helper text
7. In signup mode: EmailForm shows "Already have an account? Sign in" link that switches back to signin mode
8. Switching back to signin mode restores the PillToggle and the previously selected tab
9. In signin mode: EmailForm bottom link says "Don't have an account? Create one" and switches to signup mode (no redirect)
10. EmailForm submit logic is unchanged in both modes (navigates to /api/oauth/login with optional email param)
11. HandleForm submit logic is unchanged (only visible in signin mode)
12. The redirectToAccountCreation function is removed
13. No TypeScript errors, app builds successfully

title: Fix: HandleForm 'Create an account' button should redirect to PDS URL, not switch to signup mode
description:
## Files
- components/login-dialog.tsx (modify)

## What to do
In the current code, the HandleForm "Create an account" button calls `setMode("signup")` which switches to the email-only signup view. This is wrong for the Handle tab ŌĆö ATProto account creation requires redirecting to the PDS.

### Restore the PDS redirect in HandleForm:
1. Re-add the `redirectToAccountCreation` function to `HandleForm`:
```tsx
const redirectToAccountCreation = () => {
  if (!process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL) {
    throw new Error("NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL is not defined");
  }
  loginMutation.mutate(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL);
};
```

2. Change the "Create an account" button onClick back to `redirectToAccountCreation`:
```tsx
<Button
  disabled={loginMutation.isPending}
  onClick={redirectToAccountCreation}
  variant="ghost"
  type="button"
  className="w-full font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)] text-muted-foreground hover:text-create-accent hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors"
>
  Create an account
</Button>
```

3. Since HandleForm no longer needs `setMode`, remove it from the props. HandleForm only needs `mode` (to keep the type signature consistent, though it is only ever rendered in signin mode). Actually, HandleForm does not need `mode` or `setMode` at all anymore since it is only shown in signin mode and its "Create an account" button always redirects. Simplify:
```tsx
function HandleForm() {
  // ... same as original, with redirectToAccountCreation restored
}
```

4. Update the `HandleForm` call site in `LoginDialog` ŌĆö remove the `mode` and `setMode` props:
```tsx
<HandleForm />
```

### Leave EmailForm unchanged:
- EmailForm still uses `setMode("signup")` / `setMode("signin")` for its bottom link ŌĆö this is correct
- EmailForm still accepts `mode` and `setMode` props ŌĆö this is correct

## Don't
- Change EmailForm behavior
- Change the signup mode rendering logic (PillToggle hidden, only EmailForm shown)
- Change the navbar buttons
- Change any API routes
acceptance_criteria:
1. In signin mode on the Handle tab, clicking "Create an account" redirects to the PDS URL via loginMutation.mutate(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL) ŌĆö NOT switching to signup mode
2. HandleForm no longer accepts mode/setMode props
3. EmailForm "Don't have an account? Create one" still switches to signup mode (unchanged)
4. The navbar "Create Account" button still opens the dialog in signup mode showing only EmailForm (unchanged)
5. No TypeScript errors, app builds successfully

Fix: vendor/hypercerts-org-lexicon-0.10.0-beta.15.tgz is in vendor/ but package.json still installs beta.14 from npm
description:
Review of the loopback branch found a mismatch between the vendored lexicon tarball and the installed version.

Evidence:
- vendor/ directory contains: hypercerts-org-lexicon-0.10.0-beta.15.tgz (new file added in this branch)
- package.json line 28: '@hypercerts-org/lexicon': '0.10.0-beta.14' (installs from npm registry)
- pnpm-lock.yaml confirms beta.14 is resolved from npm (not from the local tgz)
- The beta.15 tgz in vendor/ is never referenced anywhere in package.json

This means the beta.15 tarball is dead weight ŌĆö it was added to vendor/ but the package.json was never updated to use it. Developers will install beta.14 from npm, not the vendored beta.15.

Two possible intended states:
1. If beta.15 should be used: update package.json to 'file:./vendor/hypercerts-org-lexicon-0.10.0-beta.15.tgz' and run pnpm install
2. If beta.14 from npm is correct: remove the beta.15 tgz from vendor/ to avoid confusion

Also: DEVELOPMENT.md documents how to update the sdk-core vendor package but says nothing about the lexicon vendor package. If lexicon is also being vendored, the docs should cover it.

Note: @hypercerts-org/sdk-core (beta.8) depends on @hypercerts-org/lexicon beta.14, so the versions are currently consistent ŌĆö but the orphaned beta.15 tgz is confusing.

title: Configure allowedDevOrigins in next.config for ngrok
description: Next.js detected cross-origin request from ngrok domain to /_next/* resources. Future versions will require explicit configuration of allowedDevOrigins. See: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/allowedDevOrigins

title: Fix: double-callback not handled ŌĆö second callback with same code succeeds silently (from hypercerts-dhz.6)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dhz found: The ePDS callback route (app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts) does not delete the epds-oauth-session cookie before the token exchange. The cookie is only cleared AFTER a successful exchange (line 148). This means:

1. If the user hits the callback URL twice (browser back button, duplicate tab, network retry), the second request will find the same cookie, pass state verification, and attempt a second token exchange with the same authorization code.
2. Authorization codes are single-use ŌĆö the second exchange will fail with 'invalid_grant' from the token endpoint, returning a 502 to the user even though they are already logged in.

The correct pattern is to delete the session cookie immediately after reading it (before the token exchange), so that any duplicate callback gets a 'No OAuth session found' 400 instead of a confusing 502.

Additionally, the cookie deletion at line 148 uses `maxAge: 0` but does not set `httpOnly: true` or `path: '/'` consistently ŌĆö some browsers may not honor the deletion if the attributes don't match the original cookie exactly.

Fix: (a) delete the cookie immediately after reading it (before token exchange), (b) ensure the deletion cookie attributes match the original (httpOnly, secure, path, sameSite).

title: Epic: hypercerts-next-optimization ŌĆö Vercel React Best Practices remediation
description:
## Context
Comprehensive audit of the codebase against Vercel's 57-rule React/Next.js performance guide found **53 violations** across 7 categories. No violations found in client-side data fetching (React Query is well-used), localStorage, event listeners, or advanced patterns.

## Audit Summary by Category
| Category | Impact | Violations | Key Issues |
|----------|--------|------------|------------|
| Eliminating Waterfalls | CRITICAL | 14 | Sequential awaits in providers, pages, API routes; missing Suspense boundaries |
| Bundle Size | CRITICAL | 12 | Barrel imports (queries/, lucide-react, @hypercerts-org/lexicon); missing optimizePackageImports; static imports of conditional components |
| Server-Side Perf | HIGH | 14 | React.cache() misuse (object args), missing LRU cache, duplicate serialization, sequential fetches, blocking revalidatePath |
| Re-render Optimization | MEDIUM | 13 | 10x functional setState violations, 3x useRouter defer-reads |
| Rendering Performance | MEDIUM | 6 | Conditional render safety, hoistable JSX, manual loading state |
| JavaScript Performance | LOW-MEDIUM | 11 | Array.find for dedup (4x), chained filter/map (3x), unhoist regex, Set lookups |

## Success Criteria
- All CRITICAL and HIGH violations resolved
- MEDIUM violations resolved where practical
- LOW-MEDIUM violations batched into sweep tasks
- No regressions: build passes, existing tests pass

## Scope
All source files in app/, lib/, components/, providers/, queries/. Does NOT include node_modules, vendor/, or UI primitives (components/ui/).

For each file listed above, change the import from the barrel index file to import directly from the specific module file. Each import should point to the exact file that exports the hook being used.

// After

If a file imports multiple hooks from the same barrel, split into separate direct imports or import from the same direct file if both hooks live there.

## Don't

## What to do

### profile/update/route.ts
1. **Lines 93-104 and 141-151 (both create and update branches):** Avatar and banner blob uploads are sequential. Parallelize:
```ts
const [avatarBlob, bannerBlob] = await Promise.all([
  avatar ? repo.com.atproto.repo.uploadBlob(new Blob([avatar], { type: avatar.type })).then(r => r.data.blob) : undefined,
  banner ? repo.com.atproto.repo.uploadBlob(new Blob([banner], { type: banner.type })).then(r => r.data.blob) : undefined,
]);
```
Apply this pattern in BOTH the create branch (~line 93) and the update branch (~line 141).

2. **Lines 207-220 (response building):** getRecord and getSession are sequential but independent. Parallelize:
```ts
const [updatedResult, session] = await Promise.all([
  repo.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({...}).catch(() => null),
  getSession(),
]);
```

### profile/bsky/update/route.ts
**Lines 50-60 and 91-101:** Same avatar/banner blob upload pattern. Apply the same Promise.all fix in both branches.

### certs/add-attachment/route.ts
**Lines 137-175:** Three sequential operations (resolveStrongRef for subject, content resolution, location resolution) are independent. Parallelize:
```ts
const [subjectRef, contentField, locationRef] = await Promise.all([
  resolveStrongRef(ctx.agent, hypercertUri, 'org.hypercerts.claim.activity'),
  resolveContent(ctx.agent, content),  // extract content resolution into helper
  location ? resolveLocation(ctx.agent, ctx.activeDid, location) : undefined,
]);
```
If extracting helpers is too complex, at minimum parallelize the subject ref and location resolution.

### certs/add-location/route.ts
**Lines 85-115:** The location record creation and existing hypercert fetch are sequential but independent. Parallelize:
```ts
const [locationRef, existingResult] = await Promise.all([
  createLocationRecord(personalRepository, personalRepository.assertDid, locationPayload),
  personalRepository.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({
    repo: hypercertParsed.did,
    collection: hypercertParsed.collection || 'org.hypercerts.claim.activity',
    rkey: hypercertParsed.rkey,
  }),
]);
```

## Don't
- Change error handling semantics
- Modify request parsing or validation logic
- Change response shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. All four route files use Promise.all for independent async operations
2. No sequential awaits remain for operations that don't depend on each other
3. pnpm build succeeds
4. Response shapes are unchanged

title: Parallelize sequential awaits in create-actions.ts (addMeasurement, addContribution)
description:
## Files
- lib/create-actions.ts (modify)

## What to do

### addMeasurement (around lines 312-334)
`resolveStrongRef` and `processLocations` are called sequentially but are independent (both only need ctx.agent and ctx.activeDid which are already available). Parallelize:
```ts
const [subject, locationRefs] = await Promise.all([
  resolveStrongRef(ctx.agent, params.subject, 'org.hypercerts.claim.activity'),
  params.locations?.length
    ? processLocations(ctx.agent, ctx.activeDid, params.locations)
    : undefined,
]);
```

### addContribution (around lines 101-164)
After getting repo context and validating ownership, the existing hypercert record fetch (line ~126) and the contributionDetails record creation (line ~160) are independent. The details record doesn't depend on the existing hypercert content. Parallelize where possible.

Note: Be careful here ŌĆö review the actual data flow. If the details record creation truly doesn't need data from the existing hypercert, parallelize. If it does, leave sequential.

## Don't
- Change the ownership validation logic (hypercertParsed.did !== ctx.activeDid check)
- Change error handling behavior
- Modify the record schemas or field values
acceptance_criteria:
1. addMeasurement uses Promise.all for resolveStrongRef and processLocations
2. addContribution parallelizes independent operations where safe
3. pnpm build succeeds
4. Ownership checks remain intact

title: Epic: Fix build failure ŌĆö missing HypercertRecord import
description: The Next.js production build (npm run build) fails with a TypeScript error: 'Cannot find name HypercertRecord' in components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx:38. This blocks all deployments. Success = npm run build passes cleanly.

title: Fix: add missing HypercertRecord import in hypercerts-base-form.tsx
description:
## Files
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Line 38 uses `HypercertRecord` as a type but it is not imported. The type is a namespace re-export defined in `lib/types.ts` line 1:

```ts
import type { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as HypercertRecord } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";
```

Add this import to the top of `components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx`:

```ts
import type { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as HypercertRecord } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";
```

This is the same pattern already used on line 11 for the `Hypercert` import (non-type import of the same module). You may combine them or keep them separate ŌĆö either is fine.

The `certInfo` prop on line 38 is typed `HypercertRecord` (the namespace). It is used on lines 64-65 to call `Hypercert.isWorkScopeString(certInfo.workScope)` and access `.workScope.scope`, and on lines 67-80 to read `.title`, `.shortDescription`, `.startDate`, `.endDate`. This is consistent with `OrgHypercertsClaimActivity.Record` fields, so the type is correct ŌĆö it just needs the import.

## Do not
- Change the type of `certInfo` ŌĆö the namespace type is correct
- Rename `HypercertRecord` to anything else
- Modify any logic or other files
acceptance_criteria: npm run build completes successfully (exit code 0). The only change is the added import ŌĆö no other lines modified.
close_reason: aa400e7 fix: add missing HypercertRecord import and use .Record type

title: Epic: Resolve CodeRabbit review for PR #7
description: Resolve all 10 open CodeRabbit inline review comments on PR #7 (quick-fixes branch). 10 comments across 7 files covering: unsafe error casts, bare catch blocks swallowing errors, unsanitized OAuth input, missing JWK validation, duplicate evaluator DIDs, and dead variables in SignedInProvider.

title: Sanitize OAuth error_description in auth callback
description:
## Files
- app/api/auth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 18.

**Original comment:**
> The error_description on line 12 is passed through from the authorization server verbatim. While React will safely render it as text (no XSS), the content is controlled by the external OAuth provider and could be unexpectedly verbose or confusing to end users. Consider capping or sanitizing it, or using a fixed user-friendly message like the catch block does.

**Suggested fix:**
On line 12, after getting the error_description from searchParams, sanitize it before using it in the redirect:
1. Trim whitespace
2. Enforce a max length of 200 characters ŌĆö if it exceeds, truncate and append '...'
3. If empty after trimming, use the fallback 'Authorization was cancelled' (already the current fallback)
4. Keep the full raw provider message in the console.warn on line 13 for server-side debugging
5. Use the sanitized version in the redirect URL searchParams on line 16

**Context:**
Line 12 currently does: `searchParams.get('error_description') || 'Authorization was cancelled'`. The value goes into a URL redirect param on line 16 that the client reads to show a toast. The console.warn on line 13 should keep the original unsanitized value.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2817065641

## Don't
- Don't change the catch block on lines 39-48 (that's a separate error path)
- Don't change HTTP status codes or redirect behavior
- Don't introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: 1. error_description values longer than 200 chars are truncated to 200 chars with '...' appended. 2. The console.warn still logs the full original error_description. 3. Empty/whitespace-only error_description falls back to 'Authorization was cancelled'. 4. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: acb98e9 Sanitize OAuth error_description in auth callback

In profile/update/route.ts, the catch block around getCertifiedProfile() has the same selective handling. 3. Neither file has a bare 'catch {}' or 'catch {' without error inspection. 4. npm run build succeeds.

title: Deduplicate evaluator DIDs in evaluation form
description:
## Files
- components/evaluation-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 177.

**Original comment:**
> No deduplication between search-selected and manually-entered evaluator DIDs. If the same DID is added via the search tab and also typed in the manual DIDs tab, it will appear twice in allEvaluatorDids. The addEvaluator function (line 113) deduplicates within evaluators, but there's no cross-check against manualDids.

**Suggested fix:**
Replace lines 173-177 with a Set-based deduplication:
```typescript
const didSet = new Set<string>();
for (const e of evaluators) didSet.add(e.did);
for (const did of manualDids) {
  const trimmed = did.trim();
  if (trimmed !== '') didSet.add(trimmed);
}
const allEvaluatorDids = Array.from(didSet);
```

**Context:**
The handleSubmit function (line 166) builds allEvaluatorDids by concatenating evaluators (search-selected) and manualDids (typed). The addEvaluator function on line 113 deduplicates within evaluators, but there's no cross-check when combining with manualDids.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818491522

## Don't
- Don't change the addEvaluator function
- Don't change the evaluationPayload structure
- Don't introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: 1. allEvaluatorDids is built using a Set (or equivalent dedup logic) so duplicate DIDs between evaluators and manualDids are removed. 2. manualDids entries are trimmed before comparison. 3. Empty strings from manualDids are still excluded. 4. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: bcb125e deduplicate evaluator DIDs using Set

title: Populate activeProfileName and activeProfileHandle in SignedInProvider
description:
## Files

## What to do

These variables are always undefined because they're declared as const and never assigned. Unlike avatarUrl and handle (which are let and populated from the profile), these remain unused, yet are passed to <Navbar>.

**Suggested fix:**

2. Change line 20 from `const activeProfileHandle` to `let activeProfileHandle`
3. Inside the `if (repo)` block (after line 30), add:
   ```typescript
   activeProfileName = profile?.displayName;
   activeProfileHandle = profile?.handle;
   ```

**Context:**
Lines 19-20 declare these as `const` initialized to `undefined`. They are passed to `<Navbar>` on lines 45-46 but are always undefined. The `avatarUrl` and `handle` variables on lines 17-18 follow the correct pattern (declared as `let`, populated inside the `if (repo)` block).

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818491529

## Don't

title: Fix unsafe error casts across 4 API routes
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged 4 related error-handling issues ŌĆö all are unsafe `(e as Error).message` casts that produce `undefined` when a non-Error value is thrown.

1. [error-handling] Line 141 in add-attachment/route.ts: `(e as Error).message` in catch block
2. [error-handling] Line 97 in add-location/route.ts: `(e as Error).message` in catch block ŌĆö also leaks internal details via `details` field
3. [error-handling] Line 102 in bsky/update/route.ts: `(error as Error).message` in catch block
4. [error-handling] Line 120 in profile/update/route.ts: `(error as Error).message` in catch block

**Suggested fixes:**

For all 4 files, replace the unsafe cast with a safe pattern:
```typescript
e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)
```

Specific changes:

1. add-attachment/route.ts line 141:
   ```diff
   -{ error: \`Failed to add attachment: ${(e as Error).message}\` }
   +{ error: \`Failed to add attachment: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}\` }
   ```

2. add-location/route.ts line 97:
   ```diff
   -{ error: 'Internal server error', details: (e as Error).message }
   +{ error: \`Internal server error: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}\` }
   ```
   Note: also remove the separate `details` field to avoid leaking internals ŌĆö fold it into the error string.

3. bsky/update/route.ts line 102:
   ```diff
   -{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${(error as Error).message}\` }
   +{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\` }
   ```

4. profile/update/route.ts line 120:
   ```diff
   -{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${(error as Error).message}\` }
   +{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\` }
   ```

CodeRabbit comments:
- https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2817065643
- https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2817065659
- https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2817065682
- https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2817065694

## Don't
- Don't change HTTP status codes
- Don't change the console.error logging lines
- Don't change the response field name 'error' (keep it as 'error')
- Don't introduce new dependencies
- Don't change unrelated code in these files
acceptance_criteria: 1. None of the 4 files contain '(e as Error).message' or '(error as Error).message'. 2. All 4 catch blocks use 'instanceof Error ? .message : String()' pattern. 3. add-location/route.ts no longer has a separate 'details' field in the error response. 4. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: 81cca2f fix: replace unsafe error casts with instanceof Error pattern

title: Add JWK structure validation in jwks.json route
description:
## Files
- app/jwks.json/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: error-handling issue at line 54.

**Original comment:**
> Unvalidated privateKey.keys will throw an unhelpful 500 if the JWK structure is unexpected. If ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE is valid JSON but doesn't contain a keys array (e.g., it's a single JWK object instead of a JWK Set), (privateKey.keys as Array<...>).map(...) will throw a TypeError that isn't caught.

**Suggested fix:**
After the JSON.parse on line 25 and before the PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS line on line 37, add a guard:
```typescript
if (
  !privateKey.keys ||
  !Array.isArray(privateKey.keys) ||
  privateKey.keys.length === 0
) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { error: "ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE must contain a JWKS with a non-empty 'keys' array." },
    { status: 500 },
  );
}
```

**Context:**
The route serves the public JWKS endpoint. Line 38 does `(privateKey.keys as Array<...>).map(...)` which will throw a raw TypeError if keys is undefined or not an array. The existing validation checks for missing env var (line 16) and invalid JSON (line 26) but not for invalid JWK structure.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2817065698

## Don't
- Don't change the existing env var check or JSON parse logic
- Don't change the key transformation logic (PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS filtering)
- Don't change the response headers or cache control
- Don't introduce new dependencies
acceptance_criteria: 1. A guard exists before the .map() call that checks privateKey.keys is a non-empty array. 2. If privateKey.keys is missing, not an array, or empty, a 500 JSON response with a descriptive error message is returned. 3. The existing env var and JSON parse checks are unchanged. 4. npm run build succeeds.
close_reason: acb98e9 Add JWK structure validation guard in jwks.json route

title: Remove personal email addresses from .beads/issues.jsonl ŌĆö replace with GitHub handles
description:
## Files
- .beads/issues.jsonl (modify)

## Context
The issues.jsonl file contains personal email addresses in the 'owner' fields that are committed to the public git repo, exposing PII:
- `kzoepa@gmail.com` appears as owner on ~26 issues (lines 1, 6-19, 20, 32-40, 46)
- `adam@hypercerts.org` appears as owner on 5 issues (lines 41-45)

The JSONL is auto-exported from the beads SQLite DB. However, the JSONL also serves as an import source (config says `no-auto-import: false`). So editing the JSONL and re-importing should work.

## What to do

### 1. Replace email addresses in issues.jsonl
Use sed or similar to do a global find-and-replace in `.beads/issues.jsonl`:
- Replace `"owner":"kzoepa@gmail.com"` with `"owner":"kzoeps"`
- Replace `"owner":"adam@hypercerts.org"` with `"owner":"aspiers"`

### 2. Also check and replace in created_by fields
Check if `created_by` fields also contain emails. From the data I see `"created_by":"kzoeps"` and `"created_by":"Adam Spiers"` ŌĆö these are names/handles, not emails, so they should be fine. But verify.

### 3. Check interactions.jsonl too
Search `.beads/interactions.jsonl` for any email addresses and replace similarly.

### 4. Verify the JSONL is still valid
After editing, run `hb list` to verify the beads DB can still read the data. If `hb sync` overwrites your changes, you may need to:
- Delete the SQLite DB (`rm .beads/beads.db`)
- Run `hb init` to re-initialize from the JSONL
- Run `hb sync` to verify

### 5. Do a repo-wide search
Run `rg -i 'kzoepa@gmail|adam@hypercerts' --type-not binary` to check for any other occurrences outside .beads/.

### 6. Note about git history
The emails are already in git history. A `git filter-repo` rewrite would be needed to fully remove them from history, but that's destructive and out of scope for this task. Just fix the current files. Add a comment in the commit message noting that history rewriting may be needed if this is a concern.

## Don't
- Run git filter-repo or any history-rewriting commands
- Modify any code files
- Change the beads config
- Delete or recreate issues ŌĆö only change the owner field values
acceptance_criteria:
1. No email addresses (containing @) remain in .beads/issues.jsonl owner fields
2. No email addresses remain in .beads/interactions.jsonl (if any existed)
3. kzoepa@gmail.com is replaced with kzoeps everywhere in .beads/
4. adam@hypercerts.org is replaced with aspiers everywhere in .beads/
5. hb list still works after the changes (JSONL is valid)
6. A repo-wide search for the email addresses returns zero results in tracked files
7. Changes are committed and pushed
close_reason: c311694 Remove personal email addresses from .beads/issues.jsonl

title: Fix: raw error leak in certs/create route (from hypercerts-1hr.2)
description:
Review of hypercerts-1hr.2 found the same raw-error-leak pattern in app/api/certs/create/route.ts that was fixed in the logout route.

Line 58: `return NextResponse.json({ error: e.message }, { status: 500 });` ŌĆö leaks internal error messages (e.g. SDK internals, network errors) to the client when `e instanceof Error`.

Line 61: `{ error: `Failed to create hypercert: ${(e as Error).message}` }` ŌĆö the fallback branch also uses the unsafe cast; if `e` is not an Error instance, `.message` is `undefined`, producing a confusing response.

Fix: replace both branches with a single generic message, keep console.error for server-side logging. Pattern to follow: app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (fixed by hypercerts-1hr.2).

Evidence: app/api/certs/create/route.ts lines 55-63.
close_reason: 9173a17 fix: replace raw error leak with generic message in certs/create

title: Fix: ePDS email login section always visible even when NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is unset (from hypercerts-dhz.8/dhz.10)
description: Review of hypercerts-dhz.8 and dhz.10 found: The email login section (the 'or' divider + email input + 'Sign in with email' button) is always rendered in LoginDialog regardless of whether NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set. If a deployer does not set NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL, clicking 'Sign in with email' navigates to /api/oauth/login which throws an unhandled Error('NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is not set') from getEpdsEndpoints() ŌĆö this results in a 500 error page with no user-friendly message. The email section should be conditionally rendered: only show it when process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is truthy. Since this is a 'use client' component and NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars are inlined at build time, the check is: {process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL && (<>...email section...</>)}. Evidence: login-dialog.tsx has no NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL guard; lib/epds-config.ts line 19-24 throws if epdsUrl is falsy.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.16 (commit dcac8b3). login-dialog.tsx line 155: hasEpds = !!process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL. Email tab and PillToggle only render when hasEpds is true (lines 168-176).

title: Fix: redundant `width: 32px` on `div.w-8` container and dead `object-position` on Ma Earth logo in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
Fix: inconsistent indentation in buildClientMetadata() production block (from hypercerts-nyk.7)
description: Review of hypercerts-nyk.7 found: In lib/config.ts, the production return block of buildClientMetadata() has inconsistent indentation. The properties logo_uri, grant_types, response_types, token_endpoint_auth_method, and application_type use 6-space indentation instead of 4-space like the surrounding properties (client_id, client_name, client_uri, redirect_uris, scope, dpop_bound_access_tokens, branding). This was introduced in commit 3e4b912 when the logo_uri line was changed and the surrounding lines were accidentally re-indented. Fix: normalize all properties in the production return block to 4-space indentation. File: lib/config.ts, function buildClientMetadata(), production return block (~lines 235-250).

title: Epic: Add Prettier, Husky, and lint-staged for pre-commit checks
description: The project has ESLint configured (flat config, eslint-config-next) but no Prettier installed (the existing .prettierrc.json has a typo and prettier isn't even a dependency), no husky, and no lint-staged. Goal: install prettier with a proper config, install husky + lint-staged, and wire them together so that on every commit: (1) staged .ts/.tsx/.mjs files are auto-formatted with prettier --write, (2) staged .ts/.tsx files are linted with eslint --fix. The existing .prettierrc.json needs to be fixed (single ŌåÆ singleQuote). Success: git commit triggers pre-commit hook that formats and lints staged files. Scope: prettier config, .prettierignore, husky setup, lint-staged config, package.json scripts. Out of scope: changing ESLint rules, adding new ESLint plugins, reformatting the entire codebase in this epic.
close_reason: ec7549c Prettier, Husky, lint-staged installed and codebase formatted

title: Install and configure Prettier, Husky, and lint-staged
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify ŌĆö add devDependencies, scripts, lint-staged config)
- .prettierrc.json (modify ŌĆö fix typo, add full config)
- .prettierignore (create)
- .husky/pre-commit (create via husky init)
- eslint.config.mjs (modify ŌĆö add eslint-config-prettier to disable formatting rules that conflict with prettier)

## What to do

### 1. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install --save-dev prettier husky lint-staged eslint-config-prettier
```

### 2. Fix and expand .prettierrc.json

Replace the current broken config with:
```json
{
  "semi": true,
  "singleQuote": false,
  "tabWidth": 2,
  "trailingComma": "all",
  "printWidth": 80,
  "plugins": [],
  "endOfLine": "lf"
}
```

Note: `singleQuote: false` preserves the existing codebase convention (double quotes are used everywhere). The old config had `"single": false` which is not a valid Prettier key.

### 3. Create .prettierignore

```
node_modules/
.next/
out/
build/
coverage/
*.tgz
pnpm-lock.yaml
package-lock.json
.beads/
vendor/
```

### 4. Add eslint-config-prettier to ESLint config

In `eslint.config.mjs`, import and add `eslint-config-prettier` to disable ESLint rules that conflict with Prettier. The config should look like:

```js
import { defineConfig, globalIgnores } from "eslint/config";
import nextVitals from "eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals";
import nextTs from "eslint-config-next/typescript";
import prettierConfig from "eslint-config-prettier";

const eslintConfig = defineConfig([
  ...nextVitals,
  ...nextTs,
  prettierConfig,
  globalIgnores([
    ".next/**",
    "out/**",
    "build/**",
    "next-env.d.ts",
  ]),
]);

export default eslintConfig;
```

### 5. Initialize Husky

```bash
npx husky init
```

This creates `.husky/pre-commit`. Replace its contents with:

```bash
npx lint-staged
```

### 6. Add lint-staged config to package.json

Add this to package.json at the top level:

```json
"lint-staged": {
  "*.{ts,tsx,mjs}": [
    "prettier --write"
  ],
  "*.{ts,tsx}": [
    "eslint --fix"
  ]
}
```

### 7. Add scripts to package.json

Add these scripts (keep existing ones):

```json
"format": "prettier --write .",
"format:check": "prettier --check .",
"prepare": "husky"
```

The `prepare` script ensures husky hooks are installed after `npm install`.

### 8. Verify

- Run `npx prettier --check .` ŌĆö it will likely show files that need formatting (that is OK, we are not reformatting in this task)
- Run `npm run lint` ŌĆö should pass
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` ŌĆö should pass
- Test the hook: stage a file, commit, verify lint-staged runs

## Don't
- Do NOT reformat the entire codebase (that is a separate task)
- Do NOT change any ESLint rules (only add eslint-config-prettier to disable conflicting formatting rules)
- Do NOT add prettier plugins (like prettier-plugin-tailwindcss) ŌĆö keep it simple
- Do NOT modify any source files (.ts, .tsx) in this task
acceptance_criteria:
1. prettier is in devDependencies and npx prettier --version works
2. husky is in devDependencies and .husky/pre-commit exists with "npx lint-staged"
3. lint-staged is in devDependencies and configured in package.json
4. eslint-config-prettier is in devDependencies and added to eslint.config.mjs
5. .prettierrc.json has valid config (no "single" typo ŌĆö uses "singleQuote")
6. .prettierignore exists and ignores node_modules, .next, build artifacts
7. package.json has "format", "format:check", and "prepare" scripts
8. npm run lint passes (no ESLint errors)
9. npx tsc --noEmit passes
10. The pre-commit hook fires on git commit (test with a dummy commit)
close_reason: d1d8dc4 feat: install and configure Prettier, Husky, and lint-staged

title: Format entire codebase with Prettier
description:
## Files
- All .ts, .tsx, .mjs, .json, .css files in the project (modify)

## What to do

Run `npm run format` (which runs `prettier --write .`) to format the entire codebase with the Prettier config established in hypercerts-v67.1.

This should be a single commit with ONLY formatting changes ŌĆö no logic changes.

### Steps

1. Run `npm run format`
2. Review the diff to confirm it is ONLY whitespace/formatting changes (quotes, semicolons, trailing commas, line width wrapping, etc.)
3. Run `npm run lint` to verify ESLint still passes after formatting
4. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify TypeScript still compiles
5. Commit with message "style: format codebase with prettier"

## Don't
- Do NOT change any logic, imports, or behavior
- Do NOT modify .prettierrc.json or any config files
- Do NOT fix ESLint warnings/errors ŌĆö only formatting
acceptance_criteria:
1. npm run format:check passes (all files are formatted)
2. npm run lint passes
3. npx tsc --noEmit passes
4. The commit contains ONLY formatting changes (no logic changes)
5. The app still builds: npm run build passes (or next build)
close_reason: ec7549c style: format codebase with prettier

title: Fix: user-did cookie missing SameSite attribute in standard OAuth callback (from security review)
description:
## File
app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, lines 34-40

## Problem
The `user-did` cookie set in the standard ATProto OAuth callback is missing the `sameSite` attribute:
```ts
cookieStore.set('user-did', session.did, {
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: config.isProduction,
  maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week
  path: '/',
  // sameSite is MISSING
});
```

Without `sameSite`, browsers default to `Lax` in modern browsers, but this is not explicit and may differ across browser versions. More importantly, the ePDS callback (app/api/oauth/epds/callback/route.ts, line 145-150) explicitly sets `sameSite: 'lax'` ŌĆö the two code paths are inconsistent.

The missing attribute also means the cookie could be sent on cross-site top-level navigations in ways that differ from the intended `lax` behavior, depending on browser implementation.

## Fix
Add `sameSite: 'lax'` to match the ePDS callback cookie:
```ts
cookieStore.set('user-did', session.did, {
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: config.isProduction,  // also see hypercerts-lv8 for the secure flag issue
  sameSite: 'lax',
  maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7,
  path: '/',
});
```

title: Fix: authEndpoint subdomain construction is injection-vulnerable if epdsUrl has a path (from hypercerts-dhz.3)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dhz found: In lib/epds-config.ts line 31, the auth endpoint is constructed as:

```typescript
authEndpoint: `https://auth.${hostname}/oauth/authorize`
```

where hostname = new URL(epdsUrl).hostname. This is correct when epdsUrl is a bare origin like 'https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app'. However:

1. If NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is set with a trailing path (e.g. 'https://pds.example.com/v1'), hostname is still 'pds.example.com' and the auth endpoint becomes 'https://auth.pds.example.com/oauth/authorize' ŌĆö which may be wrong if the PDS is path-mounted.

2. More critically: if NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL contains a port (e.g. 'https://pds.example.com:8080'), hostname is 'pds.example.com' (no port), so the auth endpoint drops the port. The auth service at auth.pds.example.com:8080 would be unreachable.

3. The epdsUrl is not validated to be a valid HTTPS URL at config load time (unlike baseUrl which has validateBaseUrl()). A misconfigured value like 'not-a-url' would cause new URL(epdsUrl) to throw an unhandled error at request time.

Fix: (a) validate NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL format in config.ts when it is set, (b) document that it must be a bare origin with no path, (c) consider using URL.origin instead of hostname to preserve port.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.14 (commit 98de1d5). Port is now preserved in auth endpoint construction using URL.host instead of hostname.

title: Fix: certs POST always creates rights record even when no rights provided (from hypercerts-czy.4)
description: Review of hypercerts-czy.4 found: app/api/certs/route.ts (lines 92-114) always creates an org.hypercerts.claim.rights record unconditionally, even when the 'rights' field is not provided in the form data. This creates an orphaned, empty rights record (containing only createdAt) in the user's repo for every hypercert creation. If the subsequent claim createRecord fails, the rights record is left stranded with no cleanup. Fix: (1) Only create the rights record when hypercertParams.rights is defined. (2) Make the rights field on the claim record optional. (3) Consider using an ATProto applyWrites transaction to make the two creates atomic.

title: Fix: PillToggle missing ARIA tablist/tab roles (login-dialog.tsx)
description:
## File components/login-dialog.tsx lines 26-51 ## Severity HIGH ŌĆö Accessibility ## Problem The PillToggle component renders two plain ``` ## Fix Add role="tablist" to the container div, role="tab" + aria-selected={active === 'handle'} + aria-controls="handle-panel" to each button, and id="handle-panel" / id="email-panel" to the corresponding form containers. ```tsx
// And on the form containers:
```

title: Fix: lint-staged runs prettier before eslint on .ts/.tsx files ŌĆö order may cause double-write
description:
Review of package.json lint-staged config (lines 15-22) found a potential ordering issue.

Evidence:
package.json lines 15-22:
  'lint-staged': {
    '*.{ts,tsx,mjs}': ['prettier --write'],
    '*.{ts,tsx}': ['eslint --fix']
  }

Issues:
1. For .ts and .tsx files, BOTH glob patterns match. lint-staged runs them in order: prettier first, then eslint --fix. If eslint --fix reformats code differently from prettier, the file will be written twice. This is generally fine but can cause unexpected diffs.

2. More importantly: eslint --fix runs AFTER prettier --write. If eslint --fix makes changes that violate prettier rules (e.g., adds trailing commas in a way prettier would reformat), the staged file will have eslint's output but NOT prettier's final pass. The committed code may not match 'pnpm format:check'.

Recommended fix:
Run prettier AFTER eslint, or combine into a single command:
  '*.{ts,tsx}': ['eslint --fix', 'prettier --write'],
  '*.mjs': ['prettier --write']

This ensures the final staged content always passes 'prettier --check'.

Alternatively, use the pattern recommended by prettier docs: run prettier last so it always wins on formatting.

title: Fix: upstream error bodies leaked to client in ePDS OAuth routes (from hypercerts-dhz)
Fix: logout route leaks internal error message to client (from security review)
description:
## File
app/api/oauth/logout/route.ts, line 19

## Problem
The logout error handler returns the raw exception message to the client:
```ts
return NextResponse.json(
  { error: `Logout failed: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}` },
  { status: 500 },
);
```

This can expose internal implementation details, Redis connection strings, SDK internals, or stack traces to the browser. The error is already logged server-side via `console.error` on line 17.

Note: hypercerts-x2b covers the same pattern in the ePDS routes (login/callback). This issue covers the standard logout route which was not included in x2b's scope.

## Fix
Return a generic error message to the client:
```ts
return NextResponse.json(
  { error: 'Logout failed. Please try again.' },
  { status: 500 },
);
```

The full error is already logged via console.error and will appear in server logs.

title: Fix: console.log left in production code (contributions-form.tsx line 105)
Fix: workScope sent by client but silently ignored by /api/certs/create route
description:
## Problem
There is a mismatch between what the client sends and what the server processes for `workScope`:

**Client side** (`lib/api/hypercerts.ts` lines 27, 52):
```ts
formData.append('workScope', JSON.stringify(params.workScope));
```

**Server side** (`app/api/certs/create/route.ts` lines 15-16):
```ts
// TODO map to proper workscope
// const workScopeRaw = formData.get('workScope') as string | null;
```

The server completely ignores the `workScope` field. The client always sends it (even serializing it to JSON), but the server never reads or passes it to `CreateHypercertParams`. This means hypercerts are created without their work scope, which is a core field of the hypercert data model.

## Fix
Either:
1. Uncomment and implement the workScope parsing on the server (parse JSON, validate, pass to `hypercertParams`)
2. Remove the `workScope` append from the client if it is intentionally not supported yet

The TODO comment suggests this is known but the client-side code was not updated to match, creating a silent data loss bug.

## Evidence
- `lib/api/hypercerts.ts` lines 27, 52: client sends workScope
- `app/api/certs/create/route.ts` lines 15-16: server ignores workScope with a TODO comment

title: Fix: rights field names mismatch between CreateHypercertParams and API route (from hypercerts-73o)
description: Review of hypercerts-73o found: CreateHypercertParams.rights uses field names {name, type, description} (lib/types.ts lines 61-65) but the API route (app/api/certs/route.ts lines 6-10, 97-104) reads {rightsName, rightsType, rightsDescription}. The form serializes rights as JSON and POSTs it; the route parses it into HypercertRights which expects the 'rights*' prefixed names. Result: rights records are always created with empty fields at runtime ŌĆö silent data loss. The lexicon schema (OrgHypercertsClaimRights.Main) uses rightsName/rightsType/rightsDescription. Fix: rename the rights sub-fields in CreateHypercertParams to match the lexicon and API route, OR update the API route to read name/type/description. Evidence: lib/types.ts:61-65 vs app/api/certs/route.ts:6-10,97-104 vs node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/types/org/hypercerts/claim/rights.d.ts:10-14.

title: Fix: Constellation measurement backlink uses singular subject.uri instead of subjects array (from hypercerts-phiw.11)
description:
Review of hypercerts-phiw.11 found: The Constellation backlink source string for measurements was updated from 'org.hypercerts.claim.measurement:subject.uri' to 'org.hypercerts.context.measurement:subject.uri' (collection NSID corrected), but the field path was NOT updated. In v0.10.0, the measurement record changed from singular 'subject' to plural 'subjects' (array). The correct source string should be 'org.hypercerts.context.measurement:subjects[com.atproto.repo.strongRef].uri'.

Evidence: lib/api/external/constellation.ts line 62:
  'org.hypercerts.context.measurement:subject.uri'  ŌåÉ WRONG field path

The measurement lexicon (org/hypercerts/context/measurement.json) defines:
  subjects: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'ref', ref: 'com.atproto.repo.strongRef' } }

The attachment backlink correctly uses the array syntax:
  'org.hypercerts.context.attachment:subjects[com.atproto.repo.strongRef].uri'

Fix: Change line 62 to:
  'org.hypercerts.context.measurement:subjects[com.atproto.repo.strongRef].uri'

Impact: getMeasurementLinks() returns zero results for all hypercerts, so the measurements section never loads.
close_reason: 2dc273b Fix measurement backlink to use subjects array

title: Spec inconsistency: task dqf.2 description contradicts its own code example (from hypercerts-dqf.2)
Fix: Buttons without type='button' inside forms may accidentally submit (contributions-form.tsx, evaluation-form.tsx)
description:
## Files
- components/contributions-form.tsx lines 230, 241
- components/evaluation-form.tsx lines 251, 278, 289, 447, 460, 513, 528

## Severity
MEDIUM ŌĆö Correctness / UX

## Problem
Several <Button> components inside <form> elements are missing type="button". In HTML, a button inside a form defaults to type="submit" if no type is specified. This means clicking 'Remove contributor', 'Add DID', 'Remove evaluator', 'Add Content URI', 'Remove Measurement URI', etc. can accidentally trigger form submission.

## Evidence
```tsx
// contributions-form.tsx line 230 ŌĆö no type prop
<Button
  variant="ghost"
  size="icon"
  onClick={() => removeManualContributor(index)}
  disabled={manualContributors.length === 1 || saving}
  className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-destructive"
>
  <Trash className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>

// evaluation-form.tsx line 278 ŌĆö no type prop
<Button
  variant="ghost"
  size="icon"
  onClick={() => removeManualDid(index)}
  disabled={manualDids.length === 1 || mutation.isPending}
  className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-destructive"
>
  <Trash className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

## Fix
Add type="button" to all Button components inside forms that are not intended to submit:
```tsx
<Button type="button" variant="ghost" size="icon" onClick={...}>
  <Trash className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

Affected buttons: all Trash/remove/add buttons in contributions-form.tsx and evaluation-form.tsx that lack type="button".

title: Fix: token response sub field not validated before use as DID (from hypercerts-dhz.6)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dhz found: In app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts, tokenData.sub is used directly as a DID (lines 122, 134, 140) without any validation that it is actually a DID string (did:plc:... or did:web:...).

If the ePDS token endpoint returns a malformed sub (empty string, null, or a non-DID value due to a server bug or MITM), the code will:
1. Write a session to Redis keyed by the invalid sub
2. Set the user-did cookie to the invalid value
3. All subsequent getSession() calls will fail silently or return undefined

The fix is to validate sub before use:
```typescript
if (!tokenData.sub || (!tokenData.sub.startsWith('did:plc:') && !tokenData.sub.startsWith('did:web:'))) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Invalid token response: missing or malformed sub' }, { status: 502 });
}
```

This also prevents the user-did cookie from being set to an attacker-controlled value if the token endpoint is somehow compromised.
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.13 (commit 6da38d4). DID validation at callback/route.ts lines 99-103 checks for did:plc: and did:web: prefixes before use.

title: Fix: next.config.ts remotePatterns are hardcoded test/staging hostnames ŌĆö not suitable for production
description:
## Problem
`next.config.ts` lines 14-41 hardcode specific hostnames in `images.remotePatterns`:

```ts
{ hostname: 'pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app' },  // test environment
{ hostname: 'epds1.test.certified.app' },          // test environment
{ hostname: 'climateai.org' },                     // specific production PDS
{ hostname: 'bsky.social' },                       // Bluesky PDS
{ hostname: 'cdn.bsky.app' },                      // Bluesky CDN
```

Issues:
1. Test/staging hostnames (`*.test.certified.app`) should not be in production config
2. The list is not exhaustive ŌĆö any PDS not on this list will have its images blocked by Next.js Image Optimization
3. `bsky.social` is a PDS hostname but blob URLs use `bsky.network` subdomains (e.g. `shimeji.us-east.host.bsky.network`), so Bluesky user avatars from non-CDN paths will be blocked

## Fix
1. Use a wildcard pattern for `*.bsky.network` to cover all Bluesky PDS shards
2. Use environment-based configuration to separate test vs production hostnames
3. Consider using `remotePatterns` with a wildcard for `*.certified.app` if that's the PDS domain pattern

```ts
{ protocol: 'https', hostname: '*.bsky.network', pathname: '/**' },
{ protocol: 'https', hostname: '*.certified.app', pathname: '/**' },
```

## Evidence
`next.config.ts` lines 14-41

title: Fix: unhandled exceptions in login/callback routes crash with 500 (from hypercerts-dhz)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dhz found: Both app/api/oauth/login/route.ts and app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts have no top-level try/catch. Any unexpected exception (network error on PAR/token fetch, JSON parse failure on PAR response, getEpdsEndpoints() throwing because NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is missing at runtime, sessionStore.set() throwing a Redis error, restoreDpopKeyPair() throwing on a malformed JWK) will propagate as an unhandled rejection and produce a Next.js 500 HTML error page instead of a structured JSON error response.

Specific unguarded paths:
1. login/route.ts line 65: `await parResponse.json()` ŌĆö if PAR returns non-JSON (e.g. a proxy error page), this throws and crashes the handler.
2. login/route.ts line 31-33: `getEpdsEndpoints()`, `getEpdsClientId()`, `getEpdsRedirectUri()` all throw if NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL is missing ŌĆö no catch.
3. callback/route.ts line 65: `restoreDpopKeyPair()` is called outside any try/catch ŌĆö a malformed JWK in the cookie (e.g. truncated by browser) throws here.
4. callback/route.ts line 134: `await sessionStore.set()` ŌĆö Redis errors are not caught; if Redis is down the user gets a 500 after a successful token exchange, and the session is lost.
5. callback/route.ts line 100: `await tokenResponse.json()` ŌĆö if token endpoint returns non-JSON, this throws.

Fix: wrap each route handler body in try/catch and return NextResponse.json({ error: ... }, { status: 502 }) for upstream failures and { status: 500 } for internal ones. Separately catch sessionStore.set() and log the error without crashing (the user-did cookie is already set, so the session can be recovered on next request).
close_reason: Already fixed in dhz.11 (commit b4f7ed9). Both routes have top-level try/catch, cookie decode has separate try/catch, PAR error returns 502.

title: Epic: Add contributor management and editor access from hypercert detail view
description:
## Goals
Enable owners to add/remove contributors and manage editor access directly from the hypercert detail view page, instead of only during creation.

## Context
- Contributors can currently only be added during hypercert creation (via the base form's optional contributors section)
- The standalone contribution form exists (contributions-form.tsx) but is disabled in add-record-form.tsx (shows 'coming soon')
- The addContribution server action already works (lib/create-actions.ts:56-78)
- Editor/collaborator management requires SDS (Shared Data Server) ŌĆö the app currently uses PDS only (lib/repo-context.ts:57)
- The SDK exposes collaborators.grant/revoke/list on Repository but throws SDSRequiredError on PDS
- The detail view already passes isOwner to sections and shows edit/delete buttons for owners

## Scope
1. Enable the contribution form in add-record-form.tsx so owners can add contributors from the detail view
2. Add an 'Add Contributor' button to the contributors section on the detail view (owner-only)
3. Add server actions for listing/granting/revoking editor access (requires SDS repo context)
4. Build an editors management section on the detail view (owner-only, SDS-only)
5. Handle the PDS-only case gracefully (hide editor management when not on SDS)

## Key Constraints
- The SDK's UpdateHypercertParams supports updating contributors inline on the record
- The SDK's addContribution creates a separate contribution record linked to the hypercert
- Editor management (collaborators) is SDS-only ŌĆö must detect isSDS and hide/show accordingly
- Reuse existing UserSelection component for searching Bluesky users
- Follow existing patterns: server actions in create-actions.ts, React Query mutations in queries/hypercerts/

title: Enable contribution form in add-record-form.tsx
description:
## Files
- components/add-record-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace the "Contribution form coming soon" placeholder (lines 90-95) with the actual `HypercertContributionForm` component, matching the pattern used for measurement/evaluation/evidence forms.

1. Add a dynamic import for `contributions-form.tsx` at the top of the file, following the same pattern as MeasurementForm/EvaluationForm/HypercertEvidenceForm (use `dynamic(() => import("@/components/contributions-form"))`)
2. Add a loading skeleton for the contribution form. Create a simple `ContributionFormSkeleton` in `components/form-skeletons.tsx` following the same pattern as `MeasurementFormSkeleton` ŌĆö a glass-panel div with a few Skeleton blocks.
3. In the `renderForm()` switch statement, replace the `case "contribution"` block (lines 90-95) with:
   ```tsx
   case "contribution":
     return (
       <ContributionsForm
         hypercertInfo={hypercertInfo}
         onNext={goBack}
         onBack={goBack}
       />
     );
   ```
4. Remove the `// ContributionsForm is currently disabled` comment (line 28)

## Don't
- Modify contributions-form.tsx itself
- Change any other case in the switch statement
- Add new props to ContributionsForm

This is used as the loading fallback for the dynamically imported ContributionsForm in add-record-form.tsx. 1. title: Update contributions-form.tsx step label and make it context-aware
description:
## Files
- components/contributions-form.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The contributions form currently shows "Step 2 of 5" (line 139) which only makes sense in the multi-step creation flow. When used standalone from the detail view (via add-record-form.tsx), this label is misleading. Update it to be context-aware.

1. The `FormInfo` component wrapping the form receives a `stepLabel` prop. Change the hardcoded "Step 2 of 5" to be conditional:
   - If `onBack` is provided (multi-step flow), show "Step 2 of 5"
   - If `onBack` is not provided (standalone), show nothing or a simple label like "Add Contribution"

Replace line 139:
```tsx
// Before
stepLabel="Step 2 of 5"

// After  
stepLabel={onBack ? "Step 2 of 5" : undefined}
```

2. Also remove the `// NOTE: contributions step is currently disabled` comment on line 139 since it is no longer disabled.

## Don't
- Change the form fields or submission logic
- Modify FormInfo component
- Change the title or description props

2. When rendered in multi-step flow (onBack is provided), "Step 2 of 5" still appears

title: Create HypercertEditorsSection component
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-editors-section.tsx (create)

## What to do
Create a new component that displays and manages editors (collaborators) for a hypercert. This section is only visible to the owner and only when the repository is on SDS. It follows the same visual pattern as HypercertContributorsSection.

### Component signature
```typescript
interface HypercertEditorsSectionProps {
  ownerDid: string;
  isOwner?: boolean;
  isSDS?: boolean;
}

export default function HypercertEditorsSection({
  ownerDid,
  isOwner,
  isSDS,
}: HypercertEditorsSectionProps)
```

### Behavior
1. **Guard**: If `!isOwner` or `!isSDS`, return `null` (render nothing).
2. **Data fetching**: Use `useEditorsQuery(ownerDid)` to fetch the list of editors.
3. **Display**: Show a section with:
   - Section header with Shield icon (from lucide-react) and title "Editors"
   - Subtitle showing count: "N editors" / "1 editor"
   - List of editors, each showing:
     - The editor's DID (truncated to first 20 chars + "...")
     - Their role badge (viewer/editor/admin)
     - A "Revoke" button (only for non-owner editors)
   - Loading state: show Skeleton placeholders (same pattern as ContributorSkeleton in hypercert-contributors-section.tsx)
   - Empty state: "No editors have been added yet."
4. **Add editor**: An "Add Editor" button that opens a Dialog (from `@/components/ui/dialog`) containing:
   - A UserSelection component for searching Bluesky users
   - A role selector (Radio group or Select) with options: "viewer", "editor", "admin"
   - A "Grant Access" submit button
   - Uses `useGrantEditorMutation` on submit
5. **Revoke editor**: Each editor row has a "Revoke" button that calls `useRevokeEditorMutation`

### Styling
Follow the same glass-panel pattern used in hypercert-contributors-section.tsx:
- `glass-panel border-border/50 rounded-xl border p-4` for editor rows
- `font-[family-name:var(--font-outfit)]` for text
- `font-[family-name:var(--font-syne)]` for headings
- Use `bg-create-accent/10` and `text-create-accent` for the icon circle

### Imports needed
```typescript
"use client";
import { useState } from "react";
import { Shield, UserPlus, Trash2 } from "lucide-react";
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from "@/components/ui/dialog";
import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from "@/components/ui/select";
import { Skeleton } from "@/components/ui/skeleton";
import UserSelection from "./user-selection";
import { useEditorsQuery, useGrantEditorMutation, useRevokeEditorMutation } from "@/queries/hypercerts";
import type { RepositoryRole } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";
import type { ProfileView } from "@atproto/api/dist/client/types/app/bsky/actor/defs";
```

Note: If `@/components/ui/select` does not exist, use a simple `<select>` HTML element styled with Tailwind instead. Check `components/ui/` directory first.

## Don't
- Show this section to non-owners
- Show this section when not on SDS
- Add transfer ownership functionality (out of scope)
- Modify any existing components
acceptance_criteria:
1. Component renders nothing when `isOwner` is false
2. Component renders nothing when `isSDS` is false
3. When both are true, shows the editors list with loading/empty/populated states
4. "Add Editor" button opens a dialog with user search and role selection
5. Granting access calls `useGrantEditorMutation` and closes the dialog on success
6. "Revoke" button calls `useRevokeEditorMutation` for the selected editor
7. Owner's own entry does not show a "Revoke" button
8. TypeScript compiles without errors (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
9. Follows the same visual style as HypercertContributorsSection

title: Pass isSDS and ownerDid to detail view and wire up editors section
acceptance_criteria:
1. `isSDS` and `ownerDid` are passed from the server page to the detail view component
2. HypercertEditorsSection is rendered between Contributors and Description sections
3. On PDS (isSDS=false), the editors section renders nothing (no visual change)
4. On SDS (isSDS=true) with isOwner=true, the editors section is visible
5. TypeScript compiles without errors (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
6. No visual regression on existing sections

title: Fix: No file size limit on evidence/location file uploads in add-attachment and add-location routes
description:
## Problem
`app/api/profile/update/route.ts` and `app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts` correctly enforce a 1MB limit on avatar/banner uploads (lines 21-32 in each). However, the other two upload routes have no equivalent limit:

- `app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts`: accepts `evidenceFile` (line 48) with only a `file.size === 0` check ŌĆö no upper bound
- `app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts`: accepts `locationFile` (line 58) with only a `file.size === 0` check ŌĆö no upper bound

An attacker or misbehaving client can upload arbitrarily large files, exhausting server memory and causing OOM crashes or denial of service.

## Fix
Add a maximum file size check immediately after the `file.size === 0` check in both routes. Choose a reasonable limit (e.g. 10MB for evidence files, 50MB for location data) and document it:

```ts
const MAX_FILE_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024; // 10MB
if (file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { error: 'File must be less than 10MB' },
    { status: 400 },
  );
}
```

## Evidence
- `app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts` line 50: only checks `file.size === 0`
- `app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts` line 60: only checks `file.size === 0`
- Compare with `app/api/profile/update/route.ts` lines 23-32 which correctly enforces 1MB limit

title: Fix: add-attachment API catch block exposes error in 'details' field not 'error' field ŌĆö toast shows generic message (from hypercerts-0h4.2)
description: Review of hypercerts-0h4.2 found: app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts was listed in the spec as a file to modify but was NOT changed by the 0h4.2 commit. The catch block currently returns { error: 'Internal server error', details: (e as Error).message } (line 122). The apiClient in lib/api/client.ts only reads the 'error' field from the JSON response (line 56), so the 'details' field with the actual error message is silently dropped. The mutation hook onError toast will show 'Internal server error' instead of the actual error reason. Fix: change the catch block to return { error: `Failed to add attachment: ${(e as Error).message}` } ŌĆö drop the 'details' field and put the message in 'error'. Evidence: git show 4a0ef47 --name-only (add-attachment not listed); cat app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts line 122; lib/api/client.ts line 56.
close_reason: 96729ad fix: put actual error message in 'error' field in add-attachment catch block

title: Fix: stagger-children CSS animation only covers 10 children ŌĆö 11th+ contributor/measurement/evidence card gets no animation delay
description:
## File app/globals.css lines 247-276 ## Severity LOW ŌĆö Visual polish ## Problem The .stagger-children CSS class defines animation delays for :nth-child(1) through :nth-child(10). Any list with more than 10 items will have the 11th+ items animate simultaneously with no delay, breaking the stagger effect. This affects contributor lists, measurement lists, evidence lists, and evaluation lists. ## Evidence ```css .stagger-children > *:nth-child(10) { animation-delay: 540ms; } /* No rule for :nth-child(11) and beyond */ ``` ## Fix Either extend the CSS to cover more children (e.g., up to 20), or use a JavaScript-based approach with inline style animation-delay: ```tsx {items.map((item, index) => (
))} ``` The JS approach scales to any number of items without CSS maintenance.
